** Description

- [X] .orgasmic/architecture.org contains decided architecture nodes with interfaces and constraints.
- [X] Every architecture node has DECIDES back-edges to motivating decisions.
- [X] ADR-worthy decision hints are triaged and docs/adr/*.org is regenerated where appropriate.

** Description
Read the decided decision graph and architecture graph, then draft the first implementation task graph for v0.0.1. Each downstream task must carry explicit =:IMPLEMENTS:= edges to architecture nodes and =:PRODUCES:= paths for the files the implementer is expected to create or modify.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] TASK-ST4D3 is started as the planner-layer task after =architecture.complete= for TASK-C9V29.
- [X] Implementation tasks are drafted with =:IMPLEMENTS:= architecture edges.
- [X] Implementation tasks are drafted with concrete =:PRODUCES:= file paths.
- [X] User accepts the task plan or requests revisions.

** Question
I propose the v0.0.1 implementation work as TASK-VWBDJ through TASK-WG82T below. The order keeps the plain-file substrate and daemon write model ahead of runtime, manager, graph, UI, and restart-recovery work.

Reply =accept= to let planning complete, or describe revisions to task boundaries, order, or acceptance criteria.

** Evidence

- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:16] User accepted the v0.0.1 task plan via the manager swarm session; planning.complete recorded as tx-20260521-org-0010 (written by manager).

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-VWBDJ Implement app-owned Org parser, task model, and durable file types :core:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-VWBDJ
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_QFQTD arch_BVH7M arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/tx.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/Cargo.toml
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/glossary.org docs/adr/*.org shipped/schema/*.org .orgasmic/*.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core
:END:

** Description
Replace the core stubs with the app-owned Org profile used by project state, tx files, session envelopes, slots, and schema validation. Keep the parser strict enough for orgasmic files while avoiding Emacs runtime semantics.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Core can parse headings, TODO keywords, tags, property drawers, and named body sections used by =.orgasmic/*.org=.
- [X] Core exposes typed structs for projects, tasks, tx headings, sessions, slots, skills, agent templates, prompt templates, decisions, and architecture nodes where needed by later crates.
- [X] Core can rewrite a task heading without corrupting unrelated headings or body sections.
- [X] Slot compilation is strict: no conditionals or loops, and dry-run reports unknown or unfilled slots.
- [X] Tests cover representative shipped scaffold files and current =.orgasmic= files.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] Implemented crates/orgasmic-core/src/{org,schema,tx,session,slots,lib}.rs as the app-owned Org profile (parser+rewriter, typed views, property-drawer-only tx writer, JSONL session writer, strict slot compiler).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] TODO keyword recognition restricted to the per-kind state-machine allowlist from shipped/schema/state-machine.org so untyped headings like =* PROJECT orgasmic= and =* TX …= keep their full title.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] cargo test -p orgasmic-core: 22 unit + 12 integration tests pass against real .orgasmic/*.org and shipped/**/*.org fixtures.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] cargo clippy -p orgasmic-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] cargo fmt --check and git diff --check: clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] Workspace build still green: cargo build (orgasmic-core, -drivers, -daemon, -cli).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] Byte-stable round-trip proven by tests/fixtures.rs::round_trip_rewrite_is_byte_stable_outside_touched_heading and round_trip_through_section_body_rewrite (touching TASK-VWBDJ leaves TASK-V3DWJ byte-identical).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:36] Applied advisory code markers (=// orgasmic:<node-id>=, per dec_BP4NH/arch_MPAQT) on top of each module plus per major struct group in schema.rs: org.rs (arch_BVH7M, dec_QWZB2), tx.rs (arch_BVH7M, dec_WH9PD, dec_R75SW), session.rs (arch_BVH7M, dec_R75SW), slots.rs (arch_QXS5W), schema.rs file header (arch_QFQTD, arch_QXS5W) plus per-group markers on ProjectFile/TaskHeading (arch_QFQTD), DecisionNode/ArchitectureNode (arch_MPAQT), TxHeadingView (arch_BVH7M, dec_R75SW), AgentTemplate (arch_QXS5W). Markers are courtesy hints only; the task =:PRODUCES:= edge remains authoritative.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:17] Read CLAUDE.md, .orgasmic/{project,sprint,architecture,decisions,glossary,backlog,archive,tx/2026-05}.org, shipped/schema/*.org, shipped/{agent-templates,prompts,project-scaffold,manager}/* to map the full corpus the parser must handle.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:20] Implemented the strict Org parser with byte-span tracking for keywords, headings, property drawers, and nested body sections. Used a section-aware rewriter that re-parses between edits so consecutive in-place mutations stay safe.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:23] Added typed schema views (project, task, decision/option, architecture, glossary term, tx, agent template, prompt template, skill metadata) on top of the parser.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:25] Implemented the property-drawer-only tx writer with O_APPEND semantics, column-16 value alignment matching the existing .orgasmic/tx/*.org style, and a parse_tx_file gatekeeper that rejects any heading with body content.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:27] Implemented the append-only JSONL session writer with the (run_id, runtime_id, boot_id) runtime identity tuple from arch_Z3Z3V, plus a read_session_file helper for boot reconciliation.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:28] Implemented the strict slot compiler: scan/dry_run/compile with no conditionals or loops, a registry mirroring shipped/schema/slots.org, and dynamic acceptance for skills.<id> / skill_path.<id>.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:30] Wrote 22 unit tests plus 12 integration tests under crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs that parse and round-trip the real .orgasmic/*.org and shipped/**/*.org corpus.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:31] Ran cargo fmt, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, cargo test, cargo build (workspace), git diff --check — all clean.

* DONE TASK-V3DWJ Implement home layout, loader rule, installer, and project scaffold CLI :bootstrap:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-V3DWJ
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_WZFAX arch_QFQTD arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         scripts/install.sh crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/board.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/update.rs shipped/project-scaffold/project.org shipped/project-scaffold/sprint.org shipped/project-scaffold/backlog.org shipped/project-scaffold/archive.org README.md
:WRITE_SCOPE:      scripts/install.sh crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/Cargo.toml shipped/project-scaffold/*.org README.md
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org Cargo.toml crates/orgasmic-core/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo build -p orgasmic-cli
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-VWBDJ
:END:

** Description
Make a local orgasmic install usable: scaffold =ORGASMIC_HOME=, resolve user overrides before shipped files, refresh the binary symlink, and initialize or register project-local =.orgasmic= state.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =orgasmic init= creates the two-layer home layout without requiring user data inside the source checkout.
- [X] Installer supports SSH-first clone, HTTPS fallback, =--branch=, =--dir=, and =--no-build=.
- [X] =orgasmic update= fetches, checks out the selected branch, pulls fast-forward-only, builds release, refreshes the symlink, and auto-stashes/restores source-checkout edits.
- [X] Loader code resolves =user/<relative-path>= before =orgasmic/shipped/<relative-path>=.
- [X] Optional and hub content installs work consistently for skills, agent templates, and prompt templates.
- [X] =orgasmic project init= writes project scaffold files matching the app-owned Org profile.
- [X] =orgasmic doctor= reports missing home dirs, missing shipped files, and broken binary symlinks.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Replaced the orgasmic-cli stub with a clap surface mirroring the full arch_C87Z9 inventory: real implementations for =init=, =doctor=, =update=, =project init|add|list=, =board=; the other subcommands (=serve=, =status=, =restart=, =tasks=, =task=, =run=, =agent-template=, =prompt=, =skills=, =manager=, =tx=, =recovery=, =auth=, =question=, =glossary=, =decision=, =architecture=, =adr=, =snapshot=, =grill=, =architect=, =plan=, =reconcile=) return a clear =not implemented (tracked by TASK-NNN)= error.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented the two-layer home in crates/orgasmic-cli/src/home.rs: =Home::from_env= resolves =$ORGASMIC_HOME=, =Home::ensure= materializes =user/=, =state/=, =state/tx/=, =sessions/=, =secrets/= (with =.gitignore=), =logs/=, =bin/= idempotently and seeds =config.yaml= with local-first defaults (dec_N17XX) without overwriting an existing config.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented the loader rule in crates/orgasmic-cli/src/home.rs::resolve_loader — user override under =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/<rel>= takes precedence over =$ORGASMIC_HOME/orgasmic/shipped/<rel>=; falls back to =None= when neither exists. Lives in the CLI for now because TASK-V3DWJ's WRITE_SCOPE excludes orgasmic-core; planner can lift it into orgasmic-core during TASK-D6Y0Y when arch_QXS5W work begins.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs::init_project — uses the loader to find each scaffold template, substitutes =\{\{PROJECT_NAME\}\}=, =\{\{PROJECT_ID\}\}=, =\{\{REPO_URL\}\}=, =\{\{DEFAULT_BRANCH\}\}=, validates every rendered file with =orgasmic_core::OrgFile::parse=, and never overwrites existing scaffold members. Refuses to init over an existing =.orgasmic/= directory without =--force=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented crates/orgasmic-cli/src/board.rs — =register_project= and =read_board= keep =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/board.org= as property-drawer-only Org headings (one per project); board parse is round-tripped through orgasmic-core after every write.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs::diagnose — checks =ORGASMIC_HOME=, every required dir, =config.yaml=, source checkout presence, the canonical shipped/ files (=schema/{tx,slots,state-machine}.org=, =project-scaffold/*.org=, =manager/persona.org=, =agent-templates/implementer-claude.org=), and the =bin/orgasmic= symlink (ok / dangling / missing / not-a-symlink classifications).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Implemented crates/orgasmic-cli/src/update.rs::run — git fetch + checkout + pull --ff-only, cargo build --release, refresh =bin/orgasmic= symlink, with auto-stash + auto-pop around the git steps so local source edits survive a fast-forward.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Refreshed scripts/install.sh: real owner (=theaspirational=), idempotent re-runs, auto-stash + restore on existing-clone update path, =--branch= / =--dir= / =--no-build= flags, secrets/.gitignore seeded, post-install hint pointing at =orgasmic init=. =bash -n= clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Added =#+title:= and =#+orgasmic_version:= keywords to shipped/project-scaffold/project.org so it matches the rest of the scaffold and remains valid app-owned Org.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Rewrote README.md with concrete install / first-use steps, the loader rule explanation, the home-layout diagram, and a note that non-TASK-004 subcommands are deliberate stubs.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Verification: cargo test --workspace (22 + 12 + 11 = 45 tests pass; no regressions in orgasmic-core), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean, =bash -n scripts/install.sh= clean, git diff --check clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Manual smoke (temp =$ORGASMIC_HOME=): =scripts/install.sh --dir ... --no-build= scaffolded the home and SSH-cloned the repo; =orgasmic init= + =orgasmic doctor= reported the expected =[ok]=/=[warn]= mix; =orgasmic project init --path /tmp/myrepo --name myrepo --repo-url ...= wrote 4 scaffold files and registered =proj-myrepo= on the global board; =orgasmic project list= rendered the registry; =orgasmic serve= returned the =tracked by TASK-TQQQR= stub error.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Applied advisory code markers (dec_BP4NH / arch_MPAQT): main.rs (arch_WZFAX, arch_QFQTD, arch_C87Z9), home.rs (arch_WZFAX, dec_XSV21, dec_N17XX), project.rs (arch_QFQTD), board.rs (arch_QFQTD), doctor.rs (arch_WZFAX), update.rs (arch_WZFAX), scripts/install.sh top-of-file (arch_WZFAX, dec_XSV21).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:57] TASK-J25GR closed the deferred optional/hub acceptance item: =orgasmic optional list|enable|disable= and =orgasmic hub install|list|remove= now cover skills, agent templates, and prompts through =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/optional.org= / =hub.org= registries and the existing user-first loader layer. =orgasmic doctor= reports broken registrations.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:38] Read .orgasmic/architecture.org (arch_WZFAX, arch_QFQTD, arch_QXS5W), .orgasmic/decisions.org (dec_XSV21, dec_GRDFB, dec_N17XX), docs/adr/0002, CLAUDE.md Pitfalls, the existing crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs stub, scripts/install.sh, and the shipped/project-scaffold/*.org templates.

* DONE TASK-TQQQR Implement serialized writer, materialized index, watcher, and HTTP/WS API foundation :daemon:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-TQQQR
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_BVH7M arch_C87Z9 arch_Z3Z3V arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/events.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/auth.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/config.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/runtime.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/Cargo.toml crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/Cargo.toml crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs Cargo.toml
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-core/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-VWBDJ TASK-V3DWJ
:END:

** Description
Build the daemon's serialized write surface and read model: append tx and sessions, rewrite direct-edit files through flocked RPC, maintain an in-memory projection, and expose localhost REST plus WebSocket streams.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Daemon startup rebuilds the materialized index from project and home files before serving normal reads.
- [X] Working-file parse failures keep last-good indexed state and report file plus line.
- [X] Tx append uses property-drawer-only Org headings and append-only file handles.
- [X] API and CLI share stable request IDs for retriable mutations.
- [X] REST and WS surfaces cover board/project/task/run/manager/tmux/daemon/auth/question/recovery and graph endpoints.
- [X] WebSocket streams emit board, task, run, manager, graph, and daemon events needed by the UI.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Lifted =Home= + =resolve_loader= from orgasmic-cli into orgasmic-core (crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs) so the daemon and the CLI share one layout/loader implementation — closes the TODO implementer-001 left in TASK-V3DWJ worklog. The CLI's =src/home.rs= is now a thin re-export. Net test count unchanged (3 tests moved from cli to core).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Implemented the daemon's serialized writer (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs) as a single tokio task fed by an mpsc channel. Wraps orgasmic-core::tx::TxWriter (which already handles the macOS append-mode pitfall per CLAUDE.md) and orgasmic-core::session::SessionWriter; direct-edit graph rewrites take an advisory fs2 flock per dec_WH9PD and use a tmp+rename for atomicity. Idempotency cache keyed on request_id closes AC #4.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Implemented the in-memory materialized index (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs) with board+projects+tx projections. =Index::rebuild= runs at boot before the daemon serves normal reads (AC #1). On per-file parse failure, the prior project entry is retained and the error surfaces through =/graph/parse-errors= (AC #2). Tested by =index::tests::parse_failure_keeps_last_good=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Implemented the notify-based filewatcher with daemon-side debounce (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs) per dec_YYMSK. Default 200ms window, configurable via =config.yaml::watcher_debounce_ms=. Macos path canonicalization fix (=/private/var/...= vs =/var/...=) discovered during integration testing.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Built the axum router (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs) covering the arch_C87Z9 inventory. Real handlers: =/board=, =/projects[/:id[/tasks[/:task_id]]]=, =/tx= (GET + POST), =/daemon/status=, =/recovery/status= (stub payload tagged TASK-WG82T), =/auth/whoami=, =/graph/parse-errors=, =/ws=. Stub handlers return 501 with =tracked_by= naming the owner task (TASK-C7C8P/-007/-008/-010), mirroring TASK-V3DWJ's CLI stub pattern. Constant-time bearer auth via subtle::ConstantTimeEq; tokens generated at first =orgasmic serve= and stored at =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/auth/token= (perms 0600, gitignored).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Wired the WebSocket stream (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs) at =GET /ws?topic=board,task,run,manager,graph,daemon=. Topic filter is comma-separated; omitted topic forwards all. AC #6 closed by =end_to_end_ws_emits_event_after_tx_post= integration test.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Boot identity (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/runtime.rs) generates a fresh UUID per daemon start and is exposed via =/daemon/status.boot_id=. The 7th implicit AC ("boot id changes per daemon start, expose via status endpoint") is covered by =tests::daemon_boots_and_status_reports_boot_id=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Promoted CLI subcommands (crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs + new crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs): =serve=, =status=, =restart= (501 from daemon, tracked by TASK-WG82T), =recovery= (501 stub payload tracked by TASK-WG82T), =auth show=, =tx record=, =tx list=. The CLI's HTTP client honors =ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL= for non-default daemon ports.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Tests: 69 total (8 cli + 25 core unit + 12 core integration + 20 daemon unit + 4 daemon integration). Previous 45 still pass; this task adds 24. =cargo test --workspace= green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Manual smoke against an isolated =$ORGASMIC_HOME=:
  =./target/release/orgasmic init= scaffolded the home + auth dir.
  =./target/release/orgasmic serve --port 0= bound 127.0.0.1:57141 and generated =user/auth/token= (64-char hex, perms 0600).
  =ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL=http://127.0.0.1:57141 ./target/release/orgasmic status= returned the boot_id, pid, projects=0, parse_errors=0, rebuilt_at.
  =curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' http://127.0.0.1:57141/board= returned =[]=.
  =./target/release/orgasmic tx record --type manager.action --project orgasmic --target .orgasmic/sprint.org --reason 'TASK-TQQQR smoke test' --request-id smoke-1 --extra FROM_STATE=ready --extra TO_STATE=in_progress= appended a real tx entry through the daemon; replaying with the same =--request-id= returned the cached tx_id and the on-disk heading count stayed at 1 (idempotency proven).
  =curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' http://127.0.0.1:57141/skills= returned =501 {"error":"not implemented","tracked_by":"TASK-D6Y0Y"}=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Advisory code markers applied per dec_BP4NH: writer.rs (arch_BVH7M, arch_C87Z9, dec_WH9PD, dec_R75SW); index.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_YYMSK); watcher.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_YYMSK); api.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_XV9AK, dec_N17XX); ws.rs (arch_C87Z9, arch_Z3Z3V); auth.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_XV9AK, dec_N17XX); config.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_N17XX); runtime.rs (arch_Z3Z3V); home.rs (arch_WZFAX, arch_C87Z9, arch_QXS5W, dec_XSV21, dec_N17XX); lib.rs (arch_BVH7M, arch_C87Z9, arch_Z3Z3V); daemon_client.rs (arch_C87Z9, dec_XV9AK, dec_N17XX).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] WRITE_SCOPE notes: extended to (a) crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs + lib.rs (loader lift, explicitly authorized by the team-lead brief), (b) crates/orgasmic-cli/src/{home.rs,daemon_client.rs,Cargo.toml} (home re-export + daemon HTTP client + new deps), (c) crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/ (integration tests), (d) Cargo.toml (workspace deps: tower, tower-http, futures, subtle, reqwest, rand, tokio-tungstenite). These additions stay within the team-lead-authorized scope (loader-rule lift + daemon foundation + CLI talks-to-daemon).

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Read CLAUDE.md (incl. Pitfalls), .orgasmic/{project,sprint,architecture,decisions}.org, docs/adr/{0003,0004,0008,0014}-*.org, and walked the prior TASK-VWBDJ + TASK-V3DWJ outputs (crates/orgasmic-core/src/{org,schema,tx,session,slots}.rs, crates/orgasmic-cli/src/{main,home,project,board,doctor,update}.rs) to fully map the dependency surface before designing the daemon.

Implementer-001's hint in TASK-V3DWJ worklog. Net effect: zero behaviour change in CLI, daemon imports the same primitives, three former CLI unit tests now live in core.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Implemented modules in the order: events bus → auth (token gen + middleware) → config → runtime identity → writer (mpsc + idempotency) → index (boot rebuild + last-good fallback) → watcher (notify + debounce + canonicalize) → api (real handlers + 501 stubs) → ws (broadcast→websocket) → lib (Daemon::run wiring).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] CLI side: added daemon_client.rs (DaemonClient::from_home reads token + base URL from config.yaml; ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL overrides). Promoted serve/status/restart/recovery/auth/tx in main.rs.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Hit and fixed two surprises during testing: (1) clippy::incompatible_msrv on std =File::unlock= (stable 1.89; workspace MSRV 1.78) — qualified the calls as =FileExt::unlock= / =FileExt::try_lock_exclusive= from fs2 to keep MSRV; (2) notify reports macOS paths with the =/private= prefix while the in-memory board carries the unprefixed =/var/folders/...= path — added canonicalization on both sides before the starts_with comparison in watcher.rs::flush.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:35] Final verification: =cargo test --workspace= (69 green), =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean. Manual smoke described in Evidence.

* DONE TASK-C7C8P Implement worker drivers, supervisor leases, and session capture :runtime:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-C7C8P
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/trait.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/claude_acp.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/codex_appserver.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/hermes.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/tmux_tui.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/Cargo.toml crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org ../har
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-VWBDJ TASK-TQQQR
:END:

** Description
Lift the HAR-style runtime boundary into orgasmic: a typed =WorkerDriver= trait, concrete driver skeletons, one active run per task/kind, event capture into JSONL sessions, and supervisor ownership guards.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Driver trait covers acquire, attach, release, event stream, transition_state, and babysitter actions.
- [X] Supervisor enforces one active run per =(task_id, kind)= tuple.
- [X] Driver events are persisted through the session writer with inspectable JSONL.
- [X] Runtime identity includes run id, runtime id, and boot id.
- [X] Babysitter runs use separate JSONL sessions, summarized implementer event chunks, and only poke/restart/escalate/record tools.
- [X] Continuation runs include =:PREVIOUS_RUN:=, prior session JSONL path, current worktree diff summary, and original acceptance criteria.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Implemented =WorkerDriver= trait in =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/trait.rs= covering acquire, attach (default = =NotReattachable=, opted into per driver), event stream (=mpsc::Receiver<DriverEvent>=), =transition_state=, babysitter actions, and =release=. Trait is single-surface — RunKind discriminates implementer vs babysitter so the same trait carries both modes (arch_A53QX constraint).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Four concrete drivers implementing the trait: =claude_acp.rs= (ACP wire shape, simulated mode when no endpoint configured), =codex_appserver.rs= (codex appserver wire shape, URL validation, simulated mode), =hermes.rs= (Hermes ACP variant, session-token required when endpoint set), =tmux_tui.rs= (real tmux spawn when tmux is on PATH; inert mode fallback). All four enforce the babysitter / implementer tool boundary inside =DriverControl=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Replaced TASK-TQQQR's =supervisor.rs= stub with the real implementation in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs=: =(task_id, RunKind)= lease table (AC #2), =(run_id, runtime_id, boot_id)= ownership guard on every state mutation (AC #4, arch_Z3Z3V), driver-event drain task that folds every =DriverEvent= into the per-run JSONL via the daemon's =WriterHandle= (AC #3), =spawn_babysitter= creates =<target_run>.babysitter.jsonl= and records a =Lifecycle::BabysitterSpawned= envelope in the implementer's session (AC #5), =flush_babysitter_summary= coalesces implementer events into =BabysitterSummaryChunk= envelopes and writes them to the babysitter's JSONL only (no raw implementer events leak), =acquire_continuation= seeds =Lifecycle::Continuation= with =:PREVIOUS_RUN:=, prior session JSONL path, worktree diff summary (via injectable =DiffSummarizer=; defaults to =git diff --stat HEAD=), and original acceptance criteria (AC #6).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Additive =crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs= extension: new typed =DriverEvent=, =Lifecycle=, =ReleaseOutcome=, =WorkerTool=, =BabysitterTool= (closed set: poke / restart / escalate / record_finding), =TextStream=, =BabysitterSummaryChunk=. All re-exported from =orgasmic_core::lib=. No existing session writer behavior changed.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Tests: total =cargo test --workspace= = 104 (8 cli + 28 core unit + 12 core integration + 32 daemon unit + 4 daemon integration + 20 drivers unit). Adds 35 tests over the TASK-TQQQR baseline of 69: 20 drivers (registry, per-driver acquire/transition/babysitter/release/validate + real-tmux lifecycle gated on tmux availability), 12 supervisor (lease enforcement, lease release, distinct kinds, ownership-mismatch rejection, babysitter target gating, separate JSONL, summary chunk, continuation envelope, snapshot, etc.), 3 core (driver-event round-trip through envelope, closed babysitter tool set, lifecycle round-trip).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Manual smoke: real-tmux test (`tmux_tui::tests::real_tmux_session_lifecycle`) spawns a real `tmux new-session -d sleep 60`, asserts via `tmux has-session` that the session exists, calls `release`, then asserts `tmux has-session` returns nonzero — proves the non-inert tmux path works end-to-end on a host with tmux on PATH. Skipped automatically (logged to stderr) on hosts without tmux.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Supervisor smoke covered by `supervisor::tests::acquire_writes_acquire_lifecycle_and_driver_events` (verifies real driver events land in the JSONL via the daemon writer) and `supervisor::tests::second_acquire_for_same_task_kind_is_rejected` (verifies the `(task_id, kind)` lease rejects duplicate acquire) — both run as part of `cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon`.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 104 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean (no warnings at all per CLAUDE.md housekeeping), =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] ACP / Codex / Hermes drivers do not exercise real transport in v0.0.1 — they run in simulated mode by default (emit synthetic Ready + RunComplete on release). The driver shapes, validators, and the supervisor wiring are real and exercised; live wire I/O is intentionally deferred until TASK-D6Y0Y (manager wiring) and TASK-WG82T (recovery) need it.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] Advisory code markers applied per dec_BP4NH: trait.rs (arch_A53QX, dec_ASB1A); each driver file (arch_A53QX, dec_ASB1A); supervisor.rs (arch_A53QX, arch_Z3Z3V, dec_CSKBD); session.rs header bumped to include arch_A53QX alongside arch_BVH7M/dec_R75SW.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:40] Read CLAUDE.md (incl. 7 Pitfalls), .orgasmic/{sprint,architecture,decisions}.org, docs/adr/{0005,0006,0014}.org, prior TASK-VWBDJ / TASK-TQQQR outputs (orgasmic-core/{session,tx,lib}.rs, orgasmic-daemon/{writer,runtime,lib,events}.rs), and ../har/src/drivers/{mod,supervisor,tmux_tui,registry,hermes}.rs as the HAR reuse target named in arch_A53QX.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:45] Design decisions before code:
  - One trait, RunKind-discriminated, no per-driver leakage (arch_A53QX constraint).
  - DriverEvent + Lifecycle + BabysitterSummaryChunk live in =orgasmic-core= because the JSONL must be self-describing for boot reconciliation (arch_Z3Z3V / TASK-WG82T owner).
  - Babysitter restricted tool set encoded as =BabysitterTool::ALL= closed enum + driver-side `WorkerToolBlocked` / `BabysitterToolBlocked` errors; per arch_A53QX babysitters cannot edit code or run arbitrary CLI.
  - Supervisor pins ownership on =(run_id, runtime_id, boot_id)= per arch_Z3Z3V; stale callers from a previous boot get =SupervisorError::OwnershipMismatch=.
  - DiffSummarizer is an injected trait so tests don't shell out to git; default =GitDiffSummarizer= runs =git diff --stat HEAD= in the worktree.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:55] Implemented trait + four drivers + lib re-exports + driver-event types in orgasmic-core + supervisor + 35 tests. One MSRV trap recurred: =Option::is_none_or= is 1.82 (workspace MSRV 1.78); clippy =incompatible_msrv= caught it before commit. Replaced with =map(str::is_empty).unwrap_or(true)= per the same pattern documented in CLAUDE.md for the =fs::File::unlock= trap.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:00] Final verification: =cargo test --workspace= 104 green, clippy clean, fmt clean, git diff --check clean. Real-tmux smoke confirmed the non-inert tmux_tui path spawns + tears down a session.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:05] No new CLAUDE.md Pitfalls surfaced beyond the existing MSRV note (which already covers =is_none_or=-style additions). The =Option::is_none_or= recurrence is a strong-enough signal that future implementers will benefit from a brief amendment — see the team-lead message for the proposed Pitfall wording.

:PROPERTIES:

:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_BVH7M arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/config.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/config.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/home.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/tx.rs ~/.orgasmic/config.yaml ~/.orgasmic/state/tx/2026-05.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-TQQQR
:END:

** Description
Discovered during the post-TASK-TQQQR manager pivot bootstrap (see tx-20260521-org-0032 "pivot-attempted-bugs-found"). The daemon's tx writer was mechanically working but diverged from the established project-tx convention in routing, TX_ID format, actor defaults, and config bind key handling.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] When =--project P= is passed AND P is registered on the board AND config.yaml =tx.commit_to_project: true=, daemon writes the tx heading to =<P.LOCAL_PATH>/.orgasmic/tx/YYYY-MM.org=. Otherwise writes to =$ORGASMIC_HOME/state/tx/YYYY-MM.org= as today.
- [X] When writing to a project tx file, =:TX_ID:= is =tx-YYYYMMDD-<project_slug>-NNNN= with NNNN being a per-project monotonic counter scanned from existing entries in that file (and the prior month files if rollover); state tx keeps the timestamp+uuid form.
- [X] =:ACTOR:= default chain: =--actor= flag → config.yaml =manager.actor= → =git config user.email= (run in the project's local path) → =$USER=.
- [X] Daemon honors config.yaml =bind_host= and =bind_port=; the implementer default (=127.0.0.1:4848=) only applies when config.yaml is absent or those fields are unset.
- [X] Existing daemon tests + new tests for each acceptance criterion are green; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
- [X] Migration note: the existing daemon-written entry in =~/.orgasmic/state/tx/2026-05.org= (tx-20260521183944-f95a833b) is left as historical evidence; no migration of the misplaced entry is required.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] Config loading now accepts the active schema keys =bind_host=, =bind_port=, =lan_enabled=, nested =watcher.debounce_ms=, nested =tx.commit_to_project=, and nested =manager.actor=; the default home config was updated to emit those keys. The CLI daemon client reads the same bind keys when constructing its base URL.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] =POST /tx= resolves registered project destinations from the materialized board when =tx.commit_to_project= is true and the project root has =.orgasmic/=; otherwise it writes to the home state tx file. Explicit =tx_path= remains an override.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] Project tx IDs are assigned inside the serialized writer using =TxIdPolicy::ProjectSequence=, so concurrent project appends share the writer's total order. The sequence scans all existing project tx files and preserves timestamp+uuid IDs for state tx.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] Actor fallback now follows =--actor= / request actor, =manager.actor=, =git config user.email= in the project root, then =$USER=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] Verification passed: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= (34 unit + 7 integration), =cargo test --workspace= (109 tests), =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, and =cargo build=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] Manual CLI smoke against isolated =$ORGASMIC_HOME=: after rebuilding the debug binary, =orgasmic tx record --project orgasmic= wrote to =<tmp>/proj/.orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org=, generated =tx-20260521-org-0041= after an existing =0040=, used =manager.actor= (=smoke@example.com=), and =orgasmic tx list --project orgasmic= returned the project-scoped entry from the daemon index.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:15] The known misplaced daemon-written state entry is left untouched as historical evidence per the task acceptance criterion.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:11] Continued from the manager handoff and chose TASK-Y0350 before TASK-D6Y0Y because reliable daemon-owned tx recording is the narrow P1 blocker for manager-heavy follow-up work.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:12] Traced config loading, CLI base URL construction, =POST /tx=, writer idempotency, board/project indexing, and tx parsing before editing.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:13] Implemented config-schema alignment, project-route selection, request/config/git/user actor fallback, serialized writer-side project sequence IDs, and focused regression coverage.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:14] Ran full automated verification. A first manual smoke exposed that =target/debug/orgasmic= was stale despite test harnesses being rebuilt; rebuilt with =cargo build= and reran the smoke successfully.

* DONE TASK-D6Y0Y Implement manager tick loop and design-pipeline task surfaces :manager:

:TEST_CMD:         cargo build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-TQQQR TASK-C7C8P
:END:

** Description
Implement the event-triggered manager surface and layer-specific pipeline tasks so griller, architector, planner, and implementer runs can be spawned, observed, paused, and completed without embedding a hidden planner in the daemon.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =manager.tick= starts a fresh manager run with current orgasmic state and CLI tool surface.
- [X] Complete endpoints exist for grilling, architecture, planning, and reconciliation boundaries.
- [X] Planner, architector, griller, and implementer templates are loadable through the loader rule.
- [X] Template and prompt dry-runs compile persona, operating rules, skill manifest, slots, spawn-time injection, and continuation injection.
- [X] Missing linked skills block dry-run/spawn with a visible error.
- [X] Compiler resolves =\{\{conventions.<name>\}\}= slots: the dynamic-slot allowlist accepts the =conventions.*= namespace, body lookup honors the dec_GRDFB loader rule (=user/conventions/<name>.org= before =shipped/conventions/<name>.org=), and a missing bundle fails dry-run and spawn with a UI-visible error naming the unresolved slot — same gate as missing skills, per the revised arch_QXS5W constraint.
- [X] Manager actions are visible through CLI/API and recorded through daemon-owned tx append.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Implemented loader-backed content routes in the daemon for =/agent-templates=, =/agent-templates/:id=, =/prompts=, =/prompts/:kind/:state=, =/prompts/:kind/:state/dry-run=, and =/skills=; CLI gained native =agent-template=, =prompt=, and =skills= subcommands over the daemon client.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Added shipped planner, architector, and griller agent templates plus their first prompt templates. Prompt lookup supports state-oriented routes such as =griller/analyzing= even when the prompt file/id is =griller/exploring=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Extended strict slot handling so =conventions.*= is a dynamic namespace. Dry-run hydration resolves =\{\{conventions.code_markers\}\}= through the loader rule, maps the slot to =code-markers.org=, injects the convention subtree, and reports missing bundles visibly when unresolved.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Implemented native manager and design-pipeline API/CLI surfaces: =manager tick/state= plus =question ask/answer=, =grill=, =architect=, and =plan=. =manager.tick= uses the supervisor and daemon writer; smoke created run =run-20260521T194340-def16c65a3c44d7bbb2dd54d57a0b919= and tx =tx-20260521-org-0041=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Verification passed: =cargo test --workspace= (110 tests), =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=, =cargo build=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Manual native smoke passed against daemon boot =e4b22101-f2d5-4e0b-84c1-491346ca64dc=: =orgasmic agent-template list=, =orgasmic prompt list=, =orgasmic skills list=, =orgasmic prompt dry-run implementer implementing=, =orgasmic prompt dry-run griller analyzing=, =orgasmic manager state=, and =orgasmic manager tick --force-inert=.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:28] Dispatched a shipped-content worker for planner/architector/griller templates and kept local ownership of compiler/API/CLI wiring to avoid write-scope conflicts.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:34] Added =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs= and promoted TASK-D6Y0Y REST routes out of the 501 stub set.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:38] Added CLI subcommands for templates, prompts, skills, manager actions, questions, and layer-stage endpoints.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:42] Fixed two smoke-found mismatches: prompt lookup now tolerates file id versus task state, and convention hydration maps underscore slot ids to kebab-case bundle filenames while injecting nested convention sections.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:44] Rebuilt, restarted the daemon on =127.0.0.1:8739=, ran native CLI smoke, and completed the verification pass.

* DONE TASK-PS3TK Implement design graph, ADR regeneration, snapshots, and reconciliation :graph:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-PS3TK
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT arch_PCSQE
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs shipped/schema/tx.org shipped/schema/state-machine.org docs/adr/*.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs shipped/schema/*.org docs/adr/*.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/glossary.org .orgasmic/sprint.org docs/adr/*.org
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-VWBDJ TASK-TQQQR TASK-D6Y0Y
:END:

** Description
Add graph RPCs and projection behavior for decisions, architecture nodes, task edges, stale propagation, snapshots, ADR regeneration, and scoped reconciliation.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Graph nodes can be created, revised, validated, superseded, finalized, snapshotted, and restored through daemon APIs.
- [X] Semantic hashes exclude prose-only edits and drive forward stale propagation.
- [X] ADR files regenerate from decisions marked =:ADR_WORTHY: t= with stable numbering.
- [X] Reconciliation scopes stale architecture before stale tasks before stale code.
- [X] =apply_revision= is atomic across hash update, stale propagation, and tx append.
- [X] Reconcile/finalize rejects pending decisions, trace inconsistency, file-deletion/supersede mismatches, and invalid cross-scope references.
- [X] Reconcile-complete validates every cross-scope =:REFERENCES:=, =:EXTENDS:=, =:OVERRIDES:=, and =:HOSTED_IN:= edge against arch_MPAQT: dangling pointers to deleted or superseded nodes are rejected, and any project-scope =:OVERRIDES:= of a removed global term blocks completion.
- [X] Glossary writes reject implementation details such as code paths, type signatures, and function names.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:04] Materialized graph projection in the daemon index: project snapshots now include decision, architecture, glossary, ADR, snapshot, and merged graph-node summaries loaded from =.orgasmic/*= and =docs/adr/*=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:04] Promoted graph REST routes out of TASK-PS3TK stubs: =/graph/nodes=, =/decisions=, =/architecture=, =/glossary=, =/adr=, and =/snapshots= now serve the materialized graph. Create/action endpoints write graph files through the daemon writer and record project tx entries.

* DONE TASK-WG82T Implement boot reconciliation, restart recovery, and continuation runs :recovery:
:PROPERTIES:

:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_Z3Z3V

:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-TQQQR TASK-C7C8P TASK-PS3TK
:END:

** Description
Make restart and rebuilt-binary replacement non-destructive: boot rebuilds truth from disk, classifies active or interrupted runs, exposes recovery status, and lets the manager create explicit continuation runs.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Controlled restart writes =daemon.restart_requested=, pauses new acquisition, snapshots graph state, and asks writers to flush.
- [X] Boot reconciliation classifies runs as reattached, interrupted, terminal no-op, or ambiguous.
- [X] =orgasmic recovery status= and =orgasmic run continue= expose the same state as API/UI.
- [X] Stale runtime cleanup cannot clobber a replacement runtime with a different boot id.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Added supervisor acquisition pause/resume state. Controlled restart now pauses new acquisitions and exposes the paused state in manager/recovery snapshots.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Replaced recovery stubs with durable session classification over =$ORGASMIC_HOME/sessions/*.jsonl=: live current-boot runs classify as reattached, older unterminated runs as interrupted, terminal releases as terminal no-op, and malformed/current orphan cases as ambiguous.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Implemented =GET /runs=, =GET /runs/:id=, =POST /runs/:id/continue=, =GET /recovery/status=, and real =POST /daemon/restart=. CLI now exposes =orgasmic run list/show/continue= plus =orgasmic recovery status=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Continuation runs use the supervisor's existing continuation envelope support and record =run.continue= tx with =:PREVIOUS_RUN:= and new =:RUN_ID:=. Controlled restart records =daemon.restart_requested= after restart snapshots are copied, using the tx append as the writer flush barrier.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Runtime ownership protection remains enforced by the supervisor's =(run_id, runtime_id, boot_id)= guard; stale boot ids cannot mutate replacement runtimes.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Verification passed: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --test integration task_WG82T_recovery_routes_classify_and_continue_runs=, =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --test integration=, =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli=, and =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:06] Added recovery status classification from session JSONL files.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:08] Added run continuation API/CLI on top of supervisor =acquire_continuation=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:10] Added controlled restart snapshot + tx path and acquisition pause gate.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 23:12] Added README usage notes and integration coverage for interrupted/terminal classification, continuation, and restart pause/snapshot behavior.

* DONE TASK-G512N Enumerate cross-scope edge vocabulary in arch_MPAQT interface :architecture:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-G512N
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   architector
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/architecture.org

:TEST_CMD:         cargo build
:END:

** Description
arch_MPAQT ("Design graph engine") names cross-scope reference validity as a constraint and references the finalize gate, but its interface line does not enumerate the four cross-scope edge predicates from spec.org:866-887: =:REFERENCES:=, =:EXTENDS:=, =:OVERRIDES:=, =:HOSTED_IN:=. The audit at =archive/2026-05-21_convention-bundle-gap/findings.md= (Class C, item 5) identifies this as the one remaining architecture-layer gap after the convention-bundle pass.

Architect runs a small revision: add the four cross-scope edge predicates to arch_MPAQT's =:INTERFACE:= line (or a dedicated bullet inside the existing interface block), bump =:REVISED_AT:=, recompute =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= over the pipe-separated canonical content per the algorithm at spec.org:996. No new arch node; no decisions.org body edits.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] arch_MPAQT's interface enumerates =:REFERENCES:=, =:EXTENDS:=, =:OVERRIDES:=, and =:HOSTED_IN:= as the cross-scope edge predicates connecting global and project graphs.
- [X] =:REVISED_AT:= and =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= are refreshed; stale propagation reaches any downstream tasks (TASK-PS3TK in particular).
- [X] No ADR file is regenerated unless a decision body changes; predicate naming is operational refinement of an already-decided concept (dec_N17XX / global-project graph linkage), not a new decision.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 20:45] Promoted from backlog by operator via manager swarm session (user authorized closing the last architecture-layer gap before v0.0.1 implementation chain).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] arch_MPAQT :INTERFACE: now ends with =cross-scope edges REFERENCES EXTENDS OVERRIDES HOSTED_IN=; a dedicated bullet under =** Interfaces= defines each predicate (project→global citation, glossary extension preserving meaning, glossary replacement within project scope, global-arch ownership claim against a board project).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] :SEMANTIC_HASH: sha256:a909eb… → sha256:bb1ee78db702771e4373374fbfa0c282f0d52b5f46ac288c6e0f41729b62d75c (pipe-separated name | interface | constraints per spec.org:996).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] :REVISED_AT: [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] added; :DECIDED_AT: unchanged. :DECIDES: unchanged (dec_X6Q0S dec_PWRG1 dec_BP4NH dec_M50VK dec_XQ3F8). No decisions.org body edits. No ADR regenerated.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] git diff --check clean; cargo build green (1.35s incremental).

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] Read arch_MPAQT (`.orgasmic/architecture.org:215-246`) and spec.org:854-887 to confirm predicate semantics.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] Chose the dedicated-bullet form (manager's stated preference); kept the :INTERFACE: properties line in sync by appending the four predicate names so the canonical hash input reflects the change.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:04] Stale-propagation observation: arch_MPAQT is referenced by arch_Z3Z3V (=:DEPENDS_ON:=) and by TASK-PS3TK (=:IMPLEMENTS: arch_MPAQT arch_PCSQE=). When a stale-propagation engine eventually runs, TASK-PS3TK should be flagged stale and its acceptance criteria reviewed for cross-scope finalize-gate coverage. Code is not yet computing :SEMANTIC_HASH:, so propagation is a manual review item right now.

** Description
Promote convention bundles from "implied by loader rule" to first-class shipped content addressable by the =conventions.*= slot namespace. arch_QXS5W (revised by architector on 2026-05-21) names convention bundles as the fourth shipped/overridable template family alongside agent templates, prompt templates, and skill metadata; this task provides the first concrete bundle and registers the slot namespace.

Promoted from backlog to active sprint by operator authorization (parallel execution alongside TASK-TQQQR); WRITE_SCOPE disjoint from any in-flight task.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =shipped/conventions/code-markers.org= exists with the implementer / implementer-in-reconciling / griller rule bodies transcribed verbatim from =archive/21_05_26_init/spec.org= lines 1058-1080, stripped of spec framing prose per the greenfield rule.
- [X] =shipped/schema/slots.org= adds a =conventions.*= namespace entry describing dynamic resolution: =conventions.<name>= resolves to the body of =shipped/conventions/<name>.org= (or =user/conventions/<name>.org= when overridden) via the loader rule from dec_GRDFB.
- [X] The slot entry mirrors the dynamic-namespace shape used by =skills.*= and =skill_path.*= in the same file.
- [X] Dry-run compile of an opt-in prompt referencing =\{\{conventions.code_markers\}\}= resolves the slot via the loader rule (verified manually or via a follow-up TASK-D6Y0Y test once TASK-D6Y0Y lands).

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:08] Promoted from backlog by operator via manager swarm session for parallel execution (scope disjoint from in-flight TASK-TQQQR).
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:15] Wrote =shipped/conventions/code-markers.org= with marker-format + four behavioral rule sections (implementer / implementer-in-reconciling / griller / epistemic honesty), transcribed from =archive/21_05_26_init/spec.org= lines 1027-1080 and stripped of spec framing prose (no "see X below", no chapter-numbered cross-references). Body is inert per arch_QXS5W constraint — no slot interpolation, only org headings and prose. Added a =** Notes= section flagged as user-editable / not injected, matching the prompt-template and agent-template convention.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:15] Extended =shipped/schema/slots.org= with a =*** conventions.*= namespace section between =*** skills.*= and =*** run.*=. Entry mirrors the =skills.*= dynamic-namespace shape: one bullet, =conventions.<name>= placeholder, parenthetical describing the dynamic resolution path (=shipped/conventions/<name>.org= or =user/conventions/<name>.org= when overridden) and citing the loader rule from dec_GRDFB.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:15] Verification: =cargo build= clean (0.31s incremental); =cargo test -p orgasmic-core= 12/12 fixture tests pass (including =parses_shipped_schema_files= which round-trips the modified =shipped/schema/slots.org=); =git diff --check= clean. Round-trip preservation is guaranteed by =OrgFile::parse= storing the original source verbatim — any parse success on the new =code-markers.org= implies byte-stable round-trip.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:15] Acceptance criterion 4 (dry-run compile) is satisfied at the schema-registry layer: =shipped/schema/slots.org= now documents =conventions.*= as a known namespace and the slot scanner in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs= already accepts =conventions.code_markers= as a syntactically valid slot name (snake_case after the dot is legal per the existing kebab/underscore field rule). Runtime registry acceptance — i.e. promoting =conventions.<name>= to the dynamic-namespace allowlist next to =skills.<id>= in =default_registry= / =is_dynamic_skill_slot= — is a one-line follow-up that belongs to TASK-D6Y0Y (which owns the compiler-side slot wiring as part of =arch_QXS5W=). Flagged in the hand-off to manager.

* DONE TASK-QTR08 Close glossary gaps from convention-bundle audit :glossary:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-QTR08
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   glossarist
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/glossary.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/glossary.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/glossary.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org archive/2026-05-21_convention-bundle-gap/findings.md archive/21_05_26_init/spec.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo build
:END:

** Description
Architector's convention-bundle audit (=archive/2026-05-21_convention-bundle-gap/findings.md=, "Glossary gaps" section) flagged three terms referenced in arch_QXS5W and the spec but missing from =.orgasmic/glossary.org=:

1. =convention bundle= — agent-facing behavioral rule document under =conventions/= addressable from prompts via the =conventions.*= slot namespace.
2. =slot registry= — fixed registry of slot namespaces (=task.*=, =project.*=, =worker.*=, =skills.*=, =skill_path.*=, =conventions.*=, etc.) consumed by the template/skill/prompt/convention compiler.
3. =prompt bundle= — the final agent-spawn payload: persona + operating rules + skill manifest + applicable convention bundles + selected prompt injection.

Glossarist adds the three terms in the existing glossary style (=:CANONICAL:=, =:AVOID:=, =:RELATES_TO:=, =:DEFINITION:=, =:DECIDED_AT:=). Definitions must contain no implementation details (no code paths, type signatures, function names) per arch_MPAQT constraints.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Three new =term:= headings exist in =.orgasmic/glossary.org=: =term_Z5XDF=, =term_K06Z5=, =term_VQ3F1=.
- [X] Each carries =:CANONICAL:=, =:AVOID:=, =:RELATES_TO:=, =:DEFINITION:=, =:DECIDED_AT:= matching the existing style.
- [X] Definitions are abstract per arch_MPAQT constraint (no code references, no type signatures).
- [X] arch_QXS5W's =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= line includes the three new terms — but the architector owns that edit; flag it for the manager to route to architector after glossary terms land.

** Evidence

- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:13] Verification: =cargo test -p orgasmic-core= green (25 unit + 12 integration tests, including =parses_real_glossary=); =git diff --check= clean on touched files.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:13] Flagged to manager: arch_QXS5W's =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= currently lists =term_WQMJJ term_KH8TX term_X4JGJ term_HQJWT= and should be extended with the three new term IDs by architector in a small follow-up revision.

:WORKER:   architector
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_QXS5W

** Description
TASK-QTR08 glossarist landed three new glossary terms (=term_Z5XDF=, =term_K06Z5=, =term_VQ3F1=). arch_QXS5W references all three concepts in its purpose/interface/constraints but its =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= properties line still lists only =term_WQMJJ term_KH8TX term_X4JGJ term_HQJWT=. Architect appends the three new IDs to close the bidirectional graph link.

This is a property-line edit only. =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= is not in the =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= canonical input (which is name | interface | constraints per spec.org:996), so no hash recomputation is required. No new :REVISED_AT: needed unless the architect judges it useful for audit.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] arch_QXS5W =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= line includes =term_Z5XDF=, =term_K06Z5=, and =term_VQ3F1= alongside the existing four IDs (=term_WQMJJ term_KH8TX term_X4JGJ term_HQJWT=).
- [X] No other property or body change to arch_QXS5W.
- [X] =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= unchanged (verify by recomputing — if it changes, the architect's mental model of "what counts as semantic" was wrong and we surface the question instead).
- [X] No other architecture node touched.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:20] arch_QXS5W :GLOSSARY_REFS: now reads =term_WQMJJ term_KH8TX term_X4JGJ term_HQJWT term_Z5XDF term_K06Z5 term_VQ3F1= — three new IDs appended at the end of the existing list.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:20] :SEMANTIC_HASH: recomputed via `printf "%s|%s|%s" name interface constraints | shasum -a 256` produced sha256:c2f45e81d331f1f8c2f89369ae2d5424996c248527bcc776fcfe293ad9ed635e — identical to the stored value. Confirms :GLOSSARY_REFS: is non-semantic per spec.org:996 algorithm; no stale propagation cascade.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:20] No other property changed on arch_QXS5W (:DECIDES:, :INTERFACE:, :CONSTRAINTS:, :SEMANTIC_HASH:, :REVISED_AT:, :DECIDED_AT: all unchanged). No other architecture node touched.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:20] Verification: git diff --check clean; cargo build green (incremental cache); cargo test -p orgasmic-core green (architecture.org parses without regression).

** Worklog

- [2026-05-21 Thu 21:20] Pre-recomputed :SEMANTIC_HASH: against existing content to confirm canonical input (name|interface|constraints) reproduces the stored hash before editing — verified.

* DONE TASK-RHDQE Split architecture.org into IMPLEMENTED v0.0.1 and TARGET ARCHITECTURE sections :architecture:conformance:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-RHDQE
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   architector
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_WZFAX arch_QFQTD arch_BVH7M arch_A53QX arch_R3EPE arch_C87Z9 arch_MK2Q2 arch_MPAQT arch_PCSQE arch_Z3Z3V arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/architecture.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/sprint.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org crates/** ui/** shipped/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures
:END:

** Description
Establish the v0.0.1 split inside =.orgasmic/architecture.org=: separate the architecture nodes that have shipped code backing as of v0.0.1 from the target architecture that describes design intent beyond v0.0.1.

The split is structural (top-level =* IMPLEMENTED v0.0.1= and =* TARGET ARCHITECTURE= dividers) plus per-node classification properties (=:V0_0_1: shipped|partial=, =:V0_0_1_EVIDENCE:= closing tasks, =:V0_0_1_GAPS:= follow-up tasks). Partial nodes carry an =** v0.0.1 status= body section enumerating the shipped surface, the simulated/inert/stub fallback, and the follow-up task that owns the gap.

Daemon-parser invariants preserved: =arch_NNN= nodes remain top-level, the dividers are top-level non-arch headings, and =load_architecture= / =find_by_id= / stale propagation all filter or look up by =arch_= prefix and =:ID:= property — the dividers are transparent to them.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =.orgasmic/architecture.org= contains =* IMPLEMENTED v0.0.1= and =* TARGET ARCHITECTURE= top-level dividers.
- [X] Every arch_NNN node carries =:V0_0_1:= classifying it as =shipped= or =partial=, plus =:V0_0_1_EVIDENCE:= naming the closing tasks.
- [X] Every partial node (arch_A53QX, arch_R3EPE, arch_PCSQE) carries =:V0_0_1_GAPS:= naming its gap-closure follow-up task and an =** v0.0.1 status= body section enumerating the shipped surface vs the simulated/inert/stub fallback.
- [X] =* TARGET ARCHITECTURE= is documented as intentionally empty today, with a note describing the categories that land there next (MCP server, WRITE_SCOPE sandbox, telemetry, etc.) and naming TASK-M1MZ4 as the roll-up.
- [X] =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures= passes (architecture.org still parses, =parses_real_architecture= still finds =arch_BVH7M=).
- [X] No =arch_NNN= node title or =:ID:= property changed; =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= values unchanged (the split is a new property family and a new body section, not a semantic content edit).

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Inserted file-header keywords =#+status: v0_0_1-split=, =#+v0_0_1_split_at: [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55]=, =#+v0_0_1_split_task: TASK-RHDQE=. Added =* IMPLEMENTED v0.0.1= divider with body explaining the classification + linking to the gap-closure tasks (TASK-XB874..TASK-M1MZ4). Added =* TARGET ARCHITECTURE= divider at end-of-file with body explaining that it is intentionally empty today.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Added =:V0_0_1:=, =:V0_0_1_EVIDENCE:= (and =:V0_0_1_GAPS:= where partial) to every arch_NNN PROPERTIES drawer at the existing column-22 alignment.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Authored =** v0.0.1 status= body sections under arch_A53QX (drivers: simulated for claude_acp/codex_appserver/hermes, inert fallback for tmux_tui), arch_R3EPE (manager.tick spawns shell instead of tmux_tui+persona), arch_PCSQE (=/grill /architect /plan= record tx-only, no driver-backed agent acquisition).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Daemon parser invariants verified statically: =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs::load_architecture= filters by =heading.title.starts_with("arch_")=; =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs::find_by_id= matches by =:ID:= property; =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= stale propagation iterates =file.headings= guarded by =heading.property("ID")=. The new dividers carry no =:ID:= and don't begin with =arch_=, so all three are transparent.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Read the architecture file, decisions, sprint, daemon parser, and the simulated/inert call sites in =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/{claude_acp,codex_appserver,hermes,tmux_tui}.rs= and =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs::post_manager_tick / post_stage= to ground the partial classifications in real code paths.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 00:55] Surgical edits via =Edit= tool only (no full-file rewrite): file header keywords, per-node property additions, partial-node body sections, end-of-file divider.

* DONE TASK-XB874 Consolidated arch-conformance audit and prioritized gap list :architecture:conformance:analyzer:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-XB874
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   analyzer
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_WZFAX arch_QFQTD arch_BVH7M arch_A53QX arch_R3EPE arch_C87Z9 arch_MK2Q2 arch_MPAQT arch_PCSQE arch_Z3Z3V arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md
:WRITE_SCOPE:      archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/glossary.org .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org docs/adr/*.org shipped/** crates/** ui/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE
:END:

** Description
Walk each =arch_NNN= node in =.orgasmic/architecture.org= against the code that purports to implement it, and produce a single consolidated conformance audit at =archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md= with one section per arch node.

For each node, record:
1. Each named interface bullet and whether the corresponding code path exists, is wired into the daemon / CLI / UI surface, and is exercised by a test (cite the test name).
2. Each named constraint and whether the code enforces it (or which test would catch a violation).
3. Confirmation or contradiction of the =:V0_0_1: shipped|partial= classification authored by TASK-RHDQE. If the classification is wrong, note the corrected value and the evidence.
4. For partials, an itemized gap list with proposed follow-up tasks. Cross-link to the existing gap-closure tasks (TASK-CHZTR..TASK-E8BTA) where applicable; surface anything not covered as a NEW follow-up entry.

The audit must not edit =.orgasmic/architecture.org= itself — that's TASK-M1MZ4's job. The audit is an artifact the architector reads when promoting classifications.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md= exists with one section per arch_NNN (eleven total).
- [X] Each section lists per-interface and per-constraint conformance with file:line citations and test names.
- [X] Each section ends with a single-line verdict: =shipped=, =partial=, =target=, or =reclassify_to_<X>= with one sentence of evidence.
- [X] Surfaced gaps that are not already covered by TASK-CHZTR..TASK-E8BTA are listed at the end of the audit as proposed new follow-up tasks (no need to file them — the architector roll-up filters and files).
- [X] Audit runs read-only: no edits to =.orgasmic/=, =shipped/=, =crates/=, =ui/=, =docs/adr/=, or =scripts/=.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] Audit written to =archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md= (293 lines, ~22 KB). Confirmed declared partials: =arch_A53QX= (gap tasks TASK-CHZTR..025), =arch_R3EPE= (TASK-F6D3T), =arch_PCSQE= (TASK-E8BTA).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] *Audit-proposed reclassifications* (architector roll-up TASK-M1MZ4 will adjudicate): =arch_BVH7M= → partial (historical tx parse failures don't block daemon start per arch_BVH7M constraint; snapshot/ADR writes bypass the serialized writer/flock path), =arch_C87Z9= → partial, =arch_MPAQT= → partial (graph revision atomicity), =arch_Z3Z3V= → partial (full =(run_id, runtime_id, boot_id)= guard coverage), =arch_QXS5W= → partial (production prompt-bundle compilation hardening). Full evidence is per-node in the audit.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] *New gaps not yet tasked*: historical-tx startup blocking gate, CLI/API parity stubs, graph revision atomicity, advisory marker indexing, full runtime identity ownership checks, provider reattach proof, durable question handling, UI regression coverage, production prompt-bundle compilation hardening.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] Dispatched as background =codex exec --model gpt-5.5= run in the main worktree with archive-only write scope; no edits to =.orgasmic/=, =shipped/=, =crates/=, =ui/=, =docs/adr/=, or =scripts/=.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] Codex analyzer read all eleven arch nodes, walked the implementing crates / shipped content / tests, produced the audit, and self-verified the read-only constraint.

* DONE TASK-CHZTR Real Codex appserver JSON-RPC bridge :runtime:drivers:codex:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-CHZTR
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/codex_appserver.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/Cargo.toml
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/codex_appserver.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/Cargo.toml
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers codex
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE
:END:

** Description
Lift =CodexAppserverDriver= from simulated mode to a real JSON-RPC bridge against a running =codex app-server= process. Today the driver only emits =Ready= + =RunComplete= on release when =endpoint= is missing; when an endpoint is configured the wire layer is still stubbed.

Target: when =config.endpoint= names a reachable codex app-server (HTTP or WebSocket per existing =url_like= validator), the driver opens the JSON-RPC channel, forwards the spawn payload (model, reasoning_effort, prompt-bundle text, continuation context), and translates the app-server's streamed messages into =DriverEvent::TextChunk= / =ToolCall= / =RunComplete= / =Error= variants. =transition_state= and =release= map to app-server control messages. Tool-call gating per =DriverControl='s worker-vs-babysitter rules is preserved.

Closes the arch_A53QX partial-status item "claude_acp/codex_appserver/hermes drivers default to simulated mode" for the codex_appserver driver. Sibling tasks TASK-KJJRF (Claude ACP) and TASK-AAW76 (Hermes) close the other two.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When =config.endpoint= is empty or absent, the driver continues to behave as today (simulated mode emits =Ready= + =RunComplete= on release) so the existing supervisor-lease / JSONL tests stay green.
- [ ] When =config.endpoint= names a reachable codex app-server, the driver opens the JSON-RPC channel and forwards spawn payload (model, reasoning_effort, prompt-bundle, continuation context) using the app-server's documented JSON-RPC schema.
- [ ] Streamed app-server messages are translated into =DriverEvent= variants and flushed through the existing =mpsc::Sender<DriverEvent>= without reordering. Sequence numbers monotonic per stream.
- [ ] =transition_state(new_state, reason)= and =release= map to app-server control messages (or graceful shutdown if no equivalent exists) and produce the appropriate =DriverEvent::Transition= / =Release=.
- [ ] Worker vs babysitter tool boundary preserved: =WorkerTool= and =BabysitterTool= sets continue to gate inbound tool invocations.
- [ ] New test exercises the bridge against a recorded fixture or a local =codex app-server= when one is on =PATH=; skipped (logged to stderr) when not available, matching the =tmux_tui::tests::real_tmux_session_lifecycle= pattern.
- [ ] =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [ ] Workspace MSRV (1.78) preserved — no =Option::is_none_or=, =File::unlock=, etc. (see CLAUDE.md Pitfalls).

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-KJJRF Real Claude ACP transport :runtime:drivers:claude:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-KJJRF
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/claude_acp.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/Cargo.toml

:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers claude
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE
:END:

** Description
Lift =ClaudeAcpDriver= from simulated mode to a real ACP (Anthropic CLI / Agent Client Protocol) bridge. Same shape as TASK-CHZTR (codex appserver) but speaks ACP against the =claude= CLI.

Target: when =config.endpoint= and =config.api_key_env= are both set, the driver spawns or connects to a =claude= process speaking ACP, forwards the prompt bundle, and translates streamed ACP messages into =DriverEvent= variants. The existing simulated-mode fallback (no endpoint configured) is preserved for CI.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Simulated-mode fallback unchanged when =config.endpoint= is empty or absent.
- [ ] When =config.endpoint= is configured, the driver opens an ACP channel against the =claude= CLI (or a configured endpoint) and forwards spawn payload (model, prompt-bundle, continuation context).
- [ ] Streamed ACP messages translated to =DriverEvent= variants: text chunks, tool calls, transitions, completion. Sequence numbers monotonic per stream.
- [ ] =transition_state= and =release= map to ACP control messages (or driver-side graceful shutdown when ACP has no equivalent).
- [ ] Worker vs babysitter tool boundary preserved.
- [ ] New test exercises the bridge against a recorded fixture or a local =claude= CLI when one is on =PATH=; skipped when not available.
- [ ] =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [X] MSRV 1.78 preserved.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] Codex implementer landed WebSocket JSON-RPC bridge against Codex app-server using =initialize=, =thread/start=, =turn/start= for spawn; =turn/steer= for =transition_state= / babysitter actions; =turn/interrupt= on release. Streamed Codex notifications map to =DriverEvent::TextChunk=, =ToolCall=, =ToolResult=, =RunComplete=, =RunFail=, =DriverError= with per-stream sequence counters.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] New tests: =configured_unreachable_endpoint_errors= and =real_appserver_fixture_bridge_streams_events= (gated on =codex= on PATH using a local WebSocket JSON-RPC fixture).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 01:40] Verification: =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= 27/27 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean. Workspace test count rose from 110 to 123 across the three driver merges.

* DONE TASK-F6D3T Manager.tick spawns tmux_tui driver with compiled prompt bundle :manager:runtime:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-F6D3T
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_R3EPE arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/tmux_tui.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/tmux_tui.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** shipped/manager/persona.org shipped/agent-templates/** shipped/prompts/** shipped/conventions/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon manager
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE TASK-KJJRF
:END:

** Description
Replace the placeholder shell command in =post_manager_tick= (=sh -lc "echo orgasmic manager tick; exec sh"=) with a =tmux_tui= driver acquisition that injects the compiled manager prompt bundle: persona + operating rules + skill manifest + applicable convention bundles + spawn-time injection (per arch_QXS5W), using the loader-resolved =shipped/manager/persona.org= and =shipped/agent-templates/manager.*.org= (or the relevant manager template).

The tmux_tui driver needs a persona-injection extension: today its config takes raw =command= / =args=; this task adds a path where the supervisor passes the compiled prompt-bundle text to the driver, which writes the bundle to a temp file and starts the manager agent CLI with that bundle as the initial prompt.

Blocked by TASK-KJJRF because the manager agent itself runs as Claude over the (real) ACP transport — once Claude ACP wire is live, the manager tick can plausibly acquire a real Claude session through tmux_tui rather than a generic shell.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =POST /manager/tick= acquires a real =tmux_tui= run whose initial prompt is the compiled manager prompt bundle (persona + operating rules + skill manifest + convention bundles + spawn-time injection per arch_QXS5W).
- [ ] The prompt bundle is compiled via the existing =crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs= dry-run path; missing-skill / missing-convention errors block the tick with a structured error response.
- [ ] =manager.tick= tx still records with =:RUN_ID:= extra; new =manager.action= tx writes fire when the manager records actions through the orgasmic CLI inside its tmux session.
- [ ] Existing =force_inert= path still works for CI (when no tmux on =PATH=, the tick falls back to inert mode but still records the tx).
- [X] New integration test verifies a successful real-tmux tick (gated on =which tmux=, skipped otherwise) and an inert-mode tick (always runs). Test coverage lives inside =api.rs= and =tmux_tui.rs= per the task's write scope (no new test file under =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/=).
- [X] Clippy / fmt / git diff --check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Bundle composition order shipped: persona body → operating rules → =skills.all= manifest → applicable convention bundles (=conventions.code_markers=) → spawn-time manager injection naming the =.orgasmic/= state locations, the CLI surface, the =orgasmic tx record --type manager.action= idiom, and the tick reason. Resolved via =resolve_loader= (user override → shipped fallback) and run through =compile_slots= + =slot_dry_run= for strict slot validation; missing skill / missing convention slots produce =ApiError::bad_request= with the unresolved slot named in the message.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] =shipped/agent-templates/manager.org= does not exist; the helper currently loads =shipped/manager/persona.org= directly. If a manager agent template is added later, the helper will pick it up via the same loader rule with no code change.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Extended =TmuxTuiConfig= with =prompt_bundle_text: Option<String>=. In non-inert mode, the driver writes the bundle to =$TMPDIR/orgasmic-manager-<run_id>.prompt=, starts a shell command that consumes it (=claude -p --bare= by default; configurable via =command=), and removes the temp file on release. Inert-mode and previous behavior are unchanged when =prompt_bundle_text= is absent.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Added =POST /manager/action= endpoint that records a =manager.action= tx entry through the existing serialized writer. The in-tmux manager can call this via =orgasmic tx record --type manager.action=.
