* DONE TASK-E8BTA Pipeline stage endpoints acquire driver-backed agent runs :pipeline:runtime:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-E8BTA
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_R3EPE arch_PCSQE arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** shipped/agent-templates/** shipped/prompts/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon stage
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE TASK-KJJRF TASK-F6D3T
:END:

** Description
Promote =POST /grill=, =POST /architect=, =POST /plan=, and =POST /reconcile= from tx-only stubs (today they only emit =<stage>.requested= via =post_stage=) to real driver-backed agent acquisitions. Each stage selects the matching agent template (=griller=, =architector=, =planner=, plus =glossarist= where applicable), compiles its prompt bundle for the target stage, acquires a driver run via the supervisor, and emits the existing =<stage>.requested= tx PLUS a follow-on =<stage>.completed= tx when the run releases.

Adds stage-completion plumbing the manager can cascade on per arch_R3EPE ("stage-complete endpoints let the manager cascade from grilling to architecture to planning to implementation").

Blocked by TASK-F6D3T because both touch =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= and TASK-F6D3T owns the persona-injection extension to tmux_tui that this task also relies on.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =POST /grill / /architect / /plan / /reconcile= acquire a driver run for the matching agent template and return the new =run_id= alongside the existing =tx_id=.
- [ ] Prompt bundle is compiled with the existing slot machinery; missing-skill / missing-convention errors block the spawn with a UI-visible error.
- [ ] On driver release with success, a =<stage>.completed= tx is emitted and the WS event stream announces stage completion (board / manager topic).
- [ ] On driver release with failure, a =<stage>.failed= tx is emitted with the failure reason; no =<stage>.completed= event.
- [ ] CLI =orgasmic grill / architect / plan / reconcile= subcommands surface the new =run_id= and (optionally with =--wait=) block until release.
- [X] New integration tests cover the success path (acquire → release → completed tx) and the failure path (acquire → release-with-error → failed tx). Tests live inside the touched files (no new test file under =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/= because the task's WRITE_SCOPE limited file additions).
- [X] Clippy / fmt / git diff --check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Per-stage mapping shipped: =grill → griller / griller/analyzing=, =architect → architector / architector/drafted=, =plan → planner / planner/planning=, =reconcile → implementer-claude / implementer/fixing= (uses =reconciling.org=). Default driver is =claude-acp= for all stages; configurable via the agent template's driver field.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] =api.rs= now compiles strict prompt bundles per stage, blocks unresolved skill/convention slots with =ApiError=, passes =prompt_bundle_text= in =DriverConfig= (same shape as TASK-F6D3T uses for the manager), acquires via =Supervisor::acquire=, records the existing =<stage>.requested= tx, and returns =run_id=, =tx_id=, =session_path=, =runtime_id=, =boot_id=. Unknown drivers fall back to inert tmux-tui; =claude-acp= / =codex-appserver= stay in simulated mode when their endpoint is empty (the simulated fallback that the TASK-CHZTR/TASK-KJJRF/TASK-AAW76 drivers preserve for CI).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Stage completion plumbing is API-local for now: an API-side watcher polls the supervisor until the run leaves the live set, then records =<stage>.completed= or =<stage>.failed= and publishes manager/board WS events. A future supervisor release-event hook would be cleaner (audit-surfaced; flagged for TASK-M1MZ4 backlog).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] CLI: =orgasmic grill | architect | plan | reconcile= subcommands added in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs= with =--project=, =--reason=, =--wait= flags. =--wait= polls =/runs/:id= until terminal status.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 131 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:10] Branch =task-027-pipeline-acquire= (commit =57f63e0=) merged as =518dc33= after resolving a single use-statement merge conflict in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= by union-of-imports.

** Worklog

:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-NX19C

:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs

** Description
arch_C87Z9 interface names =contradictions= and =ADR promote/demote= routes that don't exist. Implement minimal versions: =GET /contradictions= surfaces decisions or architecture nodes whose semantic hashes or status fields disagree across project/global graphs; =POST /adr/:id/promote= and =POST /adr/:id/demote= flip =:STATUS:= between =accepted= and =deprecated= and record a tx entry. CLI mirrors via =orgasmic adr promote|demote=, =orgasmic graph contradictions=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =GET /contradictions= returns a list of contradiction findings with type / nodes / detail; covers (at minimum) pending-decision conflicts and stale-architecture mismatches.
- [ ] =POST /adr/:id/promote= and =POST /adr/:id/demote= mutate =:STATUS:= via the serialized writer, record a tx, and emit a graph event.
- [ ] CLI subcommands shipped; new integration test covers each route.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-WASWJ Atomic graph revision across rewrite, stale propagation, and tx append :graph:arch_MPAQT:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-WASWJ
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-M1MZ4
:END:

** Description
arch_MPAQT constraint "apply_revision is atomic across hash update, stale propagation, and tx write succeed or fail together" is not honoured. Today the revise handler rewrites the file, refreshes the index, propagates stale, then writes tx — failures between steps leave partial effects.

Implementation: introduce a =WriterHandle::transaction= primitive that batches multiple file rewrites + a tx append behind a single serialized commit point. The transaction prepares all rewrites to a staging area (e.g., =target.tmp= files), syncs them, atomically renames into place, then appends tx. On error before commit, all staging is discarded; after commit, the changes are durable.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =WriterHandle::transaction= API accepts a batch of =FileRewrite= + =TxAppend= and commits all-or-nothing.
- [ ] =/decisions/:id/revise= and =/architecture/:id/revise= use the transaction; an injected failure between stale propagation and tx append leaves no on-disk effect.
- [ ] New integration test covers the failure rollback path.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-ZFMTD Reconcile/finalize cross-scope predicate gates :graph:arch_MPAQT:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZFMTD
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-M1MZ4 TASK-WASWJ
:END:

** Description
arch_MPAQT finalize/reconcile constraint mentions "file-deletion/supersede mismatches, and invalid cross-scope references" but =validate_reconcile= only rejects pending decisions and dangling outgoing edges. Implement gates for supersede mismatch (e.g., node referenced via =:REFERENCES:= was superseded) AND cross-scope predicate semantics for =:REFERENCES:=, =:EXTENDS:=, =:OVERRIDES:=, =:HOSTED_IN:= (predicates currently parsed but not validated).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reconcile rejects: a project-scope =:OVERRIDES:= whose target global term is deleted/superseded; =:REFERENCES:= to a deleted node; =:EXTENDS:= that breaks the global term's meaning (best-effort heuristic: extension only allowed when the global term still exists).
- [ ] Reconcile rejects file-deletion mismatches: graph file lists a node but the file doesn't contain it (or vice versa).
- [ ] New tests cover each rejection.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-ZPNQA Auto-snapshot before /grill and /reconcile :graph:arch_MPAQT:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZPNQA
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-M1MZ4 TASK-ZFMTD
:END:

** Description
arch_MPAQT interface: "Auto-snapshots precede user-initiated grilling and reconciliation kickoffs." Today =/grill= and =/reconcile= do not snapshot. Wire snapshot-take into both handlers before they record =<stage>.requested=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =/grill= and =/reconcile= take a graph snapshot before acquiring/recording the stage tx.
- [X] Snapshot ID is recorded in the stage tx as an extra (=:SNAPSHOT_ID:=).
- [X] New integration tests assert the snapshot exists post-call.
- [X] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] Codex implementer (gpt-5.5) extracted snapshot-take logic from =post_snapshot= into a reusable =take_snapshot(...)= path and added helper =auto_snapshot_for_stage(state, project_id, stage_name) -> Result<String, ApiError>= that wraps failures as =pre-stage snapshot failed: ...=.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] =post_grill= and =post_reconcile= now call =auto_snapshot_for_stage= BEFORE =post_stage=; acquisition is not attempted if the snapshot fails. Snapshot id is threaded into =post_stage(..., Some(snapshot_id))= and appended to the stage-requested tx extras as =:SNAPSHOT_ID:=.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] New test =api::tests::stage_routes_auto_snapshot_and_record_snapshot_id= covers both =/grill= and =/reconcile= — asserts the snapshot file exists post-call and the stage tx contains =:SNAPSHOT_ID:=.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] Verification (from worktree + re-verified on main): cargo test --workspace 153/153 pass (was 152, +1 from this task); clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt --check clean; git diff --check clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] Branch =task-034-auto-snapshot= (commit =1aa4941=) merged to =main= as =53a98a0=.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:25] Codex-noted surprise: snapshot creation was inline in =post_snapshot=, so the implementer extracted it before adding the stage helper — a minor refactor inside the authorized write scope.

** Worklog

* DONE TASK-T6KNE Advisory source-code marker indexing :graph:arch_MPAQT:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-T6KNE
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/** shipped/conventions/code-markers.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-M1MZ4 TASK-ZPNQA
:END:

** Description
arch_MPAQT interface promises advisory source-code markers are "indexed as hints". The =code-markers= convention specifies the format (=// orgasmic:<node-id>= at file top, group level, etc.). Today there's no marker index. Walk source files at rebuild, regex-match marker comments, build a per-node-id → file-list map, and expose via =GET /graph/markers/:node_id=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =Index::rebuild= scans project-root source files (Rust + TypeScript + shell + Org) for =// orgasmic:<id>=, =// orgasmic:<id>,<id2>=, and the shell/Python equivalent (=# orgasmic:<id>=).
- [X] Index exposes =markers: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<PathBuf>>= and surfaces it through =GET /graph/markers/:node_id= returning files referencing the node.
- [X] New tests cover scanning, multi-id markers, and the API route.
- [X] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:42] Codex implementer (gpt-5.5) used std-only recursive =read_dir= stack plus substring parsing for =orgasmic:= — no new workspace deps (regex/walkdir/ignore deliberately avoided).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:42] Project root canonicalized once at scan start (CLAUDE.md =/var/= vs =/private/var/= pitfall). Scanned extensions: =.rs=, =.ts=, =.tsx=, =.js=, =.jsx=, =.py=, =.sh=, =.bash=, =.zsh=, =.yaml=, =.yml=, =.toml=. Skipped: =.git=, =target=, =node_modules=, =.orgasmic/snapshots=, =dist=, =build=, plus top-level =.gitignore= directory entries (best-effort). =.org= scanning deferred for v0.0.1 to avoid Notes-section false positives.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 12:42] Data model: =IndexSnapshot.projects[*].markers: BTreeMap<String, Vec<PathBuf>>= keyed by normalized node id. Multi-id lines are split; =:opt_*= suffixes are stripped from the index key (option is a hint, not part of node identity); paths are project-relative and sorted.

** Description
arch_MPAQT graph-stream interface enumerates typed events: node creation/update, stale propagation, snapshots, stage completion, finalization, glossary, ADR, contradictions. Today everything is folded into a generic =GraphChanged= envelope. Replace with typed variants and emit at the right sites.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 13:40] Codex implementer (gpt-5.5) added eleven typed variants to =EventPayload= (events.rs +135 lines): =GraphNodeCreated=, =GraphNodeRevised=, =GraphNodesStale=, =SnapshotTaken=, =SnapshotRestored=, =AdrRegenerated=, =AdrPromoted=, =AdrDemoted=, =StageRequested=, =StageCompleted=, =StageFailed=. The legacy =GraphChanged= variant remains and continues to be emitted from =writer.rs= per file rewrite (compatibility layer untouched).
- [2026-05-22 Fri 13:40] Emission sites (api.rs +652 lines): =write_graph_and_record= emits =GraphNodeCreated= or =GraphNodeRevised= based on action plus =GraphNodesStale= when =compute_stale_propagation= surfaces downstream nodes. =take_snapshot= → =SnapshotTaken=. =post_snapshot_restore= → =SnapshotRestored=. =post_adr_regenerate= → =AdrRegenerated=. =mutate_adr_status= → =AdrPromoted=/=AdrDemoted= (chosen by =status= parameter). =post_stage= → =StageRequested= (carries =snapshot_id= for grill/reconcile auto-snapshots). =spawn_stage_completion_watcher= → =StageCompleted=/=StageFailed= alongside existing =ManagerNotice=/=BoardRefreshed=. Topics: =Graph= for node/snapshot lifecycle, =Daemon= for ADR, =Run= for stage lifecycle.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 13:40] New tests in =api.rs= boot a real daemon via =Daemon::run=, open WS subscription via =tokio-tungstenite= (already a dev-dep) filtered to the relevant topic, and assert typed payloads arrive: =graph_node_created_emits_typed_event=, =graph_node_revised_emits_typed_event=, =graph_nodes_stale_emitted_on_propagation=, =snapshot_taken_and_restored_emit_typed_events=, =adr_regenerate_promote_demote_emit_typed_events=, =stage_requested_event_emitted_for_grill_and_reconcile=, =stage_completion_event_emitted_when_run_completes=, =stage_failed_event_emitted_when_run_fails=. :TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-M1MZ4 TASK-DNF8Y

** Description
arch_Z3Z3V interface: restart "asks writers to flush". Implementation does not drain the writer task before reporting restart-ready. Also: arch_Z3Z3V constraint says "Reattach only when the driver can prove a live runtime handle"; today all drivers use the trait-default =NotReattachable=. Implement a real =attach= path: per-driver attempts to reconnect to a known-runtime-id session/process; on success the driver returns =DriverSession= bound to that runtime; on failure the supervisor flips the run to =interrupted=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =POST /daemon/restart= drains the writer queue (writer signals empty) before returning restart-ready.
- [ ] At least the =tmux_tui= driver implements =attach= against a known tmux session name; the ACP drivers return a structured =NotReattachable= classification (their wire protocols are stateless across reconnect, so attach is not possible — narrow the constraint in the worklog if needed).
- [ ] Recovery integration test covers writer-flush-on-restart and tmux attach proof.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy/fmt/diff clean; MSRV 1.78.

* DONE TASK-AQY23 Block daemon start on modified historical tx files :daemon:audit:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-AQY23
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-codex
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_BVH7M
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org docs/adr/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** archive/2026-05-22_arch-conformance/audit.md
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-RHDQE TASK-XB874
:END:

** Description
TASK-XB874 audit identified that arch_BVH7M's "modified historical tx files block daemon start" constraint is not enforced. Today, =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs::rebuild= scans historical tx files and collects parse errors into the per-file =parse_errors= map, but =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs::Daemon::run= continues booting normally even when historical-tx parse errors exist.

The intent of the constraint: working files (=sprint.org=, =backlog.org=, etc.) tolerate parse failures by keeping the last-good index — but historical tx files (=.orgasmic/tx/YYYY-MM.org= and =$ORGASMIC_HOME/state/tx/YYYY-MM.org=) are append-only audit logs that should never change retroactively. If one fails to parse, that's evidence of corruption or unauthorized retroactive edits, and the daemon refuses to start until the operator either reverts the edit or explicitly reseals.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] During =Index::rebuild=, classify per-file parse errors as either =working-file= (existing tolerant behavior) or =historical-tx= (new strict behavior).
- [X] When any historical-tx parse error is found during rebuild, =Daemon::run= refuses to bind / serve requests and returns a structured error naming the offending file and the line, with a hint about reverting / sealing.
- [X] Existing working-file last-good fallback behavior is preserved (=parse_failure_keeps_last_good= still passes).
- [X] New test in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs= covers: (a) a corrupted historical tx file blocks daemon start with a specific error, (b) a corrupted working file does NOT block start (last-good fallback).
- [X] =cargo test --workspace= green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [X] MSRV 1.78 preserved.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Classified parse errors in =Index::rebuild= as =working-file= (default, last-good fallback) or =historical-tx= (files under =$ORGASMIC_HOME/state/tx/= or project =.orgasmic/tx/=). Parser line numbers preserved with a first-heading fallback for tx-validation errors.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Added startup gate in =Daemon::run= that checks the rebuilt snapshot before writer/watcher/listener startup; on a historical-tx parse error, returns =HistoricalTxStartupError { path, line, message }= with display text including revert/git/reseal guidance.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] New integration tests in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs=: =corrupted_historical_tx_blocks_daemon_start= and =corrupted_working_file_does_not_block_start=.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Verification: workspace tests green (35 daemon tests, up from 28), =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Surprise: =*TX= (no space after the asterisk) lines are treated as prelude rather than failed-heading parses. Worth noting for the architector when assessing other parse-error edge cases.
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Branch =task-029-historical-tx-startup-gate= (commit =873113b3=) merged to =main=.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-22 Fri 11:05] Dispatched in parallel with TASK-F6D3T and TASK-E8BTA from an isolated git worktree; write scope disjoint from =api.rs= so the three ran independently.

* DONE TASK-YFAER Realign task lifecycle + add subtasks/comments/ownership-lock (UI-grill model refactor) :model:refactor:ui-redesign:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-YFAER
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_EVJYP arch_NAFA1 arch_QFQTD arch_BVH7M arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         shipped/schema/state-machine.org shipped/schema/tx.org shipped/schema/slots.org crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org .orgasmic/archive.org .orgasmic/project.org shipped/project-scaffold/project.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      shipped/schema/*.org shipped/project-scaffold/*.org crates/orgasmic-core/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org .orgasmic/archive.org .orgasmic/project.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/glossary.org docs/adr/*.org crates/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       dec_MKC0A dec_RECYG dec_CX17K dec_JW9TJ dec_A4V2N dec_JW11K
:MERGE_SHA:        397d89a
:CODEX_COMMIT:     2183138
:END:

** Description
Implement the schema and daemon changes that the 2026-05-23 UI grilling session locked in. Five model additions, one model realign, one new tx type, one new projection, one new slot. All landed in a single sprint task so the new UI sessions (3 onwards) can build against the new model without serialization tricks.

The five model additions:
1. *Task lifecycle realign* (dec_MKC0A). Collapse per-kind state machines to a single 6-stage lifecycle on the task heading: BACKLOG / EXPLORE / PLAN / IMPLEMENT / REVIEW / SHIP / DONE plus BLOCKED / CANCELLED. Update =shipped/schema/state-machine.org=, =crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs::TaskState=, =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs::TODO_KEYWORDS=. Migrate the existing 45 sprint headings to the new keywords (most map directly: =DONE=→=DONE=; =READY=→=BACKLOG= or =IMPLEMENT= depending on task; =analyzing/implementing/testing/fixing/merging=→folded into =IMPLEMENT= stage).
2. *Run sub-state* (dec_RECYG). Per-kind sub-states (=analyzing=, =implementing=, =testing=, =fixing=, =merging=, =planning=, =drafted=, =findings_recorded=, =reviewing=, =approved=, =requested_changes=) move off the task heading and onto the run record as a string field with three namespaces: =agent.<verb>=, =human.<verb>=, =ci.<verb>=. Add a =RunState= field on =RunSummary= and serialize through =DriverEvent= lifecycle.
3. *Subtasks* (dec_JW11K). Add =:PARENT_TASK:= property to =TaskHeading=. ID format =TASK-NNN.M=. Daemon's materialized index rolls up =subtasks: BTreeMap<TaskId, Vec<TaskId>>= from back-edges; index rejects orphan subtasks at boot/refresh. Subtask creation via =POST /tasks/:id/subtasks=. UI never directly mutates the org file.
4. *Comments + activity projection* (dec_A4V2N). New tx type =comment= per =shipped/schema/tx.org=: properties TX_ID, TIME, TYPE=comment, ACTOR, PROJECT, TASK, RUN_ID (optional), ARTIFACTS (optional), IN_REPLY_TO (optional), BODY (escaped markdown). New endpoint =POST /tasks/:id/comments= (writer routes through =POST /tx= internally). New projection in =index.rs=: =activity_index: BTreeMap<TaskId, Vec<TxEntry>>=; rebuilt on boot, incrementally updated on each =comment= or =manager.action= tx append. New endpoint =GET /tasks/:id/activity= returns chronologically-merged comments + state-transition tx + run lifecycle events from the JSONL session.
5. *Ownership lock model* (dec_CX17K, dec_JW9TJ). Add =:STAGE_AGENT_BACKLOG:= through =:STAGE_AGENT_DONE:= properties to =shipped/project-scaffold/project.org= and parse them in =ProjectFile= view. On a stage transition (whether DnD-via-tx or supervisor-driven), the daemon consults the stage-agent map and auto-spawns a run if a template is configured (empty = human-only). Supervisor reports =owner= on the run record: =human= when no active run, =agent.<kind>= when active. Add =POST /runs/:id/release= endpoint that cancels the active run and returns ownership to =human= (used by the UI's "Release agent" affordance).
6. *Activity slot for agent prompt compiler* (dec_A4V2N). Register =task.activity= slot namespace in =shipped/schema/slots.org=; extend =crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs::is_dynamic_skill_slot= to recognize it; loader fills from the same =activity_index= projection. Existing agent templates can opt in with ={{task.activity}}=; not required for v0.0.1 dogfood.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =shipped/schema/state-machine.org= rewritten with the 6-stage lifecycle as the unified task state machine. Per-kind run-sub-state machines documented separately.
- [X] =TaskState= enum in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs= replaced with =LifecycleStage= (9 keywords: BACKLOG EXPLORE PLAN IMPLEMENT REVIEW SHIP DONE BLOCKED CANCELLED).
- [X] =TODO_KEYWORDS= in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs= narrowed to the new lifecycle keywords.
- [X] Existing task headings in =.orgasmic/sprint.org= migrated. (40 headings, not 45 as the brief estimated; worktree was authoritative.)
- [X] New =RunSubState= field on the run record; allowed values gated by namespace (=agent.= / =human.= / =ci.=) per the dec_RECYG verb list.
- [X] =:PARENT_TASK:= property added to =TaskHeading=; subtask ID format validated; index rolls up =subtasks=; orphan rejected (surfaced via =/graph/parse-errors=).
- [X] New tx type =comment= written to =shipped/schema/tx.org= with the property list above.
- [X] New endpoints =POST /tasks/:id/comments=, =GET /tasks/:id/activity=, =POST /runs/:id/release=, =POST /tasks/:id/subtasks= implemented and exercised by integration tests.
- [X] =activity_index= projection in =index.rs= rebuilt on boot + incrementally updated on append; tested with rebuild-from-disk + post-append assertions.
- [X] =:STAGE_AGENT_*:= properties parsed from =project.org=; defaults populated in =shipped/project-scaffold/project.org=. Auto-spawn deferred via =EventPayload::AutoSpawnRequested= event (TASK-MGFZ7 follow-up) per the brief's scope clarification.
- [X] =task.activity= slot registered in =shipped/schema/slots.org= and accepted by =crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs::is_dynamic_skill_slot=.
- [X] All workspace tests green (183, +13 from 170 baseline); clippy =--workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; fmt clean; =git diff --check= clean.

** Migration plan
1. Inventory existing task states across =.orgasmic/sprint.org= (45 headings), =.orgasmic/backlog.org= (0), =.orgasmic/archive.org= (0). Map each to the new lifecycle keyword.
2. Snapshot before migration via =POST /snapshots= (label =pre-task-lifecycle-realign=).
3. Update =state-machine.org=, =schema.rs=, =org.rs= in one commit so parser + types stay in sync.
4. Update sprint.org headings in a second commit (with the snapshot as rollback if parsing breaks).
5. Add the new properties + tx types + endpoints in subsequent commits.
6. Verify =cargo test --workspace= after each commit (boot reconciliation must still succeed against the migrated files).

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 11:18] Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh, ~20 min wall, 371k tokens) implementer pass committed as =2183138= on branch =task-042-lifecycle-refactor=; merged no-ff into main as =397d89a=.
- =shipped/schema/state-machine.org= rewritten: one 9-keyword unified lifecycle (BACKLOG/EXPLORE/PLAN/IMPLEMENT/REVIEW/SHIP/DONE/BLOCKED/CANCELLED) at the top, per-kind run sub-state machines documented separately below.
- =crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs::TaskState= → =LifecycleStage= (9 variants); FromStr / serde / display aligned; =TaskHeading::lifecycle_stage= replaces =task_state=.
- =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs::TODO_KEYWORDS= narrowed to the 9 new uppercase keywords; the per-kind verbs no longer parse as TODO keywords (per CLAUDE.md pitfall — heading + parser change landed in the same commit).
- =crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs= adds =RunSubState= newtype with namespace-gated validation (=agent.= / =human.= / =ci.= prefix; non-empty lowercase verb).
- =:PARENT_TASK:= added to =TaskHeading=; daemon index rolls up =subtasks: BTreeMap<TaskId, Vec<TaskId>>=; orphan parent surfaces via =/graph/parse-errors=.
- New =comment= tx type (TX_ID, TIME, TYPE=comment, ACTOR, MACHINE, PROJECT, TASK, optional RUN_ID/ARTIFACTS/IN_REPLY_TO, escaped-markdown BODY); routed through the existing daemon writer; project-routing rules from TASK-Y0350 apply.
- =activity_index: BTreeMap<TaskId, Vec<ActivityEntry>>= projection in =index.rs= (rebuilt on boot, incremental on tx append); =GET /tasks/:id/activity= returns the chronologically-merged feed in O(comments_in_task).
- =POST /tasks/:id/comments=, =POST /tasks/:id/subtasks=, =POST /runs/:id/release= endpoints implemented + integration-tested.
- =:STAGE_AGENT_*:= property group added to =shipped/project-scaffold/project.org= and =.orgasmic/project.org= (defaults: EXPLORE=griller, PLAN=planner, IMPLEMENT=implementer-claude, REVIEW=reviewer; BACKLOG/SHIP/DONE empty). Auto-spawn emits =EventPayload::AutoSpawnRequested= for a future async consumer (TASK-MGFZ7) rather than synchronously acquiring; the brief explicitly authorised this scope clarification.
- =task.activity= slot registered in =shipped/schema/slots.org= and allowlisted in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/slots.rs::is_dynamic_skill_slot= (per CLAUDE.md pitfall — both files updated together).
- =.orgasmic/sprint.org= migrated: TASK-YFAER → IMPLEMENT (now DONE), TASK-X3YV2 → BACKLOG, 40 DONE headings unchanged keyword. No old-keyword stragglers (=grep -E '^\* (READY|ANALYZING|IMPLEMENTING|...)'= returns empty).
- Tests: workspace 170 → 183 (+13). New clusters: =crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs= (sub-state validation), =crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs= (lifecycle parsing, parent parsing), =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs= (activity_index rebuild + orphan detection + subtask roll-up), =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs= (comments / subtasks / release / activity / stage-agent map).
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 183 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.
- Codex notes (filed verbatim): "backlog.org has 120 lines and archive.org has 4 lines in this worktree (brief said empty), so left untouched"; "40 task headings, not 45". Both are honest sample-vs-brief drift; manager confirms both are intentional.

** Notes
This is a substantial single-task refactor. It is filed as one task rather than a Round of N because the schema/projection/endpoint changes are mutually-dependent: you cannot land =comment= tx type without the writer accepting it; cannot land the activity index without the comment tx type; cannot land subtasks without the index roll-up. Splitting would force ordering serialization without the benefit of parallel execution.

Verification command after each landing:
=cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check && git diff --check=.

After this task lands, UI sessions resume:
- *Session 3*: Tasks page (list + kanban + DnD + ClickUp-style modal + subtasks + activity rail) — depends on subtasks model, comment tx, activity projection.
- *Session 4*: Manager popover (peek/workbench/focus + xterm.js bridge) + bell + LED + gear + theme + TanStack Router migration — depends on the new tx topology only loosely.
- *Session 5*: Decisions + Architecture + Glossary + ADR + Snapshots + Activity + cross-domain Graph — depends on existing graph endpoints; no new model needs.

multica-ai/multica's =packages/views/= ships near-1:1 implementations of nearly every component we need. Use them as reference (read, don't bundle — their Next.js+Go stack diverges from ours). Key files:

* DONE TASK-X3YV2 Backfill existing sprint/backlog/archive tasks with new schema fields :model:dogfood:ui-redesign:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-X3YV2
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_EVJYP arch_NAFA1
:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org .orgasmic/archive.org .orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/backlog.org .orgasmic/archive.org .orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org docs/adr/*.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-YFAER
:MERGE_SHA:        0d73e48
:CODEX_COMMIT:     15a586c
:END:

** Description
Backfill the existing 45+ tasks in =.orgasmic/sprint.org= (and any in =.orgasmic/backlog.org=, =.orgasmic/archive.org=) with the new schema fields introduced by TASK-YFAER so that orgasmic becomes its own *first demo*. The goal: someone opening the new UI against orgasmic's data should see a populated dashboard with realistic task hierarchies, ownership history, completion comments, and an activity timeline — not a sea of empty fields.

This task is *synthesis*, not invention: every backfilled field has its source in the existing task body (Description / Acceptance Criteria / Evidence / Worklog) and existing tx entries. We're projecting the rich history we already have into the new typed schema.

** Scope: per-task backfill operations
For each existing task, apply the following operations (where applicable):

1. *Lifecycle stage migration*. Map current TODO keyword to the new keyword:
   - =DONE TASK-NNN= → =DONE TASK-NNN=
   - =READY TASK-NNN= (no active run, no DEPENDS_ON gating) → =BACKLOG=
   - =READY TASK-NNN= (committed to current sprint) → =BACKLOG= or =IMPLEMENT= per kind/intent
   - All other current keywords (analyzing/implementing/testing/...) → fold into =IMPLEMENT= for tasks not yet =DONE=
2. *Owner property*. Add =:OWNER: human= since no current task has an active agent run. This is the default state for any task without an active run per dec_CX17K.
3. *Synthesized agent-completion comment*. For each =DONE= task, write one =comment= tx entry to =.orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org= with:
   - =:TYPE: comment=
   - =:ACTOR: agent.<kind>= (using the task's :KIND: property)
   - =:TASK: TASK-NNN=
   - =:RUN_ID:= synthesized from the run record in the task's Evidence section if available, else =synthesized=
   - =:ARTIFACTS:= list of refs extracted from the Evidence section (merge SHAs, produced files, decision IDs, arch IDs)
   - =:BODY:= one-paragraph summary distilled from the Evidence section's first 1-3 lines
   - =:TIME:= synthesized from the task's =:LAST_UPDATED:= property
3. *Subtask back-edge inference*. Where a task is clearly a follow-up to another (TASK-NX19C was Round 2 of TASK-M1MZ4's rollup gap-closure; TASK-ZPNQA/TASK-T6KNE/TASK-XBT65 were Round 3; etc.), set =:PARENT_TASK:= to point to the parent. The current sprint has implicit hierarchy:
   - TASK-M1MZ4 (architecture rollup) → parent of the gap-closure tasks (TASK-BX0K0 through TASK-XBT65)
   - TASK-XB874 (conformance audit) → parent of any tasks the audit named
   - Other discretionary parent-child relationships per the implementer's review of Evidence sections
   - Use path-style IDs only for NEWLY created subtasks per dec_JW11K; existing tasks keep their flat IDs and gain =:PARENT_TASK:= properties.
4. *Activity log seed*. Comments + the existing =manager.action= tx + run lifecycle events form the per-task activity feed automatically via the new index. No extra seed needed beyond #3.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Every =DONE= task heading uses the new uppercase lifecycle keyword (=DONE=). (Verified by TASK-YFAER migration; codex confirmed during sweep.)
- [X] Every non-=DONE= task heading uses one of: =BACKLOG=, =EXPLORE=, =PLAN=, =IMPLEMENT=, =REVIEW=, =SHIP=, =BLOCKED=, =CANCELLED=.
- [-] Owner property intentionally skipped — TASK-YFAER's index derives owner from active-run presence, not heading properties. (Original AC superseded.)
- [X] At least one =comment= tx exists per =DONE= task: 39 synthesized comments at =tx-20260523-org-0069..0107= in =.orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org=.
- [X] Implicit task hierarchies captured via =:PARENT_TASK:= (19 back-edges; TASK-BX0K0..TASK-XBT65 → TASK-M1MZ4 plus additional discretionary edges codex found).
- [-] =GET /tasks/:id/activity= live-verification deferred — daemon needs rebuild+restart on the TASK-MGFZ7 binary first (manager prerequisite, not codex's responsibility).
- [-] Live UI verification deferred — UI sessions 3-5 ahead.
- [X] All workspace tests green (183 unchanged; no code touched); clippy clean; fmt clean; =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 11:43] Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh, parallel run with TASK-MGFZ7, ~12 min wall total, 174k tokens) committed as =15a586c=; merged no-ff as =0d73e48=.
- Appended 39 synthesized =comment= tx entries (=tx-20260523-org-0069..0107=), one per =DONE= sprint task. Each carries =:ACTOR: agent.<kind>= derived from task =:KIND:=, =:ARTIFACTS:= extracted from the task's Evidence section (merge SHAs, decision/arch IDs, ADR refs), =:SYNTHESIZED: t= flag, and a one-paragraph =:BODY:= distilled from the Evidence section's first 1-3 lines. =:TIME:= synthesized from each task's =:LAST_UPDATED:=.
- Added 19 =:PARENT_TASK:= back-edges in =.orgasmic/sprint.org=: TASK-BX0K0..TASK-XBT65 → TASK-M1MZ4 (the gap-closure rounds explicit in those tasks' Evidence) + additional discretionary edges codex found by scanning Evidence sections.
- Appended one trailing =manager.action= tx (=tx-20260523-org-0108=) documenting the backfill batch (codex authored this itself; slight scope overshoot but harmless self-narration).
- =.orgasmic/backlog.org= and =.orgasmic/archive.org= needed no migration (no task headings; only the file headers).
- No source-code change; tests 183 (unchanged from baseline). Clippy clean, fmt clean, diff check clean.
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 183 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.

** Notes
This task is the "make orgasmic be its own demo" task. It is *not* re-running history — it is *projecting* the existing rich history into the new typed schema. The truth-of-record (commits, merge SHAs, evidence text) is unchanged; we're adding shape so the new UI has something realistic to render.

Risk: synthesized agent.completed comments are post-hoc narration of work that happened weeks ago. Mark them clearly with =:SYNTHESIZED: t= in the tx properties so future readers can distinguish back-fill from live activity. The UI may render synthesized comments with a subtle "(backfilled)" badge.

This is best done sequentially after TASK-YFAER lands (schema must exist before backfill can validate). Single implementer round.

* DONE TASK-MGFZ7 Wire async AutoSpawnRequested consumer to Supervisor::acquire_task :daemon:supervisor:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-MGFZ7
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_EVJYP arch_A53QX arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/events.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/events.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org docs/adr/*.org crates/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-YFAER
:MERGE_SHA:        a9a0916
:CODEX_COMMIT:     70fbbd8
:END:

** Description
TASK-YFAER added the =:STAGE_AGENT_*:= property group on =project.org= + scaffold and emits =EventPayload::AutoSpawnRequested { task_id, kind, agent_template }= from the api/writer path on stage transitions whose target stage has a configured agent. Codex deliberately deferred the async consumer that actually calls =Supervisor::acquire_task= because invoking the supervisor synchronously from the writer/index path was out of scope for a single sequential implementer round.

This task wires the consumer: subscribe to =AutoSpawnRequested= events in the daemon's event bus (next to the existing typed-event consumers added by TASK-YRK91), call =supervisor.acquire_task(task_id, agent_template, default_overrides)=, and emit a new =run.auto_spawned= tx (or reuse =run.created= with a =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn= property if cleaner). The consumer must be idempotent against re-emission (e.g., daemon-restart re-fires of pending events) using the same =(task_id, kind)= lease check the supervisor already enforces; duplicate =AutoSpawnRequested= for an already-leased task should be a no-op, not an error.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Async consumer task spawned during =Daemon::run= subscribes to =EventPayload::AutoSpawnRequested=.
- [X] Consumer invokes =Supervisor::acquire_task= with the =agent_template= from the event payload; on success, emits =run.created= with =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn= (codex chose the property route over a new tx type to minimize vocabulary churn; documented in =shipped/schema/tx.org=).
- [X] Consumer is idempotent: =SupervisorError::LeaseHeld= on a duplicate event is a no-op (logged at debug, not a tx).
- [X] On supervisor acquire failure, the consumer emits =run.failed=; the daemon does not crash.
- [X] Integration test exercises the full path: POST =/tx= =task.state_transitioned= → consumer fires → =run.created= tx with =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn= appears → supervisor =active_runs= reflects the run → second identical transition is idempotent.
- [X] =:ORIGIN:= property documented in =shipped/schema/tx.org=.
- [X] All workspace tests green (183 → 184, +1); clippy clean; fmt clean; =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 11:43] Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh, parallel run with TASK-X3YV2, ~12 min wall total, 198k tokens) committed as =70fbbd8=; merged no-ff as =a9a0916=.
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs= +5 lines: =Daemon::run= now spawns an async =AutoSpawnRequested= consumer task next to the existing typed-event subscribers (TASK-YRK91 territory).
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/events.rs= +2 lines: =EventPayload::AutoSpawnRequested= payload extended with =project_id= + =stage= so the consumer can produce correct tx routing + STAGE extras.
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= +293 lines: the consumer implementation itself. Successful =Supervisor::acquire_task= emits =run.created= with =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn=. =SupervisorError::LeaseHeld= path is a no-op (debug log only). Acquire/setup failure emits =run.failed= with the error reason.
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs= +142 lines: end-to-end test. Spins up isolated daemon with a project whose =:STAGE_AGENT_IMPLEMENT:= maps to a stub-driver agent template; POSTs =task.state_transitioned=; polls for the consumer; asserts =run.created= tx with =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn= appears, =Supervisor::active_runs()= reflects the run, and a duplicate transition is idempotent.
- =shipped/schema/tx.org= +5 lines: =:ORIGIN: auto_spawn= documented as an optional property on =run.created=.
- Tests: 183 → 184 (+1). Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 184 green, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean.

** Notes
Write scope is strictly within =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/= + the daemon's integration tests + one schema file. Disjoint from TASK-X3YV2 (which lives in =.orgasmic/=), so the two can run in parallel codex worktrees without merge conflicts.

* DONE TASK-FB8CK CLI: `orgasmic manager dispatch` — atomic worktree+brief+nohup+pre-flight-tx :daemon:cli:manager-pipeline:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK
:PRIORITY:         P2
:MERGE_SHA:        ce5ff70
:CODEX_COMMIT:     c4cbd3a
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_R3EPE arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org shipped/manager/persona.org CLAUDE.md docs/adr/*.org crates/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
The manager handoff discipline (=shipped/manager/persona.org= rules 3+4 / =CLAUDE.md= "Manager role — dispatch-only, killable at any time") requires the manager to append a =manager.dispatch_started= tx before launching any background work that outlives a single manager reply (codex implementer, reviewer agent, etc.), so a future recall can find orphaned dispatches when a manager is killed mid-session.

Today this sequence is hand-coded by the manager each time:
1. =git worktree add /tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-NNN -b task-NNN-<slug>=
2. Write =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-NNN-brief.md=
3. =nohup codex exec ... < brief.md > stdout.log 2>&1 &=
4. Append =manager.dispatch_started= tx with =:TASK:=, =:WORKTREE:=, =:CODEX_BRIEF_PATH:=, =:STARTED_AT:=, =:CODEX_PID:=

This task wraps that sequence as one atomic CLI subcommand: =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-NNN --kind implementer --brief /path/to/brief.md --model gpt-5.5 --effort xhigh= (with reasonable defaults).

Behavior:
- Validates the task ID exists in sprint.org and is in a dispatchable lifecycle stage (BACKLOG / IMPLEMENT / REVIEW depending on =--kind=).
- Creates the worktree at the conventional path.
- Spawns codex with the standard flags from the CLAUDE.md contract.
- Appends the =manager.dispatch_started= tx via the daemon writer (atomic — if the writer rejects, the worktree is removed and codex is killed).
- Prints the inner codex PID and the watcher command for the manager to install.

Companion subcommand =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-NNN --status done|aborted= for the integration step: records =implementer.done= / =manager.dispatch_aborted= tx and removes the worktree.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-NNN --kind implementer --brief <path>= creates worktree, spawns codex, appends pre-flight tx, all atomically.
- [ ] On any step failure, cleans up: removes worktree, kills codex if spawned, deletes pre-flight tx if appended (best-effort; manager tx is append-only so deletion fails gracefully and the manager can record =manager.dispatch_aborted= instead).
- [ ] =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-NNN --status done= records =implementer.done= tx, removes worktree, optionally deletes the branch.
- [ ] =orgasmic manager dispatch-status= lists all open =manager.dispatch_started= tx without a close, with worktree state + codex PID liveness.
- [ ] New tx types (or `:KIND:` properties on existing tx) registered in =shipped/schema/tx.org=.
- [ ] Integration test exercises spawn → check status → close-done path end-to-end against a stub-driver codex (or a simulated dispatch).
- [ ] All workspace tests green; clippy =--workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; fmt clean; =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 20:32] Shipped in single commit =c4cbd3a= on =task-045-impl=; merged no-ff as =ce5ff70=. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, 32m42s wall, 372,921 tokens, session 019e55d6-5bcd-75f0-93da-be94612a2deb.
- 7 files changed, +1463 / -14 net. New: =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs= (1086 LOC — over the brief's 400-600 estimate, but justified by the full helper surface), =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs= (286 LOC — stub-codex + temp daemon integration smoke). Modified: =Cargo.lock=, =crates/orgasmic-cli/Cargo.toml= (libc dep), =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs= (+39 / -14 for the three new =ManagerCmd= variants), =shipped/schema/tx.org= (+62 schema additions for the four dispatch-cycle tx types), =crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs= (-2/+2 to relax actor-regex to accept =agent.*= actors — existing tx history needed it, noted in commit message).
- Three subcommands wired exactly as specified: =dispatch= (atomic worktree + tx + spawn detached via setsid; rollback on tx-fail or spawn-fail; pre-flight tx has no =:CODEX_PID:= per persona rule 4); =dispatch-close= (derives close tx type from open dispatch =:KIND:= → implementer.done / reviewer.done / manager.dispatch_aborted; removes worktree; optional branch delete); =dispatch-status= (scans tx files for open dispatches, annotates worktree existence + PID liveness via process-table lookup, =--orphans-only= filter).
- Schema additions register =manager.dispatch_started= / =implementer.done= / =reviewer.done= / =manager.dispatch_aborted= under the Manager section of =shipped/schema/tx.org= with the property set the manager has evolved (includes reviewer-specific =:VERDICT:= / =:FINDINGS_*:= / =:REPORT_PATH:= / =:RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS:= extras).
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace= clean (workspace tests + new dispatch.rs integration test), =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.
- Manager dogfooded the new CLI for TASK-FB8CK's own close: =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-FB8CK --status done --merge-sha ce5ff70 --codex-commit c4cbd3a --codex-session 019e55d6-... --tokens 372921 --wall 32m42s --branch-delete= → appended tx-0142, removed worktree, deleted branch. First end-to-end use of the new tool, successful.
- *Two limitations noted (small follow-ups):* (1) =dispatch-status= can't auto-resolve bundled-task closes (TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5} / TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4} dispatches list as orphans because individual subtask close txs use different =:TASK:= values than the bundled dispatch's =:TASK: TASK-EBWZ9.1 TASK-EBWZ9.2 ...=); (2) daemon writes =:TIME:= in UTC while manager hand-coded entries have been in local time, causing minor tz inconsistency in the audit log. Both acceptable for v0.0.1; could fold into a TASK-FB8CK.1 polish round if pressing.

** Notes
Priority P2 because the hand-coded pattern works; this is ergonomic + reliability hardening. Lower priority than UI Sessions 4-5 which directly unblock user-facing value. File before sessions 4-5 dispatch so the wrapping is available, but ship in the next-but-one cycle.

Reference: see CLAUDE.md "Manager role — dispatch-only, killable at any time" rule 4 and =shipped/manager/persona.org= operating rule 4 for the contract this subcommand wraps.

* DONE TASK-FB8CK.1 dispatch-status PID-derivation correctness + reviewer-stage validation :cli:manager-pipeline:bug:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK.1
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_R3EPE arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-045-review-report.md .orgasmic/sprint.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FB8CK
:MERGE_SHA:        c6a47a5
:CODEX_COMMIT:     803a4c3
:END:

** Description
Two correctness gaps in the dispatched CLI:

- *F-1 (medium bug, reviewer):* =dispatch-status= PID derivation (=manager.rs:955= =find_codex_pid=) treats any =codex exec= process containing the task id as a match. Reproduced live: with a stale implementer dispatch tx-0141 still recorded as open, =dispatch-status= reported its =CODEX_PID= as 31247, but ps showed 31247 was the reviewer dispatch's codex (different =--output-last-message= path). Result: =--orphans-only= can incorrectly suppress real orphans when a same-task reviewer/fix dispatch is running.
- *F-A (medium bug, manager-discovered via dogfood):* =dispatch= validation rejects =--kind reviewer= when the task is in lifecycle stage =done=. The manager workflow per persona rule 2 is: merge → implementer.done tx → flip subtask DONE → dispatch reviewer. So reviewer dispatch from DONE is the *normal* case, not an exception. Brief's "REVIEW | IMPLEMENT" allowlist was wrong; should be "REVIEW | IMPLEMENT | DONE" for reviewer kind (or add a =--force= / =--allow-stage= escape hatch).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1 fix.* =find_codex_pid= (or its replacement) matches the exact =--output-last-message= path derived from the open dispatch's =:CODEX_BRIEF_PATH:= property, not a task-substring on the command line. Brief filename → last filename pattern: =task-NNN-brief.md= ↔ =task-NNN-last.txt= (or whatever convention the dispatch CLI established — read =cmd_dispatch= for the actual last-path derivation). If exact match fails, fall back to "pid unknown" rather than weakly attributing.
- [ ] Add a regression test in =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs= that spawns two stub-codex processes for the same task id (one implementer, one reviewer) with different =--output-last-message= paths, then asserts =dispatch-status= attributes each to the right dispatch.
- [ ] *F-A fix.* =cmd_dispatch= validation for =--kind reviewer= accepts stages =REVIEW=, =IMPLEMENT=, AND =DONE=. Optionally also =SHIP= (between DONE and shipped). Update the error message to match. Document the rationale in the validation helper (persona rule 2: reviewer follows implementer.done flip).
- [ ] Add a regression test that =cmd_dispatch= accepts reviewer-from-DONE without =--force=.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 21:02] Shipped in bundled coupled commit =803a4c3= on =task-045.fixes-impl=; merged no-ff as =c6a47a5= (with TASK-FB8CK.2). Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~12 min wall, 191,661 tokens (shared with TASK-FB8CK.2), session 019e55f7-2974-7ff2-9887-83f335efc02a.
- F-A: =cmd_dispatch= validation now accepts reviewer kind from stages =REVIEW=, =IMPLEMENT=, =DONE=, =SHIP= (was REVIEW|IMPLEMENT only). Reject still applies to =BACKLOG / EXPLORE / PLAN / CANCELLED=. Code comment cites persona rule 2 + the manager workflow ordering.
- F-1: =find_codex_pid= now derives the exact =--output-last-message= path from open dispatch's =:CODEX_BRIEF_PATH:= property and matches that exact path against running =codex exec= command lines. No task-substring fallback. Returns =None= when not found rather than weakly attributing. Live verified: =dispatch-status= no longer false-positives across same-task dispatches.
- Regression tests in =tests/dispatch.rs= (+292/-? LOC across both subtasks): two stub-codex processes for same task id with different output paths attribute correctly; reviewer-from-DONE dispatch accepted via dry-run.
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.
- Manager rebuilt =target/debug/orgasmic= post-merge; live smoke =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-FB8CK --kind reviewer --dry-run= now prints the full plan instead of rejecting on stage.

** Notes
Tiny scope per finding (~30-80 LOC change in manager.rs + 50 LOC of tests). Both fixes ship in one bundled commit since they share the same file.

* DONE TASK-FB8CK.2 dispatch-close reviewer-outcome metadata + schema split :cli:schema:correctness:docs:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK.2
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_R3EPE arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-045-review-report.md .orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FB8CK
:MERGE_SHA:        c6a47a5
:CODEX_COMMIT:     803a4c3
:END:

** Description
- *F-2 (medium correctness, reviewer):* =DispatchCloseArgs= and =close_done_request= write a fixed extras subset (commit/session/tokens/wall/reason). CLI-produced =reviewer.done= records therefore cannot carry the reviewer-outcome metadata the manager has been recording by hand (=:VERDICT:=, =:BLOCKS_SHIP:=, =:FINDINGS_TOTAL:=, =:FINDINGS_BY_SEVERITY:=, =:FINDINGS_BY_KIND:=, =:REPORT_PATH:=, =:REVIEWED_DIFF:=, =:RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS:=). Also =--merge-sha= is required for both kinds even though reviewer.done has no merge.
- *F-3 (low docs, reviewer):* =shipped/schema/tx.org= documents =reviewer.done= as sharing the =implementer.done= property set, making =:MERGE_SHA:= and =:BRANCH:= look required for read-only reviewer closes. Recent real =reviewer.done= tx omit them.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-2 fix.* Add a repeatable =--property KEY=VALUE= passthrough to =dispatch-close= so any =:KEY: value= property can be appended to the close tx extras. Validate KEY is uppercase-snake. The hand-coded reviewer-outcome properties (=VERDICT/BLOCKS_SHIP/FINDINGS_*/REPORT_PATH/REVIEWED_DIFF/RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS=) all become CLI-recordable.
- [ ] *F-2 follow-up.* Make =--merge-sha= kind-aware: required for =implementer.done= closes, optional for =reviewer.done= and =manager.dispatch_aborted=. Replace with =--reviewed-diff= as the reviewer-specific structured input (still recordable via =--property= but elevated to a flag for ergonomics).
- [ ] *F-3 fix.* Split =shipped/schema/tx.org= per-type required vs optional lists. =implementer.done= keeps =:MERGE_SHA:= + =:BRANCH:= required + the optional metadata. =reviewer.done= has its own block with =:CLOSED_TX:= + =:VERDICT:= + =:REPORT_PATH:= required; =:BLOCKS_SHIP:= + =:FINDINGS_*:= + =:REVIEWED_DIFF:= + =:RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS:= optional. Common base (=:TX_ID:= / =:TIME:= / =:TYPE:= / =:ACTOR:= / =:MACHINE:= / =:PROJECT:= / =:TASK:= / =:CLOSED_TX:=) stays shared.
- [ ] Update integration test to exercise =dispatch-close --status done --kind reviewer-derived --property VERDICT=clean --property FINDINGS_TOTAL=0= and assert the appended tx carries those extras.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 21:02] Shipped in bundled coupled commit =803a4c3= on =task-045.fixes-impl=; merged no-ff as =c6a47a5= (with TASK-FB8CK.1). Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~12 min wall, 191,661 tokens (shared with TASK-FB8CK.1), session 019e55f7-2974-7ff2-9887-83f335efc02a.
- F-2: =DispatchCloseArgs= gained repeatable =--property KEY=VALUE= flag with uppercase-snake KEY validation (=[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*=); each property becomes a tx extra. =--reviewed-diff <git-range>= convenience flag added (also recordable via =--property REVIEWED_DIFF=...=). =--merge-sha= is now kind-aware: REQUIRED for =implementer.done=, OPTIONAL for =reviewer.done= / =manager.dispatch_aborted= with clear error message naming the failing case.
- F-3: =shipped/schema/tx.org= now splits =implementer.done= and =reviewer.done= into separate Required/Optional blocks. =implementer.done= keeps =:MERGE_SHA:= + =:BRANCH:= required + the optional metadata list. =reviewer.done= has =:CLOSED_TX:= + =:VERDICT:= + =:REPORT_PATH:= required + reviewer-outcome optional list (=:BLOCKS_SHIP:= / =:FINDINGS_*:= / =:REVIEWED_DIFF:= / =:RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS:= / etc.). Common base (=:TX_ID:= / =:TIME:= / =:TYPE:= / =:ACTOR:= / =:MACHINE:= / =:PROJECT:= / =:TASK:= / =:CLOSED_TX:=) shared.
- Integration tests (in the bundled +292 LOC under =tests/dispatch.rs=): exercise =dispatch-close --status done --property VERDICT=clean --property FINDINGS_TOTAL=0 --property REPORT_PATH=...= against a stub reviewer dispatch + assert the appended tx carries all three extras; exercise reviewer-kind close WITHOUT =--merge-sha= + assert success; exercise implementer-kind close WITHOUT =--merge-sha= + assert clear error.
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.

** Notes
Bundled with TASK-FB8CK.1 in a single fix-implementer dispatch because both touch =manager.rs= heavily and ship in adjacent commits would conflict. Single coupled commit pattern (like TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4} fix-implementer round). Close pattern: TWO =implementer.done= tx (one per subtask) sharing =:MERGE_SHA:= + =:CODEX_COMMIT:=.

* DONE TASK-EBWZ9 UI Session 4 — Manager popover + Notification bell + cross-domain Graph view :ui:ui-redesign:manager:notifications:graph:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-EBWZ9
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A1FGY arch_B7YH2 arch_NAFA1 arch_M8JQT
:PRODUCES:         ui/src/components/manager/ManagerPopover.tsx ui/src/components/manager/ManagerPeekBar.tsx ui/src/components/manager/ManagerWorkbench.tsx ui/src/components/manager/ManagerChatTranscript.tsx ui/src/components/manager/ManagerTmuxPane.tsx ui/src/components/notifications/NotificationBell.tsx ui/src/components/notifications/NotificationPopover.tsx ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx ui/src/app/App.tsx ui/src/app/routes.ts ui/src/lib/types.ts ui/src/components/ui/popover.tsx ui/package.json ui/package-lock.json
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/** ui/package.json ui/package-lock.json
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/goal.org .orgasmic/project.org .orgasmic/sprint.org docs/adr/*.org shipped/manager/persona.org shipped/schema/*.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cd ui && npm run build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-YFAER TASK-X3YV2 TASK-MGFZ7
:MERGE_SHA:        c362cd0
:CODEX_COMMIT:     cbeecd8
:END:

** Description
Replace the three remaining v0.0.1 UI placeholders that survived UI Sessions 1-3:
1. The Manager nav page (=ManagerView.tsx=) is a raw-JSON dump of =/manager/state= and a JSON tx feed. Per dec_DTJPD / dec_HY3E0 / dec_C6510, the manager UI should be a *permanent bottom popover* available on every page, with three sizes (peek / workbench / focus expand) and driver-adaptive content (xterm.js terminal for tmux_tui driver, structured chat bubbles for ACP-family). The dedicated Manager nav link goes away.
2. The top header has only a "WS live" badge + theme dropdown. Per dec_H9P38 / dec_JZDKT it should carry connection LED + notification bell + theme + gear. The bell exposes "needs you" items in three grouped sections: Questions / Interrupted Runs / Parse Errors.
3. =GraphView.tsx= is a tabbed table of raw decision/architecture/glossary/ADR/snapshot/node rows. Per dec_TQZTE, the Graph page should be a single *cross-domain react-flow visualisation* covering Decisions + Architecture + ADRs + Glossary (Tasks deliberately excluded), with per-type filter toggles, 1-hop hover highlight, and a click-into-side-panel for the focused node's body. Tabs and the raw-JSON details panel go away.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *Manager popover lives at the bottom of =AppShell=* on every page (board, project, tasks, task, runs, org, status, settings, graph), with three sizes: peek (~80px desktop / ~70px mobile), workbench (~50vh desktop / ~80vh mobile), focus (100vh). Drag handle (44px hit target) transitions between sizes. Keyboard: Cmd/Ctrl+\` toggles peek↔workbench; Esc collapses to peek. Persistent state (current size + open/closed) is stored in =sessionStorage=.
- [ ] *Manager popover peek bar* shows: driver tag (=tmux_tui= / =claude_acp= / =codex_appserver= / =hermes=), active-run count chip, last activity line truncated to one row, "Tick" button on the right. Pulsing teal dot when a run is acquiring; grey when paused.
- [ ] *Manager popover workbench/focus* shows: header strip (driver tag + run picker dropdown if >1 active run + size controls) on top; body adapts to the active run's driver (per dec_HY3E0):
  - =tmux_tui=: =ManagerTmuxPane= renders an xterm.js terminal. Daemon does NOT yet ship the =/ws/tmux/:run_id= WS bridge — render the terminal *but* fill it with a single line "Terminal bridge pending (TASK-EBWZ9 follow-up)" and leave the xterm.js component wired so the future endpoint can connect. Do not implement a fallback REST-poll; the placeholder is intentional.
  - everything else: =ManagerChatTranscript= renders the active run's session events as chat bubbles (user/assistant/tool alternation), seeded from =GET /runs/:id= (which already exists). Auto-scroll on new events arriving via the existing =useEventStream= hook (topic =run= or =manager=).
  - if no active run: empty-state card with "Tick manager" button (calls =postManagerTick= as today) + a one-paragraph explanation.
- [ ] *Top header* gains four icon-buttons on the right side (per dec_H9P38): connection-state LED (replaces the existing =WsIndicator= role; click → navigate to =view=status=), notification bell with badge count, theme toggle (existing), gear (click → navigate to =view=settings=). On viewports below =md= (768px) the theme + gear collapse into a single =MoreHorizontal= ⋯ dropdown. The LED renders four states matching =WsConnectionState= ('connecting' amber pulse / 'open' teal steady / 'reconnecting' amber pulse / 'closed' red).
- [ ] *Notification bell popover* (radix popover anchored to the bell button on desktop, full-screen Sheet below =md=) renders three grouped sections in this order: *Questions*, *Interrupted Runs*, *Parse Errors*. Each section header shows section name + count. Each row carries an inline primary action (Answer / Continue / Reload) and a Dismiss (✕) button. Bell badge count = sum of unread across sections. Empty state per section: "No <name>." When all three are empty: "All clear — nothing needs you." Footer link "View activity →" (navigates to =view=runs= for now since the Activity page is Session 5).
  - *Data sources*: Questions from any tx with =:TYPE: question.raised= without a matching =:TYPE: question.answered= for the same =:QUESTION_ID:= (codex: inspect =shipped/schema/tx.org= for property names and =fetchTx= results); Interrupted Runs from =fetchRecoveryStatus().interrupted_runs= (already exists); Parse Errors from =fetchParseErrors()= (already exists).
  - Primary-action behaviour: Answer scrolls/navigates to the related task drawer; Continue calls =postRunContinue(run_id)= (already exists); Reload calls =fetchParseErrors()= again after a toast.
- [ ] *GraphView rewrite* (=ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx=) is a single react-flow canvas, dagre-laid-out top-down, showing Decisions (teal), Architecture (blue), ADRs (amber), Glossary (gray). Edges: =DECIDES= (dec → dec), =DEPENDS_ON= (arch → arch), arch → motivating dec (=:DECIDES:= back-edge), dec → ADR (when =:ADR_WORTHY: t= and =:ADR_NUMBER:= set), dec/arch → glossary refs. Filter bar (top of the canvas): four toggle chips =[Decisions] [Architecture] [ADRs] [Glossary]= (default all on) — toggling hides/shows nodes of that type and edges touching only that type. Minimap bottom-right. Pan + zoom + fit-view controls bottom-right. Hover a node → 1-hop highlight (neighbors + edges full opacity, others ≈25% opacity). Click a node → open a right-side panel (=Sheet= with =side="right"=) showing the node's body fields (definition / interface / constraints / chosen / status / glossary refs / source link). The panel has a close button; navigating elsewhere closes it.
  - =GraphPanel= type (=decisions | architecture | glossary | adr | snapshots | nodes=) is no longer used by GraphView; remove the =graph= query-state param from =initialGraphPanel= / =useQueryState('graph', ...)= in =App.tsx= and =routes.ts=. Snapshots + raw graph-nodes inspection move out of this page (Session 5 / future).
- [ ] *Manager nav link removed* from =AppShell= GROUPS list. =view='manager'= becomes a no-op route (App.tsx renders nothing). The =View activity= footer-link in the bell still points at =view=runs= for now.
- [ ] *Dependencies added* to =ui/package.json= via =npm install --save= (commit the resulting =package-lock.json= changes): =@xterm/xterm=, =@xterm/addon-fit=, =reactflow=, =dagre=, =@types/dagre=. Reuse =@radix-ui/react-popover= via the existing radix-ui shadcn pattern (add =ui/src/components/ui/popover.tsx= following the shape of =ui/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx=). No CSS framework changes; everything keeps using Tailwind v4 tokens + shadcn primitives.
- [ ] *Mobile behaviour* (=<md=, 768px) per dec_NZ9PS: top header collapses theme+gear to ⋯ overflow; bell popover becomes full-screen =Sheet=; manager popover heights become peek 70px / workbench 80vh / focus 100vh; popover drag handle is 44px tap target. Graph filter bar wraps; the side panel becomes a full-screen Sheet.
- [ ] *Out-of-scope guard*: do *not* migrate routing to TanStack Router (dec_JSX48) — keep the existing =useQueryState= seam. Do *not* implement per-page Decisions / Architecture / Glossary / ADR / Snapshots / Activity views (dec_PA23Y / dec_E394G) — those are Session 5. Do *not* implement the project-selector-as-footer / 4-primary-nav restructure — that's a follow-up. Do *not* implement the =/ws/tmux/:run_id= daemon bridge — leave the placeholder line in =ManagerTmuxPane=. Do *not* remove =StatusView= or =SettingsView= or their nav links yet — the new LED/gear click-throughs work alongside the existing nav entries.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes (tsc --noEmit and vite build both clean).
- [ ] No unrelated lint regressions in =ui/src/=. If you touch a file with pre-existing TS or ESLint diagnostics in scope, fix them (Housekeeping rule from =shipped/manager/persona.org=).
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean on =main..task-046-session-4-ui=.

** Evidence
- [X] *Codex run.* gpt-5.5, xhigh, ~17 min wall (16:30 → 14:59:30), 239,673 tokens. Single commit =cbeecd8= on =task-046-session-4-ui= worktree; merged no-ff as =c362cd0= on main.

Created: =ui/src/components/manager/{ManagerPopover,ManagerPeekBar,ManagerWorkbench,ManagerChatTranscript,ManagerTmuxPane}.tsx= (237+73+178+208+52 lines), =ui/src/components/notifications/{NotificationBell,NotificationPopover}.tsx= (232+156 lines), =ui/src/components/ui/popover.tsx= (48 lines, shadcn-style radix wrapper). Modified: =ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx= (+138 net: LED + bell + theme + gear top-header cluster with sub-md ⋯ overflow; bottom-fixed ManagerPopover slot), =ui/src/app/App.tsx= (+24 net: ManagerView import gone, GraphPanel props removed from GraphView render branch), =ui/src/app/routes.ts= (+17 net: =initialGraphPanel= removed), =ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx= (751-line rewrite to reactflow v11 + dagre canvas), =ui/src/lib/types.ts= (+13 net: =QuestionEntry= type added, =GraphPanel= removed, =TxRecord.entry.extra= added). Deleted: =ui/src/components/ManagerView.tsx= (101 lines).

- [X] *Build.* =cd ui && npm run build= (=tsc --noEmit && vite build=): tsc clean; vite v6.4.2 transformed 2369 modules in 2.09s. Only warning: bundle > 500kb after minification (1062.21 kB → 312.89 kB gzip) — expected with reactflow + xterm; addressed in a future code-split polish pass.
- [X] *Chrome smoke* (claude-in-chrome MCP, desktop 1456x822, vite dev :5180, daemon :8739): Board page loads with new top header (LED steady teal / bell badge "2" / theme / gear); bell popover opens to "Needs you" with three grouped sections (QUESTIONS empty / INTERRUPTED RUNS 2 entries with per-row Continue + dismiss / PARSE ERRORS empty) + "View activity →" footer; Graph view loads as reactflow canvas with 116 nodes / 423 edges, 4 filter chips, dagre layout, minimap + controls; toggling Glossary + ADRs off reduces to 63 nodes / 245 edges (chip filtering wired correctly); manager peek bar shows driver tag "chat" + activity dot + "No active manager run" + Tick button; Cmd+\` expands to workbench mode (driver header + size controls + centered "No active manager run" empty-state card with "Tick manager" CTA); Esc collapses back to peek; Manager nav link gone from Coordination group. No JS exceptions during navigation. Mobile (=<md=) check is code-level via Tailwind breakpoints; not visually smoke-tested in this pass.

No project-selector / 4-primary-nav restructure. =/ws/tmux/:run_id= daemon bridge deliberately not implemented — =ManagerTmuxPane= writes a single "Terminal bridge pending (TASK-EBWZ9 follow-up)" placeholder line through the wired xterm.js component. StatusView + SettingsView and their nav entries retained (LED + gear click-throughs work alongside the existing nav).

** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-2, F-3, F-9 from =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-046-review-report.md=. Brings manager popover and notification bell to a11y + interaction-soundness parity with shadcn/radix conventions.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-2 (medium a11y) — Manager popover dialog semantics.* When =size === 'focus'=, root carries =role="dialog"= + =aria-modal="true"= + =aria-labelledby= pointing at a heading element; focus trapped within the popover; background is inert (or focus-trap library equivalent). Opening peek→workbench or peek→focus moves keyboard focus to the first actionable control inside the popover (currently nothing receives focus). Collapsing restores focus to the original trigger. Peek-bar trigger button carries =aria-expanded={size !== 'peek'}= + =aria-controls={id-of-content-region}=.
- [ ] *F-2 (medium a11y) — Global Escape disambiguation.* The Escape keydown handler in =ManagerPopover= must not collapse the popover when focus is inside a text input/textarea/contenteditable, OR when another radix overlay is open (radix Sheets/Popovers manage their own Escape). Simplest path: bail out of the collapse handler if =document.activeElement= is editable or if a higher-priority radix overlay has the focus root.
- [ ] *F-3 (low a11y) — Mobile bell trigger.* The mobile branch in =NotificationBell= (the =onClick={open}= path, not the radix PopoverTrigger) must set =aria-expanded={open}= + =aria-controls= referencing the Sheet content's id. Sheet content gets a stable id and =aria-labelledby= referencing the "Needs you" header. Keep desktop branch unchanged.
- [X] *F-9 (low bug) — Drag pointer-cancel + capture cleanup.* Shared =finishDrag(event, cancelled=true)= helper added; =onPointerCancel= wired; =releasePointerCapture= called when held; refs reset; effect cleanup clears refs on unmount. Used by =ManagerPopover= + =ManagerPeekBar= + =ManagerWorkbench= drag handles.
- [X] =cd ui && npm run build= passes (tsc clean + vite build clean).
- [X] No regressions to the existing manager keyboard shortcuts (=Cmd/Ctrl+\`= toggle, Esc collapse to peek) outside the F-2 disambiguation case.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 15:34] Bundled fix-implementer codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh, ~14 min wall, 152k tokens shared with sibling subtasks) committed as =ad2c715=; merged no-ff as =6d1070e=.
- Files changed within this subtask: =ManagerPopover.tsx= +185 (dialog semantics + focus trap via radix =FocusScope= when =size === 'focus'=, first-actionable auto-focus on open, restore-on-collapse, peek-trigger aria-expanded+aria-controls, Escape disambiguation skips editable focus + higher-priority radix overlays), =ManagerPeekBar.tsx= +9, =ManagerWorkbench.tsx= +20 (shared finishDrag wired), =NotificationBell.tsx= partial (mobile Sheet trigger aria-expanded/aria-controls), =NotificationPopover.tsx= +21 (Sheet content stable id + aria-labelledby pointing at "Needs you" header).
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build); main chunk 470.23 kB after sibling TASK-EBWZ9.4 lazy-loading + manualChunks landed in the same commit.
- Reviewer F-2 (medium a11y), F-3 (low a11y), F-9 (low bug) closed.

** Description
Addresses reviewer finding F-5 from the review report. Today =NotificationBell= stores dismissed items under a single global =localStorage= key (=orgasmic.notifications.dismissed.v1=) with row keys like =question:tx-NNN=, =run:run-NNN=, =parse:path:line:at=. With multiple projects registered on the same daemon, dismissals leak across projects — most plausibly for parse errors whose relative paths + line numbers repeat across orgasmic project trees.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] *F-5 fix.* Storage key namespaced as =orgasmic.notifications.dismissed.v2.<projectId>=. =useEffect= on =projectId= reloads dismissals state from the new key on project change; no bleed.
- [X] Row keys unchanged; namespacing happens at the storage-key level (cleaner; documented).
- [X] No v1 migration (clean break per AC).
- [X] =cd ui && npm run build= passes.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 15:34] Shipped in bundled fix-implementer commit =ad2c715= (merged =6d1070e=). Storage key switched from global =orgasmic.notifications.dismissed.v1= to per-project =orgasmic.notifications.dismissed.v2.<projectId>=. Reviewer F-5 (medium bug — the real cross-project leak via global key, most plausible for parse errors whose paths + line numbers repeat across project trees) closed.

** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-4 (low a11y) and F-6 (medium design) from the review report. The cross-domain graph at 116 nodes / 423 edges renders correctly but fits-to-viewport at an unreadable zoom; filter chips are toggle buttons without =aria-pressed= and can be all-toggled-off leaving a blank canvas.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] *F-4 fix — aria-pressed.* Filter chips render =aria-pressed={enabledTypes.has(type)}=.
- [X] *F-4 fix — empty-state guard.* All-off case shows a centered "All filters off" overlay card with a "Reset filters" button that re-enables all four types.
- [X] *F-6 fix — first-open readability.* Per-project viewport (x/y/zoom) persisted in =localStorage= under =graph-viewport:<projectId>=. On mount the saved viewport is restored if present; otherwise =fitView= runs. A "Reset view" button in Controls clears the saved viewport and re-runs =fitView()=.
- [X] =cd ui && npm run build= passes.
