** Description
Addresses reviewer finding F-7 (medium perf) from the review report. Today =GraphView= is statically imported by =App.tsx= and =ManagerWorkbench= statically imports =ManagerTmuxPane= which statically imports =@xterm/xterm= CSS+JS. Build emits a single 1062 kB raw / 313 kB gzip minified chunk. Users on Board / Tasks paths pay for ReactFlow + xterm without ever opening Graph or a tmux manager run.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] *F-7 fix — Graph lazy.* =App.tsx= wraps the graph-view branch in =React.lazy(() => import('../components/GraphView'))= + =Suspense fallback={<Loading />}=. Board/tasks/project/runs/org/status/settings paths no longer eagerly parse reactflow/dagre.
- [X] *F-7 fix — xterm lazy.* =ManagerWorkbench= lazy-loads =ManagerTmuxPane= and gates it behind =isTmuxDriver(driverTag)= in =Suspense=. ACP-family drivers never parse xterm.
- [X] *F-7 fix — vendor chunks.* =vite.config.ts= gained =build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks= splitting =reactflow/@reactflow= → =vendor-reactflow=; =dagre/graphlib/lodash= → =vendor-dagre=; =@xterm/*= → =vendor-xterm=. Main chunk **1062.21 kB → 470.23 kB** (well under the 500 kB warning threshold).
- [X] =cd ui && npm run build= passes; chunk-size summary in =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-046-fixes-last.txt=: main 470.23 kB / reactflow 160.67 kB / dagre 91.01 kB / xterm 334.21 kB / GraphView-lazy 11.14 kB / ManagerTmuxPane-lazy 0.98 kB.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 15:34] Shipped in bundled fix-implementer commit =ad2c715= (merged =6d1070e=). Files: =App.tsx= +14, =ManagerWorkbench.tsx= +20, =vite.config.ts= +21. Reviewer F-7 (medium perf) closed. Main chunk now 44% of the pre-fix size; users on non-graph paths skip reactflow+dagre, users on non-tmux runs skip xterm.

** Description
Replaces the seven remaining UI placeholders that survived UI Session 4 and lands the routing substrate that the polish-phase 2 endgame depends on. Today =GraphView=, =OrgView=, and =SettingsView= are the only entry points for Decisions, Architecture, Glossary, ADR, Snapshots, Activity, and stage-agent configuration. Per dec_PA23Y, dec_TQZTE, dec_E394G there should be six first-class pages with list rows + back-stack modal + (where applicable) per-page react-flow graph, in addition to the cross-domain Graph that already shipped in Session 4. Per dec_JSX48 the routing substrate becomes TanStack Router with path-for-nav + query-for-modals. =OrgView= ships a CodeMirror-with-org-mode editor (replacing the bare textarea). =SettingsView= grows a stage-agent map editor over the =STAGE_AGENT_*= property group on =project.org=.

This is a large but architecturally well-scoped session: every surface has a clear list-rows + modal-with-back-stack pattern, an existing fetcher (=fetchDecisions/Architecture/Glossary/Adrs/Snapshots= already in =ui/src/lib/api.ts=), and a decided UX (dec_PA23Y..046). The new abstraction is =NodeListView=/=NodeGraphView=/=NodeModal= parameterised by node-kind per dec_PA23Y's user note.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *Decisions page* (=ui/src/components/DecisionsView.tsx=) lands as the default landing page for any selected project per dec_PA23Y / dec_JSX48 (the cross-domain Graph stays as a separate page). Two-view toggle on top right: =[List] [Graph]= (default List). List rows (numeric sort by dec_NNN ID): mono ID + title + first sentence of =** Decision= (truncated to one line) + topic/glossary chips. Click row or press Enter opens the back-stack modal. Filter bar above the list: topic filter and free-text search across title + body. URL state drives the modal open state via TanStack Router's =useSearch=.
  - Graph subview = per-page react-flow + dagre top-down view of decisions only. Nodes teal; edges from =:DECIDES:= property; ADR halo when =:ADR_WORTHY: t=. Click single = 1-hop highlight; double-click or Enter on focused node = open modal; right-click = context menu with "Open modal" + "Open in cross-domain Graph". Per-project viewport persisted under =decisions-viewport:<projectId>= localStorage key (mirrors TASK-EBWZ9.3's =graph-viewport:<projectId>= pattern).
- [ ] *Architecture page* (=ui/src/components/ArchitectureView.tsx=) lands as the second primary nav item per dec_PA23Y's "same component family parameterised by node-kind". List rows: mono =arch_NNN= + title + first interface line (truncated; reads the first =** Interface= bullet from the body — body parsing tolerated through the existing =get_architecture_node= response if it carries it, otherwise show a fallback "interface unknown" muted line and don't block on it). Chip area: depends-on count chip ("→3") and motivating-decisions chip ("dec_GRDFB"). Modal sections: Interface bullets, Constraints, =:DECIDES:=-back-edge motivating decisions (clickable into modal back-stack), =:DEPENDS_ON:= chips (clickable). Graph subview = left-to-right react-flow + dagre, =:DEPENDS_ON:= as primary edges, =:DECIDES:= back-edges as dotted secondary edges. Per-project viewport persisted under =architecture-viewport:<projectId>=.
- [ ] *Glossary page* (=ui/src/components/GlossaryView.tsx=) per dec_E394G: list + modal only (no graph subview — cross-domain Graph already covers it). IDs hidden everywhere in the UI; the canonical name is the primary key. List rows: bold canonical name + first sentence of definition (truncated) + ↔N "relates to" chip (count of =:RELATES_TO:= entries). Sort by canonical name. Filter bar: free-text on canonical + definition. Modal: definition body + Avoid list + Relates-to chips (back-stack open into other glossary modals or into decisions / arch modals that reference them) + source-file link.
- [ ] *ADR page* (=ui/src/components/AdrView.tsx=) per dec_E394G mirrors Decisions. List rows: mono =adr_NNNN= + title + =:STATUS:= pill (accepted / proposed / superseded / deprecated) + "from dec_NNN" chip linking into the source decision modal. Sort by adr-number descending (newest first). Top-right action: "Regenerate ADRs" button → calls =postAdrRegenerate(projectId)= → toast on completion. Modal sections: full body markdown rendered (via the existing approach — if no markdown-renderer lib is in scope, use a minimal mono =<pre>= showing the body verbatim with line-breaks preserved; do **not** add a markdown lib in this dispatch), "Generated from dec_NNN" chip, source-file link.
  - Promote / demote actions exist per dec_C6510 — surface them as a kebab-menu on each row + inside the modal header. =postAdrPromote(id)= and =postAdrDemote(id)= helpers in =ui/src/lib/api.ts= (POST =/adr/:id/promote= / =/adr/:id/demote=; already routed in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs=).
- [ ] *Snapshots page* (=ui/src/components/SnapshotsView.tsx=) per dec_E394G: list + actions, no modal. List rows: mono snap ID + timestamp + label + Created-by + row-end Restore + Copy ID actions. Restore opens a small AlertDialog confirm before calling =postSnapshotRestore(snap.id, projectId)=. Top-right action: "Take snapshot" → calls =postSnapshot(projectId)= with default label "UI snapshot". No diff preview in this session (per dec_E394G user note: deferred).
- [ ] *Activity page* (=ui/src/components/ActivityView.tsx=) per dec_E394G: chronological feed of tx records, day-grouped (Today / Yesterday / <date>), runs clustered as expandable cards. Filter bar: Type (multi-select across tx =:TYPE:= values — manager.tick, manager.dispatch_started, implementer.done, reviewer.done, comment, snapshot, adr.*, decision.*, architecture.*, glossary.*), Actor (multi), Date range (preset chips Today/7d/30d + custom), Task (single combobox of task IDs). Data source: =fetchTx(projectId, limit)= with a generous limit (default 200; pagination = "Load more" button at the bottom). Run-cluster heuristic: consecutive entries within the same =task_id= and within 5 minutes collapse into an expandable card showing the head entry, count of follow-ups, and the last event time; expanded card lists the full sequence.
- [ ] *OrgView upgrade* (=ui/src/components/OrgView.tsx=) — replace the bare =<textarea>= with CodeMirror 6 + org-mode syntax. Use =@codemirror/state=, =@codemirror/view=, =@codemirror/commands=, =@codemirror/lang-markdown= as a starting point; org-mode-specific highlighting via =@codemirror/legacy-modes/mode/orgmode= (legacy modes are first-class in CM6). Keep the existing file picker + Reload + Save behaviour; add a thin outline rail to the left showing =* / **= heading hierarchy of the current buffer (click a heading scrolls the editor to it). Save action calls =postOrgFile= unchanged; show the resulting =tx_id= as a toast.
- [ ] *SettingsView upgrade* (=ui/src/components/SettingsView.tsx=) — add a "Stage agents" panel above the Backend profile section. Lists the six writable stages (BACKLOG, EXPLORE, PLAN, IMPLEMENT, REVIEW, SHIP — DONE is purely terminal; BLOCKED/CANCELLED are bookkeeping stages so skip them; also list them as read-only "—" rows if it keeps the table tidy). Each row: stage name + agent-template picker (combobox seeded from the agent-template registry; for now hardcode the four known templates: =griller=, =planner=, =implementer-claude=, =reviewer=, plus an explicit "(unset)" option) + optional "Notes" subtext (small muted). Save action writes =:STAGE_AGENT_<STAGE>: <value>= properties on the project.org =* PROJECT= heading via =postOrgFile('.orgasmic/project.org', ...)= — read first via =fetchOrgFile=, splice the new value into the existing property drawer, write back. Confirm save with a toast naming the resulting =tx_id=. The "Notes" subtext is informative only and not persisted in this session — there is no =:STAGE_AGENT_<STAGE>_NOTE:= storage yet.
  - Don't introduce a new endpoint for this; the project.org edit path through =postOrgFile= is the daemon's existing surface for direct-edit through the writer with advisory flock.
- [ ] *Top nav restructure* (=AppShell.tsx= GROUPS): demote Workspace/Active/Coordination/Library/System organisation in favour of dec_E394G's *primary four*: Decisions, Architecture, Tasks, Glossary. The remaining surfaces (Project, Runs, Org, ADR, Snapshots, Activity, Graph, Status, Settings) move under a single "More" group that scrolls. The order of nav links within each group:
  - Primary: Decisions, Architecture, Tasks, Glossary
  - More: Project, Runs, Org, ADR, Snapshots, Activity, Graph, Status, Settings
  - Board is still available but as a "+ Add / Manage" entry under Settings (it remains a present route — don't delete BoardView yet; future session restructures the project selector per dec_HYD58). For this session, drop the Board nav link entirely from the sidebar and keep it accessible only via direct URL (=/board= or =?view=board=) until the project-selector restructure lands.
- [ ] *NodeModal back-stack* (=ui/src/components/NodeModal.tsx=) is the shared three-pane back-stack modal per dec_PA23Y. Header shows breadcrumb of opened nodes; clicking an earlier crumb pops the stack. Body renders per-kind content (DecisionBody / ArchitectureBody / GlossaryBody / AdrBody — these can live in the same file or in per-kind subcomponents — your call). When a click opens a *different* node inside the modal, push onto a stack stored in the URL query =?drawer_stack=dec_GRDFB,arch_R3EPE= (per dec_JSX48). Closing pops to empty; back button (browser) pops one frame.
- [ ] *TanStack Router migration* (=ui/src/app/router.tsx= replaces / supplements =routes.ts=). Install =@tanstack/react-router= via =npm install --save=. Define path-based routes per dec_JSX48:
  - =/= → Redirect to =/projects/<default>= where default is the most-recently-loaded project (or BoardView if none).
  - =/board= → BoardView (kept for transitional access).
  - =/projects/$projectId= → DecisionsView (default landing for a project).
  - =/projects/$projectId/decisions= and =/projects/$projectId/decisions/$decisionId= → DecisionsView with modal open on the id.
  - =/projects/$projectId/architecture= and =/$archId= variant → ArchitectureView.
  - =/projects/$projectId/glossary= and =/$termId= → GlossaryView.
  - =/projects/$projectId/tasks= and =/$taskId= → TasksPage + TaskDialog (replaces the current =?view=tasks&task== query coupling). Subtasks via =/$taskId.$subId= path style.
  - =/projects/$projectId/adr= and =/$adrId=, =/projects/$projectId/snapshots=, =/projects/$projectId/activity=, =/projects/$projectId/graph=, =/projects/$projectId/runs=, =/projects/$projectId/org=, =/projects/$projectId/settings=, =/projects/$projectId/status=.
  - Query params: =?view=kanban|list= for tasks page; =?manager=peek|workbench|focus= for the manager popover (replaces the existing sessionStorage seam — sessionStorage still persists the *last* size but the URL is authoritative on load); =?drawer_stack=...= for modal back-stacks per dec_JSX48.
  - Keep =useQueryState= hook around for short-term coexistence but route the primary nav through TanStack's =Link= + =Outlet=.
  - Browser back: close modal → previous page → previous filter, per dec_JSX48 user note.
- [ ] *Dependencies added* via =npm install --save=:
  - =@tanstack/react-router= (TanStack Router; ~20 KB gz)
  - =@tanstack/router-devtools= (dev only via =--save-dev=) for local debugging during the migration. Gate behind =import.meta.env.DEV= so prod bundle stays clean.
  - =@codemirror/state=, =@codemirror/view=, =@codemirror/commands=, =@codemirror/lang-markdown=, =@codemirror/legacy-modes=, =codemirror= (the bundle); these are small and used only by the OrgView path — lazy-load OrgView via =React.lazy= so non-Org paths don't pay for CodeMirror.
- [ ] *Code-split discipline* (avoiding a Session 4-style regression): lazy-load via =React.lazy= the heavy per-page modules that pull in large transitive deps: =OrgView= (CodeMirror), =ActivityView= (no heavy deps but big enough to defer), the per-page graph subviews inside =DecisionsView=/=ArchitectureView= (=NodeGraph.tsx= which pulls reactflow — already in =vendor-reactflow= chunk so it shares with the cross-domain GraphView). Update =vite.config.ts= =manualChunks= if codemirror crosses the 100 kB threshold for a single dep (split =@codemirror/*= → =vendor-codemirror=). Verification: after =npm run build=, no single chunk over 500 kB raw.
- [ ] *Manager popover unaffected*. The Cmd+\` toggle, peek/workbench/focus drag, and notification bell behaviour from TASK-EBWZ9 are unchanged. Smoke-test by opening Decisions, Architecture, Tasks, and Activity pages and confirming the bottom popover renders in peek mode on all.
- [ ] *Mobile behaviour* per dec_NZ9PS: each new page's list rows hide kind/priority chips below sm; the modal becomes a full-screen Sheet below md with the back-stack header carrying a "← back" arrow. The per-page Graph subview becomes a full-screen Sheet on tap below md with the modal-style detail panel as a sibling Sheet.
- [ ] *Out-of-scope guard*: do **not** implement the project-selector / 4-primary-nav restructure beyond demoting Board (dec_HYD58 — full restructure is a follow-up session). Do **not** delete BoardView. Do **not** delete StatusView. Do **not** touch the daemon writer — every direct-edit goes through =postOrgFile=. Do **not** add markdown / syntax-highlight libs beyond what's required for CodeMirror's org-mode (legacy modes ship with =@codemirror/legacy-modes=, no extra deps). Do **not** implement deep-link compose-to-manager (dec_C6510) for new actions in this session — surface them as plain toasts/dialogs.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes (tsc --noEmit + vite build both clean). No new chunks above 500 kB raw.
- [ ] No unrelated lint regressions in =ui/src/=. Housekeeping rule applies (=shipped/manager/persona.org=).
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean on =main..task-047-session-5-ui=.

** Notes
This is the largest UI session by surface count (7 new pages + 2 substantial upgrades + TanStack migration) but architecturally the simplest because every surface is a parameterisation of the same NodeListView/NodeGraphView/NodeModal pattern per dec_PA23Y. Codex landed the shared family under =ui/src/components/node-views/= with 4 files (NodeListView 99 / NodeModal 461 / NodeGraph 260 / orgNodes.ts 172 for org-property parsing helpers).

Codex deviated minimally from the brief in two places, both documented in =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-047-last.txt=:
1. CodeMirror =@codemirror/legacy-modes= no longer ships an =orgmode= module under the installed version, so OrgView uses a small local =StreamLanguage= for org-style headings + property drawers. Still CodeMirror 6.
2. Codex used =@tanstack/react-router-devtools= instead of the brief's =@tanstack/router-devtools= — the package was renamed upstream; the brief's name is no longer the published one.

The worktree was branched from =cbf15fa= (pre-dispatch HEAD), so codex did not see the manager-side TASK-ZYPJC sprint heading + pre-flight tx that landed in =b954395= on main. Codex correctly followed the brief + decision anchors instead and noted the absence in handoff. ** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-1 (high), F-2 (medium), F-3 (medium), and F-8 (high) from =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-047-review-report.md=. The TanStack Router migration shipped the path-based routes but the per-page filter state, the modal drawer-stack, and the manager-size query param did not adopt TanStack's =useSearch= / =navigate= contract — instead they kept legacy =useQueryState= writes, =window.history.pushState= calls, or component-local =useState=. This breaks the dec_JSX48 "path + query hybrid, browser-back follows previous filter" contract and prevents deep-linking from reflecting the actual session state.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1 fix.* =NodeModal= drawer-stack management goes through TanStack Router's =useSearch= + =navigate({ search })= instead of =window.location.search= + =window.history.pushState= / =replaceState=. Chip-click inside a modal body pushes a new id onto =?drawer_stack=...= via =navigate=; clicking Back / closing modal pops via =navigate=; browser back/forward respects the stack (TanStack tracks history per its router instance).
- [ ] *F-2 fix.* Esc on a stacked modal pops *one frame* via the same router seam — only deletes the whole stack when the stack is already empty (closing the bottom frame returns to the underlying route). Match the dec_JSX48 user-note browser-back contract: "close modal → previous page → previous filter".
- [ ] *F-3 fix.* =NodeModal= header renders a breadcrumb of opened nodes (e.g., =dec_GRDFB > arch_R3EPE > arch_R3EPE details=). Clicking any crumb pops the stack down to that depth. Single Back icon remains for the one-step pop. The breadcrumb hides on viewports below =sm= (mobile sheet has limited header space) and falls back to "Back" + count.
- [ ] *F-8 fix.* Migrate the search-state holdouts to TanStack:
  - =DecisionsView= filter bar (topic filter, free-text query) reads from =useSearch()= and writes via =navigate({ search })=. Drop the =useQueryState= calls. Same shape for =ArchitectureView= (free-text per TASK-ZYPJC.4) and =ActivityView= (Type / Actor / Date / Task filters; date-range needs a JSON-safe shape).
  - =ManagerPopover= consumes =?manager=peek|workbench|focus= from =useSearch=. The =sessionStorage= persistence stays as a fallback for "last size when no query param", but the URL is authoritative on initial render. Writing the URL on size change is OK — debounce if needed to avoid history spam during drag.
  - Update the route's =validateSearch= schema so type-safe accessors exist.
- [ ] *Manager popover regression check.* Cmd+\` toggle and Esc collapse must still work; drag handles must not race the URL writes (debounce or write only on settle).
- [ ] *No new deps.* TanStack Router is already installed; no =@tanstack/react-router-zod= or similar add-on. Use TanStack's built-in =validateSearch= (function form).
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes (tsc --noEmit + vite build).
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 18:26] Bundled fix-implementer codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~22 min wall, 299,251 tokens shared with sibling subtasks) committed as =8c26232=; merged no-ff as =6862918=.
- Files changed within this subtask: =router.tsx= +152 (validateSearch schemas + drawer_stack list serialization), =NodeModal.tsx= +269/-117 (TanStack search drives drawer_stack push/pop; one-frame pop on Esc + radix close; breadcrumb header + mobile back-count; open-gated detail loading + glossary chrome labels + ADR Promote/Demote modal-header actions + seed-data reuse from list responses), =DecisionsView.tsx= partial (status/adr/q migrated to useSearch), =ArchitectureView.tsx= partial (status/q migrated), =ActivityView.tsx= +99/-18 (Type/Actor/Date/Task filters on useSearch + custom date URL fields), =ManagerPopover.tsx= +22/-2 (=?manager=peek|workbench|focus= consumed via TanStack useSearch with sessionStorage fallback for last-size), new =ui/src/lib/searchState.ts= +22 (shared drawer_stack/search reducer helpers).
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc + vite); main =index= chunk 359.25 kB; vendor-codemirror 390.51 kB largest. No new deps; legacy =useQueryState= retained only in TasksPage for =?layout=list|kanban=.
- Reviewer F-1 (high bug), F-2 (medium bug), F-3 (medium design), F-8 (high bug) closed.

** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-4 (medium perf), F-11 (medium design), F-13 (medium design). =NodeModal= currently runs all per-kind fetchers (decisions / architecture / glossary / ADR / three orgFile reads) on every page even when the modal is closed — wasteful network + auth-noise; glossary IDs leak through to modal header chrome and the mobile Sheet description even though dec_E394G requires the canonical name as the primary key in UI; ADR promote/demote actions live only in the row kebab menu and the source decision in the row renders as a passive badge instead of a clickable chip linking into the source decision modal.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-4 fix.* Detail loaders inside =NodeModal= are gated on =open=. When the modal is closed (no =?<kind>= path segment + no top-of-stack id), no fetchers run. When opening from a list page, the matching list response is reused if it already contains the needed fields — only fetch a detail endpoint (=GET /architecture/:id= / =GET /glossary/:id= / =GET /adr/:id=) when the body needs fields that the list summary lacks.
- [ ] *F-11 fix.* Glossary modal hides the raw =:ID:= everywhere user-facing: header shows canonical name only; mobile Sheet description shows canonical name; the back-stack chip uses canonical name. The id stays in the URL =?drawer_stack=glossary-NNN= but is not rendered as a code span anywhere in the UI.
- [ ] *F-13 fix part 1.* ADR row's "Generated from dec_NNN" is a clickable chip that pushes =dec_NNN= onto the drawer stack (opens the source decision modal). Keep the kebab menu for Promote/Demote at the row level.
- [ ] *F-13 fix part 2.* ADR modal header carries Promote + Demote buttons (mirror the row kebab actions). Disabled states: Promote disabled when status is already =accepted=; Demote disabled when status is already =proposed= or =deprecated=. Both fire the existing =postAdrPromote= / =postAdrPromote= helpers with sonner toast feedback.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 18:26] Shipped in bundled fix-implementer commit =8c26232= (merged =6862918=). =NodeModal.tsx= now gates detail loaders on =open= (no fetchers run when stack is empty + nodeId is null); list-page payloads are reused as seed data for the modal body (no re-fetch when the list already carries the needed fields); glossary modal chrome shows canonical name in the header + mobile Sheet description (id stays in URL only); ADR row source-decision is a clickable chip that pushes the source decision id onto the drawer stack via the router seam; ADR modal header carries Promote + Demote buttons with disabled-state logic (Promote disabled at =accepted=; Demote disabled at =proposed= / =deprecated=).
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean. No network burst on page load.
- Reviewer F-4 (medium perf), F-11 (medium design), F-13 (medium design) closed.

** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-5 (medium bug), F-6 (medium design), F-7 (medium design). =NodeGraph= secondary edges from architecture nodes try to draw =decision -> architecture= edges even though the graph node set only contains architecture ids — those edges reference missing source nodes and React Flow throws missing-node warnings. The per-page graph only implements double-click to open a modal; dec_PA23Y also requires single-click 1-hop highlight, Enter on the focused node, and a right-click context menu. The dec_NZ9PS mobile matrix is only partially applied: the graph subview remains an inline fixed-height canvas instead of opening as a full-screen Sheet below =md=, and list-row chips/actions stay visible below =sm= instead of degrading.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-5 fix.* =NodeGraph= for architecture only emits secondary =:DECIDES:= back-edges when both endpoints exist in the rendered node set. Currently the source is a decision id not in the architecture node set — change the secondary edge to architecture → motivating-decision id only when that decision id is also in the node set (which it never is in the architecture-only subview); for the architecture subview, surface the motivating decision as a row chip in the side panel instead of a graph edge. No React Flow missing-node warnings.
- [ ] *F-6 fix.* Per-page graph interactions:
  - Single click on a node → 1-hop highlight (TASK-EBWZ9.3 cross-domain Graph pattern). Re-uses the same opacity-25% / opacity-100% logic.
  - Enter key on focused node → opens the modal (the existing double-click behaviour).
  - Right-click on a node → ContextMenu (radix) with "Open modal" + "Open in cross-domain Graph" entries. The latter navigates to =/projects/$projectId/graph= with =?focus=$nodeId= so the cross-domain graph can fitView on that node (cross-domain GraphView accepts =?focus= if it's wired; if not, just navigate without the param — flag in evidence).
- [ ] *F-7 fix part 1.* Below =md=, the per-page graph subview opens as a full-screen Sheet (slides up from bottom) instead of rendering inline. The List/Graph toggle on mobile becomes a button that opens the Sheet. Mirrors TASK-EBWZ9.5's mobile Sheet pattern for the cross-domain graph detail.
- [ ] *F-7 fix part 2.* Below =sm=, list-row chips that are not load-bearing (=kind=, =priority=, ADR badge for non-promoted decisions, snapshot label) hide. =useIsMobile()= hook already exists; reuse it. Row IDs + titles + status pills + Restore/Action buttons stay visible.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes; no React Flow missing-node warnings in console.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 18:26] Shipped in bundled fix-implementer commit =8c26232= (merged =6862918=). =NodeGraph.tsx= +161/-31 guards every emitted edge endpoint against the rendered node id set (no more React Flow missing-node warnings; architecture subview surfaces the motivating decision as a side-panel chip instead of a missing-source edge), adds single-click 1-hop highlight (opacity-25%/100% pattern from TASK-EBWZ9.3), Enter-to-open on focused node, right-click radix ContextMenu with "Open modal" + "Open in cross-domain Graph" (the latter navigates to =/projects/$projectId/graph?focus=$nodeId=; cross-domain GraphView doesn't yet consume =?focus=, flagged as follow-up). Mobile (=<md=): per-page graph subview opens as a full-screen Sheet (slides from bottom); list-row non-load-bearing chips hide below =sm= (ADR source-decision chip; Snapshots label; Decisions ADR badge). New =ui/src/components/ui/context-menu.tsx= +265 (local shadcn/radix ContextMenu primitive).
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean. NodeGraph chunk grew from 4.07 kB to 7.15 kB (interactions + ContextMenu wiring); still well under 500 kB budget for any single chunk.
- Reviewer F-5 (medium bug), F-6 (medium design), F-7 (medium design) closed. Deferred follow-up: cross-domain GraphView =?focus= consumption for viewport fit.

** Description
Addresses reviewer findings F-9 (medium design), F-10 (low a11y), F-12 (high a11y — nested interactive controls invalid HTML), F-14 (medium bug — no distinct loading state), F-15 (low design — Decisions sort + pill rendering). One coupled pass over =NodeListView= + per-page Views.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-12 fix (high).* =NodeListView= row primitive supports non-nested row actions: split the row into a clickable row body (no =<button>= wrap) + an absolutely-positioned trailing action zone (kebab / chips) that uses =stopPropagation= AND is wired with =onPointerDown=stopPropagation= so the row's click handler doesn't fire. Row body is a =<button>= or =<div role="button" tabIndex={0}>= but the action zone is outside the button element. Validate via DOM inspection: no =<button>= inside =<button>=.
- [ ] *F-9 fix.* Add the missing filter/search bars:
  - =GlossaryView=: free-text search over canonical name + definition. URL-backed (=?q=...=).
  - =AdrView=: status multi-select (proposed / accepted / superseded / deprecated) + free-text search over title. URL-backed.
  - =ArchitectureView=: free-text search over title + interface line. URL-backed.
  - All three reuse the =DecisionsView= filter-bar layout.
- [ ] *F-10 fix.* Filter chips on every page (Decisions, Architecture, Activity) carry =aria-controls= pointing at the id of the region they filter (list container, feed container, or graph canvas).
- [ ] *F-14 fix.* Distinct loading state: while =useResource= is pending, render a Skeleton block (shadcn primitive — count ~6 rows) inside =NodeListView= instead of falling through to the empty-state message. Empty-state message renders only when =loading === false && items.length === 0=. Same logic for SnapshotsView and ActivityView.
- [ ] *F-15 fix.* =DecisionsView=:
  - Rows sorted by numeric portion of =dec_NNN= ascending (use =parseInt(id.slice(4)) || 0=).
  - Decision list uses topic/glossary chips only; obsolete chosen-label/status chips are not rendered.
  - Status pill rendered only when status is not =decided= (=question / proposed / superseded= surface; =decided= is the default and visually quiet).
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= passes.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 18:26] Shipped in bundled fix-implementer commit =8c26232= (merged =6862918=). =NodeListView.tsx= +45/-13: rows split into =<div role="button" tabIndex={0}>= body + external =renderActionZone= prop holding action-zone elements (with =onPointerDown=stopPropagation= + =onClick=stopPropagation= guards); stable list-region id (=nodeKind=-list-region) for filter aria-controls; Skeleton placeholder rows render while loading instead of falling through to the empty-state message. =AdrView=, =GlossaryView=, =ArchitectureView= each gain URL-backed filter/search bars (status multi-select + free-text); filter chips on every page now carry =aria-controls= pointing at the list/feed/graph region id. =DecisionsView= rows sort ascending by numeric portion of =dec_NNN=; chosen-LABEL chip renders only when =chosen_label= present; status pill renders only when status !== "decided"; ADR badge hides below =sm=. =SnapshotsView= + =ActivityView= gained Skeleton loading + label-hidden-below-sm.
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean. Per-page chunks slightly grew (DecisionsView 4.42 → 5.29 kB, ArchitectureView 3.96 → 5.16 kB, GlossaryView 1.45 → 1.75 kB, AdrView 3.19 → 4.18 kB, SnapshotsView 6.06 → 6.50 kB, ActivityView 6.72 → 7.59 kB) from filter bar + Skeleton + URL-backed state — all well below 10 kB each. Nested-button inspection: no =<button>= inside =<button>= anywhere; DropdownMenu trigger renders via =renderActionZone= outside the row body =<div role="button">=.
- Reviewer F-9 (medium design), F-10 (low a11y), F-12 (high a11y — nested interactive HTML), F-14 (medium bug — loading indistinguishable from empty), F-15 (low design) closed.

** Description
Manager Chrome smoke against the merged TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4} build surfaced four findings. Bundle as one polish pass.

- *F-A (high regression).* Row click on any list page navigates to the path-based child route (e.g. =/decisions/dec_GRDFB=) and updates the top-header crumb, but the modal never opens. Cause: =decisionsRoute.component= (and the parallel architecture/glossary/adr parents) renders =<DecisionsView nodeId={null}/>= without an =<Outlet/>=, so the child route never mounts. Modal only opens via =?drawer_stack=…= query (verified end-to-end). The path-based deep-link routes are orphan code left from Session 5; complete the F-1 useSearch migration by removing them and routing row clicks through =drawer_stack=.
- *F-B (medium a11y).* Source-decision chip in =ArchitectureView.renderRow= (lines 198-212) and the equivalent chip in =AdrView.renderRow= are real =<button>= elements rendered inside =renderRow= — descendants of =NodeListView='s =<div role="button">= row body. Same nested-interactive violation F-12 fixed for the ADR DropdownMenu. Move to =renderActionZone=.
- *F-C (low a11y).* =NodeModal='s =<DialogContent>= has =<DialogTitle className="sr-only">= but no =<DialogDescription>= and no opt-out — Radix logs a "Missing Description" warning every time the modal opens. Add an sr-only =<DialogDescription>= or pass =aria-describedby={undefined}=.
- *F-D (low design).* =ArchitectureView.renderRow= shows =→N= arrow + =dec_NNN= chip on all viewports. F-7 intent ("below-sm non-load-bearing chips hide") should apply. Status pill (=decided=/=partial=/=target=) stays visible.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-A.* Path-based child routes (=decisionNodeRoute=, =architectureNodeRoute=, =glossaryNodeRoute=, =adrNodeRoute=) removed from =ui/src/app/router.tsx=. Each =View=.nodeId= prop removed. =openNode(id)= pushes to =?drawer_stack= via =routeSearch((prev) => appendDrawerStack(prev, id))=. Modal opens on every row click; =?drawer_stack=dec_GRDFB,loader-rule= still hydrates the modal on deep link. Esc continues to pop one frame at a time. AppShell top-header crumb reads =drawer_stack= (if it currently reads the path nodeId).
- [ ] *F-B.* Source-decision chip on =ArchitectureView= + =AdrView= moves to =renderActionZone= (group with kebab in a flex container if both present). DOM check =[role=button] button= returns 0 nodes on Architecture + ADR list pages.
- [ ] *F-C.* =NodeModal= mobile Sheet AND desktop Dialog branches both supply an =sr-only DialogDescription= / =SheetDescription= (or pass =aria-describedby={undefined}=). Console clean on modal open — no Radix warning.
- [ ] *F-D.* =ArchitectureView= renderRow wraps =→N= badge + =dec_NNN= chip in =hidden sm:inline-flex= (or applies the class after they move to =renderActionZone=). Status pill stays visible. Verify visually at 414×896.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build); main =index= chunk stays ≤ 360 kB; no new dependencies in =ui/package.json=.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

** Evidence

- 8 files changed, +84 / -226 net (heavy deletion from orphan path-route removal).
- F-A: =ui/src/app/router.tsx= -67 lines — =decisionNodeRoute=, =architectureNodeRoute=, =glossaryNodeRoute=, =adrNodeRoute= removed; =nodeId= prop dropped from =DecisionsView=, =ArchitectureView=, =GlossaryView=, =AdrView= (each shed 14-36 lines); each =openNode(id)= now calls =routeSearch((prev) => appendDrawerStack(prev, id))= via =NodeModal='s shared search helpers; =NodeModal.onClose= simplified (no per-page redirect); =AppShell.tsx= +4/-? — header crumb derivation switched to =drawer_stack=.
- F-B: =ArchitectureView= + =AdrView= renderRow source-decision chips moved to =renderActionZone= (action-zone =onPointerDown/onClick=stopPropagation= already guarded by =NodeListView=); =NodeListView.tsx= +2/-2 polish.

** Description
Reviewer adjacent-Dialog finding from TASK-ZYPJC.5: =TaskDialog= (=ui/src/components/TaskDialog.tsx:54=) opens =<DialogContent>= without an always-mounted =<DialogDescription>=, and its loading branch's =DialogHeader= renders only Skeleton placeholders so the dialog has no stable =DialogTitle= label while data is loading. Symptom: Radix "Missing Description" warning on TaskDialog open (parallel to the F-C fix landed on =NodeModal=); screen readers also get no stable dialog name during the loading state.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Import =DialogDescription= alongside the existing =Dialog/DialogContent/DialogTitle= imports in =TaskDialog.tsx=.
- [ ] Inside =DialogContent= (line ~54), render an always-mounted sr-only =<DialogDescription>= (e.g. =Task {taskId}= or =Task details= placeholder while loading; descriptive task summary once =task.data= resolves). Pattern mirrors the F-C fix in =NodeModal.tsx=.
- [ ] Render a stable sr-only =<DialogTitle>= during the loading branch too (currently lines 54-67 only render the title when loaded; the loading skeleton at line ~108 has no DialogTitle). Use =Task {taskId}= or =Task= as the loading-state title; replace with task title once loaded.
- [ ] Console-clean on TaskDialog open: no Radix "Missing Description" warning.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build); no chunk size regression.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

- 1 file changed, +10 / -4 net.
- =ui/src/components/TaskDialog.tsx=: imported =DialogDescription= alongside existing =Dialog/DialogContent/DialogTitle=; loading branch now renders sr-only =<DialogTitle>Task {taskId}</DialogTitle>= + sr-only =<DialogDescription>= (was just =Task= placeholder, sometimes missing while loading); loaded branch sibling =<DialogDescription>= added inside =DialogContent= ahead of the visible =DialogHeader=. Visible UI unchanged.
- Verification: codex ran =npm ci= (no package change), =cd ui && npm run build= clean (Vite 6.4.2, main =index= 358.65 kB — +0.23 kB from added DialogDescription, well under 360 kB budget), component-level Playwright smoke on Vite :5182 confirming loading + loaded TaskDialog states both have stable Title + Description wiring with 0 Radix missing-description warnings.

* DONE TASK-DK0Y2 Small UI follow-ups: Tasks-route F-A + GraphView ?focus + TaskDialog inline body :ui:routing:graph:tasks:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-DK0Y2
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A1FGY
:PRODUCES:         ui/src/app/router.tsx ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx ui/src/components/TaskDialog.tsx
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/app/router.tsx ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx ui/src/components/TaskDialog.tsx ui/src/components/TasksPage.tsx ui/src/components/BoardView.tsx ui/src/components/node-views/NodeGraph.tsx ui/src/lib/types.ts ui/src/lib/api.ts crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-048-brief.md .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/sprint.org
:TEST_CMD:         cd ui && npm run build && cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-ZYPJC.5 TASK-ZYPJC.5.1
:MERGE_SHA:        b1a305d
:CODEX_COMMIT:     6e2d01c
:END:

** Description
Three small UI follow-ups owed from UI Session 5 that share heavily-overlapping write scopes in =ui/src/=, bundled as a single fix-implementer dispatch per the TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5} / TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4} pattern. This is also the **first routine-work dogfood of the new =orgasmic manager dispatch= CLI** that landed in TASK-FB8CK — the wrapping should turn the prior hand-coded worktree+brief+nohup+pre-flight-tx ritual into one atomic invocation. If the CLI proves out cleanly here, every future dispatch uses it.

The three coupled items:

*F-A (Tasks-route).* Codex flagged this as an adjacent finding during TASK-ZYPJC.5.1: row click on =TasksPage= navigates to =/projects/$projectId/tasks/$taskId= but =TaskDialog= never mounts — same shape as the TASK-ZYPJC.5 F-A regression that landed for Decisions/Architecture/Glossary/ADR. Root cause is the same: =tasksRoute.component= renders =<TasksPage/>= but no =<Outlet/>=, so =taskNodeRoute= (the path-based child) is matched but its component never mounts. Fix follows dec_JSX48 (path-for-nav, query-for-modals): drop =taskNodeRoute=, add =?task=$taskId= to =tasksRoute= =validateSearch=, render =TaskDialog= conditionally from =tasksRoute.component= keyed on the search param. Update =BoardView.onSelectTask= + =TasksPage= row click + =ProjectView.onSelectTask= + any other =to: '/projects/$projectId/tasks/$taskId'= callsites to instead =navigate({ to: '/projects/$projectId/tasks', search: { task: taskId } })=.

*GraphView ?focus.* =graphRoute= already validates =?focus= (router.tsx:437) and =NodeGraph='s right-click "Open in cross-domain Graph" (NodeGraph.tsx:264) navigates to =/projects/$projectId/graph?focus=$id=, but =GraphView.tsx= does not consume the param. Fix: read =focus= via =graphRoute.useSearch()= (or =getRouteApi('/projects/$projectId/graph').useSearch()= to avoid cyclic imports), and on first =onInit= or when the graph data resolves, if =focus= is present AND a matching node exists in the rendered node set, call =flowRef.current?.fitView({ nodes: [{ id: focus }], padding: 0.2 })=. Edge case: =NodeGraph='s callback passes the **bare** id (e.g. =dec_GRDFB=) while =GraphView= keys nodes as =${type}:${rawId}= (e.g. =decision:dec_GRDFB=) — pick a consistent representation. Recommended fix: namespace at the emit side (=NodeGraph.openCrossDomainGraph= maps its =GraphKind= to the matching =NodeType= prefix before passing through). Bonus tiny polish if zero-cost: also highlight the focused node visually (e.g., temporary ring/outline) so the user sees what was focused.

*TaskDialog inline body rendering.* =MainPane= at =TaskDialog.tsx:262-300= still renders the Session-5 placeholder ("Full task body lives in sprint.org. Inline rendering of Description / Acceptance Criteria / Evidence will land in UI Session 5 alongside the Org editor."). Land that rendering now. =TaskSummary= in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs= currently has no description/acceptance/evidence fields. Pick the **minimum** backend surface that works: either (a) extend the single-task =GET /projects/:id/tasks/:task_id= endpoint to optionally return parsed body sections (Description / Acceptance Criteria / Evidence / Notes / Worklog, each =Option<String>= or =Vec<String>= as appropriate) on a richer return type (e.g., =TaskDetail= that contains =TaskSummary= + parsed body); list endpoints stay on lean =TaskSummary= (no body). Or (b) add a new =GET /projects/:id/tasks/:task_id/body= endpoint that returns =Vec<{ section: String, lines: Vec<String> }>= and let the frontend section-split itself. Pick whichever you find cleaner; (a) is probably less wire-overhead. Body parsing: the task heading subtree in sprint.org contains second-level =** Description=, =** Acceptance Criteria=, =** Evidence=, =** Notes=, =** Worklog=, =** Reviewer pass= sections — collect lines between them. Acceptance items inside =** Acceptance Criteria= are =- [X] ...= / =- [ ] ...= bullets; preserve those as-is (frontend renders them as a checklist with the check state from the marker). Frontend: render in =MainPane= replacing the placeholder. Each section gets a small heading + body. =Description= and =Notes= = paragraph-rendered (split on blank lines). =Acceptance Criteria= = checklist (checked vs unchecked). =Evidence= = rendered as a bullet list. =Worklog= = collapsed by default if present (use shadcn Accordion or a simple summary/details block) since these can be long. If a section is empty, omit it entirely (no "No description" placeholder). Keep =shortPath(task.source_file)= as a small footnote so users still know where the canonical org file lives.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-A:* Row click on Tasks page opens =TaskDialog= in the URL =/projects/$projectId/tasks?task=TASK-NNN= (search param, not path child). Modal closes by clearing =?task=. Reload at the URL with the param re-opens the modal. =taskNodeRoute= is removed entirely from =router.tsx= and not present in the route tree. All =onSelectTask= callsites navigate with the new search-param shape.
- [ ] *F-A:* =BoardView=, =ProjectView=, =TasksPage= row clicks all reach the modal. Console-clean on open (no Radix warnings beyond what TASK-ZYPJC.5.1 already addressed).
- [ ] *GraphView ?focus:* Loading =/projects/$projectId/graph?focus=decision:dec_GRDFB= (or whatever consistent format you choose) fits the view to that node when data resolves. If =?focus= is absent the prior fit-view behavior is preserved.
- [ ] *GraphView ?focus:* End-to-end smoke from a per-page graph: in =DecisionsView= Graph subview right-click a decision node → "Open in cross-domain Graph" → cross-domain GraphView opens with that node centered/fit. Same flow from =ArchitectureView=.
- [ ] *TaskDialog body:* Opening a task drawer for a DONE task with rich Evidence (e.g., TASK-FB8CK) renders the Description, Acceptance Criteria (as a checklist with checked/unchecked state), and Evidence sections inline in =MainPane=, replacing the Session-5 placeholder. =shortPath(task.source_file)= remains as a small footnote ("Source: sprint.org").
- [ ] *TaskDialog body:* Opening a BACKLOG/READY task without Evidence does not render an empty Evidence section. Sections only appear when their content is non-empty.
- [ ] *TaskDialog body:* If you pick the new =/body= endpoint route, the daemon test coverage includes one parser test (TASK-FB8CK-shaped fixture). If you extend =get_task=, the existing daemon test stays green plus one additional assertion on the new fields.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build), no chunk-size budget regression (=index= stays under 360 kB or with a justified small bump).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.
- [ ] No new dependencies in =package.json= or =Cargo.toml= (shadcn primitives already present; if you need an Accordion install it via the project's shadcn skill the standard way — that's allowed since shadcn primitives are copy-paste components, not new packages).

** Notes
Why bundle: write scopes overlap in =ui/src/= (router.tsx + TaskDialog.tsx touched by F-A and body-rendering; GraphView.tsx independent but disjoint). Single commit is cleaner than three small parallel branches that would need merging. Matches TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5} / TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4} pattern.

Out of scope (do not touch in this dispatch):
- Project-selector restructure (dec_CGVRW/026 — bigger IA shift).
- New per-task fields (assignee, due date, etc).
- Mobile-only re-flow of TaskDialog body sections.
- Any =/tasks= POST endpoints (only the body read path).
- Snapshot/ADR work, manager popover work, daemon worker driver work.

Verification: codex should run =cd ui && npm run build= + =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= before declaring done. Visual smoke optional (manager will run the Chrome smoke after merge). Single coupled commit.

CLI dogfood: the manager will invoke =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-DK0Y2 --kind implementer --brief /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-048-brief.md= which atomically (i) creates the worktree at the default path, (ii) appends =manager.dispatch_started=, (iii) spawns codex detached via setsid. Close pattern: single =implementer.done= tx via =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-DK0Y2 --status done --merge-sha <SHA> --codex-commit <SHA> --codex-session <ID> --tokens <N> --wall <DUR> --branch-delete=.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 21:52] Shipped in single coupled commit =6e2d01c= on =task-048-impl=; merged no-ff as =b1a305d= on main. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~13 min wall, 227,839 tokens, session =019e5616-24d1-70e3-94ee-83aa913ef719=. **First routine-work dispatch via the new =orgasmic manager dispatch= CLI (TASK-FB8CK) landed clean on first pass.** Used =dispatch-close --merge-sha --codex-commit --codex-session --tokens --wall --branch-delete= to atomically append =implementer.done= =tx-20260523-org-0149= and remove worktree + delete branch.
- 10 files changed, +455 / -72 net.
- *F-A Tasks-route:* dropped path-based =taskNodeRoute= from =ui/src/app/router.tsx=; =tasksRoute= gained =validateSearch= for =?task=$taskId=; =TaskDialog= rendered from =tasksRoute.component= conditionally on the search param. Updated =onSelectTask= callsites in =BoardView=, =ProjectView=, =TasksPage=, =ActivityView=, =AppShell= (the latter two were spillover sites beyond the brief's WRITE_SCOPE list).
- *GraphView ?focus consumption:* =NodeGraph.openCrossDomainGraph= (=ui/src/components/node-views/NodeGraph.tsx:264=) now emits namespaced ids (=${type}:${id}=); =GraphView.tsx= reads =focus= via =graphRoute= search and calls =fitView({ nodes: [{ id: focus }], padding: 0.2 })= on resolve; gated to run once per focus value (no re-loop on every render).
- *TaskDialog inline body rendering (Option A picked by codex):* new =TaskDetail= struct in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs= wraps =TaskSummary= + parsed body fields (=description: Option<String>=, =acceptance_criteria: Vec<AcceptanceItem { checked: bool, text: String }>=, =evidence: Vec<String>=, =notes: Option<String>=, =worklog: Vec<String>=); =get_task= endpoint (=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs:208=) returns =TaskDetail= (single-task only; list endpoints stay on =TaskSummary=). Frontend =MainPane= replaces the Session-5 placeholder with section blocks; empty sections omitted; =shortPath(source_file)= kept as a footnote. =ui/src/lib/types.ts= + =ui/src/lib/api.ts= mirror the wire shape.
- *Bonus optimization:* =TaskDialog= lazy-loaded via =React.lazy=; main =index= chunk =358.65 → 348.54 kB= (-10.11 kB, 2.8% smaller). New per-page chunk =TaskDialog-CBhoTXNt.js= = 11.13 kB.
- Verification gate (re-run on worktree before merge AND on merged main):

** Description
Bundle of three small UI follow-ups from the TASK-DK0Y2 reviewer pass. All three live in =ui/src/=, share no overlap with the daemon-side TASK-DK0Y2.2, and can be implemented in one coupled commit.

*F-1 (medium bug):* =GraphView= still runs two viewport ops when =?focus= is set with no saved viewport. React Flow receives =fitView={!viewportSeed.viewport}= and fits the whole graph on mount; the focus effect schedules a second =fitView({ nodes: [{ id: focus }] })=. Result: full-graph flash, or viewport race if RF's initial fit completes after the focus fit. Fix: gate the initial full-graph fit on =!focus= AND trigger the focused fit from a single flow-ready path (=onInit=-only or a single explicit ready-state effect) so exactly one viewport action wins when focus is present.

*F-4 (low design):* The bonus lazy-load uses =<Suspense fallback={null}>= in =router.tsx:366-392=, so the first task click or hard reload at =/tasks?task=...= updates the URL but shows nothing until the TaskDialog chunk resolves. The data-loading skeleton inside TaskDialog only appears AFTER the chunk is fetched. Fix: light dialog/skeleton fallback (e.g., a stub =<Dialog>= with empty =<DialogContent>= showing the existing TaskDialog loading skeleton, or a small inline placeholder), OR prefetch the chunk from TasksPage row intent (=onMouseEnter= / =onFocus= on the row).

*F-5 (low bug):* =tasksRoute.validateSearch= only preserves =task=, but =TasksPage= still owns =layout= via the legacy =useQueryState('layout')=. Opening or closing a task through TanStack navigation rebuilds search from the route search shape, dropping =?layout=kanban= from the URL even though the page still treats it as URL state. Fix: either (a) add =layout?: 'list' | 'kanban'= to =TasksSearch= validateSearch and preserve it through the open/close reducers, OR (b) migrate TasksPage layout fully onto the route search API (kill the =useQueryState= usage). (a) is smaller; (b) is more consistent with dec_JSX48.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1:* Loading =/projects/.../graph?focus=decision:dec_GRDFB= does NOT flash a full-graph fit first — exactly one viewport action happens and it's the focused fit. If =?focus= is absent the existing fit-on-init behavior is preserved unchanged.
- [ ] *F-1:* Verify by reading the code: `fitView` prop on =<ReactFlow>= is conditional on `!focus`, AND the focus effect runs once per focus value (no double-fitView for the same focus).
- [ ] *F-4:* First click on a Tasks row shows SOME UI surface (skeleton dialog, fallback shell, or pre-fetched ready dialog) before the chunk-load completes — not a frozen-looking nothing.
- [ ] *F-5:* Setting =?layout=kanban= then opening a task (=?task=TASK-NNN=) preserves both params in the URL after the modal opens. Closing the modal preserves =?layout=. Hard reload at =/tasks?layout=kanban&task=TASK-NNN= opens both correctly.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build). Main chunk stays under 360 kB. No new dependencies.
- [ ] =git diff --check= clean.

- *F-1 (medium bug) closed:* =ui/src/components/GraphView.tsx= (+16 LOC net) — initial RF =fitView= prop now gated on =!focus= (i.e., only fires when no focus param); focused-fit driven from a single ready-state path (no double-fitView). No more full-graph flash when =?focus= is set.
- *F-4 (low design) closed:* =ui/src/app/router.tsx= (+75 LOC net for both F-4 and F-5) — lazy =TaskDialog= now renders a visible dialog skeleton shell as Suspense fallback while the chunk loads, instead of =fallback={null}=. First task click shows immediate UI feedback.
- *F-5 (low bug) closed:* =tasksRoute.validateSearch= now also reads =layout?: 'list' | 'kanban'=; open/close reducers spread =prev= so =?layout=kanban= survives task-modal navigation; verified by hard-reload at =/tasks?layout=kanban&task=TASK-NNN= opening both correctly.
- Verification (re-run on worktree + merged main): =cargo test --workspace= 190 → 194 (+4 tests across parser robustness + last-good behavior); =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; =git diff --check= clean; MSRV 1.78 preserved. =cd ui && npm run build= clean (vite 6.4.2; main =index= chunk 348.54 → 351.33 kB = +2.79 kB from lazy fallback shell, well under 360 kB budget; vendor-codemirror 390.51 kB unchanged).

** Description
Bundle of two daemon-side correctness fixes from the TASK-DK0Y2 reviewer pass. Both live in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/=, share no overlap with the UI-side TASK-DK0Y2.1.

*F-2 (medium correctness):* =get_task= (=api.rs:208-222=) finds the task in the materialized snapshot, then =TaskDetail::from_summary= rereads and reparses =summary.source_file= from disk. If =.orgasmic/sprint.org= is temporarily malformed, moved, or otherwise in a state where the index is serving its last-good projection, the task list can still show the task while opening the dialog returns a 500 from the fresh parse. Fix: either (a) parse/cache task bodies during project indexing (=ProjectIndex= gets a =task_bodies: BTreeMap<TaskId, TaskBody>= alongside =tasks=), OR (b) on fresh-parse failure during =TaskDetail::from_summary=, degrade to an empty body with a parse diagnostic in the response (e.g., =parse_warning: Option<String>= field) instead of failing the whole detail endpoint. Pick (a) if the body parse is cheap and worth the memory; pick (b) if you'd rather keep =ProjectIndex= lean. Either way: NO endpoint-500 when the list is serving last-good.

*F-3 (low correctness):* =parse_task_body= (=index.rs:975-1004= + =1006-1027=) only reads named child sections, so free body text BEFORE the first =**= subsection is silently lost. Inside =** Acceptance Criteria=, any non-empty non-bullet line is converted into an unchecked checklist item, so explanatory prose before the actual =- [ ]= bullets renders as an unchecked "criterion". Fix: (i) fold pre-section task body into =description= when no explicit =** Description= subsection exists (or surface as a separate =preamble: Option<String>= field — your call); (ii) inside =** Acceptance Criteria=, only emit checklist items from lines that actually start with =- [X]= / =- [x]= / =- [ ]= / =- [-]= (rule on the marker prefix); non-bullet prose should be preserved separately (e.g. a =prose: Vec<String>= field on the section) OR concatenated into a leading =description-style= block above the list.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-2:* If =.orgasmic/sprint.org= is malformed (intentionally introduce a parse error mid-file for a manual test, but no need to commit it), =GET /projects/:id/tasks/:task_id= for a task whose summary IS in the last-good index does NOT return 500. It returns either the parsed body (if disk is still readable) or a TaskDetail with empty/None body fields + an optional parse-warning surface.
- [ ] *F-2:* Add at least one daemon test simulating the last-good case (project index has a task; disk parse fails or returns different shape). Test asserts no 5xx.
- [ ] *F-3:* Task body with free prose before the first =** Description= subsection: prose is captured (either folded into =description= or surfaced separately, your choice) — not silently dropped.
- [ ] *F-3:* Task body with =** Acceptance Criteria= containing a non-bullet line BEFORE the first =- [X]/[ ]= bullet: the non-bullet line is NOT emitted as an unchecked checklist item. Existing checklist parse is unchanged. Add at least one parser test for this case.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.

** Notes
Single coupled commit bundled with TASK-DK0Y2.1 per TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5} pattern (one merge SHA, two implementer.done tx). Fix-implementer dispatch — no reviewer-of-reviewer per discipline.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 22:02] Shipped in single coupled commit =1e1aa30= (same as TASK-DK0Y2.1); merged no-ff as =005a423=. =implementer.done= =tx-20260523-org-0154= hand-coded (CLI no-multi-task gap, fold into TASK-FB8CK.3 polish #3). Shares =:MERGE_SHA:= + =:CODEX_COMMIT:= + =:CODEX_SESSION:= + token/wall metadata with TASK-DK0Y2.1's =tx-20260523-org-0153=.
- *F-2 (medium correctness) closed:* =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs= (+165 LOC net for both F-2 and F-3) — new =TaskBody= struct + =task_bodies: BTreeMap<TaskId, TaskBody>= field on =ProjectIndex=, populated during project indexing. =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= (+46 LOC) — =get_task= now joins =TaskSummary= from the snapshot with =TaskBody= from the same snapshot (Option A picked: last-good model). No more fresh-disk reparse on request. If indexing serves a last-good projection, =GET /projects/:id/tasks/:task_id= returns that same projection — no 500.
- *F-3 (low correctness) closed:* =parse_task_body= refactored — free body text BEFORE the first =**= subsection is now folded into =description= (when no explicit =** Description= block exists), not silently dropped. =** Acceptance Criteria= parsing restricted to lines starting with =- [X] / - [x] / - [ ] / - [-]=; non-bullet prose inside the section preserved separately (NOT emitted as unchecked checklist items).
- Verification: =cargo test --workspace= 190 → 194 (+4 tests: parser pre-section prose handling, AcceptanceCriteria non-bullet preservation, last-good behavior on indexed body, body extraction unit test). =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean. =cargo fmt --check= clean. =git diff --check= clean. * DONE TASK-FB8CK.3 Manager CLI polish: auto-transition lifecycle + multi-task dispatch + scaffold keyword + cleanup status :cli:dispatch:dogfood:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK.3
:MERGE_SHA:        372099b
:CODEX_COMMIT:     effffdd
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MK2Q2
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/Cargo.toml
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/goal.org .orgasmic/sprint.org .orgasmic/tx/2026-05.org shipped/schema/state-machine.org shipped/manager/persona.org shipped/schema/tx.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FB8CK TASK-FB8CK.1 TASK-FB8CK.2 TASK-DK0Y2
:END:

** Description
CLI polish round driven by the TASK-DK0Y2 end-to-end dogfood. The new =orgasmic manager dispatch= / =dispatch-close= surface worked across implementer + reviewer + fix-implementer phases with zero hand-coded tx for 4 of the 5 close operations — but **four specific gaps** required manual hand-edits or surfaced reviewer-recommended polish. Close them now so every future dispatch is fully mechanical.

Plus a fifth gap (daemon watcher reload investigation) that is daemon-side rather than CLI-side; filed as TASK-FB8CK.4 separately so its scope doesn't bleed into this CLI dispatch.

The four gaps in this scope:

*Gap 1 — auto-transition lifecycle stage on dispatch / close.* Today =dispatch --kind implementer= validates the heading is in =Backlog= or =Implement= state but does NOT write back. Manager has to hand-edit =* BACKLOG TASK-NNN= → =* IMPLEMENT TASK-NNN= (and =:STATE: backlog= → =:STATE: implement=) in a separate commit. Same gap on close: =dispatch-close --kind implementer --status done= leaves the heading at IMPLEMENT; manager hand-edits to REVIEW; on reviewer close it should hand-edit to DONE. Lifecycle correctness matters for the kanban — surfaced this session by the user when the Tasks page IMPLEMENT/REVIEW columns showed empty during in-flight work.

Transition matrix:
- =dispatch --kind implementer= on a task at BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT
- =dispatch --kind implementer= on a task at IMPLEMENT (already there) → no-op (idempotent)
- =dispatch-close --kind implementer --status done= → IMPLEMENT → REVIEW
- =dispatch-close --kind implementer --status aborted= → IMPLEMENT → BACKLOG (revert)
- =dispatch --kind reviewer= on a task at REVIEW → no-op
- =dispatch --kind reviewer= on a task at IMPLEMENT / DONE / SHIP → REVIEW (broader matrix per existing validator)
- =dispatch-close --kind reviewer --status done= with =VERDICT=clean= and no recommended subtasks → REVIEW → DONE
- =dispatch-close --kind reviewer --status done= with findings + recommended subtasks → REVIEW (stay; manager will dispatch fix-impl next)
- =dispatch-close --kind reviewer --status aborted= → REVIEW → IMPLEMENT (revert to pre-review state) OR leave alone (acceptable — pick simpler)
- For *fix-implementer* dispatches (=:PARENT_TASK:= set on subtask): =dispatch-close --status done= on a fix-subtask flips subtask BACKLOG → DONE directly (one-round-of-fixes discipline; no separate REVIEW). The CLI can detect this via the heading's =:PARENT_TASK:= property.

Atomicity: the heading edit + tx append must succeed-together-or-fail-together. Existing pre-flight pattern (validate → worktree → tx → spawn) extends to (validate → worktree → tx → heading-edit → spawn); on heading-edit fail, roll back tx (or accept tx-as-source-of-truth and surface heading-edit failure as a warning — pick the simpler robust path).

*Gap 2 — READY keyword reconciliation.* TASK-YFAER narrowed the lifecycle allowlist to 9 keywords (BACKLOG/EXPLORE/PLAN/IMPLEMENT/REVIEW/SHIP/DONE/BLOCKED/CANCELLED). But =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs= scaffold still emits =* READY TASK-XXX= for new task headings, which the org parser drops (treats READY as part of title) and the dispatch CLI rejects with "missing lifecycle TODO keyword". Two valid fixes:
- (a) Add READY to the TODO_KEYWORDS allowlist + state-machine. Backwards-friendly but pollutes the narrowed vocabulary.
- (b) Update the scaffold to emit =* BACKLOG=. Cleaner, matches the post-TASK-YFAER vocabulary.

**Pick (b).** Find every place in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs= (and any related project-init code) that emits =READY= and change to =BACKLOG=. Update any scaffold tests in the same crate that assert =* READY= in fixtures.

*Gap 3 — multi-task dispatch.* =--task= currently accepts ONE task slug. Bundled fix dispatches (TASK-DK0Y2.{1,2} pattern, also seen in TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5} / TASK-ZYPJC.{1..4}) need to write =:TASK: TASK-A TASK-B ...= on the pre-flight tx AND append N matching =implementer.done= tx on close. Today the manager hand-codes the N-th tx. Fix:
- =dispatch=: accept =--task TASK-A --task TASK-B= (repeated) OR =--task TASK-A,TASK-B= (comma-separated). Prefer **repeated arg** (clap idiom; less shell-quoting drama). Validate all tasks exist + all are in a dispatchable state. Pre-flight tx writes =:TASK:= as space-separated slugs.
- =dispatch-close=: accept the same repeated/comma form. On close, append N =implementer.done= tx (one per task) sharing =:MERGE_SHA:= + =:CODEX_COMMIT:= + =:CODEX_SESSION:= + =:WALL:= + =:TOKENS:= + =:CLOSED_TX:= + =:BRANCH:= — single tx-write transaction that appends N records (atomic via the existing fs append + advisory flock).
- =dispatch-status=: detect bundled-task close (currently flagged "accept-as-v1" limitation in TASK-FB8CK dogfood notes); a dispatch is "closed" when any one of its tasks has a matching =implementer.done=/=reviewer.done= tx since the manager.dispatch_started timestamp.

*Gap 4 — dispatch-close cleanup ordering / error recovery.* Reviewer flagged: close tx appended BEFORE worktree-remove + branch-delete. If cleanup fails after tx, durable state says "closed" while local state has a dangling worktree/branch. No retry path. Fix: rearrange cleanup logic and add a =:CLEANUP_STATUS:= property to the close tx:
- Run worktree-remove + branch-delete FIRST (best-effort).
- Then append the close tx with =:CLEANUP_STATUS: ok= (both succeeded), =:CLEANUP_STATUS: worktree_failed= (worktree-remove failed), =:CLEANUP_STATUS: branch_failed=, or =:CLEANUP_STATUS: partial=. Include error message in =:CLEANUP_ERROR:= optional property.
- Add =orgasmic manager dispatch-status --cleanup-failed= filter that lists open / closed dispatches whose =:CLEANUP_STATUS:= is non-ok, so the manager can run a retry pass.

Alternative simpler approach if the above seems heavy: keep tx-append-before-cleanup ordering but wrap cleanup in =.with_context()= and emit a =manager.cleanup_failed= follow-up tx if cleanup errors after the close tx is written. Both approaches are acceptable; pick whichever is cleaner to test.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *Gap 1 (dispatch transition):* =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-X --kind implementer= on a task heading at =* BACKLOG TASK-X ...= AND =:STATE: backlog= writes the heading to =* IMPLEMENT TASK-X ...= AND =:STATE: implement= atomically with the pre-flight tx append. Verify by reading sprint.org immediately after dispatch.
- [ ] *Gap 1 (close transition — implementer done):* =dispatch-close --task TASK-X --kind implementer --status done= on a task NOT having =:PARENT_TASK:= flips heading IMPLEMENT → REVIEW. If the task has =:PARENT_TASK:= set (fix-subtask), flip directly to DONE (one-round-of-fixes).
- [ ] *Gap 1 (close transition — reviewer done):* =dispatch-close --task TASK-X --kind reviewer --status done --property VERDICT=clean --property RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS=""= flips REVIEW → DONE. If =--property VERDICT=has-issues...= and =--property RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS="TASK-X.1 TASK-X.2"= (non-empty), leave at REVIEW.
- [ ] *Gap 1 (idempotency):* re-dispatching on a task already at IMPLEMENT is a no-op (no tx, no heading change, no error — the existing CLI may not need this if it errors on "already dispatched", which is also acceptable, but the heading-flip must not fail on idempotent retry).
- [ ] *Gap 1 (atomicity):* if heading-edit fails (e.g., sprint.org is read-only), the pre-flight tx must NOT be appended (or must be rolled back / followed by a cancel tx). No durable "tx says started, heading says still backlog" state.
- [ ] *Gap 2 (READY → BACKLOG scaffold):* =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs= and any related init code emit =* BACKLOG TASK-NNN= for new headings, not =* READY=. =orgasmic init= on a fresh project produces sprint.org that the dispatch CLI accepts without manual edits. Existing scaffold tests updated.
- [ ] *Gap 3 (multi-task dispatch):* =orgasmic manager dispatch --task TASK-A --task TASK-B --kind implementer --brief ...= validates both tasks exist + both in dispatchable state, creates one worktree (the WRITE_SCOPE is union), writes ONE pre-flight tx with =:TASK: TASK-A TASK-B=. =dispatch --kind reviewer= same.
- [ ] *Gap 3 (multi-task close):* =dispatch-close --task TASK-A --task TASK-B --status done --merge-sha XXX ...= appends N =implementer.done= tx (one per task), all sharing the merge metadata + =:CLOSED_TX:= pointing to the same pre-flight tx. Hand-coding the N-th tx is no longer needed.
- [ ] *Gap 3 (dispatch-status):* bundled-task closes are correctly resolved (no false "open" reports when one of the bundled tasks has a matching close tx).
- [ ] *Gap 4 (cleanup status):* close tx carries =:CLEANUP_STATUS: ok= when both worktree-remove and branch-delete succeed; =worktree_failed= / =branch_failed= / =partial= otherwise. =:CLEANUP_ERROR:= populated with a one-line error message when status is non-ok. =orgasmic manager dispatch-status --cleanup-failed= (or equivalent filter) lists non-ok closes.
- [ ] Tests: at least one new integration test per gap (Gap 1: dispatch-flip-then-close-flip round-trip on a task fixture; Gap 2: scaffold emits BACKLOG; Gap 3: two-task dispatch + close writes 1 pre-flight + 2 close tx; Gap 4: simulate worktree-remove failure → close tx records =CLEANUP_STATUS: worktree_failed=).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved.

** Notes
This is a Rust-workspace dispatch; no UI changes. Estimated 400-600 LOC across =manager.rs= + =project.rs= + tests. Single coupled commit per the prior TASK-FB8CK / TASK-FB8CK.{1,2} pattern.

Lifecycle correctness for THIS dispatch itself: manager will (manually, since auto-transition doesn't exist yet) flip TASK-FB8CK.3 BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT at dispatch, then IMPLEMENT → REVIEW at implementer.done, then REVIEW → DONE on reviewer-clean. After this lands, every subsequent dispatch is fully mechanical.

Schema update: =shipped/schema/tx.org= for the =:CLEANUP_STATUS: / :CLEANUP_ERROR:= optional fields on =implementer.done= / =reviewer.done= / =manager.dispatch_aborted=. Update the Optional property list per the per-type split TASK-FB8CK.2 shipped.

Reviewer pass dispatches after merge per persona.org rule 2; reviewer will spot-check the lifecycle transition matrix + the bundled-task close behavior + the cleanup-error path.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 22:32] Shipped in single coupled commit =effffdd= on =task-045.3-impl=; merged no-ff as =372099b= on main. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~15m18s wall, 191,746 tokens, session =019e5643-6d55-77d0-be6a-1d07b1837944=. 5 files changed, +1115 / -107 net. =implementer.done= =tx-20260523-org-0156= via =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-FB8CK.3 --status done --merge-sha 372099b --codex-commit effffdd --codex-session ... --tokens 191746 --wall 15m18s --branch-delete= — **first close that exercised the new auto-transition feature this dispatch shipped**.
- *Gap 1 (auto-transition lifecycle) closed:* =manager.rs= +663 LOC net. =dispatch= reads heading state via the org parser, validates against the per-kind dispatchable set, then writes the heading TODO keyword + =:STATE:= property atomically via the same =set_title_line= / =set_property= pattern used elsewhere in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs=. =dispatch-close= does the matching close-side flip. Full transition matrix per the brief: BACKLOG→IMPLEMENT (implementer start), IMPLEMENT→REVIEW (implementer.done, parent task), IMPLEMENT→DONE (implementer.done, fix-subtask with =:PARENT_TASK:=), IMPLEMENT→BACKLOG (aborted), IMPLEMENT/DONE/SHIP→REVIEW (reviewer start), REVIEW→DONE (reviewer.done with =VERDICT=clean= + empty =RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS=), REVIEW (stay) (reviewer.done with non-empty subtasks).
- *Gap 2 (READY → BACKLOG scaffold) closed:* =shipped/project-scaffold/sprint.org= updated to emit =* BACKLOG= (was missing or =* READY=); =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs= scaffold also updated. Scaffold tests updated to assert =* BACKLOG=. No change to =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs::TODO_KEYWORDS= — TASK-YFAER narrowing preserved.
- *Gap 3 (multi-task dispatch) closed:* =--task= now accepts repeated values (clap =ArgAction::Append=). =dispatch= validates all tasks, creates one worktree, writes one pre-flight tx with =:TASK: TASK-A TASK-B=, spawns one codex, applies heading flip to each task atomically. =dispatch-close= accepts repeated =--task= and appends N close tx all sharing =:MERGE_SHA: + :CODEX_COMMIT: + :CODEX_SESSION: + :WALL: + :TOKENS: + :BRANCH: + :CLOSED_TX:=. =dispatch-status= resolves bundled closes only when ALL bundled tasks have matching close tx (stricter than the v1 "any" heuristic).
- *Gap 4 (cleanup status) closed:* =dispatch-close= now runs worktree-remove + branch-delete (best-effort) BEFORE appending the close tx. Close tx carries =:CLEANUP_STATUS:= ∈ {=ok=, =worktree_failed=, =branch_failed=, =partial=, =worktree_missing=} and =:CLEANUP_ERROR:= when non-ok. =dispatch-status --cleanup-failed= filter lists records with non-ok cleanup. Verified on this very close: =tx-20260523-org-0156= carries =:CLEANUP_STATUS: ok=.
- *Schema update:* =shipped/schema/tx.org= adds =:CLEANUP_STATUS:= + =:CLEANUP_ERROR:= as Optional properties on =implementer.done= / =reviewer.done= / =manager.dispatch_aborted=.
- Verification (worktree + merged main): =cargo test --workspace= 194 → 198 (+4 integration tests covering lifecycle transitions, reviewer outcomes/abort, multi-task dispatch+close round-trip, cleanup-failure reporting, scaffold emission). =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean. =cargo fmt --check= clean. =git diff --check= clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved. No new deps. UI build N/A.
- **First-close dogfood finding (heuristic gap, not a regression):** The auto-transition matrix uses =:PARENT_TASK:= presence as the signal for "fix-subtask → direct-to-DONE (one-round-of-fixes)". On this very close, TASK-FB8CK.3 has =:PARENT_TASK: TASK-FB8CK= (it's a polish subtask of the TASK-FB8CK cluster) so the close auto-flipped IMPLEMENT → DONE — but TASK-FB8CK.3 is a NEW-scope polish task, not a reviewer-finding fix subtask, and SHOULD get a reviewer pass per persona.org rule 2. Manager hand-corrected DONE → REVIEW immediately (this Evidence section is being written on REVIEW). **Root cause:** =:PARENT_TASK:= conflates two distinct semantics: (a) fix-subtask born from reviewer findings (TASK-ZYPJC.5.1, TASK-EBWZ9.{1..5}, TASK-DK0Y2.{1,2}) where one-round-of-fixes-skip-review is correct, and (b) polish/follow-up subtask under a parent for organizational grouping (TASK-FB8CK.3, future TASK-FB8CK.4) which should get a reviewer pass like any other task. **Suggested follow-up:** TASK-FB8CK.5 — either (i) add a =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= boolean property that the manager sets explicitly when filing reviewer-finding fix subtasks, and the auto-transition keys off that instead of =:PARENT_TASK:=; or (ii) add a =--fix-subtask= flag to =dispatch-close= so the manager decides per-close; (i) is more idiomatic (declarative on the heading), (ii) is more explicit at the action point. **Accept-as-v1 alternative:** stop using =:PARENT_TASK:= for non-fix subtasks (file them as flat TASK-VEZYR, TASK-B2P1Z with separate IDs); the heuristic is correct for the intended discipline.
- Worktree =/tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-045.3= removed; local branch =task-045.3-impl= deleted (origin retains as audit ref).
- *Auto-flip on dispatch worked too:* BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT on this task's dispatch fired automatically via the new CLI (the dispatch ran with the OLD binary so this specific transition was still hand-coded; but the test suite verifies the flip works end-to-end on stub-codex fixtures).

Produced =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-045.3-review-report.md=. Verdict: =has-issues, no blocks-ship=. **3 findings, all medium** (1 bug + 2 correctness): F-1 overlapping multi-task dispatches bypass open-dispatch guard (open A then dispatch A+B passes validation but creates two live dispatches owning TASK-A); F-2 spawn-fail leaves lifecycle flip orphaned (no pre-state restore — BACKLOG task stuck at IMPLEMENT even after =manager.dispatch_aborted=); F-3 bundled close writes not atomic (N tx via separate /tx requests; first appends + later fails → partial-bundle-close + worktree-cleanup-already-ran + =dispatch-status= shows as plain "open" with no PARTIAL_CLOSED warning). =reviewer.done= =tx-20260523-org-0158= via =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-FB8CK.3 --status done --property VERDICT=...= — second auto-transition live verification: =:RECOMMENDED_SUBTASKS=TASK-FB8CK.5= non-empty → heading correctly stayed at REVIEW (matrix rule fired).

Rationale: declarative is visible in =.orgasmic/sprint.org=, survives retries + resumed manager sessions, doesn't rely on closer remembering semantic context at action time. An imperative override flag could still be added later for emergency manual correction, but the default surface should be heading-property-driven.

Once multi-task dispatch exists (Gap 3 shipped this round), status output should distinguish "open and never closed" from "some close tx landed, but the bundle is incomplete". Fold into F-3 fix.
