* DONE TASK-FB8CK.5 Dispatch hardening: overlap detection + spawn-fail rollback + bundled-close atomicity + :FIX_SUBTASK: heuristic :cli:dispatch:dogfood:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK.5
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MK2Q2
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs shipped/schema/tx.org
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-045.3-review-report.md .orgasmic/sprint.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FB8CK.3
:END:

** Description
Bundled polish round closing the 3 reviewer findings on TASK-FB8CK.3 + the already-known =:PARENT_TASK:= heuristic gap. All four live in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs= + tests; landed as ONE coupled commit per the reviewer's explicit bundle recommendation.

*F-1 (medium bug):* =build_dispatch_plan= overlap check only rejects when an open dispatch contains ALL requested tasks. Open =TASK-A= + new =dispatch --task TASK-A --task TASK-B= passes validation because =latest_open_dispatch_for_tasks= misses the partial overlap. Two live dispatches end up owning =TASK-A=. Fix: reject any open record whose =:TASK:= set INTERSECTS the requested task set. Regression test: open A, then attempt to dispatch A+B → must error with a clear "already open for TASK-A" message.

*F-2 (medium correctness):* =cmd_dispatch= flips the heading lifecycle (e.g., BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT) BEFORE spawning codex. If =spawn_codex= fails, the code appends =manager.dispatch_aborted= + cleans up the worktree, but never restores the heading — leaving a durably-aborted task stuck at IMPLEMENT. Fix: capture pre-dispatch stages (per task in the bundle), and on spawn-fail rollback path restore them via the same heading-writer helpers. If the restore itself fails, append a warning to stderr but keep the abort tx as durable record.

*F-3 (medium correctness):* =dispatch-close= for a bundled dispatch appends N close tx via N separate /tx requests. First-append-succeeds + later-append-fails leaves the tx log with a partial bundle close AND the worktree is already cleaned up (per gap 4's cleanup-before-tx ordering). =scan_open_dispatches= correctly requires all bundled tasks to mark the dispatch closed — so =dispatch-status= shows it as plain "open" with NO PARTIAL_CLOSED warning. Fix: pick one — (a) make bundled close appends a single daemon-side batch/transaction (write all N records inside ONE /tx POST or with a daemon-side multi-write API); OR (b) make =dispatch-close= idempotent in the partial case (subsequent calls only append the missing close tx for tasks that don't yet have one), AND surface =PARTIAL_CLOSED=<closed>/<total>= in =dispatch-status= output with the missing task ids. **Pick (b)** — idempotent + visible-in-status. Simpler than batch tx semantics, gives the manager an explicit retry path.

*Heuristic gap (already-known, from TASK-FB8CK.3 close-dogfood):* =:PARENT_TASK:= conflates "reviewer-finding fix subtask" (skip review, one-round-of-fixes) with "polish/follow-up subtask" (normal cycle, gets reviewer pass). Reviewer recommends: add =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= boolean property to fix-subtask headings; the auto-transition keys off that property instead of =:PARENT_TASK:= presence. Manager sets =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= when filing reviewer-finding fixes; polish-subtasks (like TASK-FB8CK.3, TASK-FB8CK.5) omit the property.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1:* =dispatch --task TASK-A --task TASK-B= when TASK-A has an open dispatch errors with a message naming TASK-A as the overlapping task. Integration test asserts the regression: open A, then attempt A+B, expect error.
- [ ] *F-2:* Spawn-fail rollback restores the lifecycle stage of each bundled task to its pre-dispatch state. Integration test: stub the codex spawn to fail; assert heading is back at BACKLOG (or whatever the pre-state was) after =manager.dispatch_aborted= lands.
- [ ] *F-3 (approach b):* If a bundled close partially completes (e.g., simulated tx-append failure on the 2nd of 2 tasks), re-running =dispatch-close --task TASK-A --task TASK-B --status done ...= with identical args completes the missing close tx without double-appending the first. =dispatch-status= surfaces =PARTIAL_CLOSED=1/2 missing=[TASK-B]= (or similar) for the dispatch.
- [ ] *Heuristic:* Auto-transition uses =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= as the "fix-subtask → direct-to-DONE" signal. =:PARENT_TASK:= alone (without =:FIX_SUBTASK:=) → normal IMPLEMENT → REVIEW cycle. Add a schema doc to =shipped/schema/state-machine.org= or =shipped/manager/persona.org= naming when the manager should set =:FIX_SUBTASK: t=.
- [ ] *Heuristic test:* fixture with =:PARENT_TASK: T --kind implementer --status done= without =:FIX_SUBTASK:= flips IMPLEMENT → REVIEW; same fixture with =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= flips IMPLEMENT → DONE.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved. No new deps.

** Notes
Single coupled commit. Polish-subtask (not a reviewer-finding fix-subtask) — so it goes through the normal lifecycle: BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT (auto on dispatch) → REVIEW (auto on close, NEW heuristic; TASK-FB8CK.5 itself does NOT have =:FIX_SUBTASK: t=) → DONE (on reviewer pass clean). This is also a dogfood opportunity: TASK-FB8CK.5's own close will exercise the NEW heuristic it's shipping.

Reviewer pass dispatches after merge per persona.org rule 2. Spot-check the overlap-detection error message, the spawn-fail restore path, the idempotent partial-close behavior, and the =:FIX_SUBTASK:= heuristic table-driven correctness.

- **FULL-CYCLE DOGFOOD PASS**: TASK-FB8CK.5 lacks =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= (polish-subtask), so close auto-flipped IMPLEMENT → REVIEW correctly via the new heuristic this commit shipped. =:CLEANUP_STATUS: ok= recorded on =tx-20260523-org-0161=. First close in session needing zero manager hand-correction end-to-end.
- *:FIX_SUBTASK: heuristic*: =manager.rs= reads =task.properties.get("FIX_SUBTASK")= true-ish parsing (=t=, =true=, =1=, case-insensitive). =:PARENT_TASK:= decoupled from lifecycle decisions.

* DONE TASK-FB8CK.4 Daemon watcher reload: direct sprint.org edits must trigger project refresh :daemon:watcher:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FB8CK.4
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_C87Z9
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests
:READ_SCOPE:       CLAUDE.md crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-C7C8P
:END:

** Description
**Symptom**: direct edits to =.orgasmic/sprint.org= (via Claude Code's Edit tool, the new TASK-FB8CK.3 CLI auto-flip writer, or any other path that's not a daemon tx-route write) do NOT trigger a daemon project-index refresh. =/projects/:id/tasks/:task_id= continues to serve the pre-edit lifecycle stage until either (a) someone POSTs to =/tx= (the dispatch CLI's pre-flight tx-file write triggers this side effect), or (b) the daemon is restarted. The Tasks-page kanban displays stale state, undermining the lifecycle-stage discipline shipped in TASK-FB8CK.3.

**Observed 3× this session**: (1) manual =BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT= flip via Edit tool for TASK-DK0Y2 mid-dispatch — daemon stale until restart; (2) post-TASK-DK0Y2 merge — sprint.org changes weren't reflected until daemon rebuild + restart; (3) TASK-FB8CK.5 auto-flip via the new CLI write path — daemon stale until restart (verified live moments before this task was filed).

**Watcher code state (current)**: The =RecommendedWatcher= IS spawned (=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs:80-89=). Per-project =.orgasmic/= directory IS watched recursively via =WatcherCommand::WatchProject= sent from =Daemon::run= for each project in the initial board snapshot (=lib.rs:170-174=). =flush()= canonicalizes both sides of the path comparison (=watcher.rs:230, 237=, mirrors the CLAUDE.md macOS pitfall). =record_event= filters to =.org=-extension files only (=watcher.rs:218=). So the watcher LOOKS like it should fire on sprint.org changes — but empirically it doesn't.

**Hypotheses to investigate** (codex picks ones to verify):

1. **Atomic-write event-shape mismatch.** Editors (and likely Claude Code's Edit tool, and the new CLI's =set_title_line= writer) often use a write-temp-file-then-rename pattern, which produces =EventKind::Create=, =EventKind::Modify(Name(_))=, possibly =EventKind::Remove= on the temp file rather than a direct =EventKind::Modify(Data)= on sprint.org. The =record_event= filter (=watcher.rs:206-216=) accepts =Create/Modify/Remove/Any/Other=. So this *should* be covered — but verify. Add debug tracing on each raw event, reproduce the edit, see what arrives.

2. **Path canonicalization mismatch on macOS.** On macOS, FSEvents may report =/System/Volumes/Data/Users/...= or =/private/Users/...= paths. The watcher's =canonical()= helper (=watcher.rs:202-204=) calls =fs::canonicalize= (resolves all symlinks). The snapshot =entry.path= used in =touched_projects= is canonicalized inside =flush= (=watcher.rs:247=). But the initial =WatchProject= passes =entry.path.clone()= raw (=lib.rs:171=). If the raw path doesn't match the FSEvent-reported canonical form, the watch handle might be registered on a path that FSEvents never reports against. Investigate.

3. **Notify watch handle silently dropped.** The watcher =watch()= return is checked (=watcher.rs:145-148= warns on error), but daemon logs don't appear to write to disk (=~/.orgasmic/logs/= is empty). Run with =RUST_LOG=debug= or capture stderr to see if =watch project failed= ever fires.

4. **=record_event= filter dropping non-=.org= temp files.** Editors writing to =sprint.org.tmp= or =sprint.org.swp= or =.#sprint.org= produce non-=.org= extensions; the filter at line 218 drops them. The atomic rename's final event (CREATE/MODIFY of sprint.org) should still fire — unless the rename event lacks the extension or arrives only on the source side. Trace it.

5. **=record_event= path-tracking gap.** =record_event= uses =path.extension()= to filter. If FSEvents reports parent-directory events (no extension), they're dropped. Verify the event paths carry sprint.org specifically, not just =.orgasmic/=.

**Approach**: write a focused reproduction first — an integration test that simulates the Edit-tool write pattern AND the new CLI's =set_title_line= write pattern. Read =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs= for the latter (it's the most relevant new-write path). Add tracing or a test bus subscriber to capture what events arrive. Then fix the gap.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reproduce the bug in an integration test: edit =.orgasmic/sprint.org= using one of the write patterns that empirically fails to trigger reload (Claude Code's Edit tool — likely temp-file + rename; OR the new TASK-FB8CK.3 CLI =set_title_line= writer — read the actual writer code to identify). Assert =index.refresh_project= is NOT called within (debounce + 100ms) on the failing path — to capture the bug.
- [ ] Fix the root cause. Add a code comment near the fix naming the failure mode + reproduction pattern.
- [ ] Regression test passes: same write pattern → daemon refreshes within (debounce + 100ms). Repurpose the bug-capture test by flipping the assertion, or add a sibling positive test. The existing =fs_event_triggers_project_refresh= test (=watcher.rs:304=) probably uses a simpler write that doesn't hit the bug — extend it OR add a sibling test that exercises the bug path.
- [ ] If the bug is in =record_event='s filter, document the new accepted event shapes in a code comment.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= clean (with the new test). =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean. =cargo fmt --check= clean. =git diff --check= clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved. No new deps.
- [ ] No regressions on EXISTING reload paths: dispatch CLI tx-file writes still trigger refresh.

** Notes
**Disjoint scope from TASK-FB8CK.5 in flight** (TASK-FB8CK.5 = =crates/orgasmic-cli/=, TASK-FB8CK.4 = =crates/orgasmic-daemon/=). Parallel dispatch is safe.

Polish-subtask of TASK-FB8CK (NOT a reviewer-finding fix-subtask). The new =:FIX_SUBTASK:= heuristic shipping in TASK-FB8CK.5 will correctly classify TASK-FB8CK.4 as polish (no =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= property) → normal IMPLEMENT → REVIEW lifecycle. BUT note: if TASK-FB8CK.4 lands BEFORE TASK-FB8CK.5, the OLD heuristic (=:PARENT_TASK:=-presence) will still apply at close time and incorrectly flip IMPLEMENT → DONE; manager will hand-correct DONE → REVIEW. If TASK-FB8CK.4 lands AFTER TASK-FB8CK.5, the new heuristic correctly flips IMPLEMENT → REVIEW. Order doesn't matter for ship — just for the dogfood-gap count.

Reviewer pass dispatches after merge per persona.org rule 2.

Tests pass in =tempfile::tempdir()= but don't reproduce. Reviewer (=tx-20260523-org-0166=) verdict =has-issues, no blocks-ship=: F-1 re-verification check (manager confirmed boot_id changed so gap IS real); F-2 medium observability gap; F-3 medium tests don't cover real path. :END:

** Description
Reviewer-finding fix-subtask of TASK-FB8CK.4 (=:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set — **first explicit use of the property that the TASK-FB8CK.5 heuristic ships**). Auto-transition will flip IMPLEMENT → DONE on implementer-close per the new heuristic (no separate REVIEW state per one-round-of-fixes discipline).

**Investigation protocol: observability FIRST, then reproduce, then diagnose against the trace, then fix.**

*Observability (F-2 closed)*: add =tracing::debug!= at three points in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs=:
1. Notify callback (=watcher.rs:80-84=, before forwarding to channel): =debug!(kind = ?event.kind, paths = ?event.paths, "raw fs watcher event")=.
2. =record_event= (after kind filter, before path push): =trace!(path = %path.display(), "recorded fs path")=.
3. =flush= (per-path after canonicalize + classification): =debug!(path = %path.display(), canon = %canon.display(), classified = ?classification, "fs path classified")= with classification ∈ {board, home_tx, project=<id>, dropped}.

Wire daemon's tracing-subscriber to honor =RUST_LOG=. Look at =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs:570-575= for the CLI subscriber setup as reference. =DaemonConfig.log_level= currently loaded but unused — wire it.

*Production-path investigation*: with observability in place, spawn fresh daemon binary on the real project. Run =touch=, =sed -i.bak=, Python =std::fs::write=-equivalent on =.orgasmic/sprint.org=. Capture trace. Diagnose:
- Events arriving but dropped? Trace shows the canonical path mismatch.
- No events at all? Check =watcher.watch()= return value; FSEvents-on-/Users firmlink quirk?
- Channel back-pressure? Less likely; unbounded channel.

*Fix based on trace*. Likely shapes:
- Switch from =RecommendedWatcher= to =PollWatcher= as fallback when FSEvents fails on /Users (heavy but reliable).
- Pre-canonicalize the snapshot =entry.path= (or compare both raw and canonical forms in classifier).
- Address a specific firmlink-vs-canonicalize mismatch surfaced by trace.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *Observability*: =RUST_LOG=orgasmic_daemon::watcher=debug ~/.orgasmic/bin/orgasmic serve= shows raw notify events AND classification decisions. =DaemonConfig.log_level= respected at subscriber init.
- [ ] *Production reproduction documented*: trace captures for =touch=, =sed=, =fs::write= on the real project. Trace either shows refresh fires OR identifies the exact drop point.
- [ ] *Fix shipped + live-verified*: all three production patterns trigger =refresh_project= within (debounce + 500ms) on ==. Manager verifies live.
- [ ] *Regression test* for the production write pattern.
- [ ] cargo test + clippy + fmt + diff-check clean. MSRV 1.78. No new deps unless tracing feature-flags.
- [ ] No regressions on existing reload paths (tx-file writes).

** Notes
**Fix-subtask of TASK-FB8CK.4** — =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set. Once 045.6 lands + live-verified, TASK-FB8CK.4 parent hand-flipped REVIEW → DONE (cluster closure). Don't add speculative event-kind handlers — reviewer was clear current =record_event= already accepts all kinds; bug is upstream (no events) or downstream (classifier dropping).

** Evidence
- [2026-05-23 Sat 23:38] Shipped =77e4486= on =task-045.6-impl=; merged no-ff as =4c1eecf= on main. Codex ~18m55s wall, 242,142 tokens, session =019e567d-a91e-7112-86b8-55f0062ddb54=. 4 files +118/-18 (=watcher.rs= +44, daemon =lib.rs= +12, CLI =main.rs= +5, new =tests/integration.rs= +49). =implementer.done= =tx-20260523-org-0168= via dispatch-close. **AUTO-FLIP DOGFOOD #10 PASS** (=:FIX_SUBTASK: t= correctly shortcut to IMPLEMENT → DONE per the new heuristic; no reviewer-of-reviewer per one-round-of-fixes discipline). =:CLEANUP_STATUS: ok=.
- **ROOT CAUSE FOUND (the hilarious finale)**: =Daemon::run= registered project watches then **dropped the =WatcherHandle=**, which closed =command_loop= and dropped the actual =RecommendedWatcher=. Synthetic unit tests masked this because their fixture retained =_handle=. Production had NO watcher at all from boot. TASK-FB8CK.4's path-canonicalize + extension-filter work was correct code but completely irrelevant — the watcher itself wasn't running in production.
- *Fix*: new =RunningDaemon= struct in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs= holds =WatcherHandle= for daemon's full lifetime. =Daemon::run= returns this handle instead of dropping it.
- *Observability shipped*: =tracing::debug!= at 4 watcher choke points (raw event, record-path, watch-registration, flush-classification). =DaemonConfig.log_level= + =RUST_LOG= wired in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs= serve entry.
- *Daemon-level integration regression* in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs= (+49 LOC) exercises real daemon process + =std::fs::write= + asserts refresh fires within debounce window.
- **LIVE VERIFICATION PASS** post-merge + rebuild + kill+spawn:
  - =touch= on sprint.org → daemon log shows =Modify(Metadata(Any))= raw event + =classified=project=orgasmic=.
  - =sed -i.bak= → editor temp-file dance (=.!<pid>!sprint.org=) + sprint.org =Modify(Name)= + sprint.org.bak Create/Remove — ALL classified =project=orgasmic=.
  - Python =Path.write_text(Path.read_text())= → =Modify(Name)=+=Modify(Metadata)=+=Modify(Data(Content))= → classified.
  - Manager hand-edit via sed to flip TASK-FB8CK.3 + TASK-FB8CK.4 REVIEW → DONE → daemon API reflected within 3 seconds **with NO restart** — first time this session.
- Tests 201 → 205 (+4 including the new daemon-level integration test). clippy/fmt/diff-check clean. MSRV 1.78 preserved. No new deps (tracing-subscriber env-filter already available).
- **TASK-FB8CK cluster FULLY CLOSED**: 045 + 045.1 + 045.2 + 045.3 + 045.4 + 045.5 + 045.6 all DONE. Manager dispatch CLI v1 + lifecycle auto-transition + watcher reliability shipped.

* DONE TASK-VEZYR Project-selector restructure: sidebar dropdown + footer Add/Manage + 4-primary nav + 4-spoiler + header LED/bell/theme/gear out-of-nav :ui:ia:routing:shell:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-VEZYR

** Description
Information-architecture restructure of the app shell per dec_CGVRW + dec_HYD58 + dec_H9P38. The current AppShell (ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx, ~500 lines) has Decisions/Architecture/Tasks/Glossary as primary nav with a sub-section "More" disclosure carrying Project, Runs, Org, ADR, Snapshots, Activity, Graph, Status, Settings (9 items). The Board page is a separate /board route surfaced via the index redirect when no last-project is stored. The user direction is to demote Board to a project-switcher affordance in the sidebar drawer, narrow primary nav to exactly 4 items + 4 spoiler items, and move Status + Settings entirely out of the sidebar into top-header icons (LED click → Status; gear → Settings). Manager popover (ManagerPopover) is already shipped per TASK-EBWZ9 and stays as-is; this task does not touch it.

*Three coupled surfaces:*

*(A) Project selector in the sidebar (dec_CGVRW).* The sidebar's first item becomes a project switcher dropdown. Selecting a project navigates to /projects/$projectId/decisions (the new default landing page) and persists to localStorage.orgasmic.active_project + localStorage.orgasmic.lastProject. The dropdown shows the currently active project (project_id + branch chip) with a small caret that opens a popover listing every registered project. A search/filter input appears once the project count exceeds ~6. The sidebar footer carries two actions: "+ Add project" (small dialog: validates a path; auto-fills project_id / repo_url / branch from .orgasmic/project.org and git config) and "Manage projects" (large modal whose contents are the existing BoardView Card grid — preserve the BoardView component as-is and mount it inside a shadcn Dialog with sm:max-w-none + h-[85vh]; Card click closes the modal AND switches the active project). Single-project users never see the Manage modal because the dropdown collapses to just the active project + the two footer actions. Empty state (no projects): dropdown disabled; footer shows "+ Add your first project" prominently.

*(B) Primary nav narrowed + spoiler (dec_HYD58).* PRIMARY = [Decisions, Architecture, Tasks, Glossary]. SPOILER ("More") = [ADR, Graph, Snapshots, Activity]. Removed from sidebar entirely: Project (overview was redundant with header; if kept route, link goes via the project-id chip click), Runs (low-value standalone page; live runs surface inside Activity per dec_CGVRW-era IA), Org (Org editor demoted; access via shipped/manager surface if needed — keep the route reachable by URL but not nav), Status (header LED is the entry point), Settings (header gear is the entry point). PROJECT_PAGE union type narrows accordingly: keep route definitions for project/runs/org/status/settings (so URL access still works), drop them from PRIMARY + MORE arrays in AppShell. The spoiler starts EXPANDED on desktop (matches current behavior); collapsed on mobile.

*(C) Top header (dec_H9P38).* Right side of the header sticks with the current LED + bell + theme + gear pattern (which already partially exists). Make the LED a real click target navigating to /projects/$projectId/status (replaces "WS live" badge entirely; that's already done). Make the gear click target navigate to /projects/$projectId/settings (already done). Mobile (<md): theme + gear collapse into the existing ⋯ MobileOverflow dropdown (already done; expand it to also expose Status in the overflow list since the LED stays visible). The current shell already implements most of (C); this task verifies the wiring stays correct after PRIMARY/MORE re-shuffle and adds a Status entry to the mobile overflow.

*(D) Index redirect (cleanup).* /board route is preserved (URL still works) but no longer the default. The root redirect should always go to /projects/$projectId/decisions; if no last-project is stored, open the "+ Add project" dialog as a startup state on /projects/none/decisions or a synthetic /welcome route (codex picks the cleanest; lean toward keeping /board as the no-project fallback route renamed to render the Manage modal full-screen).

The four BoardView Card click handler stays callable from the Manage modal (onSelectProject prop wired to "switch + close").

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *(A) Project selector*: sidebar's first menu item is a project dropdown that shows the active project; opening the dropdown lists all projects fetched from /projects; clicking a project switches active (navigate + localStorage write) and closes the dropdown. Footer shows "+ Add project" and "Manage projects" buttons. Manage modal mounts BoardView inside a shadcn Dialog with h-[85vh] + sm:max-w-none; Card click in the modal switches active project AND closes the modal. Single-project state: dropdown shows just the active project; Manage button still works (renders the single card).
- [ ] *(A) Add-project dialog*: form with a path input; on submit POST /projects (or the existing add-project endpoint — check ui/src/lib/api.ts for what's already wired and either reuse it or surface the missing piece in a clear FIXME comment, do NOT add new daemon routes in this dispatch). On success: dropdown refreshes via the existing useRefreshBus pattern, the new project becomes active, modal closes.
- [ ] *(B) Sidebar nav*: PRIMARY group has exactly 4 items in this order — Decisions, Architecture, Tasks, Glossary. MORE group has exactly 4 items — ADR, Graph, Snapshots, Activity. No other nav items in the sidebar. URLs for the removed pages (/project, /runs, /org, /status, /settings) still resolve via their existing routes.
- [ ] *(C) Header*: LED click navigates to /projects/$projectId/status. Gear click navigates to /projects/$projectId/settings. Bell + theme behavior unchanged. On <md viewports the ⋯ MobileOverflow dropdown contains: Theme submenu (Light/Dark/System), Status link, Settings link.
- [ ] *(D) Index redirect*: root path / redirects to /projects/$projectId/decisions when a last-project is in localStorage. If no last-project: redirect to /board (preserved fallback) where the Manage modal opens automatically to drive first-project setup.
- [ ] *Routing integrity*: removing PRIMARY/MORE entries does not break any deep-link URL. Bookmarks like /projects/foo/runs or /projects/foo/org still render those views correctly.
- [ ] *Active project persistence*: switching project via dropdown writes to BOTH localStorage.orgasmic.active_project and localStorage.orgasmic.lastProject (preserve dual-key compat with router.tsx::readLastProject). Reload preserves selection.
- [ ] *Responsive matrix*: desktop ≥md — full sidebar with PRIMARY + MORE expanded by default; <md — sidebar collapses to icon mode per shadcn Sidebar collapsible='icon'; MobileOverflow dropdown shown.
- [ ] *Theme/colors*: dropdown + dialog inherit shadcn tokens; no hand-rolled colors. Dark + light themes both render correctly (verify visually).
- [ ] *Verification gate*: =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build), no JS exceptions in dev or build, no chunk-size budget regression beyond +20 kB on the main =index= chunk (current baseline 359.25 kB).
- [ ] *Workspace gate*: =cargo test --workspace= + =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= + =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= all clean (no daemon changes expected; if any TypeScript-side type drift forces a tiny daemon-side adjustment, document it explicitly).
- [ ] *No new dependencies*. shadcn primitives already present (Dialog, DropdownMenu, Popover, Button, Separator); use them. If a Command palette is wanted for the project dropdown, install via the project's shadcn skill (copy-paste primitive, not a new package); otherwise a plain DropdownMenu + filter input suffices.

** Notes
*Why bundle three surfaces*: they share the same write file (=ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx=) and (B) is dependent on (A) being present (you can't drop nav items without giving users a way to reach all projects). Single coupled commit; matches TASK-EBWZ9 / TASK-DK0Y2 pattern.

*Out of scope (do not touch in this dispatch)*:
- Manager popover redesign — already shipped TASK-EBWZ9; ManagerPopover stays as-is.
- New daemon /projects endpoints (Add project may need a daemon route; if missing, leave a TODO + clear stub in the dialog, file a tiny follow-up sprint task in the implementer's last-message; do NOT block this dispatch on a daemon change).
- Per-page IA inside any primary page (Decisions/Architecture/Tasks/Glossary internal layout unchanged; just the shell around them moves).
- TanStack Router migration (already done in prior sessions).
- Manager prompt / persona / dispatch CLI work.
- Mobile-specific re-flow of internal page contents.

*Reference patterns*:
- multica-ai/multica's project selector in =packages/views/projects/= (read-only inspiration; do not bundle their code).
- shadcn DropdownMenu + Popover + Command primitives.
- Current shell's MobileOverflow + ThemeToggle patterns at =AppShell.tsx:455-499=.

*Close pattern*: single =implementer.done= tx via =orgasmic manager dispatch-close --task TASK-VEZYR --status done --merge-sha <SHA> --codex-commit <SHA> --codex-session <ID> --tokens <N> --wall <DUR> --branch-delete=. Auto-transition: BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT on dispatch; IMPLEMENT → REVIEW on close (TASK-VEZYR lacks =:FIX_SUBTASK: t=, so it's a polish task that needs a reviewer pass per persona rule 2). Reviewer dispatch follows merge.

** Description
Two low-severity reviewer findings from TASK-VEZYR bundled as a single fix-implementer round per the one-round-of-fixes discipline.

*F-1 (a11y).* =ui/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx:46-51= trigger sets =aria-label="Active project"=. Because that explicit aria-label overrides the visible text for the accessible name, screen reader users hear only "Active project" — they don't hear which project is currently selected. The selector is now the sidebar's identity and primary multi-project control; the active value must be in the accessible name.

*F-2 (design).* =ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx:272-281= MORE disclosure button renders =<button>{chevron} {literal "More" text}</button>= with no =group-data-[collapsible=icon]= adaptation. The surrounding =SidebarMenuButton= items collapse to icons via shadcn's data attribute, but this hand-rolled button sits as text-mode in the 3rem icon column when collapsed.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1*: =ProjectSelector= trigger's accessible name includes the active project's identifier (e.g., =aria-label={`Active project: ${activeLabel}`}= or =aria-labelledby= pattern). Screen reader announces the selected project. No regression on icon-collapsed mode rendering.
- [ ] *F-2*: MORE disclosure adapts to icon-collapsed mode — either (a) hide the disclosure button in icon mode while keeping spoiler items visible as icon-buttons, or (b) render an icon-only toggle with tooltip and dimensions matching =SidebarMenuButton=. Pick whichever reads cleaner.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= + clippy/fmt/diff-check unchanged (no Rust touched).
- [ ] No new deps. Stays within the 2-file WRITE_SCOPE.

** Notes
*Fix-subtask of TASK-VEZYR* — =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set. On close, auto-transition flips IMPLEMENT → DONE (skip-review per one-round-of-fixes discipline). Then manager hand-flips TASK-VEZYR REVIEW → DONE for cluster closure.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-24 Sun 13:06] Shipped =4131c5b= on =task-049.1-impl=; merged no-ff as =5587585= on main. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, 2m54s wall, 124,804 tokens, session =019e5970-fc13-7c73-8c45-afa35f80b168=. =implementer.done= =tx-20260524-org-0174= via dispatch-close. **DOGFOOD #14+#15 PASS**: BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT on dispatch; IMPLEMENT → DONE via =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= shortcut (one-round-of-fixes discipline — no reviewer-of-reviewer per persona).
- 2 files changed, +14 / -16 (exactly within WRITE_SCOPE).
- *F-1 (a11y)*: =ui/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx= — =aria-label="Active project"= → =aria-label={`Active project: ${activeLabel}`}=. Visible value (project id) now part of the accessible name. Icon-collapsed mode preserved (same =activeLabel= renders in both modes).
- *F-2 (design)*: =ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx= — MORE disclosure button gains =group-data-[collapsible=icon]:hidden=; MORE items render unconditionally via =cn(!moreOpen && 'hidden group-data-[collapsible=icon]:block')=. In expanded mode, =!moreOpen= hides the items (preserves toggle gesture); in icon-collapsed mode the disclosure button hides AND items always render as icons (no more text "More" in 3rem icon column). Clean shadcn-pattern fix.
- Verification: =cd ui && npm run build= clean. Main =index= chunk 360.83 → 360.93 kB (+0.10 kB, well within +0.5 kB limit). No new deps. Cargo workspace untouched.

** Description
Close the =FIXME(TASK-VEZYR-followup)= stub left in =ui/src/components/ProjectAddDialog.tsx:51-58= by giving the daemon a =POST /projects= route and wiring the dialog to use it.

*Current state*:
- Daemon exposes =GET /projects= and =GET /projects/:id...= (read-only).
- =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/project.rs::init_project()= scaffolds =.orgasmic/= files; =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/board.rs::register_project()= appends to =$ORGASMIC_HOME/state/board.org= and parses for validation. Both are pure file/Org operations with no CLI-only deps.
- =orgasmic project init <path>= calls =init_project= then =register_project=. =orgasmic project add <path>= calls =register_project= only.
- Daemon watches =$ORGASMIC_HOME= and refreshes index on board.org changes (per TASK-FB8CK.6 watcher fix).
- ProjectAddDialog's submit handler returns a static "register from CLI" message.

*Scope*:

*(1) Move shared logic to a common crate (or expose via daemon-callable surface).* =register_project= and =init_project= currently live in =orgasmic-cli=. The daemon needs to call them. Cleanest path: move them to =orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs= (new module). Update =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs= callsites + tests in =cli/src/{project,board}.rs= to import from =orgasmic_core::projects=. =orgasmic-cli/src/{project,board}.rs= become thin shims OR delete them entirely if the CLI now imports directly from core. Pick the cleanest option that preserves all existing tests.

*(2) Daemon =POST /projects= route.* Accept JSON body:
#+begin_src json
{
  "path": "/absolute/or/relative/path/to/repo",
  "project_id": "optional-explicit-id",
  "repo_url": "optional-explicit-url",
  "default_branch": "optional-explicit-branch",
  "scaffold": false
}
#+end_src
- =path= required; if relative, resolved against the daemon's cwd (or rejected — pick the safer behavior; the daemon doesn't know the user's cwd, so probably require absolute and return 400 otherwise).
- If =scaffold=true= AND =.orgasmic/= doesn't exist at the path: call =init_project= first (need =name=, =project_id=, =repo_url=, =default_branch= — derive missing ones the way =ScaffoldInputs::derive= does).
- If =scaffold=false= AND =.orgasmic/= doesn't exist: 400 with a clear error message ("path is not an orgasmic project; pass scaffold=true to initialize").
- Always call =register_project= after (or skip if already in board, with appropriate error).
- Trigger a board refresh so =GET /projects= reflects the new entry immediately (don't rely on the watcher debounce).
- Return =201 Created= with the new =ProjectIndex= entry (the same shape =GET /projects= returns).

*(3) UI client.* Add to =ui/src/lib/api.ts=:
#+begin_src ts
export type AddProjectRequest = {
  path: string;
  projectId?: string;
  repoUrl?: string;
  defaultBranch?: string;
  scaffold?: boolean;
};
export async function addProject(req: AddProjectRequest): Promise<ProjectIndex> { ... }
#+end_src

*(4) ProjectAddDialog wire.* Replace the FIXME stub:
- Submit handler calls =addProject({ path, projectId, repoUrl })=. (Skip =scaffold= for v1 — the dialog is for registering existing projects per its current DialogDescription "Register an existing repo that already has an orgasmic project directory.")
- On success: trigger refresh via =useRefreshBus=, switch active project via =useActiveProject.setActiveProject(returned.project_id)=, close dialog.
- On error: surface daemon error message inline (keep the amber callout block; replace the static message with the actual error). If the error is "project does not have .orgasmic/" THEN show a follow-up message + checkbox/button "Scaffold a new orgasmic project here" that re-submits with =scaffold=true=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Daemon =POST /projects= route registered in =api.rs= and reachable by the same auth as GET endpoints.
- [ ] Submitting valid body for an existing-but-unregistered orgasmic project succeeds: returns =201= with the =ProjectIndex= entry; subsequent =GET /projects= includes it.
- [ ] Submitting for a path without =.orgasmic/= AND =scaffold=false= → =400= with clear error message.
- [ ] Submitting for a path without =.orgasmic/= AND =scaffold=true= → daemon scaffolds + registers + returns =201=.
- [ ] Submitting for an already-registered project → =409 Conflict= (or =400=, pick consistent with other daemon errors) with "project already on the board" message.
- [ ] =register_project= + =init_project= now live in =orgasmic-core::projects= (or equivalently accessible from daemon); CLI tests still pass after the move; CLI subcommands (=orgasmic project init=, =orgasmic project add=) behave unchanged.
- [ ] UI: clicking "+ Add project" → filling path → submitting → dialog closes + the new project appears as the active project + nav reflects new project. Tested in Chrome (manager will smoke after merge).
- [ ] UI: error path renders the daemon error inline; if it's the "not initialized" error, the scaffold-CTA appears.
- [ ] UI: =useRefreshBus= fires after success so the ProjectSelector dropdown re-fetches.
- [ ] Verification gate: =cargo test --workspace= clean (existing 205 + new daemon route tests, expect ~+3-6 tests); =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; =git diff --check= clean. MSRV 1.78. =cd ui && npm run build= clean, main =index= chunk under +5 kB over current 360.93 kB.
- [ ] No new external dependencies in =Cargo.toml= or =package.json=.

* DONE TASK-B2P1Z.1 Daemon project-registration hardening: Org-injection validation + board.org write lock + safe error envelope + dialog a11y + Cancel-during-submit :daemon:security:concurrency:a11y:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-B2P1Z.1
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:       dec_CGVRW
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs ui/src/components/ProjectAddDialog.tsx
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs ui/src/components/ProjectAddDialog.tsx
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-050.1-brief.md /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-050-review-report.md
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace && cd ui && npm run build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-B2P1Z
:END:

** Description
Five reviewer findings from TASK-B2P1Z bundled as a single fix-implementer round per one-round-of-fixes discipline. P1 because F-1 is a HIGH security finding that blocks-ship.

*F-1 (HIGH security) — Org injection in board.org writes.* =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs:225-238= + =crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs:98-103,181-190= accept caller-provided =project_id=, =repo_url=, =default_branch=, path and render them directly into Org headings/property drawers. Re-parse-after-write only validates syntax, not absence of injected properties/headings. A bearer-auth caller can inject =:STATUS: active\n:PRIORITY: corrupted\n* INJECTED HEADING=-style payloads via newlines/control chars.

*F-2 (MEDIUM correctness) — Concurrent POST /projects races board.org.* =orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs:163-175= + =api.rs:256-263= do read-modify-write without locking. Two concurrent POSTs both read old board, append different entries, later write overwrites earlier (lost entry). Same-ID race bypasses duplicate check.

*F-3 (LOW security) — Filesystem paths leaked in error envelope.* =api.rs:298-304,318-324,5435-5438= pass raw filesystem context into JSON error responses (=write /Users/.../state/board.org=, canonical project paths). UI displays inline → developer strings as user-facing copy + path-disclosure smell.

*F-4 (LOW a11y) — Error callout has no live-region semantics.* =ProjectAddDialog.tsx:130-155= renders error callout without =role="alert"= / =aria-live=. Screen readers don't announce errors.

*F-5 (LOW design) — Cancel disabled during submit.* =ProjectAddDialog.tsx:157-168= disables both Submit AND Cancel. User can't back out via visible Cancel control.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1*: =register_project= + =init_project= validate every caller-provided string before rendering. Strict grammar for =project_id= (=[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+=, no whitespace/control chars/newlines, max length ~64). =repo_url= + =default_branch= reject newlines/control chars, max length cap. Path rejects newlines/null bytes. Integration tests: newline injection in each field reliably rejected (400 or 422) with no file write.
- [ ] *F-2*: Board read-modify-write serialized. Use =fs2::FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file)= (per CLAUDE.md trait-vs-stable pitfall; do NOT use =std::fs::File::lock= which is MSRV 1.89). Wrap read/duplicate-check/write/reparse in lock. Integration test: two concurrent POSTs with distinct IDs both land; same-ID race produces exactly one 201 + one 409.
- [ ] *F-3*: Public error messages stable, generic, path-free (e.g., "failed to read project.org", "failed to update project board"). Detailed errors logged server-side via =tracing= with original cause. UI receives the safe message.
- [ ] *F-4*: =ProjectAddDialog= error callout gets =role="alert"= AND =aria-live="polite"=. Optionally =aria-describedby= from form/inputs to the error region.
- [ ] *F-5*: Cancel button stays enabled (=disabled=false=) during submit. Clicking Cancel during pending request closes the dialog.
- [ ] All 210 tests still pass; new tests added per F-1 + F-2 (estimate +6-10). clippy/fmt/diff clean. MSRV 1.78. =cd ui && npm run build= clean, main =index= chunk under +1 kB over 361.63 kB.
- [ ] No new deps in =Cargo.toml= or =package.json=.

** Notes
*Fix-subtask of TASK-B2P1Z* — =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set. On close, auto-transition flips IMPLEMENT → DONE (skip-review per one-round-of-fixes discipline). Manager hand-flips TASK-B2P1Z REVIEW → DONE for cluster closure.

*Priority bump to P1*: F-1 affects a production HTTP surface. Fix promptly.

- 6 files changed, +392 / -17 (within WRITE_SCOPE; one allowed exception was =fs2.workspace = true= addition to =crates/orgasmic-core/Cargo.toml= per brief explicit allowance for F-2).

Integration tests in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs= +248 race two concurrent POSTs via =tokio::join!=: distinct IDs both land; same-ID race produces exactly one 201 + one 409.
- *F-3 fix*: =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= +61 — error envelope sanitized. Public messages stable + path-free ("failed to read project.org", "failed to update project board", "project file is not valid orgasmic markup"). Detailed cause logged server-side via =tracing::warn!= / =error!=.
- *F-4 fix*: =ui/src/components/ProjectAddDialog.tsx= +7 — error callout gains =role="alert"= + =aria-live="polite"=.
- *F-5 fix*: Cancel button no longer disabled during submit (=disabled=false= preserved).

* DONE TASK-PYW86 Sanitize GET endpoint error envelopes (apply TASK-B2P1Z.1 F-3 pattern to read paths) :daemon:security:hardening:followup:smoke:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-PYW86
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-B2P1Z.1
:END:

** Description
TASK-B2P1Z.1 F-3 hardened the =POST /projects= envelope so failures emit stable, generic, path-free strings ("failed to read project.org", "failed to update project board"). The hardening did not extend to GET endpoints. Live evidence surfaced during the [[2026-05-24]] TASK-B2P1Z chrome smoke: navigating to =/projects/smoke-2026-05-24/decisions= on a freshly-registered project with no =decisions.org= rendered:

#+begin_example
404 read /private/tmp/orgasmic-smoke-2026-05-24/.orgasmic/decisions.org: No such file or directory (os error 2)
#+end_example

Raw =io::Error= Display strings leak filesystem paths AND the underlying OS error number. Low severity (registered-project paths aren't truly secret — they were just submitted by the same caller), but inconsistent with the POST surface and adds developer-string-as-user-copy noise.

Audit all daemon read handlers in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= that surface =io::Error= Display via JSON error responses. Apply the TASK-B2P1Z.1 F-3 pattern: stable public message, detailed cause via =tracing::warn!= / =error!=. Endpoints to cover include (non-exhaustive — full grep is part of the task): =/projects/:id/decisions=, =/projects/:id/architecture=, =/projects/:id/glossary=, =/projects/:id/snapshots=, =/projects/:id/tasks=, =/projects/:id/tasks/:tid=, =/projects/:id/tasks/:tid/activity=, =/projects/:id/adr=, =/projects/:id/org=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grep =api.rs= for all handlers that build a JSON error from an =io::Error= and pass it through to the response body. Confirm the inventory in the PR description.
- [ ] Each such handler returns a stable, generic, path-free message (e.g., "decisions file not found", "failed to read decisions"). 404 distinct from 500.
- [ ] Detailed cause logged server-side via =tracing= at appropriate level (=warn!= for expected-not-found, =error!= for unexpected I/O).
- [ ] Integration test per endpoint family: trigger a missing-file 404 + assert the response body contains NO filesystem path (regex on response JSON).
- [ ] All tests pass; clippy/fmt/diff clean. No new deps. MSRV 1.78.

* DONE TASK-FWBYB orgasmic doctor: warn when running daemon binary is older than most-recent daemon-code merge :cli:doctor:dx:dispatch:followup:smoke:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FWBYB
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/doctor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Live evidence from the [[2026-05-24]] TASK-B2P1Z chrome smoke: the running daemon (PID 97809, ~15h uptime) predated the TASK-B2P1Z merges, so the first =POST /projects= attempt returned =405 Allow: GET, HEAD= and the dialog rendered "405 Open Manage" — confusing for a real user who'd hit the same case after pulling latest without restarting their daemon.

This is a class problem: any merge that adds/changes daemon-facing routes (api.rs, projects.rs, watcher.rs, drivers, supervisor, writer, RPC) needs a daemon restart, but the developer has no surface telling them. =orgasmic doctor= already exists as the catch-all DX/diagnostics command (per polish-phase-1 plans in goal.org). Add a check:

1. Read the running daemon's =/daemon/status= (=boot_id=, =started_at=, optionally =git_sha=).
2. Read the on-disk binary at =$ORGASMIC_HOME/bin/orgasmic= mtime (or =stat=).
3. Read =git log --since=<daemon start> --oneline -- crates/orgasmic-daemon/ crates/orgasmic-core/= for merges since boot.
4. If binary mtime > daemon start_at OR there are daemon-code commits since boot, emit a yellow warning: =⚠ running daemon predates recent merges; restart recommended (orgasmic restart)=. Print the offending SHAs.

Optionally surface in =orgasmic status= too (since users hit that more often than =doctor=).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic doctor= acquires daemon status + binary mtime + recent commits and emits the warning when stale.
- [ ] Warning includes: daemon uptime, binary build time, count of daemon-code commits since boot, first 3 SHAs + subjects.
- [ ] Clean state (no stale): silent or single ✓ line.
- [ ] Daemon down: skip the check, emit "daemon not running" once (don't double-warn).
- [ ] Bracket-aware: don't false-positive on doc-only commits under =crates/orgasmic-daemon/= (e.g., commits that only touch =*.md=). Acceptable to be conservative — first cut can flag any commit under those paths and refine later.
- [ ] Integration test: spawn an older binary, fake a newer mtime + git commit, assert =doctor= emits the warning. (Use =tempfile= for the binary stub.)
- [ ] All tests pass; clippy/fmt/diff clean. No new deps. MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
Cluster-traceable to the 2026-05-24 TASK-B2P1Z chrome smoke. Manager-discipline gap that exposed this: TASK-B2P1Z cluster-close commit did NOT include a daemon restart even though merges shipped a new daemon route. The discipline rule will likely be folded into =shipped/manager/persona.org= ("after merging any daemon-facing commit, restart the daemon before continuing") once this check exists to make it mechanical.

Companion thought (out of scope for this task; capture only): could also become a =dispatch-close= post-hook that auto-restarts daemon when =--merge-sha= touched daemon-facing paths. Cleaner DX but more invasive — defer until the doctor check proves the heuristic.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-24 Sun 15:24] Implementer shipped =56eae40= on =task-052-impl=; merged no-ff as =81f60e9=. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~7m wall, dispatched via =orgasmic manager dispatch=. =implementer.done= =tx-20260524-org-0184=. 2 files +369/-0: =doctor.rs= +313 (reuses existing =DaemonClient= bearer-auth path for =GET /daemon/status=; canonicalizes =$ORGASMIC_HOME/bin/orgasmic= for mtime; shells out =git log --since=<started_at> --oneline --no-merges -- crates/orgasmic-daemon/ crates/orgasmic-core/=; emits multi-line =Finding::Warn= with uptime/boot/pid/start/build-time/commit-count/SHAs+subjects/restart-hint), =tests/doctor.rs= +56 (binary stub + status injection). Tests 219 → 225 (+5). clippy/fmt/diff clean. MSRV 1.78. **DOGFOOD #25+#26 PASS**.
- *Live first-use validation* (META-DOGFOOD): rebuilt binary post-merge; ran =orgasmic doctor= against the still-running pre-merge daemon (PID 10673, ~52m uptime). The brand-new TASK-FWBYB check fired correctly: =[warn] running daemon predates recent daemon-code merges= with uptime, pid+boot, binary build time, =1 daemon-code commit since boot: 08133ac TASK-PYW86: sanitize GET endpoint error envelopes=, and =restart recommended (orgasmic restart)= action. After =kill + serve=, doctor went silent (clean). End-to-end heuristic validated on first try.
- Reviewer pass (codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~3m wall, =tx-20260524-org-0188=): verdict =has-issues, blocks-ship=. 6 findings. **F-1 HIGH bug** — acceptance breach: when daemon's down, doctor emits ZERO liveness signal (stale check skips on =None= status AND no other check covers liveness). F-2 medium design (decision fn reads wall clock → non-deterministic); F-3 low (=<1m= renders as =Ns=); F-4 medium test (git-path integration test missing); F-5 medium test (connection-refused/401 not tested through acquisition layer); F-6 low (truncation lacks =...and N more=).

* DONE TASK-PYW86.1 GET envelope hardening fix-subtask: typed content-loader errors + path-aware tests + artifact-aware labels :daemon:security:test:bug:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-PYW86.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-051-review-report.md /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-051-brief.md
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-PYW86
:END:

** Description
Seven reviewer findings from TASK-PYW86 bundled as one fix-implementer round per one-round-of-fixes discipline. No critical/high; bulk is test-strength gaps + one medium content-loader classification bug. Report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-051-review-report.md=.

*F-1 (MEDIUM test) — Path-free assertions miss the actual leaked path.* =tests/integration.rs:115-134= rejects only =/tmp=, =/private/=, =os error=. Fixture root from =tempfile::tempdir()= can be elsewhere (=/var/folders/...=, custom =TMPDIR=). A leak of =ctx.project_root= without those tokens still passes.

*F-2 (MEDIUM test) — Loader-backed GET endpoint families have no failing-path coverage.* Commit changes =/agent-templates=, =/prompts=, =/skills= handlers (=api.rs:917-978=) hitting reads in =content.rs:407-504=, but the only integration coverage is the success-only smoke at =tests/integration.rs:1750-1778=.

*F-3 (MEDIUM bug) — Content loaders classify NotFound by string matching.* =content_load_error= decides 404 only when =error.to_string()= contains =not found through loader rule= (=api.rs:351-361=). If the file disappears between =resolve_loader= and =read_to_string=, the underlying =io::ErrorKind::NotFound= becomes a generic 500.

*F-4 (MEDIUM design) — Loader-backed errors lose structured =path= field.* Helpers log only =error = %error= (=api.rs:346-361=); the path is embedded in the =anyhow= context from =content.rs:407-504=. Sanitized body, but server diagnostics lose consistent =path= field.

*F-5 (MEDIUM test) — Decisions/Architecture/Glossary/ADR tests all hit =/org/file=.* (=tests/integration.rs:479-508=) only proves =get_org_file= / =read_artifact= (=api.rs:3542-3552=). They do not exercise the route families registered at =api.rs:112-132=.

*F-6 (LOW test) — Tests don't assert JSON envelope shape.* =assert_path_free_error= checks raw text contains, not =error= field of =\{"error": ...\}=.

*F-7 (LOW design) — Raw org-file errors not artifact-aware.* =get_org_file= passes generic =org file= to =read_artifact= (=api.rs:3542-3552=). All raw-file misses say =org file not found= regardless of which file.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1*: Pass the project root + home root into =assert_path_free_error= and assert body does not contain those exact paths or their canonical forms. Keep =/tmp= + =/private/= + =os error= as extra smoke coverage. Make fixture-path assertion load-bearing.
- [ ] *F-2*: Add failing-path tests for at least one list route AND one load route per family =/agent-templates=, =/prompts=, =/skills=. e.g., a =.org= directory where a file is expected, or unreadable user content. Assert status + path-free invariant (extended per F-1).
- [ ] *F-3*: Replace string-based content-loader error classification. Either propagate a typed content load error carrying =kind=, =path=, =source=, OR inspect the =anyhow= chain for =std::io::Error= and map =ErrorKind::NotFound= → =ApiError::not_found= and everything else → =ApiError::internal=. Add a regression test: file present at resolve-time, race-deleted before read (use a deterministic shim or =tempfile= manipulation).
- [ ] *F-4*: Content loader errors log =path = %p.display(), error = %e= structured fields consistent with =file_read_error=. Either via the typed-error route (F-3) or by re-extracting path from anyhow context.
- [ ] *F-5*: Keep one raw org-file missing-read test. Add targeted tests for the loader-backed route families claimed in the inventory. For genuine index-only routes that never hit filesystem errors (=/contradictions=, =/tasks/:id/activity=), assert their not-found/empty-state response is path-free + properly typed.
- [ ] *F-6*: Tests parse response as JSON and assert =error= field is present + non-empty. Use endpoint-specific predicates (=contains("not found") && !contains("read ")= or =starts_with("failed to")=). Preserves copy-edit flex without losing envelope contract.
- [ ] *F-7*: Derive artifact label from validated relative path in =get_org_file= before calling =read_artifact=. Map =decisions.org= → ="decisions file"=, =architecture.org= → ="architecture file"=, =glossary.org= → ="glossary file"=, ADR paths → ="ADR file"=, fall back to ="org file"= for unknown allowed files.
- [ ] All 232 tests still pass; new tests added per F-2 + F-3 + F-5 (estimate +6-12). clippy/fmt/diff clean. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.

** Notes
*Fix-subtask of TASK-PYW86* — =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set. On close, auto-transition flips IMPLEMENT → DONE (skip-review per one-round-of-fixes discipline). Manager hand-flips TASK-PYW86 REVIEW → DONE for cluster closure.

Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~12m wall. =implementer.done= =tx-20260524-org-0191= via =dispatch-close=. **DOGFOOD #23+#24 PASS** (BACKLOG→IMPLEMENT on dispatch; IMPLEMENT→DONE via =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= shortcut).
- 3 files changed, +663 / -149 within =WRITE_SCOPE=: =api.rs= +138/-36, =content.rs= +374/-104 (typed =ContentLoadError= with =NotFoundByRule=, =NotFoundOnDisk=, =Read=, =Parse= variants), =tests/integration.rs= +300/-49.
- *F-1 fix*: =assert_path_free_error= refactored to accept fixture paths; load-bearing assertion is project_root/home_root absence, not just =/tmp= + =/private/= tokens. Catches =/var/folders/...= leaks.
- *F-2 fix*: Failing-path integration tests added for =/agent-templates= (list + load), =/prompts= (list + load), =/skills= (list + load) — directory-where-file-expected + permission-denied triggers.
- *F-3 fix*: Typed =ContentLoadError= replaces string-based classification. =NotFoundOnDisk= (race-deleted file between =resolve_loader= and =read_to_string=) correctly maps to 404, not generic 500. Regression test for the race shipped.
- *F-4 fix*: Content-loader errors emit structured =path = %p.display(), error = %e= consistent with =file_read_error=.
- *F-5 fix*: One raw /org/file missing-read test retained; added targeted tests for loader-backed route families; verified =/contradictions= and =/tasks/:id/activity= are path-free.
- *F-6 fix*: Tests parse response body as JSON and assert =error= field present with predicate-based string checks (no golden-string).
- *F-7 fix*: =get_org_file= derives artifact label from validated relative path: =decisions.org= → ="decisions file"=, =architecture.org= → ="architecture file"=, =glossary.org= → ="glossary file"=, =adr-*= → ="ADR file"=, fallback ="org file"=.

:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-FWBYB.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:   implementer-claude
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/doctor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-052-review-report.md /tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-052-brief.md
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FWBYB
:END:

** Description
Six reviewer findings from TASK-FWBYB bundled as one fix-implementer round per one-round-of-fixes discipline. F-1 is HIGH (acceptance breach: daemon-down emits zero liveness signal). Report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-052-review-report.md=.

*F-1 (HIGH bug) — =orgasmic doctor= emits no daemon-down signal.* Stale check skips when =daemon_status= returns =None= (=doctor.rs:83,122=); =daemon_status()= collapses connection failures, token read failures, 401s, decode failures into =None= (=doctor.rs:130=); =cmd_doctor= only prints =diagnose()= findings (=main.rs:674=); no other check emits =daemon not running=. Acceptance breach: criterion says daemon-down should skip stale AND emit =daemon not running= once.

*F-2 (MEDIUM design) — Decision fn reads wall clock.* =check_daemon_staleness()= calls =SystemTime::now()= internally for uptime (=doctor.rs:203=). Non-deterministic; prevents precise unit coverage of boundary cases.

*F-3 (LOW bug) — Sub-minute uptime renders as seconds.* =human_duration()= falls through to =format!("{secs}s")= for <60s (=doctor.rs:260=). Focus list called out =<1m= explicitly.

*F-4 (MEDIUM test) — Integration test misses the git-backed warning path.* Only test (=tests/doctor.rs:23=) creates a newer binary symlink and asserts =0 daemon-code commits=. Git path is only unit-tested (=doctor.rs:364=). Acceptance says integration test must spawn older binary + fake newer mtime + git commit + assert warning.

*F-5 (MEDIUM test) — Daemon-down + unauthorized not tested through acquisition layer.* Only daemon-down test calls =check_daemon_staleness(None, ...)= directly (=doctor.rs:405=). Doesn't prove connection-refused / missing-token / 401 are correctly classified by =daemon_status()= (=doctor.rs:130=).

*F-6 (LOW design) — Commit truncation loses remaining-count.* Lists first three, then bare =...= (=doctor.rs:226,229=). Focus area asked for =...and N more=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1*: Make daemon status acquisition return a small status enum (=Running(DaemonStatus)=, =Unavailable=, =Unauthorized=) OR add one separate daemon-liveness doctor finding. Keep stale check silent on unavailable/unauthorized status, but emit exactly ONE non-stale warning (=daemon not running= on connection refused; =daemon auth token mismatch= on 401, or a single combined message — pick one deliberately). NOT a double-warn with the stale check.
- [ ] *F-2*: Pass =now: SystemTime= into =check_daemon_staleness()= (or split rendering so uptime is supplied by caller). Unit tests assert exact uptime strings for =<1m=, =59s=, =1m=, =1h 4m=, =2d 3h=, =30d 5h= without sleeping or racing the clock.
- [ ] *F-3*: =human_duration()= returns =<1m= for =secs < 60=. Add direct tests for the listed boundaries.
- [ ] *F-4*: Extend or add a CLI integration test that creates =home.source()= as a git repo with a commit under =crates/orgasmic-daemon/= or =crates/orgasmic-core/=, seeds the minimal required =shipped/= files so doctor doesn't fail layout checks, boots the daemon, asserts CLI output includes the commit SHA/subject.
- [ ] *F-5*: Once F-1's daemon liveness is represented explicitly, add tests for connection-refused AND token-mismatch acquisitions. Assert stale warning is absent AND daemon-down/auth finding emits at most once.
- [ ] *F-6*: Render =...and N more= for =len > 3=. Update unit test to assert remaining count.
- [ ] All 232 tests still pass; new tests per F-2 + F-3 + F-4 + F-5 + F-6 (estimate +6-10). clippy/fmt/diff clean. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.

** Notes
*Fix-subtask of TASK-FWBYB* — =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= set. On close, auto-transition flips IMPLEMENT → DONE (skip-review per one-round-of-fixes discipline). Manager hand-flips TASK-FWBYB REVIEW → DONE for cluster closure.

*F-1 priority bump to HIGH*: it's an acceptance-criteria breach surfaced by the reviewer, not a polish item. Without daemon-down liveness, =doctor= gives users zero signal when the daemon's down — defeats the diagnostic purpose.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-24 Sun 15:51] Shipped =ad51da0= on =task-052.1-impl=; merged no-ff as =fc8e152= on main. Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, ~12m wall. =implementer.done= =tx-20260524-org-0192= via =dispatch-close=. **DOGFOOD #27+#28 PASS** (BACKLOG→IMPLEMENT on dispatch; IMPLEMENT→DONE via =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= shortcut).
- 2 files changed, +363 / -34 within =WRITE_SCOPE=: =doctor.rs= +303/-34, =tests/doctor.rs= +94/-0.
- *F-1 fix (HIGH)*: =DaemonLiveness= enum introduced — =Running(DaemonStatus)= / =Unavailable= (connection refused, DNS, timeout) / =Unauthorized= (401, missing token, decode mismatch). =diagnose()= now emits exactly ONE liveness Warn when not-Running (=daemon not running= or =daemon auth token mismatch=); stale check stays SILENT on not-Running (no double-warn). Acceptance criterion now satisfied: daemon-down case emits =daemon not running= once.
- *F-2 fix*: =check_daemon_staleness= now takes =now: SystemTime= parameter; pure decision function. Thin wrapper acquires =now= via =SystemTime::now()= and passes it in. Tests assert exact uptime strings without clock racing.
- *F-3 fix*: =human_duration()= returns =<1m= for =secs < 60=. Direct tests added for =<1m= / =59s= / =1m= / =1h 4m= / =2d 3h= / =30d 5h=.
- *F-4 fix*: CLI integration test creates tempdir git repo, seeds minimal =shipped/= layout, commits under =crates/orgasmic-daemon/=, sets up =Home= with stub binary mtime EARLIER than commit (so git-staleness is load-bearing), boots fixture daemon with =started_at= BEFORE commit, runs =cmd_doctor=, asserts output includes commit SHA + subject.
- *F-5 fix*: Tests for connection-refused acquisition (=DaemonLiveness::Unavailable= path → one =daemon not running= Warn, zero stale Warns) and token-mismatch (=DaemonLiveness::Unauthorized= path → one =daemon auth token mismatch= Warn, zero stale Warns). "At most one" assertion load-bearing.
- *F-6 fix*: Commit truncation renders =...and N more= for =len > 3=. Unit test updated to assert remaining count.
- Verification: 232 → 241 tests in worktree (+9); 247 on merged main post both fix-subtasks. clippy/fmt/diff clean. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.
- *Live re-validation*: post-merge daemon restart, =orgasmic doctor= silent on fresh daemon (clean state). Pre-restart, doctor correctly warned about TASK-PYW86.1 daemon-code merge — heuristic now end-to-end validated AND robust to daemon-down (F-1 acceptance criterion live-confirmed).

** Description
Three correlated CLI polish slices that all touch =crates/orgasmic-cli/= (overlapping write scope makes parallel dispatch unsafe within this slice; this is the single-implementer bundle for the cli surface): (1) help text per subcommand — every clap =Subcommand= variant gets a concrete =///= doc + top-level user-facing commands gain =after_help= examples; (2) =orgasmic init= ergonomics — current output is paths-only print, gains source-checkout-missing hint, broken-bin-symlink hint, and "Next:" footer pointing at =doctor=/=serve=/=project init=; (3) =orgasmic status= surfaces the TASK-FWBYB staleness check at the top of its output via a small =pub fn check_daemon_for_status= helper in =doctor.rs= (closes the explicit TASK-FWBYB deferral).

Brief: =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-053-brief.md=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every clap =Subcommand= variant in =main.rs= has a concrete =///= doc comment (no "Stubbed in TASK-NNN" placeholders for shipped commands).
- [ ] Top-level user-facing commands (=Init=, =Doctor=, =Serve=, =Status=, =Project Init=, =Manager Dispatch=) carry =#[command(after_help = "…")]= examples blocks.
- [ ] =cmd_init= emits =hint:= lines for missing source checkout AND broken bin symlink; ends with a "Next:" footer.
- [ ] =cmd_status= prints a single =[warn] <msg>= line ahead of the JSON when the daemon is running AND staleness was detected; pure JSON output when not.
- [ ] New helper =doctor::check_daemon_for_status(home: &Home) -> Option<String>= exposed without leaking the =Finding= enum.
- [ ] New unit/integration tests cover the init hint paths and the staleness-helper boundary cases.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + git diff --check= all green. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.

** Description
Audit every =ApiError::internal/bad_request/not_found= construction site across the daemon crate; classify each as safe / leaks / ambiguous; apply the TASK-PYW86 sanitization pattern (stable public message + structured =tracing::warn!/error!= with unsafe details in private fields) to every leak site; standardize the wire format if the JSON envelope shape is inconsistent across handlers; add path-leakage regression tests covering each fixed surface using the =assert_path_free_error= helper TASK-PYW86.1 introduced. Known leak: =api.rs:697= surfaces =format!("writer transaction: {e}")= in the public envelope.

Brief: =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-054-brief.md=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit table in commit message classifies every =ApiError= call site as safe / leaks / ambiguous.
- [ ] All leak sites refactored to use the TASK-PYW86 helper pattern (or a new sibling helper) — no raw =format!("…{e}")= in any =ApiError= construction with non-public-safe values.
- [ ] JSON error envelope shape (=impl IntoResponse for ApiError=) verified consistent across handlers; ad-hoc =(StatusCode, Json(…))= tuples that bypass =ApiError= are folded into the canonical envelope.
- [ ] Path-leakage regression test added per fixed endpoint family (writer transaction error, supervisor acquire error, snapshot save error, plus any others surfaced by the audit).

** Description
Add =cursor-agent= (Cursor Agent CLI, model =composer-2.5-fast=) as a first-class orgasmic worker type alongside =claude-acp= / =codex-appserver= / =hermes= / =tmux-tui=. Unblocks parallel implementer dispatch when Codex quota is exhausted; introduces a second production-grade implementer driver with independent quota. Mirror =claude_acp.rs= structure exactly: =CursorAgentDriver= + =CursorAgentConfig= + simulated-vs-real branch gated on =which cursor-agent= + =DriverEvent= stream from =cursor-agent --print --output-format stream-json --stream-partial-output --model <cfg.model> --force=. Register in =lib.rs= =TRANSPORTS= + =driver_for=. Add =shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer.org= with =:DEFAULT_DRIVER: cursor-agent= and =:DEFAULT_MODEL: composer-2.5-fast=. Integration test gated on =which cursor-agent= validates supervisor acquire → driver event flow → release cycle.

Brief: =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-056-brief.md=. Local prereqs verified by manager (=cursor-agent= at =~/.local/bin/cursor-agent= v2026.05.20-2b5dd59).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New file =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/cursor_agent.rs= implements =WorkerDriver= for transport id ="cursor-agent"=. Mirror =claude_acp.rs= structure: =Config= deserialize, simulated-vs-real branch in =acquire=, =cursor_agent_available()= probe, =CursorAgentControl: DriverControl= with =transition_state= / =babysitter_action= / =release=.
- [ ] Real-wire mode spawns =cursor-agent --print --output-format stream-json --stream-partial-output --model <cfg.model> --force= via =tokio::process::Command=. stream-json events parsed line-by-line; mapped to =DriverEvent= variants per existing patterns.
- [ ] Simulated mode emits deterministic =Ready= + small canned flow + =Completed= for tests without the binary on PATH.
- [ ] =lib.rs= updated in three places: =pub mod cursor_agent=, =pub use cursor_agent::CursorAgentDriver=, =TRANSPORTS= adds ="cursor-agent"=, =driver_for= match adds the arm.
- [ ] =shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer.org= matches the format used by =implementer-claude.org= with =:DEFAULT_DRIVER: cursor-agent= + =:DEFAULT_MODEL: composer-2.5-fast= + =:DEFAULT_EFFORT: high= + the same persona/operating-rules sections.
- [ ] Unit tests in =crates/orgasmic-drivers/= cover: registry, simulated-mode acquire, real-wire acquire (gated on =which cursor-agent=), validate-config rejection paths.
- [ ] Integration test in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/= exercises supervisor acquire → driver events → release path against the new template (gated on =which cursor-agent=).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + git diff --check= all green. MSRV 1.78. No new heavyweight deps.
- [ ] Manager-side end-to-end: post-merge, manager dispatches a tiny throwaway test task that acquires the new driver via the daemon's supervisor seam (NOT via =orgasmic manager dispatch=, which is out of scope), watches a real =cursor-agent= process emit events, and releases cleanly. Result captured as an Evidence-section note on this task.

** Notes
Unblocks the polish-phase-1 batch (TASK-G0C5R/TASK-DFEDX/TASK-CPA74) when codex quota is hit — once 056 lands, those three can re-dispatch via cursor-agent / composer-2.5-fast. Driver follows the goal.org constraint: detect matching CLI at acquire-time; if present, real wire; otherwise simulated. Tests gated on =which cursor-agent=. Manager-dispatch CLI (=orgasmic manager dispatch=) integration is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (separate follow-up candidate); end-to-end test uses the daemon's supervisor seam instead.
