** Description
TASK-HY0QT reviewer F-4 (LOW, deferred): extend the existing =acp_stdio_codex_jsonrpc_handshake_mock_peer= fixture (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/driver_modes.rs:219=) to assert ToolCall ordering + intervening-notification handling on the stdio path. The WS-side equivalent test exists (=codex_turn_start_terminal_notification_does_not_emit_followup_error=); stdio side lacks it.

Concretely: extend the mock peer to emit an =item/tool/call= notification BEFORE =turn/completed=, OR send an intervening notification between =thread/start= request and response. Assert the driver-emitted events arrive in the expected order through the supervisor (ToolCall envelope appears before RunComplete; intervening notification doesn't get lost or reordered).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1 ordering test*: extend =acp_stdio_codex_jsonrpc_handshake_mock_peer= (or add a sibling test, implementer's choice) where the mock peer scripts an =item/tool/call= (or codex's actual tool-call notification method) before =turn/completed=. Assert the driver session emits a =ToolCall= envelope BEFORE =RunComplete= in the event stream.
- [ ] *F-2 intervening-notification test*: in the same test or a separate one, the mock peer scripts an intervening notification between the =thread/start= request and its response (e.g., =status/agent_message= or whatever codex emits as an in-flight notification). Assert the in-flight notification is routed through =parse_event= and appears in the event stream alongside the regular handshake events — not lost, not reordered.

* DONE TASK-XQ89W cascade-release babysitter when implementer run terminates (no orphans) :daemon:supervisor:babysitter:lifecycle:bug:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-XQ89W
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/r#trait.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-KFGDS TASK-KFGDS.1
:END:

** Description
Deferred-MEDIUM finding from TASK-KFGDS reviewer pass: when the implementer run terminates, the spawned babysitter should also release. Currently =RunRecord= (=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs:148-162=) stores a =babysitter_target: Option<String>= field that's set on BABYSITTER runs (pointing back at their parent implementer run_id) but NOT a companion =babysitter_run_id= on IMPLEMENTER runs. So when the implementer's =release()= fires (=supervisor.rs:814=), the supervisor doesn't know which babysitter run is its companion and the babysitter run continues writing to its .babysitter.jsonl indefinitely.

This was a known gap on the original cursor/subprocess-stream-json path too; TASK-KFGDS.1's babysitter spawn just extended the pattern to acp-stdio without fixing the cascade-release. With babysitters now spawning on every CLI dispatch (post-TASK-KFGDS.1), orphan babysitter runs accumulate on every clean implementer.done. Worth fixing.

Fix shape (small, ~30-60 LOC):
1. Add =babysitter_run_id: Option<String>= to =RunRecord= (set when supervisor auto-spawns the babysitter after implementer acquire).
2. In =Supervisor::release= when =rec.kind == RunKind::Implementer= AND =rec.babysitter_run_id.is_some()=, cascade-release the babysitter via the same release path.
3. Regression test: spawn implementer + auto-babysitter; release implementer; assert babysitter run is also released (= no longer in supervisor.runs).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *F-1 RunRecord companion field*: add =babysitter_run_id: Option<String>= to =RunRecord=. Set it at babysitter auto-spawn site (search for where =AcquireRequest.babysitter: Some(...)= triggers the babysitter spawn — that's where the new babysitter run gets its run_id; record it on the parent implementer's RunRecord).
- [ ] *F-2 cascade release*: extend =Supervisor::release= so that when an Implementer run releases AND has a non-null =babysitter_run_id=, it also calls release on the babysitter run (with a reason like ="cascade from implementer <run_id>"=). Order: release implementer first, then babysitter (avoid the babysitter writing a "lost parent" event mid-cascade). Idempotency: if the babysitter is already released (e.g., user manually released), don't error.
- [ ] *F-3 regression test*: in =supervisor.rs= tests, synthesize an implementer acquire with =babysitter: Some(BabysitterAutoSpawn { ... })=. Assert that AFTER spawn, =supervisor.runs= contains both runs AND the implementer's RunRecord has =babysitter_run_id == Some(<bs run id>)=. Then call =release()= on the implementer. Assert: (a) implementer is released, (b) babysitter is also released (gone from =supervisor.runs= OR has =terminal_outcome.is_some()=).
- [ ] All gates green: =cargo test --workspace=, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=. MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *NOT* =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= — full IMPLEMENT → REVIEW → DONE lifecycle. Small scope but earns a proper reviewer pass.
- *Worker*: =implementer-codex-stdio= — continued dogfood; cargo test --workspace will likely exercise approval-grant again (5th in-row implementer dispatch).
- *Out-of-scope*: don't restructure RunKind / RunRecord beyond adding the field. Don't change the babysitter spawn LOGIC; just track the resulting run_id. Don't touch =acp_stdio.rs= or driver code.

** Evidence
(populated at cluster close)

* DONE TASK-MEX44 babysitter idempotency-branch-coverage test (release-babysitter-first ordering) :daemon:supervisor:babysitter:test:coverage:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-MEX44
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-XQ89W
:END:

** Description
TASK-XQ89W reviewer left one LOW deferred follow-up: idempotency-branch-coverage test for the release-babysitter-first ordering path in =Supervisor::release=.

TASK-XQ89W shipped the cascade-release logic with idempotency via RunNotFound swallowing — i.e., if the babysitter run was already released (e.g., user manually released it, or it auto-released after its parent), the cascade-release call sees =SupervisorError::RunNotFound= and swallows it. The existing TASK-XQ89W regression test =implementer_release_cascades_to_auto_spawned_babysitter= asserts the happy path (both runs released after one implementer.release call). It does NOT assert the idempotency branch: what happens when the babysitter was released BEFORE the implementer? Or the implementer.release is called twice?

This is a pure test-coverage win to lock in the idempotency invariant — small, no production code changes.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *AC-1 babysitter-already-released branch*: write a new test (=babysitter_release_then_implementer_release_is_idempotent= or similar) that (a) spawns implementer + auto-babysitter, (b) calls =Supervisor::release= on the BABYSITTER first, (c) then calls release on the implementer, (d) asserts the implementer.release does NOT error (RunNotFound on the babysitter cascade-call is swallowed), (e) asserts both runs end up released. This locks in the F-2 idempotency contract from TASK-XQ89W's brief.
- [ ] *AC-2 double-release of implementer*: write a new test (=implementer_release_twice_is_idempotent_for_cascade= or similar) that (a) spawns implementer + auto-babysitter, (b) calls release on implementer (cascades to babysitter — happy path, both released), (c) calls release on implementer AGAIN, (d) asserts the second call either no-ops cleanly or returns RunNotFound (whichever is the existing contract — check =Supervisor::release= implementation; the new test should assert whichever behavior the code already exhibits, not force a contract change). Idempotency means same end state regardless of repetition.
- [ ] *AC-3 (optional, only if trivial) cascade-ordering invariant*: if it's easy to inspect, also assert via a log/event side-channel that the implementer release HAPPENS BEFORE the babysitter cascade-release (the "implementer first, then babysitter" ordering documented in TASK-XQ89W's brief). If the existing supervisor has no easily-observable ordering signal, skip this AC — don't add new instrumentation just to test it.
- [ ] All gates green: =cargo test --workspace=, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.

** Notes
- *NOT* =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= — small but earns a proper reviewer pass.
- *Worker*: =implementer-codex-stdio= (continued dogfood; this will be the 6th in-row codex-stdio implementer dispatch).
- *Write scope is TESTS-ONLY in supervisor.rs.* Do NOT touch production code. If a test reveals that the existing cascade behavior is actually buggy (e.g., the second release call panics or corrupts state), DO NOT fix it here — file a separate task and leave the test commented with =// FIXME(TASK-XXX): existing cascade is non-idempotent on double-release=. This task documents and locks in the EXISTING idempotency contract; it does not change the contract.
- *Anchor read*: =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs= (look at the existing TASK-XQ89W test =implementer_release_cascades_to_auto_spawned_babysitter= as a template — same setup, different assertion shape).
- *Pre-existing diagnostics*: per persona.org housekeeping rule, fix any pre-existing clippy/test/MSRV warnings encountered in the task's read scope as part of this dispatch. Don't expand write scope to other files for this.

- *Reviewer*: =reviewer-codex-stdio= (3rd in-row clean). Verdict =clean, ships-as-is= with 0 findings. AC-3 (optional ordering test) correctly skipped — no production instrumentation added.

* DONE TASK-X8B48 orgasmic status surfaces doctor staleness check :cli:status:doctor:dx:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-X8B48
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/lib.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-FWBYB TASK-FWBYB.1
:END:

** Description
TASK-FWBYB shipped =orgasmic doctor=' staleness check (binary mtime vs daemon-code merge SHA + uptime; multi-line =Finding::Warn=). TASK-FWBYB.1 hardened it (=DaemonLiveness= enum + exactly-one liveness signal when daemon not-Running). The check is real, validated end-to-end, and silent when daemon is fresh.

Open follow-up (cited in goal.org and TASK-FWBYB notes): users running their normal =orgasmic status= command don't see the staleness warning — only =orgasmic doctor= surfaces it. =orgasmic status= is the more common liveness check (one-line output: boot_id + uptime + projects + parse_errors + tx count). Surfacing the staleness Warn there means users notice "you're running a stale daemon binary" without needing to remember to run doctor.

Fix shape (small, ~30-100 LOC):
1. =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs= currently renders the daemon-status JSON envelope from =/daemon/status= (bearer-authed via =DaemonClient=).
2. After rendering the normal status output, call into =doctor.rs='s existing =check_daemon_staleness= (or equivalent function — read =doctor.rs= for the exact name) using the same =SystemTime::now()= + git-log path as TASK-FWBYB.1. If the check returns =Finding::Warn=, render it as a trailing block (e.g., =WARN: daemon binary appears stale: <message>=).
3. Skip the staleness check entirely if =DaemonLiveness= is not =Running= (= same TASK-FWBYB.1 F-1 invariant: don't double-warn when daemon is down).
4. New integration test in =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/= (or wherever status has tests) that seeds a tempdir git repo + stale binary mtime + running daemon fixture and asserts =orgasmic status= output contains the WARN line.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *AC-1 status emits staleness Warn when daemon is Running AND check fires*: =orgasmic status= output includes a trailing =WARN: ...= block matching the same format =orgasmic doctor= uses, when the daemon-code-since-binary-mtime check fires. Format: multi-line Warn (binary path + most-recent merge SHA + commits-since-boot count), same as doctor.
- [ ] *AC-2 status is silent when daemon is fresh*: =orgasmic status= does NOT emit the WARN block when binary mtime is newer than all daemon-code merges since boot. No extra noise on the happy path.
- [ ] *AC-3 status skips check when daemon not-Running*: same =DaemonLiveness::Running= gate as TASK-FWBYB.1 F-1. When daemon is unreachable / unauthorized, the existing status error path stays untouched; do NOT add a staleness check on top of it (no double-warn).
- [ ] *AC-4 integration test*: =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= covers (i) status-with-stale-binary outputs WARN, (ii) status-with-fresh-binary does NOT, (iii) status-with-daemon-down does NOT call the staleness check. Reuse the test seeds from TASK-FWBYB.1 if they're in a shared test-helper module; otherwise duplicate the tempdir-git fixture pattern.
- [ ] *AC-5 no helper duplication*: do NOT copy-paste the doctor staleness logic into status.rs. Extract =check_daemon_staleness= (or rename if private) to be callable from both =doctor.rs= and =status.rs=. If it currently lives in =doctor.rs= as a private fn, lift it to =pub(crate)= or move to a shared module under =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/=. Choose the minimum-disruption refactor.
- [ ] All gates green: =cargo test --workspace=, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=. MSRV 1.78. No new deps.

** Notes
- *NOT* =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= — earns a reviewer pass.
- *Worker*: =implementer-codex-stdio=.
- *Out-of-scope*: don't change the doctor staleness HEURISTIC itself (no bracket-aware filter — that's a separate TASK-FWBYB follow-up). Don't touch the daemon side. Don't change the =DaemonLiveness= enum.
- *Anchor read*: =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs= (find =check_daemon_staleness= and =DaemonLiveness=); =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs= (current status command implementation); the existing TASK-FWBYB.1 integration test (template for the new one).
- *Pre-existing diagnostics*: per persona.org housekeeping rule, fix any pre-existing clippy/test/MSRV warnings encountered in the task's read scope.

** Evidence

Self-commit =74b49be=. 4 files +224/-59 across =crates/orgasmic-cli/=: =src/main.rs= (status fn wires staleness Warn to stderr after JSON stdout), =tests/common/mod.rs= (new +76, shared fixtures lifted from doctor), =tests/doctor.rs= (-58, consumes shared fixtures), =tests/status.rs= (new +138, stale/fresh/daemon-down coverage). Helper reuse via existing =doctor::check_daemon_for_status_with_status= — no duplication (AC-5 satisfied without new module extraction). Merged no-ff as =27abf41=.
- *Brief vs reality*: brief named =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/status.rs= as write scope, but status command lives in =main.rs= (no separate status.rs file). Implementer correctly routed to =main.rs= — brief was imprecise, not a design issue.
- *Reviewer*: =reviewer-codex-stdio= (4th in-row clean). Verdict =clean, ships-as-is= with 0 findings. Stream split confirmed (JSON stdout / Warn stderr), daemon-down skip preserved (AC-3), 3 integration test branches (stale/fresh/daemon-down) with stale/fresh spawning real daemons (AC-4 fully covered).

** Description
Self-contained HTML file rendering the current orgasmic wiring (backend + UI) as a React Flow diagram. Validates the mechanism-split decomposition (~25–35 functional-component leaves grouped under subsystem clusters arch_WZFAX..arch_QXS5W) before promoting the model to =.orgasmic/architecture.org= (TASK-ENXWA).

Source of truth is a hand-authored JSON model inlined in the same HTML file. =architecture.org= is NOT modified in this task — this is the model-validation step. The point is to see whether the decomposition reads cleanly before committing it to canonical Org.

Grilling context: dec_X6Q0S + dec_TEKEB + dec_BP4NH + dec_PA23Y refinements [2026-05-27]. Locked outcomes: functional-component ontology with 6 typed edges + artifact pseudo-nodes (dec_X6Q0S refinement); mechanism-split decomposition under 10 principles (dec_TEKEB refinement); two-way back-edges with =// arch:= markers (dec_BP4NH refinement; markers themselves are added in TASK-BJHKN, not here); semantic zoom + side-panel disclosure (dec_PA23Y refinement).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =docs/architecture/diagram.html= opens directly in a browser with no build step. React Flow + dagre via ESM CDN imports.
- [ ] Inlined JSON model contains: ~25–35 functional-component leaves under subsystem clusters arch_WZFAX..arch_QXS5W (shipped only; arch_A1FGY..arch_M8JQT target nodes either excluded or visually distinct), ~10 artifact pseudo-nodes (files / projections / sockets), and typed edges using the six edge vocabularies (=READS=, =WRITES=, =EXPOSES_REST=, =EXPOSES_WS= / =SUBSCRIBES_TO=, =SPAWNS=, =CALLS=).
- [ ] Each leaf carries: id (=arch_NNN.M=), parent (=arch_NNN=), label, one-line verb description, status (=shipped= | =planned=), source paths (1..10 real file paths).
- [ ] Semantic zoom: 3 LOD levels (low: subsystem clusters only; mid: leaves visible with typed edges; high: leaves plus source paths + edge labels).
- [ ] Click on a leaf opens a right-rail detail panel showing: source paths, outgoing edges with type+target, incoming edges with type+source, realized decisions (text), producing tasks (text). Phase 1: text only, no deep links.
- [ ] Decomposition follows the 10 mechanism-split principles from dec_TEKEB refinement. Cross-cutting infra (=useResource=, Axum router, Watcher) gets first-class leaves; feature-level boxes are forbidden.
- [ ] Edge styling differentiates types visually (dashed READS, solid WRITES, blue EXPOSES_REST, green WS pair, orange SPAWNS, thin gray CALLS, dotted ugly DEPENDS_ON).

** Notes
- *Out of scope*: modifying =.orgasmic/architecture.org= (TASK-ENXWA); adding =// arch:= markers to code (TASK-BJHKN); daemon parser changes (TASK-YWRG5); architector persona update (TASK-EZ0ST); in-app /architecture route (TASK-MW5BX).
- *Granularity reference for the leaf list*: arch_A53QX ≈ Supervisor + WorkerDriver trait + Driver adapters (one leaf covering 5 adapter files via the shared trait) + Babysitter (~4 leaves); arch_C87Z9 ≈ Axum HTTP router + WS event broadcaster + Watcher + Materialized index (~4 leaves); arch_MK2Q2 ≈ TanStack router + lib/api + useResource + useEventStream + Manager popover + Manager transcript renderers + Architecture graph + Tasks board (~7–8 leaves). Final count converges to ~32 shipped leaves + ~10 artifact nodes.
- *Worker*: =implementer-claude-acp= — TS/React/HTML work, not Rust.

** Description
Promote the validated model from TASK-KSFG0's standalone HTML into =.orgasmic/architecture.org=. Each existing shipped =arch_NNN= subsystem keeps its ID and gains 2–6 second-level child headings (=** arch_NNN.M ...=) with full property drawers (=:ID:=, =:STATUS:=, =:SOURCE_PATHS:=, =:READS:=, =:WRITES:=, =:EXPOSES_REST:=, =:EXPOSES_WS:=, =:SUBSCRIBES_TO:=, =:SPAWNS:=, =:CALLS:=, =:DEPENDS_ON:=) and a one-paragraph verb description. Artifact pseudo-nodes (files / projections / sockets) land as a sibling =* arch_substrate= group with its own children, or whichever convention the architector picks under the new persona (TASK-EZ0ST).

Grilling context: dec_X6Q0S + dec_TEKEB refinements [2026-05-27]. Per arch_MPAQT semantic-hash policy, this is an interface change to the architecture nodes — bump =:SEMANTIC_HASH:= of each affected =arch_NNN= and let downstream stale propagation fire on next graph refresh.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each shipped =arch_NNN= (arch_WZFAX..arch_QXS5W) carries 2–6 child leaves =** arch_NNN.M ...= with the full property schema named above.
- [ ] Total child leaf count is ~25–35 across all subsystems, matching TASK-KSFG0's validated count.
- [ ] Artifact pseudo-nodes (~10) live under a new group heading (architector picks the convention; reuse =arch_substrate= or similar).
- [ ] Target nodes (arch_A1FGY..arch_M8JQT) are NOT decomposed; they remain design-intent only.
- [ ] Each shipped leaf has its =:SOURCE_PATHS:= populated with real paths from the workspace; no placeholder paths.
- [ ] Semantic hashes of edited parent nodes are recomputed (or cleared for daemon recompute on next index rebuild).
- [ ] No new decisions are added unless genuinely new design space surfaces during decomposition — per the [2026-05-27] user instruction, refinements go onto existing decisions in place.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures= still parses the new =architecture.org= cleanly (requires TASK-YWRG5's parser).

** Notes
- *Depends on TASK-YWRG5* (daemon parser child-node syntax) for the new schema to parse, and *TASK-EZ0ST* (architector persona update) so the architector composes per the mechanism-split principles.
- *Out of scope*: inline code markers (TASK-BJHKN), drift tooling (TASK-BJHKN), in-app surface (TASK-MW5BX).

** Evidence

** Description
The =orgasmic-core= architecture parser currently recognizes top-level =* arch_NNN ...= headings. Extend it to also accept second-level =** arch_NNN.M ...= children under each parent, with the typed-edge property set (=:SOURCE_PATHS:=, =:READS:=, =:WRITES:=, =:EXPOSES_REST:=, =:EXPOSES_WS:=, =:SUBSCRIBES_TO:=, =:SPAWNS:=, =:CALLS:=, =:DEPENDS_ON:=). Architecture nodes gain a parent/child relation derivable from the ID prefix.

Grilling context: dec_X6Q0S refinement [2026-05-27]. CLAUDE.md pitfall reminder: the existing parser is opinionated about =TODO_KEYWORDS=; the architecture-prefix recognition is similarly opinionated. Read =crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs= and =crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs= before adjusting.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parser recognizes =** arch_NNN.M ...= children under each =* arch_NNN ...= parent and extracts: id, label, status, source paths, and all typed edges. Body paragraph is the verb description.
- [ ] Typed-edge properties parse as space-separated values: each value is either another arch ID (=arch_C87Z9.3=) or an artifact pseudo-node ID (=file:tx=, =projection:materialized-index=, =socket:events=).
- [ ] Materialized index exposes the child layer through =GET /architecture/nodes= (or extension of the existing endpoint) so the in-app route (TASK-MW5BX) can consume it.
- [ ] Artifact pseudo-nodes parse as a separate node kind distinguishable from arch nodes; scheme prefix (=file:=, =projection:=, =socket:=) is the only allowed namespace.
- [ ] Backward compat: existing =arch_NNN= top-level headings without children still parse and serve normally.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Independent of TASK-ENXWA*: this can ship speculatively against a tiny fixture; TASK-ENXWA lands the real architecture.org content using the new schema.
- *Out of scope*: drift comparator (TASK-BJHKN), in-app UI consumption (TASK-MW5BX), architector persona (TASK-EZ0ST).

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =59f34d5= (codex =51cc7e0=, ~16 min wall, clean codex-stdio dispatch — no manager-rescue).* 6 files +590/-20 LOC: schema.rs +298 (new ArchitectureNode child fields + ArchEdge/ArchEdgeKind/ArchEdgeTarget/ArtifactNode/ArtifactScheme types; ArchitectureNode::from_org walks parent + direct ** arch_NNN.M children); fixtures.rs +111 (6 new parser tests); index.rs +127 (materialized index stores children + artifact pseudo-nodes + typed edges); api.rs +15 (new =GET /architecture/nodes= endpoint returning ={ nodes, edges }=, backward-compat =GET /architecture= still returns ArchitectureSummary including child rows); integration.rs +53 (daemon endpoint test); lib.rs +6 (re-exports). Typed-edge properties parse space-separated arch IDs OR =file:/projection:/socket:= artifact pseudo-nodes. Invalid scheme returns SchemaError "unknown architecture namespace".
- *Daemon glitch note (recovered):* the initial dispatch hit a transient daemon connection error; the run+codex DID spawn correctly but the dispatch_started tx was never written. Result: dispatch-close errored with "no open manager.dispatch_started tx". Recovery: manually flipped TASK-YWRG5 BACKLOG → REVIEW in sprint.org before reviewer dispatch (sprint state did not auto-flip via dispatch handler given the missing tx). Reviewer dispatch then worked normally. **Not a code defect** — symptom of a momentary daemon glitch under load (cargo-build + dispatch contention). No lasting effect on the merge or the work shipped.
- *Reviewer pass verdict =clean, ships-as-is=* via =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-087-reviewer-last.txt=: 0H/0M/0L findings. Scope confirmed in-bounds. No daemon-disturbing commands.
- *Gates on merged main =59f34d5=:* cargo test --workspace ✓ (exit 0); cargo check --workspace ✓; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓; cargo fmt --check ✓; git diff --check ✓.
- *Unblocks*: TASK-ENXWA (first architector run authoring real arch graph content using the new schema), TASK-BJHKN (drift comparator CLI reads via the new parser + API), TASK-MW5BX (in-app UI consumes =GET /architecture/nodes=).

** Description
Validate =implementer-claude-tmux= end-to-end on real =claude= TUI and harden any gaps surfaced. Today the worker spec at =shipped/workers/implementer-claude-tmux.org= is boilerplate, and =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= has scaffolding-grade fixtures (hard-coded =agent_template_id: "tmux-tui-default"= at lines 396/646; =inert= gate at line 89 mirrors the simulated-mode pattern that bit TASK-EXFTV). This driver has never been dogfooded against a real claude TUI dispatch.

Pattern reference: the sibling HAR app (=../har/=) drives claude interactively through tmux with a compose surface below the pane — port that pattern for orgasmic's worker side first (TASK-GMR53 handles the manager side).

Driver flow under test (autonomous worker):
1. =acquire= → =spawn_tmux_session= creates =tmux new-session -d -s orgasmic-claude-<run_id>=.
2. Send the compiled prompt bundle to the pane (=tmux send-keys -t <session> 'claude' Enter; sleep; tmux send-keys -t <session> '<prompt>' Enter=).
3. Pane output captured as =DriverEvent::TextChunk= envelopes via periodic =tmux capture-pane -p=.
4. Terminal signal: claude TUI emits a recognizable end-of-turn marker (= "esc to interrupt" prompt disappearing, or a sentinel the brief asks claude to echo). Driver converts to =RunComplete=.
5. Cleanup: =tmux kill-session -t <session>= on release.

Suspected gaps to investigate + close (the live dogfood will surface the actual list):
- =inert= gate over-fires (same shape as TASK-EXFTV claude-acp endpoint-gate bug).
- =agent_template_id= hard-coding ignores the worker's =:DEFAULT_MODEL:= / =:DEFAULT_EFFORT:= properties.
- No model/effort plumbing: claude TUI needs =--model= flag, but the driver doesn't pass it.
- Terminal detection heuristic (end-of-turn marker) may not exist yet — needs design.
- Worker spec is boilerplate: add =:DEFAULT_MODEL: claude-sonnet-4-6=, =:DEFAULT_EFFORT: high=, real persona body, dispatch-flow docs.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =implementer-claude-tmux= worker spec at =shipped/workers/implementer-claude-tmux.org= has real persona text + operating rules tuned for TUI mode (not the boilerplate currently shipped).
- [ ] =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= real-wire path validated against actual =claude= binary:
  - =Ready{inert:false}= when tmux + claude available; =Ready{inert:true}= only when binary missing.
  - Pane output streamed as =TextChunk= envelopes (capture-pane polling at ~500ms intervals; debounced for chunked output).
  - End-of-turn detected → =RunComplete= envelope → lifecycle =release=.
  - Model + effort propagated from the worker config to the =claude --model <m>= invocation in the pane.
- [ ] Dogfood matching TASK-YFM6X pattern: dispatch a tiny task (doc-comment-only edit, e.g., add a one-line comment to =crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs=) via =--worker implementer-claude-tmux=, confirm: real-wire ready → TextChunk envelopes from real claude TUI → RunComplete → run.created tx → no orphans → tmux session cleaned up post-release.
- [ ] Babysitter spawns for tmux-tui runs the same way it does for acp-stdio runs (cascade-release pattern from TASK-XQ89W applies).
- [ ] Mock-peer-style unit test for the new code paths.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Strategic priority*: P1. The user redirect [2026-05-28 Thu] put claude-tmux ahead of TASK-EXFTV (claude-acp endpoint-gate fix demoted to P5). Hardening this path unlocks both autonomous claude dispatches AND the manager popover (TASK-GMR53) which renders the same tmux pane through xterm.js.
- *Reference*: HAR app sibling repo at =../har/= drives claude via tmux. Read =../har/crates/har-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= (or equivalent path) for the proven pattern — capture-pane cadence, end-of-turn detection, send-keys quoting rules.
- *Out of scope*: manager popover composer surface (TASK-GMR53); claude-acp endpoint-gate fix (TASK-EXFTV, now P5).

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =c38bb14= (commit =3388a6f=, manager-rescued).* Codex (gpt-5.5, high) hardened the tmux-tui driver with 21 helper functions over +577/-103 LOC: =build_spawn_plan=, =should_use_default_command=, =configured_command_is_dispatch_placeholder=, =default_command_for_harness=, =inert_reason=, =command_available=, =tmux_eot_marker=, =prompt_with_eot_instruction=, =spawn_tmux_session=, =paste_text_into_pane=, =send_keys=, =run_tmux=, =sanitize_tmux_name=, =start_capture_loop=, =capture_loop=, =capture_pane=, =pane_delta=, =pane_contains_eot_marker=, =pane_requests_approval=, =emit_run_complete_once=, =has_tmux_session=. Worker spec at =shipped/workers/implementer-claude-tmux.org= got real persona text (terminal-pane-friendly output discipline, EOT marker rule, trusted-permissions context). Original implementer self-terminated trying to restart the daemon (manager-rescue commit after).
- *Reviewer pass.* =reviewer-codex-stdio= verdict =has-issues, blocks-ship= via =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-093-review-report.md=: 2 HIGH + 2 MEDIUM + 1 LOW findings. F-1 HIGH: EOT detection emits RunComplete but no supervisor release + tmux session leak. F-2 HIGH: tmux session vanished pre-EOT falsely classified as success. F-3 MEDIUM: no missing-marker timeout. F-4 MEDIUM: no mock-peer lifecycle test + gated real-wire smoke. F-5 LOW: pane_delta byte-prefix mishandles truncation/scrollback. Live-dogfood readiness: not-ready.
- *Fix-cluster TASK-B5GFJ.{1..5} merged as =7ca73d7= (commit =1964d3c=, manager-rescued).* Bundled fix covering all 5 reviewer findings. 531 LOC across 2 files (tmux.rs +257, supervisor.rs +298). New helpers: =deserialize_duration_secs_opt=, =turn_timeout()=, =split_lines_keepends=, =redraw_delta=, =emit_fatal_driver_error_once=. New tests: =pane_delta_handles_line_window_edges=, =pane_delta_caps_redraw_payload=, =real_tmux_eot_marker_emits_run_complete=, =real_tmux_early_exit_before_eot_is_failure=, =real_tmux_missing_marker_times_out=. Codex (gpt-5.5, high) died mid-flight at session seq 3364 with fatal driver_error "driver transport unavailable: invalid stdio JSON" after =write_stdin failed: Unknown process id 86388= — manager-rescued after diff coherence check + tests-passed evidence at seq 3362 ("23 passed; 0 failed; finished in 32.93s").
- *Gates on merged main =7ca73d7=:* cargo test --workspace ✓ (exit 0); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓ (exit 0); cargo fmt --check ✓; git diff --check ✓; cd ui && npm run build ✓ (per TASK-GMR53 cluster). MSRV 1.78 preserved.

* DONE TASK-GMR53 Manager popover tmux pane + HAR-style composer-below surface :ui:manager:popover:tmux:compose:phase-2:

:END:

** Description
Extend the manager popover (shipped in TASK-EBWZ9, lives at =ui/src/components/manager/ManagerPopover.tsx=) so that when the manager run uses the =tmux-tui= driver, the popover renders a live xterm.js pane attached to the underlying tmux session + a composer input field BELOW the pane that sends formatted text into the TUI via =tmux send-keys=.

This closes the =arch_B7YH2 :V0_0_1_GAPS: popover xterm bridge compose prompts pending= line and lands the HAR-app pattern the user named explicitly: "input field below tmux so user can format text in the ui and then send it to the claude tui."

Surfaces:

1. *Daemon WebSocket bridge* (if not already present from TASK-EBWZ9):
   - =GET /ws/tmux/:run_id= for outbound pane stream (=tmux capture-pane -p= or =tmux pipe-pane= feed).
   - Inbound frame type (e.g., =SendKeys { text }=) that the daemon maps to =tmux send-keys -t <session> -- <text> Enter=.
   - Authorization: only the project's manager session can attach (reuse the bearer-token guard already on daemon HTTP routes).
2. *xterm.js terminal pane* in =ManagerPopover.tsx= (peek+workbench+focus variants):
   - Lazy-load xterm.js (already in =package.json= from TASK-EBWZ9).
   - Resize handle plumbing matches HAR's =use-chat-resize= pattern.
   - Connect to =/ws/tmux/:run_id=; render pane bytes through =term.write(...)=.
   - Fallback: when manager driver is =acp-stdio= or chat-family, popover keeps rendering =ManagerChatTranscript= as today (driver-adaptive per arch_B7YH2 constraints).
3. *Composer input field BELOW the tmux pane* (new component =ManagerComposer.tsx=):
   - Markdown-aware textarea (mirror HAR's compose surface).
   - Submit (Cmd/Ctrl+Enter OR a Send button) sends the WS =SendKeys= frame; pane echoes the input on the next capture-pane tick.
   - Empty-state hint: "Send a message to claude" — disabled when no tmux run is attached.
   - History (arrow-up recalls previous sends within the popover session; ephemeral, not persisted).
   - Drag-resize between pane and composer (=use-chat-resize= analog).
4. *Driver-adaptive routing in ManagerPopover.tsx*:
   - If active run's =driver= field is =tmux-tui= → render =ManagerTmuxPane= + =ManagerComposer=.
   - Otherwise → render existing =ManagerChatTranscript= unchanged.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manager popover renders an xterm.js pane when the active manager run's driver is =tmux-tui=, attached to =/ws/tmux/:run_id=.
- [ ] Composer input field renders BELOW the pane (HAR-style stacked layout, not side-by-side).
- [ ] Submitting from the composer fires a WS =SendKeys= frame; pane visibly echoes the submitted text within ~500ms (next capture-pane tick).
- [ ] Markdown-style formatting accepted in the composer (textarea, no rich-text WYSIWYG — keeps it light); raw text sent to tmux verbatim with newlines escaped as send-keys-safe.
- [ ] Drag-resize between pane (top) and composer (bottom) works; default split ~70/30.
- [ ] Driver-adaptive: =acp-stdio= / =codex-appserver= manager runs continue to render the existing chat transcript — no regression.
- [ ] Chrome smoke at =orgasmic.trydev.app=: open popover during a tmux-tui manager run, type a message, submit, observe the pane echo + claude's response stream in.
- [ ] Daemon: bearer-token gate on the new WS endpoint; integration test for the =SendKeys= → =tmux send-keys= path with a mocked tmux session.
- [ ] =cd ui && npm run build= clean (tsc --noEmit + vite build); =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean.

** Notes
- *Depends on TASK-B5GFJ* for a working tmux-tui driver to render against. If TASK-GMR53 dispatches early (parallel with TASK-B5GFJ), the implementer should stub the data wiring against a mock tmux session for testability and assume the driver will land in parallel.
- *Reference UI*: HAR app (=../har/=). Walk =../har/packages/views/= for the chat-window + compose surface — the user explicitly named HAR's pattern as the reference.
- *Out of scope*: cross-cutting refactor of ManagerPopover layout machinery (peek/workbench/focus state — already shipped in TASK-EBWZ9). Tmux pane integrates into the existing layout slots, not a redesign.
- *Out of scope*: composer shortcuts beyond Cmd/Ctrl+Enter (the user's overall direction excludes keyboard-shortcut polish during this UI phase per goal.org Constraints).
- *Strategic priority*: P1, paired with TASK-B5GFJ (worker hardening). Together they validate the entire claude-tmux flow — autonomous worker + interactive manager — which the user named as the priority next step ahead of TASK-EXFTV.

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =cb7bca8= (codex =fcf8549=, ~24 min wall, clean dispatch).* 7 files +553/-24 LOC. This pushes arch_MK2Q2 to 7 leaves (over the principle-5 ceiling of 6 by one). Reviewer suggests either accepting this exception (with documented rationale) OR introducing a manager-approved subcluster. **Pick the first option**: accept arch_MK2Q2 with 7 leaves and add a brief =description= note on one of the new leaves explaining the exception. Splitting catch-all is more important than principle-5 ceiling rigidity (principles 3 + 6 outrank 5 when forced to choose).

* DONE TASK-KSFG0.3 fix-subtask: correct edge semantics (SPAWNS / WRITES misuse) :architecture:diagram:fix:edge:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-KSFG0.3
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MK2Q2 arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         docs/architecture/diagram.html
:WRITE_SCOPE:      docs/architecture/**
:READ_SCOPE:       docs/architecture/** .orgasmic/decisions.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/events.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs
:TEST_CMD:         (manual: open docs/architecture/diagram.html in a browser)
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-4 (MEDIUM): =arch_A53QX.4 -> file:sessions SPAWNS= is semantically wrong (file:sessions is a JSONL artifact, not a child process or async task). Conversely, the manager-tick path that creates a manager run (=post_manager_tick= calling =supervisor.acquire=) is modeled only as =CALLS=, despite the brief naming "manager tick spawns runs" as the canonical high-value SPAWNS edge.

Reviewer F-5 (MEDIUM): Three edges use =WRITES= for event-bus publishes to =socket:events= (=arch_C87Z9.3 -> socket:events=, =arch_MPAQT.1 -> socket:events=, =arch_Z3Z3V.1 -> socket:events=). Per dec_X6Q0S refinement, =WRITES= is for append/replace file or projection content; these are in-memory broadcast bus publishes (=events.rs::publish=) that the WebSocket bridge (=ws.rs=) consumes. Should be =CALLS= (to a first-class event-bus/broadcaster leaf if one exists, or directly).

Fixes:
1. Remove the =arch_A53QX.4 -> file:sessions SPAWNS= edge. Replace with =WRITES= to file:sessions (the supervisor writes session JSONL).
2. Add a SPAWNS edge from the manager-tick path leaf (=arch_R3EPE.1= or similar) → =arch_A53QX.1= (supervisor acquire). If supervisor.acquire's spawn role is on a different leaf, route the edge there.
3. Retag the three =WRITES -> socket:events= edges as =CALLS= to the event-bus leaf if =arch_C87Z9= already has one (likely =arch_C87Z9.2 WebSocket event broadcaster=), OR as =CALLS= directly to the broadcaster if no separate event-bus leaf exists.
4. Keep =arch_C87Z9.2 -> socket:events EXPOSES_WS= as the only socket-targeted edge of its kind.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No SPAWNS edge targets an artifact pseudo-node.
- [ ] At least one SPAWNS edge models manager tick OR dispatch creating a run (real runtime acquisition path).
- [ ] No WRITES edge targets =socket:events= (only EXPOSES_WS does).
- [ ] DEPENDS_ON usage remains 0 (don't introduce escape hatches).

* DONE TASK-B5GFJ.1 fix-subtask: EOT terminal detection closes driver stream + releases supervisor + kills tmux session :drivers:tmux:claude:fix:lifecycle:high:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-B5GFJ.1
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/**
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-1 (HIGH): EOT marker observation emits =RunComplete= but never releases the supervisor run or kills the tmux session. Cleanup lives only in =DriverControl::release= which is not called by the EOT path. Run stays live; tmux orphans.

Files / line refs:
- =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs:653-660= (EOT detection)
- =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs:294-304= (release path with kill_tmux_session)
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs:416-420= (supervisor records terminal outcome but does not auto-release)

Fix shape (two options; pick the cleaner):
- (A) Teach supervisor: when a terminal driver event (=RunComplete= / =RunFail= / fatal =DriverError=) is observed on a tmux-tui run, auto-trigger release (calls into DriverControl::release which kills the tmux session).
- (B) Refactor tmux driver: on EOT detection, the capture loop itself calls the same cleanup path that external release uses (kill_tmux_session + drop event senders), so the supervisor sees the natural Ready→TextChunk→RunComplete→drop sequence and releases per its normal terminal handling.

Either approach must satisfy: EOT detected → tmux session killed → capture loop terminated → event channel closed → supervisor sees terminal outcome → run transitions to released state automatically (matching acp-stdio pattern).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EOT marker detection triggers full cleanup: tmux session killed, capture task aborted, event senders dropped.
- [ ] Supervisor records run as released (not stuck "active") after terminal driver event for tmux-tui runs.
- [ ] No tmux session orphans after EOT in tests OR live dispatch.
- [ ] No regression to acp-stdio release path (this fix should not change codex/claude-acp behavior).
- [ ] Cargo test green; clippy clean; MSRV 1.78.

:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-B5GFJ.2
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs

:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-2 (HIGH): If tmux session disappears before EOT marker observed, driver emits =RunComplete= (success) instead of =DriverError { fatal: true }= or =RunFail=. A failed claude TUI launch (auth, model, startup, worktree problems) gets recorded as completed → false-positive dogfood evidence → task auto-flips DONE despite no work.

Files / line refs:
- =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs:614-620= (current missing-session handling emits RunComplete)
- =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs:1246-1249= (release outcome classification)

Fix: distinguish "EOT marker observed" (legitimate success) from "session vanished" (failure). Missing-session-before-EOT → emit =DriverError { fatal: true, message: format!("tmux session {} ended before EOT marker", session) }= OR =RunFail= (whichever matches the acp-stdio failure pattern). Supervisor classification path (=ReleaseOutcome::Failed=) must apply.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tmux session-disappears-before-EOT path emits =DriverError= or =RunFail=, not =RunComplete=.
- [ ] Supervisor classifies the run as failed, not completed.
- [ ] Test added: spawn a TUI command that exits immediately (e.g., =bash -c "echo started; exit 0"=) → driver should emit failure, not success.
- [ ] No regression to legitimate EOT-driven success path (TASK-B5GFJ.1 covers).
- [ ] Cargo test green; clippy clean.

* DONE TASK-B5GFJ.3 fix-subtask: bounded missing-marker path (timeout + idle fallback) :drivers:tmux:claude:fix:robustness:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-B5GFJ.3
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-3 (MEDIUM): Capture loop only exits on =terminal_emitted=, missing tmux session, or exact EOT-marker match. No deadline / idle-prompt heuristic / deterministic escalation when claude forgets the marker, hits a token/tool limit, or runs out of context. Live dogfood can stall forever with a live tmux session — babysitter escalation eventually helps, but driver has no bounded failure mode.

Fix: add a turn-timeout config (default ~10 min from acquire) AND/OR a quiescence heuristic (pane content unchanged for N seconds + idle-prompt marker visible like claude's =>= prompt). On timeout/quiescence: emit failed terminal event ("tmux TUI turn exceeded {N}s without EOT marker") and release cleanly.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =TmuxTuiConfig= gets a =turn_timeout: Option<Duration>= field (default e.g. 600s).
- [ ] Capture loop checks deadline each tick; on exceed → emit DriverError/RunFail + cleanup (TASK-B5GFJ.1's path).
- [ ] Optional: quiescence detection (pane unchanged for ~60s + idle prompt visible) triggers same path with a different reason string.
- [ ] Test: spawn a TUI that prints text then sleeps forever without EOT → driver fails the run after timeout, not stalls.
- [ ] Cargo test green.

:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-B5GFJ.5
:PRIORITY:         P3

:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX

:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-5 (LOW): =pane_delta= byte-prefix logic at tmux.rs:685-700 mishandles:
- Pane truncation/clear/rewrite ("current is prefix of previous" returns empty → entire rewrite dropped).
- Scrollback wrap (old prefix rotated out → common-prefix scan fails → entire pane replayed).

Fix: line-aware delta. On each tick, compute new lines vs previous as: detect if =current= shares a contiguous suffix with =previous= (then emit only the trailing new lines); else (detected truncation/reset) emit a bounded-replay marker like =[orgasmic-tui-redraw]\n= followed by the current pane content, capped at some max length.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =pane_delta= correctly handles: empty pane, single-line, multi-line additive, full truncation, content replacement, scrollback wrap.
- [ ] Tests for each case.
- [ ] No bloat (TextChunk count bounded even on wrap scenarios).
- [ ] Cargo test green.

* DONE TASK-GMR53.1 fix-subtask: route tmux UI on activeRun.driver field (not JSONL inference) :ui:manager:popover:tmux:fix:correctness:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GMR53.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_B7YH2
:PRODUCES:         ui/src/components/manager/ManagerPopover.tsx ui/src/components/manager/ManagerWorkbench.tsx crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/runs.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/manager/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/runs.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/components/manager/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:TEST_CMD:         cd ui && npm run build && cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
TASK-GMR53 reviewer F-1 MEDIUM: tmux UI selection currently uses =detectDriverTag(activeRun?.kind, detail.data?.source)= which derives the driver tag from run kind and only upgrades to =tmux-tui= when the fetched session JSONL contains a =protocol_version=. This means a tmux-tui manager run can render the old chat transcript until =fetchRun= returns a Ready event, and any run whose JSONL is missing/delays Ready never exposes the tmux pane/composer despite being owned by the tmux driver.

The brief asked for driver-adaptive routing on the active run's =driver= field directly. Fix: propagate the driver/transport onto the live run summary (for manager runs, derive from =WorkerDriver::transport()= at acquire time), and route on =activeRun.driver === 'tmux-tui'=. Keep JSONL protocol inference only as a legacy fallback for runs that truly lack the field.

** Acceptance criteria
- [ ] =RunSummary= / live run summary gains an explicit =driver= field populated at acquire time from the driver implementation.
- [ ] =ManagerWorkbench= routes on the driver field, not on JSONL inference.
- [ ] JSONL inference becomes a legacy fallback used only when =driver= is missing.
- [ ] Test covers: tmux-tui run renders pane+composer BEFORE Ready event fires (which is the seam F-1 exposes).
- [ ] All gates green.

* DONE TASK-GMR53.2 fix-subtask: explicit /ws/tmux frame contract (delta vs full) + xterm replace-not-append :daemon:ws:tmux:ui:fix:robustness:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GMR53.2
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_B7YH2
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs ui/src/components/manager/ManagerTmuxPane.tsx
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs ui/src/components/manager/ManagerTmuxPane.tsx
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** ui/src/components/manager/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cd ui && npm run build && cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
TASK-GMR53 reviewer F-2 MEDIUM: the daemon's real path may send either an append-only delta or the entire current capture as a plain text frame, but the UI always writes every text frame as append-only terminal data. For full-screen TUI redraws, scrollback truncation, clears, or any non-prefix pane change, the server falls back to sending a full capture and the client appends that full screen below the previous screen — turning the manager pane into repeated snapshots rather than a live terminal view.

Fix: make the WS frame contract explicit. Either structured JSON frames =\{type: 'delta' | 'full', text\}= (binary text frames stay plain text for the delta case, JSON for full), OR adopt a real PTY-attach stream (xterm.js + pty bytes through). Lighter-touch path: =\{type, text\}= JSON frames; client clears/homes xterm on =full= before writing.

** Acceptance criteria
- [ ] WS frame format documented: distinguish delta (append-only) from full (replace-screen).
- [ ] Daemon =ws.rs= picks the correct frame type per the delta/full branch in =pane_delta=.
- [ ] UI =ManagerTmuxPane= handles =full= via =term.clear() + term.write(...)= or equivalent reset path.
- [ ] Inline test for non-prefix pane update (server emits =full=; client expects replace).
- [ ] All gates green.

** Description
First live-dogfood run of =implementer-claude-tmux= worker against real =claude= TUI after both TASK-B5GFJ fix-cluster (worker side) and TASK-GMR53 fix-cluster (manager UI side) landed. Validates end-to-end: claude TUI launches in tmux session, prompt pastes via buffer + Enter, capture-pane streams TextChunk, EOT marker triggers RunComplete + supervisor release + tmux session kill.

Trivial scope: add one =// arch: arch_A53QX — see decisions dec_X6Q0S= comment above any module declaration in =crates/orgasmic-core/src/lib.rs= that lacks one. The point is the wire-up, not the comment.

Brief at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-dogfood-claude-tmux-brief.md=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] claude-tmux driver acquires real tmux session (not inert).
- [ ] Pane streams TextChunk envelopes via capture-pane delta path.
- [ ] EOT marker =[ORGASMIC_EOT]= detected → RunComplete envelope → supervisor release → =tmux kill-session=.
- [ ] No tmux session orphan after release (=tmux ls= clean).
- [ ] Commit landed with the doc-comment.
- [ ] Capture run_id, total wall, token count, any surprises in Evidence section.

** Notes
- One-shot scope; if it works, promotes claude-tmux to Fallback B in the worker ladder.
- If it fails, surfaces concrete next bugs to fix in TASK-B5GFJ follow-ups.

** Evidence
- *Live-dogfood landed as commit =70db83d= merged =270820c=.* run_id =run-20260528T034434-b6cb2e8f32324c15860b64527dad8add=; daemon pid 37161 (just-rebuilt); claude-sonnet-4-6 high effort; 75k context; ~2k thinking tokens; total wall ~90s for the working portion.
- *6/10 driver wire-up components validated:* (1) =tmux new-session -d -s <name> claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --dangerously-skip-permissions= spawn ✓; (2) babysitter spawn (706 B JSONL) ✓; (3) capture-pane cadence ~500ms ✓; (4) =pane_delta= line-window detection (TASK-B5GFJ.5) ✓; (5) =redraw_delta= with =[orgasmic-tui-redraw]= marker on non-prefix updates ✓; (6) when prompted, claude does the work end-to-end (read file → edit → cargo check → git commit → echo "EOT") ✓.
- *4/10 wire-up bugs surfaced:* (a) =paste_text_into_pane= fires immediately after spawn, BEFORE claude TUI input field is ready (~2-5s render delay) → prompt is lost → claude sits at empty cursor forever → filed *TASK-HJBXF.1 HIGH*; (b) because prompt never reached claude through the driver, =prompt_with_eot_instruction= injection never reached claude → claude doesn't know the per-run-id EOT marker format → emits a placeholder marker that =pane_contains_eot_marker= doesn't recognize → no RunComplete envelope, no supervisor release, no =tmux kill-session= → filed *TASK-HJBXF.2 MEDIUM* (depends on .1); (c) =CODEX_PID:-= visible in dispatch-status (claude doesn't have a stable parent pid the way codex app-server does; the babysitter watches the tmux session instead) — not a bug, but a UX gap (=dispatch-status= should show a meaningful identity for claude-tmux runs); (d) per-run-id EOT marker isn't visible in capabilities or session JSONL anywhere, so a manual rescue is hard — filed implicitly under .2.
- *Manual rescue path used*: manager kill-session + force dispatch-close with merge-sha + REASON noting partial-validation.
- *claude-tmux does NOT yet promote to Fallback B in the worker ladder.* Promotion gated on TASK-HJBXF.{1,2} fix landing + second dogfood pass green.

** Description
TASK-HJBXF live-dogfood found: =claude= TUI takes ~2-5s to render its banner + input field after =tmux new-session= spawns it. The driver's current code calls =paste_text_into_pane= immediately after =spawn_tmux_session= returns, which races claude's startup → prompt enters the void → claude sits at empty cursor forever.

Two viable fixes:
1. *Wait-for-input-prompt heuristic*: capture-pane periodically (~250ms) after spawn; detect claude's prompt indicator (=❯ = at the start of a line, or => = for older claude versions) → only then paste. Add ~10s timeout fallback (if no prompt detected, log warning + paste anyway).
2. *Sleep-after-spawn*: simpler but less robust — =sleep 3-5s= after spawn before paste. Works for claude today but brittle if claude TUI startup gets slower or faster.

Reference HAR app (=../har/=) likely solves this — read =../har/crates/har-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= or equivalent for the proven pattern.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Driver waits for claude's input field to be ready before pasting prompt.
- [ ] If no input field detected after timeout (~10s default), log warning + paste anyway (don't block forever).
- [ ] Inline test: mock-peer that takes >1s to render its prompt; driver still successfully pastes.
- [ ] Real-wire smoke test (gated on =which tmux && which claude=) confirms the fix.
- [ ] All gates green.

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =823be91= (codex =dae58c3=, ~18 min wall, clean codex dispatch — no rescue needed for the codex side).* +164/-1 LOC across 2 files. New helper =wait_for_input_ready(session, timeout)= polls =capture_pane= every 250ms until prompt indicator (line starting with =❯= or => = followed by whitespace) appears OR =DriverError::InputNotReady(timeout)= fires. Acquire path calls it ONLY when =spawn_plan.command == "claude"=. On timeout, logs warning + pastes anyway. Real-world detail caught by codex: claude v2.1.153 uses NBSP (U+00A0) after =❯=, not ASCII space → detection uses generic =char::is_whitespace=. Inline tests: =wait_for_input_ready_returns_ok_when_mock_pane_has_prompt= + =wait_for_input_ready_returns_input_not_ready_on_timeout= + =pane_has_input_prompt_detects_claude_indicators= + =real_claude_input_ready_smoke= (gated on which tmux + claude). Configurable knob =TmuxTuiConfig.input_ready_timeout= default 10s.
- *Manager 2-line follow-up patch (this commit) restores 800ms settle-sleep for NON-claude commands.* Codex's diff inadvertently dropped the existing =tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(800)).await= that gated the paste path for the entire acquire loop, replacing it with a claude-only =wait_for_input_ready= branch. =real_tmux_prompt_bundle_is_consumed= (which uses =cat= as a mock TUI without a =❯= prompt) became flaky → race-dependent ordering between Ready emission and TextChunk emission from cat's instant stdout echo. Fix: when =command != "claude"=, sleep 800ms before paste (mirrors pre-TASK-HJBXF.1 behavior). 57/57 driver lib tests pass post-patch.
- *Gates on merged main =823be91= + manager patch:* cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers --lib (57 passed) ✓; cargo test --workspace ✓ (1 failure pre-patch — see manager note); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓; cargo fmt --check ✓; git diff --check ✓.
- *Lesson learned*: =cargo test 2>&1 | tail -30; echo "exit: $?"= captures tail's exit code, NOT cargo's. Use =set -o pipefail= or check =${PIPESTATUS[0]}= for the leftmost-pipe exit code in future gate runs. Logged for housekeeping.
- *Unblocks TASK-HJBXF.2* (second live-dogfood); next manager action.

** Description
Second live-dogfood pass after TASK-HJBXF.1 lands. Validates the path that the first dogfood couldn't reach because the prompt never carried the EOT marker instruction:

- prompt_with_eot_instruction injection into the bundle.
- claude echoes per-run-id =[orgasmic-eot:<run_id>]= marker on its own line.
- Driver's =pane_contains_eot_marker= fires on detection.
- Driver emits =RunComplete= envelope.
- Supervisor auto-releases run via TASK-B5GFJ.1's supervisor.rs path.
- Driver kills tmux session via TASK-B5GFJ.1's emit_fatal_driver_error_once + capture-loop cleanup.
- Worktree cleanup happens via standard dispatch-close.

Run as another TASK-HJBXF dogfood after TASK-HJBXF.1 fix-implementer lands.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [x] claude-tmux dispatch completes end-to-end without manager intervention.
- [x] Run lifecycle: Ready → TextChunks → RunComplete via EOT detection → tmux kill-session → supervisor release.
- [x] No tmux session orphan post-release.
- [x] Worktree + branch cleaned via dispatch-close.
- [x] claude-tmux promotes to Fallback B in worker ladder.

** Evidence
- *Live dogfood landed as commit =4775c44= merged =2b03595= (manager merge no-ff, then 2b03595 tracked as the merge SHA).* run_id =run-20260528T051724-f95dbf196646412ea6c634235a094860=; daemon pid 80577 (rebuilt to 823be91 with manager patch 76f7bdc for the 2-line non-claude settle-sleep restoration).
- *All 10 wire-up components validated end-to-end* (vs 6/10 in the first dogfood TASK-HJBXF): tmux spawn ✓, babysitter spawn ✓, capture-pane cadence ✓, pane_delta line-window ✓, redraw_delta with [orgasmic-tui-redraw] marker ✓, **wait_for_input_ready landed paste correctly (TASK-HJBXF.1)** ✓, **prompt_with_eot_instruction reached claude** ✓, **pane_contains_eot_marker fired on the per-run-id [orgasmic-eot:<run_id>] marker** ✓, **supervisor auto-release on terminal driver event (TASK-B5GFJ.1)** ✓, **tmux kill-session on release** ✓.
- *Dispatch-status final state*: =[exists] [pid-gone] [run-gone]= for TASK-HJBXF.2 — full clean lifecycle. dispatch-close cleaned the worktree + branch standardly.
- *Total wall*: ~3 min (spawn ~5s; claude ready ~5s; paste landed cleanly; claude work ~2 min including read brief + edit lib.rs + cargo check + git commit + EOT marker echo; driver detection + cleanup ~10s).
- *Promoting claude-tmux to Fallback B* in the worker ladder per goal-20260528 constraint. Stability order: codex-stdio primary (6+ clean in a row this session) → implementer-cursor (Fallback A, TASK-QSBDB in flight will widen its tool-policy) → implementer-claude-tmux (Fallback B, now validated end-to-end).
- *TASK-HJBXF parent closes the loop:* the original dogfood's 4 wire-up bugs all addressed across TASK-HJBXF.{1,2}. No further fix-subtasks needed.

** Description
Real-tmux integration tests (=real_tmux_prompt_bundle_is_consumed=, =real_tmux_session_lifecycle=, etc.) leave orphan tmux sessions named =orgasmic-run-prompt-real-<uuid>= after the test completes. The test calls =s.control.release("done")= but evidently the release path doesn't fully kill the tmux session under test isolation, OR the test ends before async cleanup completes.

Manager housekeeping during the TASK-HJBXF.2 dogfood found 3 such orphans accumulated from various test runs.

Fix: either (a) ensure =DriverSession::Drop= or explicit cleanup tears down the tmux session synchronously, or (b) use =std::process::Command::new("tmux").args(["kill-session", "-t", session_name])= as a final guard in each test's cleanup block.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Real-tmux tests don't leave orphan sessions after =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers --lib modes::tmux= run.
- [x] Mock-peer + non-tmux tests unaffected.
- [x] All gates green.

** Evidence
- *Date*: [2026-06-05 Fri].
- *Fix shape*: added a =SessionGuard(String)= Drop-guard struct to the tmux test module that calls the existing =kill_tmux_session_sync= on drop. Each real-tmux test that spawns a session now binds =let _guard = SessionGuard(tmux_session_name(&s.identity));= (or the explicit session name for =real_claude_input_ready_smoke=) immediately after spawn — so the session is killed unconditionally on success, assert-failure unwind, or panic. Tests covered: =real_tmux_session_lifecycle=, =real_claude_input_ready_smoke=, =real_tmux_eot_marker_emits_run_complete=, =real_tmux_early_exit_before_eot_is_failure=, =real_tmux_prompt_bundle_is_consumed= (the named orphan source), =real_tmux_attach_proves_existing_session=. =real_tmux_control_drop_without_release_kills_session= left unguarded by design (it asserts the driver's own Drop cleanup). =kill-session= is a no-op when the session already exited.
- *Gates*: =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= EXIT=0 (all real_tmux tests RAN — tmux was on PATH — and passed); =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= EXIT=0; =cargo fmt --check= EXIT=0; =git diff --check= EXIT=0.
- *Orphan proof*: post-run =pgrep -fl "worker-server|sleep 3600"= → none; =tmux ls= shows no leftover =orgasmic-test=/=run-real=/=run-prompt-real=/=run-attach=/=run-mock-eot=/=run-early-exit=/=input-ready= sessions (only the pre-existing daemon session + an unrelated June-3 run session remain).

:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor.rs

:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
TASK-QSBDB reviewer F-1 HIGH security: =command_has_shell_control= at =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor.rs:993= rejects =;=, =|=, =&=, newlines, backticks, =$(=, but does NOT reject shell redirection (=<=, =>=) or process substitution. Commands like =git -C <worktree> diff >/etc/orgasmic-leak= or =grep --path <worktree> pattern < /etc/passwd= pass the first-token + path checks while the shell performs I/O OUTSIDE the worktree before running the allowed binary.

This violates the TASK-QSBDB negative-policy guarantee that cursor still cannot read arbitrary host paths or write outside the write_scope.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =command_has_shell_control= rejects =<= and =>= (and =>>=, =<<=, =>&=) along with the existing list.
- [ ] OR replace generic shell-string gate with exact per-command argv parsers that allow only intended shapes.
- [ ] Special-case =kill 12345 2>/dev/null= if that's a needed pattern (narrow stderr-to-/dev/null only for kill/pkill).
- [ ] Negative test in driver_modes.rs asserting redirection on allowed commands is blocked.
- [ ] All gates green.

** Evidence
(populated at task close)

:PROPERTIES:

** Description
TASK-QSBDB reviewer F-2 MEDIUM security: =read_args_allowed= and =search_args_allowed= at =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor.rs:764-770= use =candidate_paths(args).into_iter().any(...)=. Since =candidate_paths= treats keys like =cwd= as path candidates, a tool call with an UNSAFE target path PLUS a SAFE =cwd= still passes the policy.

Separately, =path_is_under= does lexical normalization only (no =fs::canonicalize=), so symlinks under the worktree or =/tmp/orgasmic-codex= resolve to arbitrary host paths while still passing the prefix check.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read/search path policy validates SCHEMA-SPECIFIC target fields (not generic any-candidate).
- [ ] ALL target paths in a tool call must pass; not just any-one.
- [ ] Canonicalize existing target paths + worktree/brief roots before prefix comparison. Lexical normalize remains the fallback for intentionally-nonexistent output paths only.
- [ ] Negative tests: unsafe target + safe cwd; symlink traversal under allowed roots.
- [ ] All gates green.

** Evidence
(populated at task close)

** Description
The CLI dispatcher (=crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs=) hard-codes =DispatchKind= to =Implementer | Reviewer=. TASK-EZ0ST shipped the architector persona, agent template, prompt template, and convention bundle — but the dispatch wiring was never extended. As a result =orgasmic manager dispatch --kind architector ...= fails clap validation, and TASK-ENXWA (first real architector run) cannot be dispatched cleanly.

Add an =Architector= variant to =DispatchKind= and thread it through every branching site so an architector dispatch behaves correctly end-to-end (worktree path, branch name, lifecycle stage transitions, dispatch_started tx KIND field, close tx type, dispatchable-stage gate).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =DispatchKind= enum gains =Architector= variant with =as_str() -> "architector"=.
- [ ] =default_worktree(task, Architector)= returns =/tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/<slug>-arch= (or similar non-colliding path; must differ from both implementer and reviewer defaults).
- [ ] =default_branch(task, Architector)= returns =<slug>-arch=.
- [ ] =lifecycle_stage_for_kind(Architector)= maps to a sensible architector stage. Two acceptable conventions: (a) re-use =LifecycleStage::Implement= since architector authors content same as implementer just on different files, OR (b) introduce a new =LifecycleStage= variant if the existing state-machine vocabulary supports it. Implementer's choice — justify in the PR.
- [ ] Close-tx-type mapping in =close_tx_type_for_kind=: =Architector= -> =architector.done=.
- [ ] =dispatchable_stage(Architector, stage)= mirrors implementer (BACKLOG or current architector-stage). Document the gate inline.
- [ ] Sprint state transitions: dispatch flips BACKLOG -> architector-stage (whatever choice was made above); close-done flips -> DONE.
- [ ] Existing dispatch CLI tests pass + at least one new test covers the architector path (worktree path, branch, kind round-trip).
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; MSRV 1.78.
- [ ] No changes to daemon supervisor or codex worker code — architector dispatch must work via the existing =--worker architector= path with the agent template loaded by =shipped/agent-templates/architector.org= (already shipped via TASK-EZ0ST).

** Notes
- *Tiny surface*: ~7 match-arm sites in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs= (enum, as_str, lifecycle stage map at ~L1118, close-tx-type map at ~L803, sprint-state transition map at ~L1135 + ~L1140, dispatchable-stage gate at ~L1290, default_worktree at ~L1374, default_branch at ~L1384). Plus tests.
- *Unblocks TASK-ENXWA* (first architector run) and any future architector tasks.
- *Daemon already supports the architector path* via =req.worker_id= → agent template loader (=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs=). The CLI gap is the only blocker.
- *Out of scope*: griller/planner DispatchKind variants (file separately if needed); architector persona changes; architecture.org content changes.

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =f72efd2= (codex =72e89b9=, ~44 min wall, codex-stdio gpt-5.5 high dispatch).* 1 file +137/-13 LOC. =DispatchKind::Architector= variant added with =as_str()="architector"=; threaded through 10 sites in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs=: enum, as_str, =done_tx_type= → =architector.done=, =lifecycle_stage_for_kind= → =LifecycleStage::Implement= (re-used per implementer's justified choice; no new state-machine variant), sprint state transition map (open: BACKLOG → IMPLEMENT; close-done: → DONE), =dispatchable_stage(Architector, _)= accepts =Backlog | Implement=, =allowed_stage_text(Architector)= = ="BACKLOG or IMPLEMENT"=, =default_worktree(_, Architector)= = =<slug>-arch= suffix, =default_branch(_, Architector)= = =<slug>-arch=, generalized worktree-collision check (iterates over all =\[Implementer, Reviewer, Architector\]= and rejects any cross-kind default path reuse). 2 new tests covering kind round-trip + default-distinctness asserts. **Surprise (non-blocking)**: codex's own sandbox denied local-socket test binds; codex retried with escalated permissions internally (orgasmic's sandbox + diff unaffected).
- *Reviewer pass verdict =has-issues-no-blocks-ship=* via =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-096-reviewer-report.md=: 0H/0M/1L. F-1 LOW: collision-rejection is implemented correctly + smoke-test passed, but committed tests don't drive =cmd_dispatch= with =--kind architector --worktree <implementer-default>= to assert the bail path. Test-coverage gap, not a functional defect. Reviewer hand-verified the smoke test: =orgasmic manager dispatch --kind architector --dry-run= shows worktree =/tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-086-arch= + branch =task-086-arch= as expected. Filed TASK-DMTDF.1 (BACKLOG, P3, fix-subtask) for the regression test.

** Description
Reviewer found that the architector worktree-collision rejection (=crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs:286-303=) is implemented correctly and smoke-test-verified, but committed tests do not exercise the rejection path end-to-end. The committed tests cover defaults distinctness via =assert_ne!=, not the =cmd_dispatch= bail.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one test in =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs= (preferred end-to-end CLI shape) or inline =#[test]= drives =cmd_dispatch= / dry-run with =--kind architector --worktree /tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-086= (the implementer default for TASK-ENXWA) and asserts the error =architector worktree must not reuse implementer default path: ...=.
- [ ] Mirror test exists for =--kind reviewer --worktree <implementer default>= and =--kind architector --worktree <reviewer default>= — exhaust the cross-kind matrix.
- [ ] Existing tests still pass.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Tiny surface*: one test file or one inline test block. ~30-50 LOC.
- *Reviewer report*: =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-096-reviewer-report.md= (verbatim finding).
- *Out of scope*: any change to the production rejection code (it's already correct); any change to dispatch CLI surface; daemon-side or driver changes.

** Evidence
- *Shipped collision semantics (verified by reading =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs:286-303=, the =cmd_dispatch= path):* after =build_dispatch_plan=, =cmd_dispatch= loops over =[Implementer, Reviewer, Architector]= and bails when =other_kind != plan.kind && plan.worktree_path == default_worktree(first_task, other_kind)=. Error text uses the =DispatchKind= Display impl (=as_str()=): ="{} worktree must not reuse {} default path: {}"=. The check fires *before* the =--dry-run= early return, so a dry-run dispatch exercises the bail with no worktree creation, no daemon dispatch, and no worker spawn — the test driver needs no stub codex.
- *Test added (=crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs=, =dispatch_rejects_cross_kind_default_worktree_reuse=):* end-to-end CLI test (mirrors the existing =run_orgasmic_failure= + dry-run idiom) that drives =orgasmic manager dispatch ... --dry-run= for the full cross-kind matrix the loop guards: (1) =--kind architector --worktree /tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-dispatch= (implementer default) → bails ="architector worktree must not reuse implementer default path:"=; (2) =--kind architector --worktree /tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-dispatch-review= (reviewer default) → ="architector worktree must not reuse reviewer default path:"=; (3) =--kind reviewer --worktree /tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-review-issues= (implementer default) → ="reviewer worktree must not reuse implementer default path:"=. Uses BACKLOG =TASK-DISPATCH= (architector-dispatchable) and REVIEW =TASK-REVIEW-ISSUES= (reviewer-dispatchable) from =seed_project=. Asserts none of the colliding paths were created on disk. PATH preserves system git (=path_with_stub=) since =build_dispatch_plan= resolves =--from/HEAD= via git. Daemon shut down at end per the established teardown discipline.
- *Non-vacuity proof:* temporarily replaced the guard condition with =if false && ...= (collision check disabled) and reran the test → it FAILED (=EXIT=101=: the architector dry-run *succeeded* and printed the dispatch plan, so =run_orgasmic_failure= panicked on the unexpected success). Restored the production condition =other_kind != plan.kind=; production code is unchanged in the commit. Log: =/tmp/t096_1-vacuity.log=.
- *Gates on this tree:* =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= ✓ (11 passed in dispatch.rs incl. the new test, 49 total across the crate's test binaries; =/tmp/t096_1-cli.log=); =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= ✓ (=/tmp/t096_1-clippy.log=); =cargo fmt --check= ✓; =git diff --check= ✓.
- *Production code untouched:* only =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs= gains the new test (+~100 LOC); the rejection code in =manager.rs= is unmodified.
