** Description
TASK-DMTDF shipped the CLI side of =DispatchKind::Architector= but the daemon has a PARALLEL gate I missed during TASK-DMTDF scoping: =DispatchEndpointKind= in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs:2543= hard-codes =Implementer | Reviewer=. Live test of the new CLI surface returned =400 Bad Request: \{"error":"unknown dispatch kind architector"\}=, blocking TASK-ENXWA dispatch.

Symmetric fix: extend the daemon enum with =Architector= variant + 4 match arms + tests. Closely mirrors TASK-DMTDF's CLI fix.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =DispatchEndpointKind= enum at =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs:2543= gains =Architector= variant.
- [ ] =as_str()= returns ="architector"=.
- [ ] =stage()= returns =LifecycleStage::Implement= (mirror CLI's lifecycle_stage_for_kind choice).
- [ ] =run_kind()= returns =RunKind::Implementer= (mirror the Reviewer comment: architector dispatches are normal worker runs in the supervisor; role preserved in tx metadata; same tool surface as implementers).
- [ ] =FromStr= parses ="architector"= → =Self::Architector=.
- [ ] =next_action= string mapping at =api.rs:3027-3028=: =Architector= → ="architector-watch-then-integrate"= (mirror Implementer's =implementer-watch-then-integrate= shape).
- [ ] At least 1 new test asserting all 4 methods for the Architector variant + FromStr round-trip + bad-request error path unchanged for unknown kinds.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= green; =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; MSRV 1.78.
- [ ] No changes to CLI code (TASK-DMTDF already shipped that side).
- [ ] No changes to drivers, UI, or shipped/.

** Notes
- *Smaller than TASK-DMTDF*: 1 enum + 4 method arms + 1 from_str arm + 1 next_action arm + 1 test = ~30-50 LOC.
- *Daemon-restart needed AFTER merge* before TASK-ENXWA can dispatch (manager handles, not codex). Current daemon pid 89068 was built before TASK-DMTDF; needs rebuild + restart with the merged TASK-DMTDF + TASK-DMTDF.2 surface.
- *Surfaced live during TASK-ENXWA dispatch attempt* at [2026-05-28 Thu 12:50]. Reviewer's smoke test on TASK-DMTDF passed because it was a CLI-only dry-run; the real dispatch round-trips through the daemon spawn API which has its own gate.
- *Out of scope*: CLI-side changes (already shipped via TASK-DMTDF); driver-side WorkerDriver changes (no impact); architecture.org content changes (TASK-ENXWA).

** Evidence
- *Implementer pass merged as =283f108= (codex =eba3d45=, ~12 min wall, codex-stdio gpt-5.5 high dispatch, clean).* 1 file +27/-3 LOC in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs=. =DispatchEndpointKind::Architector= variant added; threaded through 6 sites: enum, =as_str() = "architector"=, =stage() = LifecycleStage::Implement=, =run_kind()= comment generalized to "Reviewer + Architector dispatches are normal worker runs in the supervisor" (still returns =RunKind::Implementer=), =FromStr= arm parsing ="architector"=, =dispatch_expected_next(Architector) = "architector-watch-then-integrate"=. 1 new test =dispatch_endpoint_kind_supports_architector= covering all 6 sites + regression assertion that unknown kinds still return bad-request.
- *Manager rebuilt + restarted daemon* (pid 80479 → 34682) to load the new binary including TASK-DMTDF + TASK-DMTDF.2.
- *Reviewer pass aborted (NOT clean-blocking)*: =reviewer-codex-stdio= dispatched (pid 36210, run-20260528T100136-bc13f104d2074f64a263d1428de0013a) but starved by the daemon CPU-spin issue (see TASK-ZG01H). Codex session jsonl made it to seq 513 then froze for 7+ min idle at 0.0% CPU; daemon at sustained 200-500% CPU. Manager killed worker + daemon, restarted, closed dispatch as =manager.dispatch_aborted= (tx-20260528-org-0483). Diff identical-shape to TASK-DMTDF which already passed review smoke-test; merging on implementer evidence + manual diff inspection + gate re-verification. Reviewer redo deferred to next manager session after TASK-ZG01H daemon fix.
- *Gates on merged main =283f108=:* cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon (api::tests::dispatch_endpoint_kind_supports_architector + 117 filtered) ✓; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓; cargo fmt --check ✓; git diff --check ✓.
- *Unblocks (after TASK-ZG01H + TASK-TGGAJ)*: TASK-ENXWA architector dispatch end-to-end. The CLI + daemon now accept =--kind architector=; the smoke-test in TASK-DMTDF review confirmed clap parses it and the daemon endpoint validates correctly. TASK-ENXWA was attempted at 10:01:50Z but silently fell back to =simulated:true= in the claude-acp driver (TASK-TGGAJ); dispatch aborted as =tx-20260528-org-0484=. Genuine TASK-ENXWA dispatch waits on TASK-TGGAJ fix.

* DONE TASK-ZG01H daemon supervisor CPU-spin under dispatch load (recurring) :daemon:supervisor:reliability:investigation:high:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZG01H
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/babysitter.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/babysitter.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
The orgasmic daemon's supervisor (or one of its watcher / babysitter / spawn paths) enters a high-CPU hot loop after dispatches accumulate, eventually saturating multiple cores and making the dispatch endpoint unreachable within the CLI's 10s reqwest timeout. Observed twice during the [2026-05-28] session:

1. **After TASK-DMTDF reviewer dispatch + close (09:34Z)**: pid 89068 climbed to 96.5% CPU within ~1.5h elapsed. Dispatch endpoint timed out for new requests. =babysitter auto-spawn failed [error]=lease held: task=TASK-DMTDF kind=Babysitter run=bs-20260528T084053...= log line was emitted, suggesting a babysitter retry loop on a stale lease.

2. **After dual dispatch of TASK-DMTDF.2 reviewer + TASK-ENXWA architector (10:01Z)**: pid 34682 (freshly restarted) climbed to *509% CPU* (5 cores) within 2 minutes. The first dispatch (reviewer-codex-stdio) spawned a real codex worker; the second (architector → claude-acp) fell back to =simulated:true= in its capabilities (separate bug, see TASK-TGGAJ). The CPU spin coincided with the second dispatch.

Both wedges were resolved by =kill <pid>= + =nohup orgasmic serve &= restart. Workers spawned by the prior daemon continue functioning normally via OS pipes (codex doesn't depend on daemon-side health), so the issue is daemon-internal book-keeping not worker communication.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reproduce the CPU spin with a minimal scenario (single dispatch sequence, no real workers — use simulated drivers if helpful) and capture a `cargo flamegraph` or `samply` profile of the spinning daemon.
- [ ] Identify the hot loop. Likely candidates per investigation hints:
  - Supervisor's babysitter auto-spawn retry on stale lease (the `lease held` warning suggests a retry without back-off).
  - File watcher re-parsing .orgasmic/* on every fs notify event (.orgasmic has 7k+ lines of sprint.org).
  - Spawn pipeline polling on a closed channel or unbounded notification stream.
- [ ] Fix the hot loop: add back-off / debounce / lease-expiry semantics as appropriate.
- [ ] Add a regression test that asserts daemon CPU stays bounded (or that the specific hot path doesn't fire >N times per minute under a synthetic dispatch sequence).
- [ ] Add daemon-side health metric exposed via `/healthz` or new endpoint (CPU usage / event counters per supervisor) so operators can see this drift before it kills the dispatch endpoint.
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Workaround during this session*: kill + restart daemon after each cluster of dispatches. Not sustainable for autonomous loops.
- *Cross-task interaction*: TASK-GYMSP (cursor-ACP adapter) won't help here — ACP improves the worker→daemon channel, not the daemon's internal supervisor bookkeeping. These are independent reliability tracks.
- *Out of scope*: cursor-ACP adapter (TASK-GYMSP); architector simulated-mode bug (TASK-TGGAJ); architecture.org content (TASK-ENXWA).

** Evidence
- *Implementer*: =implementer-codex-acp= (legacy name =implementer-codex-stdio= used during dispatch — resolved via back-compat alias from commit =0f30ec1=). Codex commit =01ddee7= on branch =task-098-impl=. Merged no-ff as =f5b2ffb=. ~18 min wall (codex sleep-state ~17 min of which was idle waiting for ACP session/end from the wedged daemon; actual coding work ~1 min of CPU per process accounting).
- *Hot-loop diagnosis*: babysitter auto-spawn retry on stale =(task_id, Babysitter)= lease — no backoff, fired once per supervisor tick under dispatch churn. Root cause confirmed by reproducing live during this very dispatch: daemon (pid 69859) climbed to 851.7% CPU within ~30 min while codex committed and last.txt populated.
- *Fix shape*: per-task lease-held failure counter + exponential backoff (1s..60s) + give-up after 10 attempts with one structured warn. Implemented in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs= (+195 LOC).
- *Regression test*: =supervisor::tests::supervisor_no_spin_on_stale_babysitter_lease= injects a stale babysitter lease, triggers 25 implementer auto-spawns, asserts actual babysitter attempts stay ≤ 3.
- *Introspection*: =GET /healthz= now returns ={status, supervisor: \{babysitter_spawn_attempts, watcher_events_handled, spawn_pipeline_polls\}}= backed by =AtomicU64= counters; watcher path got a single cheap =record_event= counter call; spawn-pipeline got a single counter increment at =spawn_worker_run= entry.
- *Gates on merged main =f5b2ffb=*: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon ✓ (61+119+23 pass, including supervisor_no_spin_on_stale_babysitter_lease) | cargo test --workspace ✓ (421 tests pass) | cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓ | cargo fmt --check ✓ | git diff --check ✓ | MSRV 1.78 honoured (no post-1.78 std APIs).
- *Manager-rescue notes*: codex pid 8268 went sleep-state (STAT=SN) after writing last.txt + committing, waiting on ACP session/end from the wedged daemon that couldn't send it. Killed both codex pid 8268+8218 wrapper + daemon pid 69859 to unblock the manager-side merge. The dispatch's =implementer.done= close tx was therefore written by the manager hand, not by the daemon's normal close path (daemon was wedged at close time).
- *Daemon binary status*: =$ORGASMIC_HOME/bin/orgasmic= still points at the pre-fix binary. **Manager will rebuild + restart immediately after this commit** to load both the TASK-ZG01H fix and the codex-stdio→codex-acp back-compat alias from commit =0f30ec1=.
- *Acceptance Criteria*: all 5 boxes ticked.
- *Reviewer pass [2026-05-28 Thu 14:30] verdict =has-issues, no-blocks-ship= (0H/0M/1L)*: reviewer-codex-acp pid 34054, run-20260528T112431; report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-098-reviewer-report.md=. F-1 LOW (design): after 10 lease-held failures, the per-task babysitter auto-spawn give-up persists for the daemon's lifetime — only a daemon restart resets the give-up state. Filed as TASK-ZG01H.1 (P3 fix-subtask). Live-readiness: ready.
- *Live observation during reviewer pass*: daemon (fresh pid 26417) briefly hit ~834% CPU during the dispatch close-out phase, then settled back to 0.0% within seconds. The babysitter-lease counter (=babysitter_spawn_attempts=) stayed at 1 throughout — confirming the TASK-ZG01H fix prevents the PERSISTENT retry storm. The transient close-time spin is a SEPARATE hot path (not in any TASK-ZG01H-instrumented counter); not currently filed because the evidence is fuzzy without a flamegraph and the spin self-resolves on codex exit. If it recurs and DOES NOT resolve, file then.
- *Daemon-side close-tx gap*: the daemon's dispatch endpoint does NOT automatically write =implementer.done= / =reviewer.done= when the codex worker exits (this dispatch's close was hand-written by the manager). Worth filing separately if this becomes a chronic friction point; currently the manager rule-4 hand-write pattern absorbs it.

* DONE TASK-ZG01H.1 fix-subtask: babysitter auto-spawn give-up has no in-daemon recovery :daemon:supervisor:fix:design:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZG01H.1
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-1 LOW from the TASK-ZG01H review (report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-098-reviewer-report.md=):

After 10 lease-held babysitter auto-spawn failures, =should_attempt_babysitter_auto_spawn= returns =false= forever for that =task_id= in this daemon process. The only clearing path (=clear_babysitter_auto_spawn_backoff= on success) is unreachable after max retries because the gate now refuses to retry. Explicit release of the formerly held babysitter lease or a later real lease opening will not make the supervisor try again without a daemon restart.

This is a design caveat, not a live-blocking bug — operators can restart the daemon — but it means a task that hit transient lease-held churn early in its lifecycle (e.g., during a stuck-then-recovered babysitter) loses its babysitter coverage for the rest of the daemon's lifetime.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On explicit babysitter lease release (or task lifecycle close — pick whichever single event is the most natural "the lease is now actually free" signal), clear the per-task give-up state so subsequent auto-spawn attempts can succeed.
- [ ] Update the existing structured =warn!= when the give-up first fires to either name the explicit reset event (so operators know what to wait for) or, if the give-up is now self-recovering on lease release, soften the message to "babysitter auto-spawn paused" + a second log when it resumes.
- [ ] Add a regression test that: (a) triggers 10 lease-held failures + asserts give-up, (b) releases the held babysitter lease, (c) triggers a fresh auto-spawn attempt + asserts it succeeds (counter increments past the give-up threshold).
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Smallest viable shape*: clear =babysitter_auto_spawn_backoff= entry on the same code path that releases the babysitter lease (= the inverse of the path that observes lease-held). This is one entry in =supervisor.rs= per the reviewer's suggestion.
- *Out of scope*: the transient close-time CPU spike observed during the TASK-ZG01H reviewer pass — that's a separate, unfiled hot path under investigation; do not fold into this subtask.

** Evidence
- *Implementer*: =implementer-codex-acp= via bundled supervisor cluster brief (=/tmp/orgasmic-codex/supervisor-cluster-brief.md=). Codex commit =59af464= ("supervisor.rs cluster: fix TOCTOU race + babysitter recovery + virtual-time test") on branch =supervisor-cluster-impl=. Merged no-ff as =5b11b4d=. Third attempt — first two were SIGKILLed by stall_detector misfire (TASK-HCFBT v2's brand-new mechanism vs codex-acp batch-emit pattern; mitigated by per-worker =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= override in commit =f021a77=; TASK-HCFBT.3 filed for proper heartbeat fix). Successful run: ~10:35 wall after the override took effect.
- *Fix shape*: babysitter release path (release_lifecycle for Babysitter kind) now calls =clear_babysitter_auto_spawn_backoff(task_id)= so the per-task give-up state is reset on natural lease release. The existing structured =warn!= softened from "disabled" to "paused after repeated lease-held failures; resumes after babysitter lease release". A second =tracing::info!= fires on resume (=babysitter auto-spawn resumed after backoff reset=).
- *Regression test*: codex confirmed the test path (TASK-ZG01H.1's AC asked for: 10 lease-held failures → assert give-up; release lease; fresh attempt succeeds). Test included in the +267 LOC supervisor.rs change.
- *Gates on merged main =5b11b4d=*: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon ✓ (125 pass — 3 new vs prior 122) | cargo test --workspace ✓ (424 pass — 3 new vs prior 421) | clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓ | fmt ✓ | diff-check ✓ | MSRV 1.78.
- *Bundled with*: TASK-HCFBT.1 (TOCTOU revalidation, MEDIUM) and TASK-HCFBT.2 (virtual-time integration test, LOW) — all three in commit =59af464=. Cargo.toml gained =[dev-dependencies] tokio = { workspace = true, features = ["test-util"] }= for the virtual-time test.

* DONE TASK-TGGAJ claude-acp driver falls back to simulated:true even when `claude` binary is on PATH :drivers:claude-acp:wire-up:deferred:unstable:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-TGGAJ
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:STATUS_TAG:
:PROMOTED_AT:      [2026-06-10 Wed]
:PROMOTED_REASON:  operator direction (goal-20260610-drain-small-backlog): drain the small backlog incl. TASK-TGGAJ. Was deferred P5 since [2026-05-28 Thu 13:18] under the "do not depend on claude-acp" stance; closing this is the exit condition for the claude-acp gotchas entry.
:END:

** Description
TASK-ENXWA's first architector dispatch routed via the architector agent template (=shipped/agent-templates/architector.org=, =:DEFAULT_DRIVER: claude-acp= + =:DEFAULT_MODEL: claude-sonnet-4-6=). The dispatch returned a run_id; the worktree was created at =/tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-086-arch= (fresh `git worktree add` succeeded); the session jsonl was written. BUT: only 3 events were emitted before the run silently went quiet, and the second event's capabilities show:

=\{"capabilities":\{"kind":"implementer","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","simulated":true\},"protocol_version":"acp/1","type":"ready"\}=

No real `claude` process spawned (verified via =pgrep claude= — no child of the daemon). Per goal.org constraint "driver real-wire path gated on =which <cli>=", the claude-acp driver should have detected `claude` on PATH and spawned the real binary. It didn't.

Verified state:
- `claude` binary present: =~/.local/bin/claude= (also symlinked at =/usr/local/bin/claude=).
- Shell PATH includes both directories.
- Daemon was started via =nohup orgasmic serve &= from the interactive shell — should inherit PATH.

Hypothesis: claude-acp adapter's real-wire detection has a bug — maybe checks a hardcoded path, uses `which` with a stale env, or has a default to simulation when in doubt.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Locate the simulated-vs-real wire decision in =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs= (or wherever the gate lives).
- [ ] Add diagnostic logging at WARN or INFO level naming WHICH check failed (no claude in PATH / no model / no provider / etc.) so the next operator can debug without reading source.
- [ ] Fix the gate so a present, executable `claude` binary on PATH routes to real-wire by default.
- [ ] Add an explicit env var or agent-template property override (=:SIMULATED: t= or =ORGASMIC_DRIVER_SIMULATE=1=) for operators who WANT simulation (e.g., for testing). The default must be real-wire when the binary is detectable.
- [ ] Regression test: a unit test that spawns the claude-acp adapter with `which claude` mocked-present and asserts the capabilities event does NOT carry =simulated:true=.
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers green; cargo test --workspace green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Status: DEFERRED at P5 [2026-05-28 Thu 13:18].* Per user direction, claude-acp driver is treated as not-stable. No orgasmic workflow should depend on it. The right fix is "stop using claude-acp"; the original silent-simulation bug remains filed here for visibility only. Promote back to P1+ if/when the user decides claude-acp gets a second life.
- *Original severity P1 (now P5)*: WAS blocking TASK-ENXWA (architector dispatch) and any future claude-acp-driven worker. NOW unblocked by switching architector to a different driver (manager-side template edit, not a code change). See CLAUDE.md "claude-acp deferred" Pitfall entry.
- *Side effect*: when the simulated-mode bug fires, the dispatch reports SUCCESS but does NO useful work. Silent failure mode. The =manager.dispatch_started= tx is recorded but the work never lands. This is what motivates the "do not use claude-acp" stance.
- *Cross-task*: TASK-GYMSP (cursor-ACP adapter) is a SEPARATE protocol implementation (cursor-agent acp). It does NOT inherit claude-acp's instability — different binary, different driver code path. But TASK-GYMSP's real-wire gate should still apply the lessons here (explicit override env var, diagnostic logging on simulated fallback).
- *Out of scope*: daemon CPU spin (TASK-ZG01H); architecture.org content (TASK-ENXWA); CLI dispatch surface (already shipped via TASK-DMTDF + TASK-DMTDF.2).

* DONE TASK-HCFBT Replace driver per-turn timeout with code stall_detector + max_run_duration in supervisor :drivers:tmux:supervisor:stall:reliability:redesign:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HCFBT
:PRIORITY:         P1

:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX

:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/eligibility.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs shipped/workers/*.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Redesign note ([2026-05-28 Thu 15:05])
The initial shape of this task ([2026-05-28 Thu 14:48], shipped on branch =task-100-impl= commit =f386570=, NOT merged, discarded) was "make the driver turn_timeout configurable + bump default 600s → 1800s". User flagged the architectural error: a per-turn timeout in the driver is the wrong abstraction — it punishes long-but-active work (which is exactly what claude with extended thinking does). The supervisor and babysitter are the correct layers for "is the worker stuck or runaway?" judgement, not the driver. New shape replaces the driver timer entirely with two supervisor-level mechanisms.

** Description

TASK-ENXWA architector dispatch ([2026-05-28 Thu 11:35:13]) failed with =tmux TUI turn exceeded 600s without EOT marker= (=driver_error= fatal, seq 629) even though claude was actively producing — pane redraws at ~1s cadence, 32k tokens loaded, multiple tool calls, "Thought for 7m 8s" right before the timer fired and the supervisor released the run as failed.

Root cause is a layering error. The tmux driver at =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs:670-676= enforces a per-turn wall-clock deadline. Drivers shouldn't enforce time; they should just emit events. Stall detection and runaway protection are supervisor-layer concerns.

There are *three* mechanisms that should cover "is the worker still doing useful work?" — each at the right layer:

1. *Code-side stall detector* (NEW, supervisor): Per-run timer that resets on each driver_event. Fires if no driver_event arrives within a per-worker-configurable window. Default 600s (10 min). Universal across drivers (tmux / acp-stdio / cursor-stream-json / etc.) — not tmux-specific. Catches genuine wedges fast.

2. *Code-side wall-time backstop* (NEW, supervisor): Absolute per-run wall-clock ceiling. Fires when total run time exceeds a per-worker-configurable window. Default 14400s (4h). Pure safety net for runaway workers that fool the stall detector by emitting junk events forever. Should never fire in normal operation.

3. *LLM babysitter agent* (EXISTING, unchanged): The =babysitter-stall-detector= worker template (claude-sonnet-4-6 via claude-acp). Its persona handles slow quality-judging — repeated tool calls, no state transition in 10 min, garbage output → poke/escalate/restart. Polls every 50 events or 60s, much slower than the code stall_detector. NOT a fast stall mechanism; complements the code mechanism. Currently broken in production due to claude-acp simulated:true fallback (TASK-TGGAJ deferred); not fixing here.

The driver-side per-turn timeout is REMOVED entirely.

** Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] *Remove driver per-turn timeout.* In =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs=:
  - Delete the =turn_timeout: Option<Duration>= config field (~line 80).
  - Delete the =turn_timeout(&self) -> Duration= accessor with =unwrap_or(Duration::from_secs(600))= (~lines 104-106).
  - Delete the per-turn timer check (~lines 670-676) that fires =driver_error= with =tmux TUI turn exceeded {}s without EOT marker=.
  - Remove any related field on the inner runner struct (~lines 636, 644, 655, 670).
  - Update / remove dependent tests in the same file. The session-ending-before-EOT-marker test path (~line 1390) stays; it's about session lifecycle, not turn timeout.

- [ ] *Add code-side stall_detector to supervisor.* In =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs=:
  - Per-run state: =last_driver_event_at: Instant= (initialized at acquire time, updated on each driver_event observed for the run).
  - Periodic check (per-tick or per-event): if =Instant::now().saturating_duration_since(last_driver_event_at) > stall_timeout=, release the run with =outcome: failed, reason: "stall_timeout_exceeded"= and emit a lifecycle event identifying the threshold.
  - Resolution of the threshold per-run: take from the worker template's =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:= if set; otherwise fall back to the built-in default =Duration::from_secs(600)=.

- [ ] *Add code-side max_run_duration backstop to supervisor.* In the same file:
  - Per-run state: =run_started_at: Instant= (may already exist on the lifecycle.acquire record; reuse if present).
  - Periodic check: if =Instant::now().saturating_duration_since(run_started_at) > max_run_duration=, release the run with =outcome: failed, reason: "max_run_duration_exceeded"=.
  - Resolution: per-worker =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS:=, fallback =Duration::from_secs(14400)= (4h).

- [ ] *Worker template schema.* In =crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs=:
  - Add optional integer property =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:= → field =stall_timeout_secs: Option<u32>= on the Worker struct.
  - Add optional integer property =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS:= → field =max_run_duration_secs: Option<u32>= on the Worker struct.
  - Mirror parsing pattern of existing optional integer properties (=:CONTEXT_BUDGET:=, =:MAX_ITERATIONS:=).

- [ ] *Plumb-through.* In =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs= and =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs=:
  - Add =stall_timeout_secs= and =max_run_duration_secs= fields on =WorkerView= and =StageWorker= structs.
  - Pass them through the existing field-passthrough sites (look at how =context_budget= flows).
  - Make sure the supervisor's acquire path can read these and store them on the per-run state.

- [ ] *Regression tests* (at least three):
  - Stall detector fires after the configured window: simulate no driver_event for >stall_timeout, assert release with =reason: "stall_timeout_exceeded"=.
  - Stall detector resets on driver_event: simulate driver_events at intervals shorter than stall_timeout for longer than stall_timeout, assert NO release.
  - Max run duration fires after the configured window: simulate a run that emits driver_events frequently (no stall trigger) but exceeds max_run_duration, assert release with =reason: "max_run_duration_exceeded"=.
  - (Bonus) Worker template parsing: a worker template with =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= and =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS: 86400= parses cleanly and the values flow to WorkerView.

- [ ] *No worker template overrides ship in this commit* — built-in defaults (600 stall / 14400 max_run_duration) apply universally to architector + claude-tmux + codex-acp + all others. Per-worker overrides can be added in a follow-up if/when production behavior reveals a need.

- [ ] *Gates clean*:
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= (the new tests live here).
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= (existing tmux tests minus the removed turn-timeout cases stay green).
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures= (org-parser-compat with the new optional properties).
  - =cargo test --workspace=.
  - =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=.
  - =cargo fmt --check=.
  - =git diff --check=.
  - MSRV 1.78 honoured.

** Notes
- *Why this shape*: per-turn driver timeouts conflate "the driver hasn't heard from the worker in N seconds" with "the worker has been running for N seconds". The first is genuine stall (worker process is wedged or claude crashed); the second is normal claude behavior under extended thinking. Separating them into stall_detector (resets on events) + max_run_duration (absolute wall-clock) gives the right semantics for both.
- *Why universal across drivers*: stall_detector watches driver_events at the supervisor layer. All drivers emit driver_events. So the stall mechanism applies uniformly to tmux + acp-stdio + cursor-stream-json. No per-driver duplication.
- *Why don't we ship per-worker overrides*: the built-in defaults (600s stall, 4h backstop) work for everything we run today: codex-acp dispatches usually 5-15 min, claude-tmux on architector is bound by claude's own context window, etc. The override mechanism EXISTS in the schema (=:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:= and =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS:= are parsed); we just don't set values on shipped workers. Operators can set them per-worker if/when a real need surfaces.
- *Cross-task interaction*: blocks TASK-ENXWA retry. With the driver timeout removed and the stall_detector resetting on each pane-redraw driver_event (which claude emits ~1s during active work), TASK-ENXWA architector will no longer abort spuriously.
- *Babysitter LLM separate concern*: the existing =babysitter-stall-detector= worker (currently broken because it routes via claude-acp / simulated:true per TASK-TGGAJ) is NOT touched here. When TASK-TGGAJ is un-deferred or the babysitter is migrated to claude-tmux/codex, it'll resume its slow-quality-judging role. Until then the code stall_detector + max_run_duration ceiling cover the safety floor.
- *Schema impact*: the org-parser-compat fixtures MUST stay green after both new optional properties are added. Existing worker templates without the new properties continue to parse and use built-in defaults.
- *Worker-name note*: dispatched via =implementer-codex-acp= (post-rename of the legacy =implementer-codex-stdio=; the alias from commit =0f30ec1= keeps both names resolving).

** Evidence
- *Implementer*: =implementer-codex-acp= dispatched [2026-05-28 Thu 12:16:46]; codex commit =ea9ff8d= on branch =task-100-impl= (~37 min wall). Merged no-ff. +428/-94 LOC across 7 files (tmux.rs net -73 LOC as expected for the deletion-heavy half).
- *Driver per-turn timeout REMOVED*: =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= no longer carries =TmuxTuiConfig.turn_timeout= field, accessor, or capture-loop timer branch. The missing-marker timeout test from line ~1390 is gone. The pane-capture loop and session-lifecycle paths stay.
- *Stall detector ADDED*: per-run =last_driver_event_at: Instant= on =RunRecord=, initialized at acquire, updated on every observed =DriverEvent= (both =acquire= and =continuation= drain paths covered). When =Instant::now() - last_driver_event_at > stall_timeout=, the run is released via =release(..., ReleaseOutcome::Failed)= with =reason: "stall_timeout_exceeded"= and the threshold+elapsed logged. Default 600s; per-worker override via =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:=.
- *Max-run-duration ceiling ADDED*: per-run =run_started_at: Instant= on =RunRecord=. When =Instant::now() - run_started_at > max_run_duration=, release with =reason: "max_run_duration_exceeded"=. Default 14400s (4h); per-worker override via =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS:=.
- *Periodic-check site*: =spawn_run_timeout_monitor()= starts one =tokio::time::interval= in =Supervisor::with_summarizer= and calls =release_first_timed_out_run()= each tick. Single watcher task handles all runs; no per-run task spawn.
- *Worker schema*: =Worker.stall_timeout_secs= + =Worker.max_run_duration_secs= as =Option<u32>= seconds, parsed from =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:= and =:MAX_RUN_DURATION_SECS:= properties by =crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs= using the same OPTIONAL-integer idiom as =:CONTEXT_BUDGET:= / =:MAX_ITERATIONS:=.
- *Plumb-through*: =WorkerView= (content.rs) + =StageWorker= (api.rs) carry both fields; =spawn_worker_run= / acquire pass them into =AcquireRequest=; babysitter auto-spawn carries its own resolved worker values so the babysitter run gets its own timeouts independent of the implementer.
- *Regression tests (4)*: =supervisor::tests::stall_detector_releases_after_no_driver_events= (advance time, assert release with =reason: stall_timeout_exceeded=); =supervisor::tests::stall_detector_resets_on_driver_event= (events at sub-stall intervals, assert NO release; then stop, assert release fires); =supervisor::tests::max_run_duration_releases_even_with_driver_events= (frequent events, advance past max_run_duration, assert release with =reason: max_run_duration_exceeded=); =worker_parses_run_timeout_properties= (parse worker template with both properties, assert Worker fields set correctly). All pass; live log line from manager-side gate verification: =supervisor run timeout exceeded run_id=run-20260528T125444-... reason="max_run_duration_exceeded" threshold_secs=1 elapsed_secs=1=.
- *Manager-side gates on merged main (post-merge)*: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon ✓ (122+23+61 pass including all 4 new tests) | cargo test --workspace ✓ (421 pass) | cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓ | cargo fmt --check ✓ | git diff --check ✓ | MSRV 1.78 honoured (no post-1.78 std APIs).
- *Scope expansion*: codex flagged two extra mechanical-field-addition sites it had to touch beyond the original WRITE_SCOPE: =crates/orgasmic-core/src/eligibility.rs= (struct field passthrough) + =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs= (test fixture updates for new struct fields). WRITE_SCOPE property updated to reflect.
- *Manager-rescue notes*: same pattern as TASK-ZG01H — codex pid 71845 entered sleep-state (STAT=SN) after writing last.txt + committing, waiting on ACP session/end from the daemon. Daemon (pid 26417) climbed to 809.6% CPU during the close window — same transient post-dispatch hot path noted in the TASK-ZG01H reviewer pass evidence (separate from the babysitter-lease retry which TASK-ZG01H fixed). Manager killed both; daemon will be rebuilt + restarted to pick up the new supervisor binary.
- *Daemon binary status*: =$ORGASMIC_HOME/bin/orgasmic= still points at the pre-fix binary. **Manager will rebuild + restart immediately after this commit** so the new stall_detector + max_run_duration logic + driver timer removal are all active.
- *Unblocks*: TASK-ENXWA retry. With the driver per-turn timer gone and the stall_detector resetting on each pane-redraw driver_event (which claude emits ~1s during active work), architector authoring of substantive scope will no longer abort spuriously. Default 600s stall window + 4h backstop are both generous enough for the architecture authoring workload.
- *Acceptance Criteria*: 9 of 9 ticked.
- *Reviewer pass [2026-05-28 Thu 16:09] verdict =has-issues, no-blocks-ship= (0H/1M/1L)*: reviewer-codex-acp pid 91403, run-20260528T130420 (~4min43s wall); report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-100-reviewer-report.md=. F-1 MEDIUM (correctness): TOCTOU race in =release_first_timed_out_run= — candidate selection drops the inner lock before release(); a driver_event drain can refresh =last_driver_event_at= in that gap, yet the monitor still releases with =stall_timeout_exceeded=. Filed as TASK-HCFBT.1 (P2 fix-subtask). F-2 LOW (test): regression tests bypass =tokio::time::pause()=/=advance()= + the spawned monitor task, calling =release_first_timed_out_run()= helper directly with manually backdated timestamps; a broken monitor cadence could pass these tests. Filed as TASK-HCFBT.2 (P3 fix-subtask). Live-readiness: PARTIAL — F-1 fix needed before treating the supervisor watchdog as fully robust.

* DONE TASK-HCFBT.1 fix-subtask: stall_detector TOCTOU race — release decision uses stale snapshot :daemon:supervisor:fix:correctness:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HCFBT.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-1 MEDIUM from the TASK-HCFBT review (report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-100-reviewer-report.md=):

In =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs::release_first_timed_out_run= (~line 1174), the function picks a timeout candidate under the =self.inner= lock, then DROPS the lock before calling =self.release(...)=. Between the drop and the release, a =DriverEvent= drain can acquire the same lock and refresh =last_driver_event_at=. But the monitor still proceeds with the release using the stale snapshot — issuing =reason: "stall_timeout_exceeded"= for a run that is, by the time of release, no longer stalled.

Same shape applies to =max_run_duration_exceeded= path (although less likely to be racy because =run_started_at= is set once at acquire and not updated by events).

This weakens the "resets on driver_event" guarantee at the timeout boundary. Symptom in production: occasional spurious stall releases on busy runs that JUST resumed activity.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Under the =inner= lock immediately before =release()=, re-check =timed_out_run(run_id, rec, now)= for the same =run_id= and the same =reason=. If the run no longer exceeds the threshold (=last_driver_event_at= was just updated), abort the release; the next monitor tick will reconsider.
- [ ] (Alternative shape, possibly cleaner): make the release conditional via the same critical section that selects the candidate — a helper that performs check + remove + release decision atomically under the lock.
- [ ] Update or add a regression test that injects a driver_event after candidate selection but before release; assert no release fires.
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Smallest viable shape*: revalidate under the lock immediately before release. ~5-10 LOC change in =release_first_timed_out_run=.
- *Cross-task interaction*: TASK-ZG01H.1 (babysitter give-up no in-daemon recovery) is also a small =supervisor.rs= change. Both touch the same file but different functions — parallel-eligible but cleaner sequentially to avoid same-file merge resolution.

** Evidence
- *Implementer*: =implementer-codex-acp= via bundled supervisor cluster brief. Codex commit =59af464= ("supervisor.rs cluster: fix TOCTOU race + babysitter recovery + virtual-time test"). Merged no-ff as =5b11b4d=.
- *Fix shape*: =release_first_timed_out_run= now revalidates the same =run_id= and same =reason= under the =inner= lock immediately before calling =release()=. If the candidate is no longer timed out (a =DriverEvent= drain refreshed =last_driver_event_at= in the gap), or if the timeout-reason changed, abort the release and let the next monitor tick reconsider.
- *Regression test*: =supervisor::tests::stall_detector_revalidates_after_driver_event_race= (visible in cargo test output as ✓ ok) injects a driver_event into the gap between candidate selection and release; asserts no spurious release fires.
- *Gates on merged main =5b11b4d=*: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon ✓ (125 pass, including the new revalidation test) | cargo test --workspace ✓ (424 pass) | clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ✓ | fmt ✓ | diff-check ✓ | MSRV 1.78.
- *Bundled with*: TASK-ZG01H.1 (babysitter give-up recovery, LOW) and TASK-HCFBT.2 (virtual-time integration test, LOW). All three in one commit since they touch different functions in the same file.

* DONE TASK-HCFBT.2 fix-subtask: stall_detector/max_run_duration tests should exercise the spawned monitor loop via tokio::time::pause :daemon:supervisor:fix:test:low:

** Description
Reviewer F-2 LOW from the TASK-HCFBT review:

The four regression tests for the new stall_detector + max_run_duration mechanisms avoid real sleeps (good), but they manually backdate timestamps via the =age_run()= helper and call =sup.release_first_timed_out_run()= directly. They do NOT use =tokio::time::pause()= / =advance()=, and they do NOT exercise the =spawn_run_timeout_monitor()= interval task.

A broken monitor (wrong tick cadence, broken spawn, dropped task handle) would pass these tests as long as the private helper still works in isolation.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add at least one virtual-time test that:
  - Calls =Supervisor::with_summarizer= (which spawns the monitor task).
  - =tokio::time::pause()= to enter virtual time.
  - Acquires a run with a short stall_timeout (e.g., 100ms).
  - =tokio::time::advance(Duration::from_millis(150))= past the threshold.
  - Observes release via the monitor task — by polling supervisor state or by checking that the run is removed.
- [ ] Optionally add a sibling test for the max_run_duration path on the same virtual-time idiom.
- [ ] Keep the existing 4 tests as fast helper-targeted unit tests; the new virtual-time test(s) are integration-level.
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Smallest viable shape*: one new virtual-time test; existing tests stay.
- *Why not delete the existing tests*: they're still useful as fast helper checks. The new test adds end-to-end coverage of the monitor task without replacing the helper checks.
- *Cross-task interaction*: parallel-eligible with TASK-HCFBT.1 but cleaner sequentially.

** Evidence
- *Implementer*: =implementer-codex-acp= via bundled supervisor cluster brief. Codex commit =59af464=. Merged no-ff as =5b11b4d=.
- *Test added*: =#[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]= virtual-time integration test that exercises the spawned =spawn_run_timeout_monitor()= task end-to-end. Pauses tokio time, advances past the configured stall_timeout, observes release via the monitor task (not by calling the private helper directly).
- *Cargo.toml change*: added =[dev-dependencies] tokio = { workspace = true, features = ["test-util"] }= so =tokio::time::pause()= / =tokio::time::advance()= / =#[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]= compile under default =cargo test=. Workspace =tokio= already had =features = ["full"]=; feature unification gives the test build both =full= and =test-util= without polluting non-test builds.
- *Existing tests kept*: the prior 4 fast helper-targeted unit tests (=stall_detector_releases_after_no_driver_events=, =stall_detector_resets_on_driver_event=, =max_run_duration_releases_even_with_driver_events=, =worker_parses_run_timeout_properties=) all still pass. New virtual-time test is additive.
- *Gates on merged main =5b11b4d=*: same as TASK-HCFBT.1 evidence (one cluster commit covers all three fix-subtasks).
- *Bundled with*: TASK-ZG01H.1 + TASK-HCFBT.1.

:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/glossary.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/glossary.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/glossary.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures
:END:

** Description
Reviewer F-2 LOW from the TASK-ENXWA review (report at =/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-086-reviewer-report.md=):

TASK-ENXWA introduced 35 new architecture leaves with their own vocabulary (e.g., =arch_A53QX.1 run lease supervisor=). Several repeated leaf-level terms are NOT canonical glossary entries:

- =run lease= — appears across arch_A53QX leaves (supervisor lease ownership semantics).
- =babysitter= / =babysitter auto-spawner= — recurring across arch_A53QX + arch_R3EPE.
- =materialized read index= / =materialized index= — appears in arch_PCSQE.

=.orgasmic/glossary.org= already has adjacent entries: =worker-driver=, =manager-agent=, =boot-reconciliation=, =semantic-hash=. The new terms should join them as canonical entries with concise definitions + cross-references to their primary =arch_NNN.M= leaf.

This was outside TASK-ENXWA's WRITE_SCOPE (architect couldn't touch glossary), hence filed as a follow-up.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add canonical glossary entries to =.orgasmic/glossary.org= for at minimum: =run lease=, =babysitter= (with sub-mention of =babysitter auto-spawner=), =materialized index= (with sub-mention of =materialized read index=).
- [ ] Each entry: kebab-case ID matching the canonical heading style of existing entries; concise one-or-two-sentence definition; cross-reference link to the primary =arch_NNN.M= leaf.
- [ ] Scan TASK-ENXWA's architecture.org for any OTHER repeated leaf-level term not yet in glossary; add it too if it's clearly project vocabulary (not just incidental phrasing).
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures green; clippy/fmt/diff-check clean (no .rs touched so most gates n/a).

** Notes
- *Cross-task interaction*: TASK-ENXWA left an extra (incorrect) =arch_WZFAX.1 → file:project= READS edge that was fixed by the manager in the same commit that closes the reviewer pass (so the F-1 MEDIUM does NOT need a separate fix-subtask). Only F-2 (glossary) is captured here.
- *Out of scope*: decisions.org (no new decisions surfaced); ADRs (none warranted by this scope).

** Evidence
- *Closed*: [2026-06-05 Fri] by glossarist.
- *Terms added to =.orgasmic/glossary.org=* (4 canonical entries, kebab-case IDs, each with a =:PRIMARY_LEAF:= cross-reference):
  - =term_DN08P= Run lease → arch_A53QX.1. Supervisor's at-most-one-active-run-per-(task_id, kind) claim, taken on acquire / surrendered on release, guarded by (run_id, runtime_id, boot_id) ownership.
  - =term_E0TAB= Babysitter → arch_A53QX.5. Restricted companion run beside a live implementer; closed tool set (poke/restart/escalate/record-finding), summarized session chunks, =sessions/<run-id>.babysitter.jsonl=. Folds in =babysitter auto-spawner=.
  - =term_H29TQ= Materialized index → arch_C87Z9.4. Daemon's in-memory read projection (board/projects/tasks/activity/graph/markers), rebuilt at boot + watcher-refreshed, last-good-on-parse-failure. Folds in =materialized read index=.
  - =term_RWJ36= Recovery classification → arch_Z3Z3V.1. Boot-reconciliation label per prior run: live-reattached / interrupted / terminal no-op / ambiguous-question. (The recurring recovered-run leaf vocabulary the AC's scan-for-others bullet asked for.)
- *Definitions grounded in code*, not guessed: =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs= module-level invariant docs (lease table, babysitter coalescing + closed =BabysitterTool::ALL= set) and =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs= (in-memory projection, last-good-on-parse-failure), plus the arch_A53QX.1 / arch_A53QX.5 / arch_C87Z9.4 / arch_Z3Z3V.1 leaves.
- *Gates*: =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures= → 29 passed, 0 failed (incl. =parses_real_glossary=); log =/tmp/t086-test.log=. =git diff --check= clean. No =.rs= touched, so clippy/fmt n/a.

* DONE TASK-HCFBT.3 codex-acp adapter should emit heartbeat driver_events during long-running subprocess tool calls :drivers:codex:acp:reliability:heartbeat:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HCFBT.3
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/codex.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/codex.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/codex.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:END:

** Description
Live dogfood discovery [2026-05-28 Thu 17:22:49]: TASK-HCFBT v2's new stall_detector misfired on a codex-acp dispatch. Forensics on the killed run (=run-20260528T134411-...=):

- Run acquired at 13:44:11Z.
- Codex was running =cargo test --workspace= which produced stdout that codex buffered for ~23 minutes.
- ZERO driver_events arrived at the daemon during that buffer window.
- stall_detector saw =Instant::now() - last_driver_event_at > 600s= at the 10-min mark.
- Supervisor released with =stall_timeout_exceeded= → SIGKILL.
- Right before SIGKILL, codex flushed its buffered stdout in a 8-event burst (seq 677-684 ALL at 14:22:22.254Z within milliseconds).

So codex WAS alive and working the whole time. The supervisor's mechanism IS correct (no driver_events arrived). The bug is that codex-acp doesn't emit anything during long-running subprocess tool calls — it batches stdout flushes.

claude-tmux doesn't have this problem because tmux pane redraws happen continuously during ALL claude activity (every ~1s — the "↓ Nk tokens" / elapsed-time counter in the pane footer alone updates).

**Pragmatic mitigation already shipped (in the same commit that files this task)**: =shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org= + =shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org= got =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= overrides (2h), using TASK-HCFBT v2's per-worker plumbing. This lets codex-acp dispatches run substantive workloads without spurious stall. But 2h is a band-aid — codex SHOULD emit heartbeats.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] codex-acp adapter (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/codex.rs= and/or =modes/acp_stdio.rs=) emits a heartbeat-style =driver_event= every ~30s while waiting for subprocess output to flush. The heartbeat should be a no-op semantically (no chunk content, just a "still alive" signal) but it MUST update =last_driver_event_at= on the supervisor's side so the stall_detector doesn't fire.
- [ ] Heartbeats don't pollute the run record's substantive view (i.e., they're filtered out of session JSONL stream that the manager reads for evidence, OR they have a distinct =type= that the manager UI can filter).
- [ ] Regression test: a unit test that constructs the codex-acp adapter, simulates a tool call taking >120s with no intermediate output, and asserts heartbeats are emitted at the expected cadence.
- [ ] After this lands, the =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= overrides on codex-acp worker templates can be REMOVED (back to the built-in 600s default that works for claude-tmux). File TASK-HCFBT.4 to do that cleanup once heartbeats are proven.
- [ ] cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers green; cargo test --workspace green; clippy + fmt + diff-check clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
- *Why a heartbeat is the right shape*: codex's ACP adapter already has an awareness of "I'm in a tool call, subprocess running". Emit a tiny periodic event from that state. Doesn't change codex's batching behavior for actual stdout content; just adds a liveness signal.
- *Heartbeat cadence*: 30s is a reasonable default. Too aggressive (5s) wastes overhead; too lazy (300s) doesn't actually help unless stall_timeout is bumped. With 30s heartbeats, a default 600s stall_timeout has 20 chances to reset.
- *Cross-task interaction*: parallel-eligible with the in-flight supervisor cluster (TASK-HCFBT.1 + TASK-ZG01H.1 + TASK-HCFBT.2) since this is a different crate (orgasmic-drivers, not orgasmic-daemon). After both land, the codex-acp worker overrides can be removed (file TASK-HCFBT.4).
- *Alternative considered + rejected*: subprocess-output forwarding (real-time stdout relay). Larger scope; affects all driver/harness pairs. Heartbeats are smaller + sufficient.

** Evidence
- *Closed*: [2026-06-05 Fri] by implementer-codex-acp.
- *Design — where the heartbeat lives*: the heartbeat is a new branch of the
  =run_acp_stdio= =tokio::select!= event loop in
  =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs=. That loop is the production
  event pump for the codex-acp path (=implementer-codex-acp= →
  =:DRIVER: acp-stdio= + =codex= harness; see =shipped/workers/=). It owns the
  session lifetime, so a =tokio::time::interval= branch inside the same select
  shares the loop task's lifetime exactly — when the loop ends (release /
  terminal event / transport EOF), the interval is dropped with everything
  else. No detached =JoinHandle=, so no leaked ticker (handle-drop pitfall
  avoided by construction: the ticker is a select arm, not a spawned task).
- *Event shape*: added =DriverEvent::Heartbeat { seq }= in
  =crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs=. Snake_case serde tag ⇒ =type:
  "heartbeat"=, distinct from all substantive events. It carries no content;
  its only job is to flow through the supervisor's event drain, which sets
  =rec.last_driver_event_at = Instant::now()= for *every* drained event
  (variant-agnostic, supervisor.rs ~L632) before any terminal-outcome
  inspection. So a heartbeat resets the stall detector without releasing the
  lease (=terminal_outcome_for_event(Heartbeat) == None=).
- *Start/stop + cadence semantics*: =tokio::time::interval= at 30s
  (=HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL=); first (t=0) tick consumed so the first real heartbeat
  is one interval into a quiet stretch. =MissedTickBehavior::Delay=. A
  =quiet_since_last_tick= flag is cleared whenever the loop emits a real event
  (command / stderr / transport events); a heartbeat fires only on a tick where
  no real event occurred since the previous tick — so heartbeats appear *only*
  during genuinely-silent stretches and never interleave with active output.
  Cadence is overridable per-session via an optional =heartbeat_interval_ms=
  driver-config field (positive int; defaults to 30s when absent) — a tuning
  knob and the hook the regression test uses to run at 60ms.
- *Substantive-view filtering (AC2)*: distinct =type: "heartbeat"= ⇒ naturally
  excluded from every substantive surface, which match on specific string tags
  with catch-alls (evidence distillation + run-summary in
  =orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs=, manager-readable text/tool views). Belt-and-
  suspenders: =update_babysitter_buffer= early-returns on =Heartbeat= so it
  cannot inflate the babysitter summary window/count;
  =SubprocessExitSummary::record= ignores it via its existing catch-all.
- *Tests*:
  - =crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/driver_modes.rs::acp_stdio_codex_emits_heartbeats_during_quiet_turn=
    — a real =sh= mock codex peer completes the JSON-RPC handshake, starts a
    turn, then goes silent until =turn/interrupt=. At a 60ms cadence the test
    asserts ≥3 monotonic-seq heartbeats arrive during the quiet window and
    nothing else; after =release()= it asserts NO heartbeat arrives post-close
    (only the terminal =RunComplete=) and a final settle window confirms the
    ticker stopped (no leaked timer). Covers AC3 (quiet turn → cadenced
    heartbeats) and the stop-on-session-end requirement.
  - =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs::heartbeat_is_non_terminal_so_drain_never_releases_on_it=
    and =::heartbeat_does_not_pollute_babysitter_summary_window= — supervisor-
    side contract: heartbeat is non-terminal (resets but never releases) and is
    a no-op in the babysitter summary, while a following real event records
    cleanly.
- *Gate results*:
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= → 73 (lib) + 23 (driver_modes) + 4/5
    (cursor_agent) pass; the one failure is
    =real_cursor_agent_stream_json_bridge_emits_events_and_releases= (TASK-AJZCK.2,
    pre-existing environmental: live =cursor-agent= auth timeout on this host).
    Log =/tmp/t100_3-drivers.log= + =/tmp/t100_3-dm.log=.
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= → 124 + 22 + 56 pass, 0 failed (incl. both
    new heartbeat unit tests). =/tmp/t100_3-daemon.log=.
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-core= → green. =/tmp/t100_3-core.log=.
  - =cargo test --workspace= → all suites green, 0 failed (the cursor smoke
    passed in this run). =/tmp/t100_3-workspace.log=.
  - =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= → clean.
    =/tmp/t100_3-clippy.log=. MSRV-1.78-safe (no post-1.78 std methods; used
    =Value::as_u64=, =tokio::time::interval=, plain combinators).
  - =cargo fmt --check= clean; =git diff --check= clean.
- *Files touched*: =crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs= (new =Heartbeat=
  variant); =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs= (heartbeat select
  arm + =heartbeat_interval= config plumbing through =AcpStdioRuntime=);
  =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs= (=update_babysitter_buffer= skip +
  2 unit tests); =crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/driver_modes.rs= (regression
  test).
- *Follow-up filed*: TASK-HCFBT.4 (P3) — remove the =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200=
  overrides on =implementer-codex-acp.org= + =reviewer-codex-acp.org= after a
  validated live codex-acp dispatch confirms heartbeats hold the 600s default.
  NOT done here because it needs a live-dispatch proof, not just unit tests.
- *Reviewer pass*: [2026-06-05 Fri] static review of =834038f= (diff
  =984afc7..834038f=), read-only, no rebuild (gates verified green by
  implementer). Verdict: SHIP. All five acceptance criteria met; AC4 correctly
  deferred to TASK-HCFBT.4. Verified the core contract end-to-end: heartbeat is a
  select arm of =run_acp_stdio= (shares loop lifetime, no detached handle);
  supervisor drain sets =last_driver_event_at= variant-agnostically
  (supervisor.rs:632) and =terminal_outcome_for_event(Heartbeat)=None= (resets,
  never releases); =max_run_duration= is computed from =run_started_at=
  (supervisor.rs:1934-1977) so the 4h ceiling is unaffected by heartbeats;
  child-death closes the transport pipe → =recv_json Ok(None)= → loop break →
  heartbeats stop promptly (acp_stdio.rs:484,645); substantive surfaces
  (api.rs evidence distillation L3313, run-summary terminal scan L5373,
  ManagerChatTranscript.tsx, ws.rs pass-through,
  =SubprocessExitSummary::record=) all ignore =type:"heartbeat"= — UI drops
  unknown types without throwing; the regression test non-vacuously proves ≥3
  monotonic heartbeats, post-release silence, and no leaked ticker. Findings
  (none blocking): (F1, medium, follow-up TASK-HCFBT.5) heartbeat gates liveness
  on transport EOF only — no =child.try_wait()= select arm, so a codex peer
  wedged at the OS level with its stdout fd still open would tick heartbeats
  indefinitely, degrading stall detection for that case from ~600s to the 4h
  =max_run_duration= backstop. (F2, low, TASK-HCFBT.5) the recovery
  mechanical-summary "driver events recorded" counter (api.rs:4751)
  blanket-counts every DriverEvent including heartbeats, over-reporting on long
  quiet runs. (F3, low/polish, TASK-HCFBT.5) =heartbeat_interval_ms= rejects 0
  but accepts 1ms (busy-spin); a documented floor would harden it. Shipped
  as-is; all three bundled into TASK-HCFBT.5.

:PROPERTIES:

:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/decisions.org crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/main.rs ui/src/components/notifications/NotificationBell.tsx ui/src/components/notifications/NotificationPopover.tsx ui/src/lib/api.ts ui/src/lib/types.ts README.md ui/README.md

** Description
Fix the recovery flow that surfaced when an older-daemon tmux Claude manager run was shown as interrupted and the UI's =Continue= button opened a placeholder shell. Recovery must be explicit, harness-aware, and auditable: reattach live tmux sessions first, resume/fork native sessions only when deterministic native metadata exists, and otherwise start a real recovery run with a staged prompt.

Before implementation, add one ADR-style decision to =.orgasmic/decisions.org= for recovery semantics. Use =archive/2026-06-04-run-recovery-run-dock-grill.md= as the accepted-answer source.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Add a concise recovery-semantics decision to =.orgasmic/decisions.org= tagged =:run-recovery:=.
- [X] Recovery classification attempts tmux attach proof for older-boot tmux sessions before marking them interrupted; a still-live tmux session exposes =reattach_tmux= as the first recovery action.
- [X] Reattach rehydrates the original run into supervisor state with the original =run_id= and =runtime_id=, appends =reattach= lifecycle metadata to the original orgasmic session JSONL, and resumes event drain into that same file.
- [X] Lease conflicts block automatic reattach and return a structured conflict with enough data for UI actions such as open active run / stop active run / start recovery run anyway.
- [X] Add typed session lifecycle events for native runtime metadata, prompt drafts, and composer sends.
- [X] All future tmux launches record harness-aware native runtime metadata. Claude gets deterministic native session id/path and exact launch/resume argv; other harnesses store only real launch metadata until their native session semantics are known.
- [X] Recovery action resolution is backend-owned, ordered, harness-aware, and embedded in recovered run responses from =/recovery/status= and =/runs=.
- [X] Replace =POST /runs/:id/continue= with =POST /runs/:id/recover= using an explicit =action=; remove the old endpoint instead of aliasing it.
- [X] Rename CLI =orgasmic run continue <id>= to =orgasmic run recover <id> --action <action>=.
- [X] =resume_native_fork= is exposed only when recorded native metadata supports it; Claude recovery uses =claude --resume <old-session-id> --fork-session=.
- [X] =start_recovery_run= uses the original harness when possible; provider-choice recovery is deferred.
- [X] =start_recovery_run= and =resume_native_fork= launch idle with a persisted recovery prompt draft and require the user to send it manually from the UI.
- [X] Recovery prompts include prior run id, absolute previous orgasmic session JSONL path, a mechanical summary from session JSONL, current diff stat, and concrete instructions to inspect before acting.
- [X] The notification bell renders concrete backend action labels instead of generic =Continue= and executes through =/recover=.
- [X] Successful recovery removes the blocker from "Needs you" while preserving history in Activity/Runs.
- [X] Regression tests cover: older-boot tmux session still live prefers =reattach_tmux=; =start_recovery_run= never launches the placeholder =sh -lc "echo orgasmic run continuation; exec sh"=; no complex best-effort discovery is attempted for older runs without native metadata.
- [X] Verification passes: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=, =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers=, =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli=, =npm --prefix ui run build=, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=.

** Notes
- The original bug was not just missing Claude JSONL tracking. A live tmux session from a previous daemon boot can still exist and should be reattached before any continuation/fork path is offered.
- Do not add complex discovery over =~/.claude/projects= for legacy runs in this task. If no live tmux and no recorded native metadata exist, offer =start_recovery_run=.
- Recovery summaries in this task are mechanical only. Model-generated summaries are deferred.
- Provider-choice recovery from a right-click menu is deferred.

** Evidence
- *Live reattach dogfood*: [2026-06-05 Fri 13:03] after rebuilding + restarting
  the daemon on today's main, boot reconciliation classified the June-3
  older-boot tmux manager run as =reattached= ("driver tmux-tui proved live
  runtime handle") with =reattach_tmux= as the FIRST recovery action — the
  exact bug scenario from this task's Description, now resolving correctly
  (the old daemon showed it interrupted with a placeholder =Continue=). The
  two codex-appserver runs without native metadata correctly offered only
  =start_recovery_run=; terminal runs got zero actions. =POST
  /runs/:id/recover {"action":"reattach_tmux"}= returned 200 preserving the
  original =run_id=/=runtime_id= (daemon tx-20260605-orgasmic-0042), the run
  moved out of "Needs you" into =live_runs=, and fresh driver events resumed
  draining into the ORIGINAL session JSONL. Full contract verified live.
- Date: [2026-06-05 Fri]
- Gates (land-agent re-run, not trusted from prior agents):
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=: PASS (lib 119, dispatch_endpoint 22 [3 ignored], api/integration suite 56 [1 ignored]; 0 failed).
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers=: PASS (5 + 22; 0 failed).
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli=: PASS (all suites; 0 failed).
  - =npm --prefix ui run build=: PASS (vite build clean).
  - =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=: PASS (exit 0, no warnings).
  - =cargo fmt --check=: PASS.
  - =git diff --check=: PASS (no whitespace errors).
- Source verification: =/runs/:id/continue= route removed (no alias); =/runs/:id/recover= present; CLI =run recover --action=; placeholder =echo orgasmic run continuation; exec sh= absent from src; lifecycle variants Reattach/NativeRuntime/PromptDraft/ComposerSend present; recovery action ordering reattach_tmux -> resume_native_fork -> start_recovery_run present; integration regression test drives =/recover= with start_recovery_run + prior-run prompt assertion.
- Reviewer verdict: SHIP. Findings: High 0 / Medium 0 / Low 4 (all non-blocking cosmetic/greenfield-cleanup: dead supervisor.acquire_continuation path now test-only; recovery_target_for duplicate match arms; driver_modes.rs test edit outside literal WRITE_SCOPE but forced by new native_runtime field; PRODUCES listed ws.rs + NotificationPopover.tsx which were not touched). No fix pass required; none promoted to follow-up.
- Deferred (per task Notes, intentional): model-generated recovery summaries; provider-choice recovery menu; ~/.claude/projects discovery for legacy runs without native metadata.
- Merge commit recorded post-commit (commit subject "TASK-T6AV7: run recovery semantics — tmux reattach, native runtime metadata, explicit /recover actions").

** Description
Replace the manager-only bottom popover with a general Run Dock. Manager remains a special tab, but worker runs can be opened explicitly from recovery actions, a =Running Agents= menu, task badges, and run history. The same live transcript/terminal/composer surface must serve manager and worker runs.

Before implementation, add one ADR-style decision to =.orgasmic/decisions.org= for the Run Dock UX/model. Use =archive/2026-06-04-run-recovery-run-dock-grill.md= as the accepted-answer source.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Add a concise Run Dock decision to =.orgasmic/decisions.org= tagged =:ui-manager:run-dock:=.
- [X] Replace =ManagerPopover= outright with =RunDock=; do not keep a compatibility wrapper.
- [X] Run Dock owns peek/workbench/focus sizing, tab selection, persisted open-tab state, and one-tab-per-run dedupe.
- [X] Manager is one special Run Dock tab; worker runs open as peer tabs using the same tmux/chat/composer renderers.
- [X] Add a =Running Agents= menu grouped into =Running=, =Recoverable=, and =Recent=.
- [X] =Running Agents > Recoverable= uses the same backend =recovery_actions= resolver data as the notification bell.
- [ ] Selecting a running agent attaches and opens a peek window with the live transcript.
- [X] Minimizing a peek window turns it into a Run Dock tab; closing a tab detaches the UI only and does not stop/release the underlying run.
- [X] Open Run Dock tabs persist in localStorage by run ID and are validated against backend state on load.
- [X] =Recent= defaults to current project; global toggle/search is deferred.
- [X] Task list/kanban rows and the task modal show running/recoverable agent badges, visually separated.
- [X] A task agent badge opens immediately when exactly one relevant run exists and opens a small grouped menu when multiple relevant runs exist.
- [X] Recovery results from =/runs/:id/recover= open the right surface: manager recovery selects the Manager tab; worker recovery opens a worker run tab/peek.
- [X] Staged prompt drafts returned by recovery are shown in the shared composer and require explicit user send.
- [X] Shared composer uses provider-neutral copy such as =Send to agent=, not =Send a message to Claude=.
- [X] All composer sends go through the shared recording path established by TASK-T6AV7.
- [X] Run Dock tab labels prioritize readable role/task/provider/transport labels such as =Manager · Claude tmux= or =TASK-NN3HB · Codex=; raw run IDs are secondary detail or tooltip.
- [ ] Browser verification covers desktop and mobile: Running Agents menu, attach/minimize/close, task badges, recovered worker open, manager recovery open, tab restoration after reload, no text overlap, and tmux pane rendering.
- [X] Verification passes: =npm --prefix ui run build=, focused component/unit tests if present, Playwright/browser smoke for the Run Dock flows, =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= if backend response shapes are touched, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=.

** Notes
- Do not auto-open tabs for every active worker. Only explicit user action and recovery actions open worker tabs.
- Stop/release remains a separate explicit action, not tab close.
- Provider-choice recovery menu is deferred.
- Keep cards to genuine repeated items only; the dock itself is an app shell surface, not a card inside a card.

** Evidence
- *Live browser smoke*: [2026-06-05 Fri 13:10] manager-side via claude-in-chrome
  against the rebuilt daemon (today's commits live) + Vite dev :5180.
  VERIFIED on desktop (1496px): Run Dock workbench renders the REATTACHED
  June-3 manager run's live tmux pane (Claude TUI, live status-bar clock);
  tab label =Manager · Claude tmux= with run id as secondary detail;
  =Running Agents= menu shows all three groups (Running: =Manager · Claude
  tmux=; Recoverable: both interrupted codex runs with =Start Recovery Run=
  — same resolver data as the bell; Recent: terminal_noop history, current
  project); notification bell renders concrete =Start Recovery Run= labels
  (no generic Continue) and the reattached run correctly cleared from "Needs
  you"; dock tab persisted across route change AND full reload (localStorage
  + backend validation); composer is provider-neutral (=Send to agent=).
  VERIFIED on mobile (390px): dock + tmux pane re-wrap cleanly, no text
  overlap, composer intact. Zero console errors. NOT VERIFIED (no live
  worker run existed): task badges, recovered-worker open, worker peer tabs;
  minimize-to-tab inconclusive (workbench persisted; see peek-coercion
  criterion). Remaining browser items + the unmet literal-peek criterion
  carried as TASK-CJWT3.1.
- Closed: [2026-06-05 Fri]
- Gates (verified at land, not trusted from prior agents):
  - =npm --prefix ui run build= — PASS (tsc --noEmit + vite build, 2507 modules, built clean).
  - =cargo fmt --check= — PASS (exit 0).
  - =git diff --check= — PASS (no whitespace errors).
  - =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= — RUN because the fixer touched backend files (supervisor.rs send_input + ws.rs send_keys now emit composer_send lifecycle events). PASS.
- Reviewer verdict: has-issues — 0 high, 2 medium, 3 low.
  - Both medium findings were fixed by the fixer before land:
    - M1 (composer sends bypassed the TASK-T6AV7 recording path; =record_composer_send= had zero callers) — fixed by wiring =record_composer_send= into both =send_input= (chat path) and the WS =send_keys= handler (tmux/manager path). Both transports now land the same durable =composer_send= lifecycle event.
    - M2 (recoverable task-badge substring over-match: =session_path.includes(taskId)= matched TASK-E11X1 -> TASK-CJWT3.jsonl) — fixed with a token-boundary matcher (=sessionPathMatchesTask=) that still matches real =dispatch-<task>-...= / =auto-spawn-<task>-...= worker session filenames.
  - 3 low findings acknowledged, not blocking (NUL byte in ManagerWorkbench driverKey literal; restored non-live worker tab renders MissingRunPanel; worker "peek" coerced to workbench).
- Deferred / honestly unmet:

  - "Browser verification (desktop + mobile, Playwright/browser smoke)" — left unchecked. No browser/Playwright smoke was executed in the land pipeline; only the static gates (build/fmt/diff/cargo test) were run.

* DONE TASK-HCFBT.5 fix-subtask: heartbeat hardening — child.try_wait() liveness arm, recovery-counter heartbeat skip, interval floor :drivers:codex:acp:reliability:heartbeat:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HCFBT.5
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers; cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Bundles the three non-blocking findings from the TASK-HCFBT.3 reviewer pass
(verdict SHIP, =834038f=):

1. *(F1, medium)* The heartbeat select arm in =run_acp_stdio= gates liveness on
   transport EOF only. A codex peer wedged at the OS level with its stdout fd
   still open ticks heartbeats indefinitely, degrading stall detection for
   that case from ~600s to the 4h =max_run_duration= backstop. Add a
   =child.try_wait()= select/poll arm so a dead-or-exited child stops
   heartbeats even when the pipe lingers; regression test with a peer that
   exits while a sibling holds the pipe open.
2. *(F2, low)* The recovery mechanical-summary "driver events recorded"
   counter (api.rs:4751) blanket-counts heartbeats; skip =type=="heartbeat"=
   so long quiet runs don't over-report.
3. *(F3, low)* =heartbeat_interval_ms= rejects 0 but accepts 1ms (busy-spin
   risk); clamp to a documented floor (≥100ms) in the config plumbing.

** Evidence
F1 (child liveness): =run_acp_stdio='s heartbeat select arm now carries an
=if child_alive= guard and polls =child.try_wait()= on every tick *before*
emitting. =Ok(Some(_))= (exit) and =Err(_)= (status unknowable) both flip
=child_alive=false=, stopping heartbeats; the existing EOF / terminal /
command-close paths still reap the child via =child.wait()= at loop end (no new
release path). Regression test
=acp_stdio_heartbeats_stop_when_child_exits_with_pipe_held_open=: the mock peer
backgrounds a =sleep 5= that inherits stdout (pipe never EOFs) then exits its
main shell; the test asserts heartbeats flow while alive then go silent for
>5 cadence windows. Verified it TIMES OUT/FAILS when the =try_wait= gate is
stubbed to always-=Ok(None)=, and PASSES with the real gate. Cleanup reaps the
peer process group (=kill -- -PID=, pid==pgid via the existing =setsid=
=detach_subprocess=) with null stdio — no orphan leak (TASK-AJZCK.1 shape).

F2 (recovery counter): =build_recovery_prompt= (api.rs) now =continue=s on
=type=="heartbeat"= before incrementing =driver_events=, so the
"driver events recorded" mechanical-summary line no longer over-reports on long
quiet runs. Untyped driver events still count exactly as before (count moved out
of the =if let Some(ty)= so only heartbeats are excluded).

F3 (interval floor): added =HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_FLOOR= (100ms);
=heartbeat_interval()= clamps any configured =heartbeat_interval_ms= up to the
floor (=.max(FLOOR)=). The existing quiet-turn test moved 60ms→100ms (which
would otherwise have been clamped); both heartbeat tests now run at exactly the
production floor, so the floor is exercised, not bypassed.

Gates (all green): =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= (73 + 5 + 24 pass, incl. the
new F1 test and the env-flaky =real_cursor_agent_stream_json_bridge…=);
=cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= (124 + 22 + 56 pass); =cargo clippy --workspace
--all-targets -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= clean.

* DONE TASK-KMWZ6 Replace /manager/tick with slim /manager/launch; collapse manager doctrine onto the /orgasmic skill :manager:daemon:cli:shipped:run-dock:dec_WTM5Y:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-KMWZ6

** Description
Remove the daemon-compiled manager tick path and replace it with a slim spawn-only launch endpoint, per dec_WTM5Y. The manager now bootstraps itself via the system-wide =/orgasmic resume= skill, so the daemon compiles no manager prompt.

1. Delete =POST /manager/tick=, =post_manager_tick=, =compile_manager_orchestrator_prompt=, and =ManagerTickRequest/ManagerTickResponse= in =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs=.
2. Add =POST /manager/launch=. Request ={ project_id, mode, harness }=; resolve the project root from the projects registry (NOT =home.source()=) for the run cwd; =supervisor.acquire= an idle manager run (=worker_id "manager"=, no prompt compile, no auto-send); return ={ run_id }=. Record a =manager.launch= tx in place of =manager.tick=; update the recent-actions tx filter (api.rs ~1588) =manager.tick= → =manager.launch=.
3. Port the manager-exclusive nuggets from =orchestration_policy.org= (typed-pipeline stage authority; "no hidden shared context — pass prior-stage summaries / changed files / gates / findings in the next brief"; disjoint parallel write scopes) into =manager.org= Policies and/or the =manager-dispatch= convention. Then DELETE =manager_orchestrator.org= and =orchestration_policy.org=, and drop =manager.org='s Notes line referencing =manager_orchestrator=. Do NOT touch =dynamic_workflow_policy=, =verification_policy=, =security_policy= (shared by worker specs).
4. Remove the CLI =orgasmic manager tick= subcommand (=ManagerCmd::Tick=) and handler.
5. Update/remove tests referencing =manager.tick= / =manager_orchestrator=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No =/manager/tick= route, handler, request/response types, or =compile_manager_orchestrator_prompt= remain.
- [ ] =POST /manager/launch { project_id, mode, harness }= acquires an idle manager run on the given driver at the project root and returns ={ run_id }=; a =manager.launch= tx is recorded and shown in recent actions.
- [ ] =manager_orchestrator.org= + =orchestration_policy.org= deleted; their nuggets present in =manager.org= / =manager-dispatch=; the other three parts untouched and all worker specs still compile.
- [ ] CLI =manager tick= is gone.
- [X] Gates green: =cargo test --workspace=, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=, =cargo fmt --check=, =git diff --check=.

** Evidence
Landed to main in merge =c363f3c= (impl =aa72bb7=). Verified live on the running daemon (boot df75b65d): =POST /manager/tick= → 404 (route gone); =POST /manager/launch {project_id,mode,harness}= → 200 ={run_id}=, run acquired idle (=worker_id=manager=, =kind=implementer=, =task_id=manager.launch:orgasmic=) at the project root, =manager.launch= tx recorded (tx-...-0050/0052). =manager_orchestrator.org= + =orchestration_policy.org= deleted (290 deletions); nuggets ported into =manager.org= / =manager-dispatch=.
