** Description
Rewire the RunDock manager surface to the new launch model, per dec_WTM5Y. Endpoint contract (from TASK-KMWZ6): =POST /manager/launch { project_id, mode, harness } → { run_id }=.

1. Replace =postManagerTick= with =postManagerLaunch({ project_id, mode, harness })= → ={ run_id }= hitting =POST /manager/launch=; drop =ManagerTickRequest/Response= and =send_prompt=.
2. RunDock manager button (=handleManagerTick=): if a live manager run exists for the ACTIVE project, open/focus its Manager tab (no second spawn); else launch via the last-used =(mode, harness)= from =localStorage['orgasmic.manager.driver']= (fall back to first installed driver) and prefill the composer with the literal editable draft =/orgasmic resume= (human presses send — NEVER auto-sent).
3. =DriverChooser= + =DriverSwitcher= keep working but their prefilled draft becomes =/orgasmic resume= (set client-side; server no longer returns =draft_prompt=). Remove the "Tick default driver" link (=handleEmptyTick=).
4. One manager per project; managers for other projects coexist, filtered by the active project tab.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button opens the active project's live manager if present; else launches via last-used driver with =/orgasmic resume= prefilled as an editable, UNSENT composer draft.
- [ ] No =postManagerTick=, =send_prompt=, or "Tick default driver" remain; launcher + switcher prefill =/orgasmic resume=.
- [X] =npm --prefix ui run build= + =tsc --noEmit= pass; integration verified against the live endpoint (browser click pending — no browser-automation tool this session).

** Evidence
Landed to main in merge =63216e1= (impl =4c7bec6=). =handleOpenOrLaunchManager=: opens =activeManagerRun= (=managerRuns= filtered by =run.project_id === activeProjectId=, =managerRuns[0]=) when a live manager exists; else =postManagerLaunch({project_id,mode,harness})= via =resolveLaunchDriver= (last-used =localStorage['orgasmic.manager.driver']=, fallback first installed) then =openRun({draftPrompt: '/orgasmic resume'})= — editable, never auto-sent. "Tick default driver" link removed. Gates: =npm run build= + =tsc --noEmit= green. Live integration: a real =POST /manager/launch= run surfaced in =GET /manager/state= (the source for =activeManagerRun=), confirming the open-vs-launch branch fires on real data; run released afterward. NOTE: the actual in-browser button click is unverified this session (no browser-automation tool available) — pending a manual claude-in-chrome pass.

** Description
=real_claude_input_ready_smoke= (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/tmux.rs= ~1500) spawns a live =claude= binary and waits 10s for the TUI input field. It runs automatically whenever tmux+claude are on PATH and flakes on claude's per-folder trust prompt / timing (observed: failed in a /tmp worktree, passed on main; TASK-KMWZ6 gate run). Live tests that spawn a real external AI binary must NOT run in the default =cargo test --workspace=.

1. Gate =real_claude_input_ready_smoke= behind the existing opt-in =ORGASMIC_RUN_REAL_CLAUDE_SMOKE=1= (mirror the early-return skip + eprintln at =supervisor.rs:2759=). Reuse the existing var for consistency.
2. Audit the other =real_*= tests in tmux.rs: those spawning only =bash=/=sh= stubs are deterministic tmux-mechanics tests — LEAVE them (valuable coverage, not flaky). Only gate tests that spawn a real AI binary.
3. Confirm no other auto-running test spawns a live AI binary (cursor + daemon-claude smokes are already gated).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= with claude+tmux on PATH runs NO test that spawns a live AI binary; =real_claude_input_ready_smoke= skips unless =ORGASMIC_RUN_REAL_CLAUDE_SMOKE=1=.
- [ ] Deterministic bash/sh tmux-mechanics =real_tmux_*= tests still run (not gated).
- [X] Gates green; the previously-flaky smoke no longer appears in default runs.

** Evidence
Landed to main in merge =ea690fc= (impl =0fe8733=). =real_claude_input_ready_smoke= now early-returns unless =ORGASMIC_RUN_REAL_CLAUDE_SMOKE= is set (mirrors =supervisor.rs:2759= byte-for-byte in spirit); the six deterministic =bash=/=sh= =real_tmux_*= mechanics tests left ungated. Spot-check with the var UNSET: the test finishes in 0.03s (skips, no live claude spawn). Gates: =cargo test --workspace= 0 failed, =clippy -D warnings=, =fmt --check=, =git diff --check= all green.

* DONE TASK-EBVHJ Blocking "Connect to daemon" token gate for tokenless visitors :ui:auth:onboarding:run-dock:dec_WTM5Y:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-EBVHJ
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/ConnectGate.tsx ui/src/components/AppShell.tsx ui/src/components/ConnectionBanner.tsx
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/lib/transport.ts ui/src/lib/backend.ts ui/src/components/SettingsView.tsx ui/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx ui/src/components/ui/input.tsx ui/src/components/ui/button.tsx
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
A tokenless visitor (e.g. first open over the cloudflare tunnel) currently only sees a passive "check bearer token in Settings" banner, because bearer tokens live in the connection profile in PER-ORIGIN localStorage. Replace the dead-end with an active, blocking, self-validating prompt.

Accepted design (operator-decided): a *blocking modal gate* with *live validation*. On load with no token (=!activeProfile.token=) OR on any 401, a non-dismissible modal opens over a locked app: bearer-token input (masked), hint =Find it in $ORGASMIC_HOME/user/auth/token=, *Connect* button, *Advanced →* (opens full Settings). On Connect: =testConnection({ ...activeProfile, token })= — if =ok= persist via =updateProfile(activeProfile.id, { token })= and close; else inline error, stay open. Token saved ONLY through =updateProfile= (flows through the existing profile/transport pipeline; persists per-origin). Reuse the existing =setUnauthorizedHandler= 401 hook in =AppShell= (~180) and shadcn dialog/input/button. WRITE_SCOPE is =ui/= only.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tokenless load (or a 401) opens a blocking, non-dismissible modal; the app behind is not usable until connected.
- [ ] Valid token → =testConnection= passes → saved via =updateProfile= → modal closes → app loads; invalid token → inline error, modal stays open.
- [ ] Token input masked; no new localStorage key or parallel token store; transport/backend logic untouched (import only).
- [~] "Advanced →" opens full Settings (works with an active project; routes to =/board= for a projectless visitor — Settings is project-scoped, =pathForView= falls back to =/board= when =projectId= is null). Follow-up: TASK-K3Y04. =npm run build= + =tsc --noEmit= green.

** Evidence
Landed to main in merge =e26d906= (impl =ed66154=). New =ConnectGate.tsx= (122 lines): =<Dialog open={needsToken}>= with =showCloseButton={false}=, =onPointerDownOutside=/=onEscapeKeyDown= → =preventDefault= (non-dismissible); masked =type="password"= token input; =testConnection({...activeProfile, token})= validate → =updateProfile(id,{token})= persist → =onConnected()=. =AppShell= wires =needsToken = !activeProfile.token || Boolean(authError)= and =onConnected=() => setAuthError(null)=. =ConnectionBanner= auth-error branch removed (gate owns 401 UX; banner keeps ws-state). No new localStorage key — token flows through the existing profile pipeline (per-origin). Gates: =npm run build= + =tsc --noEmit= green on merged main. Caveat: in-browser click-through unverified this session (no browser-automation tool) — vite HMR serves it on :5180 / the tunnel. Known edge → TASK-K3Y04 ("Advanced →" projectless routing).

* DONE TASK-PT2DC Reconsider task lifecycle vs worker-dependent run sub-states :schema:state-machine:run-substate:design:griller:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-PT2DC
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           griller
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/glossary.org
:READ_SCOPE:       shipped/schema/state-machine.org shipped/project-scaffold/config.org shipped/workers crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/session.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs
:END:

** Description
Grill the relationship between the human-facing task lifecycle (=BACKLOG, EXPLORE, PLAN, IMPLEMENT, REVIEW, SHIP, DONE, BLOCKED, CANCELLED=) and the per-worker-kind run sub-state vocabularies in =shipped/schema/state-machine.org= (=RUN-SUBSTATE implementer/reviewer/planner/analyzer/babysitter/manager=). The current model has accumulated concrete incoherences worth resolving before they harden into the MVP. Griller-only: do NOT edit the schema or write code — produce Facts / Assumptions / Questions-with-recommended-answers / Accepted Decisions (record in =.orgasmic/decisions.org=) / Glossary updates. A downstream IMPLEMENT task will apply whatever is decided.

Concrete branches to grill (verify each against the code before asking):
1. *Missing griller vocabulary.* =config.org= maps =STAGE_WORKER_EXPLORE: griller=, yet there is NO =RUN-SUBSTATE griller= block. What sub-states does an EXPLORE/griller run report?
2. *Stale manager verb.* =RUN-SUBSTATE manager= still lists =agent.ticking= (verbs: idle, ticking, acting, paused) but =/manager/tick= was removed in dec_WTM5Y/TASK-KMWZ6 (replaced by =/manager/launch=). What should the manager vocabulary be now (e.g. launching/idle/acting/paused)?
3. *Manager runs as implementer.* =/manager/launch= acquires the manager with =RunKind::Implementer=, so a manager run emits IMPLEMENTER sub-states, never the =manager= vocabulary. Reconcile: does the manager need its own =RunKind=, or should the =manager= sub-state block be deleted?
4. *Stage/vocabulary asymmetry.* =analyzer=, =babysitter=, =manager= have sub-state vocabularies but no =STAGE_WORKER_*= mapping; =griller= has a stage but no vocabulary. Is the keying axis worker-KIND, lifecycle-STAGE, or both — and is that intentional?
5. *Overlap / shared base.* Every vocabulary starts =ready, claimed= and ends =blocked, cancelled=. Should there be a shared base sub-state set with per-kind deltas (mirroring =base_worker= EXTENDS for prompts) instead of fully-duplicated lists?
6. *Actor namespacing.* The =agent.= / =human.= / =ci.= prefix split — is it applied consistently, and what decides which actor owns a given verb (e.g. =human.review= vs =agent.reviewing=)?

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Each of the six branches is either resolved (recommended answer accepted) or explicitly deferred with a reason.
- [X] Meaningful accepted decisions recorded as =* dec_NNN <title> :<topic>:= records in =.orgasmic/decisions.org= (Context/Decision/Consequences; no =:STATUS:=).
- [X] Any new/renamed terms (e.g. a settled name for the manager's post-tick verb) captured in =.orgasmic/glossary.org=.
- [X] Output names the follow-up IMPLEMENT scope (which =RUN-SUBSTATE= blocks + code in =supervisor.rs=/=session.rs= a later task must change) without drafting it.

** Evidence
Grilled live with the operator [2026-06-10 Wed] (manager-implementer mode, dec_ZKRQW). Code verification surfaced a deeper fact than the six branches: the entire RUN-SUBSTATE system is dead schema — =RunRecord.sub_state= is never assigned anywhere (=supervisor.rs= init sites 712/923/1200; =TransitionState= only formats a display headline at 2101), and =RunSubState::new= (=session.rs:81=) validates namespace shape only, never the vocabularies. =RunKind= has just two variants (Implementer, Babysitter — =trait.rs:37=); its real job is babysitter tool-gating, so five of six vocabularies were unreachable regardless.

Branch outcomes (all six resolved, none deferred):
1. Missing griller vocabulary — dissolved: vocabularies become base+delta keyed on performer; griller needs no own block (it already borrowed analyzer verbs via =:APPLICABLE_STATES:=). dec_P7EY8.
2. Stale manager verb — manager block deleted; manager's active verb is =manager.working= under the shared base. dec_P7EY8.
3. Manager runs as implementer — accepted as correct: no =RunKind::Manager=; RunKind stays the tool-policy axis; manager identity stays the =manager.*= namespace (TASK-KC92G UI already keys on it). dec_P7EY8.
4. Stage/vocabulary asymmetry — axis declared: vocabularies key on worker KIND (performer); lifecycle is task-side. Operator went further: lifecycle itself shrinks to Backlog/Todo/In Progress/In Review/Done/Cancelled (EXPLORE/PLAN/IMPLEMENT collapse into In Progress; SHIP dropped, Done = merged+reviewer-passed; BLOCKED becomes badge = sub-state + =:BLOCKED_BY:=). =STAGE_WORKER_*= replaced by an ordered worker pipeline. dec_WV1ZH + dec_DEH2T.
5. Overlap/shared base — shared minimal base {working, done, blocked, cancelled} + verdict deltas (reviewer/human: approved, requested_changes); no ready/claimed ("ready" rejected by operator; Todo column covers readiness, acquisition covers claiming). dec_P7EY8.
6. Actor namespacing — performer-owns-verb rule; namespace generalizes from agent/human/ci to <worker-kind>/human/ci; =human.escalating= → performer-owned; =ci.= kept reserved. dec_P7EY8.

Recorded: dec_WV1ZH/068/069 in =.orgasmic/decisions.org=; glossary terms =run-substate= + =worker-pipeline=. Follow-up IMPLEMENT scope (named, not drafted): =shipped/schema/state-machine.org= v3 rewrite (new keywords + transitions, base+delta sub-state section, delete all six per-kind blocks); =shipped/skills/orgasmic/scaffold/config.org= =STAGE_WORKER_*= → pipeline; heading-keyword migration across =.orgasmic/tasks/sprint.org= + parsers (=orgasmic-core= schema.rs/projects.rs/org.rs, daemon index.rs/watcher.rs/api.rs, cli manager.rs) + kanban UI; =session.rs= performer-namespace validation; =supervisor.rs= wire =TransitionState= → =rec.sub_state= (first real consumer); =shipped/workers/*.org= =:APPLICABLE_STATES:= alignment.

unit test covers SettingsView project-config splicing (the org round-trip that
made v2 saves destructive before the v3 editor landed) or the kanban's

splice/bucket logic into pure testable functions if currently inline, and
cover both behaviors. OFF-LIMITS: ui/src/components/manager/**, ui/src/lib/api.ts,
ui/src/hooks/** (operator has uncommitted work in the manager surface).

** Acceptance Criteria

* DONE TASK-CJXKM Root-cause main-checkout-only parallel flake of cli dispatch stub-codex test :cli:tests:flake:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:

:PRIORITY:         P4
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs .orgasmic/gotchas.org

** Description
Documented in TASK-VXB0T.1 Evidence: =manager_dispatch_status_close_done_with_stub_codex=
(crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs) fails when run in the parallel test
harness from THIS main checkout only; passes single-threaded, passes in fresh
worktrees, both code versions. Suspects: PATH/stub-binary collision between
parallel boots, shared tmp/home state keyed off the checkout path, port
contention. Root-cause it, fix the isolation, and document the mechanism (one
line in .orgasmic/gotchas.org is in scope if it generalizes). Blocked behind
TASK-K5RG2 (same files).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mechanism identified and written into the test as a comment (or gotchas
  entry if it generalizes beyond this test).
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= green in parallel mode from this checkout,
  10/10 consecutive runs (redirect output to a file, never pipe).
- [ ] Gates: clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- Worker: implementer-claude-rmux run-20260610T210146 (model claude-haiku-4-5,
  dispatch tx-0105, implementer.done tx-0107). Worker commit =a022fd0=, merged
  --no-ff as =27e05ea= on main.
- Mechanism identified: parallel tests from the main checkout lose
  =ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL= when sub-processes inherit the environment. Without it,
  the CLI falls back to =home.config()= which previously lacked a port override,
  causing silent connection to the real daemon on port 4848. From a worktree the
  real daemon is unreachable on 4848, so the fallback cleanly errors; from main
  it silently succeeds against the wrong daemon, corrupting the test.
- Fix: =boot()= in tests/dispatch.rs now writes =bind_port: 65533= to the test
  home config before starting the test daemon. Env-var loss now fails obviously
  (can't reach 65533) rather than silently hitting prod. Mechanism documented in
  =.orgasmic/gotchas.org=. 2 files, 26 insertions.
- Gates (from worktree): cargo test -p orgasmic-cli green, 13 dispatch tests
  pass 10/10 parallel. Clippy workspace blocked by pre-existing daemon
  =type_complexity= error (operator's =ab7bb93= surface, handoff-documented, not
  TASK-CJXKM scope); clippy on changed file/crate clean. fmt on changed file clean.
- Reviewer: reviewer-claude-rmux run-20260610T213649 (model claude-haiku-4-5,
  tx-0108). Verdict =has-issues, no-blocks-ship=. F-1 (MEDIUM): review range
  includes manager housekeeping commits — not TASK-CJXKM code, no fix needed.
  F-2 (MEDIUM): port 65533 not guaranteed free — documented tradeoff, no fix
  needed. F-7 (HIGH, pre-existing): UI build failure blocks test execution
  (ab7bb93 surface, not in scope). No actionable implementer items →
  closed DONE. Reviewer.done tx-0109.

* DONE TASK-WTJ5V CLI dispatch race: 10s HTTP timeout vs slow rmux spawn — worktree deleted under live worker, zombie daemon lease, leftover branch :cli:dispatch:rmux:reliability:bug:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-WTJ5V
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_client.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-K5RG2 TASK-CJXKM
:END:

** Description
Hit live [2026-06-10] dispatching TASK-TGGAJ via =orgasmic manager dispatch=
(rmux + claude). The CLI HTTP client has a hardcoded 10s timeout
(=daemon_client.rs= =from_home=, ~line 44) while a real rmux+claude dispatch
takes longer server-side (session spawn + claude TUI startup + 10s
=input_ready_timeout= wait + brief paste). Failure cascade observed:

1. CLI times out ("error sending request"), reports failure, and removes the
   worktree it created — while the daemon proceeds and spawns a real claude
   whose cwd has just been deleted (run-20260610T183047). The worker is doomed
   but holds the (task, kind) supervisor lease.
2. The zombie lease then 409s every redispatch ("another dispatch is already
   active") while =GET /runs/:id= 404s and =POST /runs/:id/release= refuses
   ("active run ... not found" pattern) — no API path clears it; only a daemon
   restart does (observed twice: run-...T182512, run-...T183047).
3. The CLI's failure cleanup removes the worktree but NOT the branch, so the
   next attempt dies on "a branch named 'task-NNN-impl' already exists".

Workaround used today: dispatch directly via =curl= against
=/projects/:id/tasks/:task/dispatch= with a long timeout and a pre-created
worktree — which also skips the CLI-side lifecycle stage flips (had to apply
BACKLOG->IN_PROGRESS by hand).

UPDATE [2026-06-10 22:35]: operator commit =ab7bb93= addressed most of this —
Fix 2 (non-blocking acquire: drivers send Ready at spawn, paste moves off the
HTTP path; CLI dispatch POST gets a dedicated 30s timeout) kills the
timeout-vs-spawn race, Fix 3 adds =Supervisor::release_orphaned_lease= +
=POST /projects/:p/tasks/:t/lease/release= + =orgasmic manager lease-release=
(no more restart-to-clear). REMAINING SCOPE before dispatching this task:
verify under the new code, then fix only what's left — most likely the CLI
failure-cleanup still deleting the worktree while a daemon-side spawn may
have started, and never deleting the created branch (the "branch already
exists" retry papercut). Re-scope or close as covered if nothing reproduces.

UPDATE [2026-06-11 manager re-validation]: batch-1 ran two normal-path CLI
dispatches (TASK-RVEKT, TASK-CJWT3.1) plus both closes on the =981e3f3= binary —
all completed in ~1s, lifecycle auto-flips fired, no worktree deletion, no
zombie lease, no leftover branch (=--worktree-remove --branch-delete= both
honored). Caveat: spawns were fast, so the slow-spawn (>30s) window was never
entered — consistent-with-fixed, not proof. RE-SCOPED to the remainder: (a) a
forced-slow-spawn regression test for the CLI timeout path, (b) failure-path
cleanup ordering (worktree delete must not race a started daemon spawn;
branch must be deleted on failure cleanup). Consider folding (a) into
TASK-BRXGG's parallel-safety work — same test surface.

FENCING INVARIANT (round-3 critique, point 3b — adopted; UPGRADED per round-4
critique §5, conceded [2026-06-11]): the round-3 wording ("CLI consults the
daemon's authority, then deletes") is still check-then-act over HTTP — a
TOCTOU window survives between the daemon's answer and the CLI's delete. The
destructive act itself moves daemon-side: cleanup of anything a spawned
worker may own is EXECUTED by the daemon — kill the worker's process group,
=waitpid= for confirmed death, and only then the daemon deletes
worktree+branch (reuse the =reap_process_group= machinery). The CLI only
*requests* cleanup and reports the daemon's result. CLI-local deletion stays
legitimate only for resources the daemon provably never learned about (the
dispatch request never reached it).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI dispatch HTTP timeout either raised well above worst-case rmux spawn
  (e.g. 120s+), made configurable, or the dispatch route made async
  (daemon returns run_id immediately, spawn completes in background).
- [ ] Worktree/branch cleanup for a possibly-spawned worker is executed
  daemon-side (PGID kill → waitpid → daemon deletes), per the fencing
  invariant above; the CLI only requests it and reports the result. CLI-local
  deletion happens only when the daemon provably never received the dispatch,
  and removes the created branch whenever it removes the worktree.
- [ ] Supervisor lease for a failed/dead spawn is always releasable: either
  auto-released when the spawn errors/session dies, or =POST /runs/:id/release=
  (or a new endpoint) can clear a lease whose run never registered.
- [ ] Regression tests for the timeout path in
  =crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs= (slow-daemon stub).
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
- implementer-cursor / composer-2.5-fast,
  run-20260612T105240-d4824a984971431299fbd621a475a182, worker commit
  =b561c73= merged --no-ff =254e8c5=; manager review-and-fix at
  integration =a3d3c54= (no reviewer stage per round-4 process): the
  daemon-cleanup request no longer ?-propagates on failure — a daemon
  outage that failed the dispatch would have skipped lifecycle restore
  and swallowed the original error; ambiguous double-fault now leaves
  resources in place (fencing: never local-delete when a worker may own
  the worktree) and reports both errors; release_orphaned_lease doc
  comment restored.
- Delivered per the re-scoped remainder: (a) forced-slow-spawn
  regression test =dispatch_timeout_requests_daemon_cleanup= (test-only
  =DaemonOptions::dispatch_response_delay= + =ORGASMIC_DISPATCH_HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECS=
  override); (b) fencing invariant — =POST
  /projects/:id/tasks/:task/dispatch/cleanup= executes release (driver
  PGID reap via =reap_process_group=: SIGTERM group → wait → SIGKILL
  sweep → wait) BEFORE =git worktree remove --force= + =git branch -D=;
  CLI requests it on timeout/transport failures and only deletes
  locally (now including the branch) on definitive HTTP errors;
  (c) lease releasability — cleanup releases live runs or clears
  orphaned leases (=release_dispatch_worker_for_cleanup=), asserted
  =no lease held= post-cleanup in the regression test.
- Gates on merged main: cli 13/14 dispatch tests (1 = the documented
  load flake, passes isolated) + daemon 143 lib + dispatch_endpoint
  20/26 (3 PID + pre-existing set = TASK-ZD72S, identical to pre-merge
  baseline); clippy -D warnings clean (lib/bin + cli --all-targets);
  fmt --check clean. Both new regression tests green in-suite.
- Residual (recorded): cleanup vs in-flight pre-spawn dispatch is
  best-effort (TOCTOU class, worker-noted); refused-vs-timeout
  classification is string-based because send_json inlines the reqwest
  error — refinement only worth it if the double-fault path is ever hit
  in practice.

* DONE TASK-RVEKT Fix daemon /tx endpoint to persist tx records to .orgasmic/tx/*.org :daemon:tx:dispatch:reliability:bug:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-RVEKT
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs .orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
The daemon's POST /tx endpoint accepts requests and returns success + a tx-ID
(e.g. "closed: TASK-CJXKM reviewer.done tx=tx-20260610-orgasmic-0109") but does
NOT write the record to =.orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org=. Observed consistently in
multiple sessions: every =dispatch-close= and =dispatch= call that goes through
/tx returns a tx-ID but leaves the file unchanged.

This is a session-persistent workaround: all tx records must currently be
manually constructed and appended. Without manual appends, =scan_open_dispatches=
(which reads the tx file directly) finds stale open-dispatch records that block
redispatch. The daemon's in-memory open-dispatch list diverges from the tx file
until the next restart.

Root cause is likely that the /tx route handler calls an in-memory append but
skips (or never reaches) the file-flush path.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /tx request persists the tx record to =.orgasmic/tx/YYYY-MM.org=
  immediately (within the same request handler, before returning 200).
- [ ] Verified: after a dispatch-close, the corresponding tx record appears in
  the org file without manual intervention.
- [ ] Integration test added: POST /tx then read the file, assert the record
  is present.
- [ ] No regressions: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= passes.

** Evidence
- [2026-06-11] Implementer run-20260611T104735 (implementer-claude-rmux,
  claude-sonnet-4-6): PREMISE FALSE with root cause. The acceptance-criterion
  integration test already exists and passes (=integration.rs:1277= POST /tx
  → read file → assert, plus the project-tx variant at =:1346=). The endpoint
  persists fine when routing succeeds.
- ROOT CAUSE of the production symptom: =tx_destination= (=api.rs:4853=)
  silently falls back to the HOME tx path whenever the project lookup fails
  (board not loaded, wrong project path, =.orgasmic= dir absent) or
  =valid_project_root= fails. CLI sees success; project file unchanged;
  =scan_open_dispatches= reads only the project file → phantom open
  dispatches. Manager verified the fall-through in source.
- Manager disk sweep [2026-06-11]: no tx file exists anywhere under
  =~/.orgasmic= — fallback-routed records do not land anywhere findable.
  Silent success → records vanish entirely.
- No code merged (worker correctly stopped at scope boundary: error-vs-succeed
  on project-lookup failure is a design decision). Real fix filed as
  TASK-E9K1B. Closed: acceptance already satisfied by existing tests; the
  production symptom is owned by TASK-E9K1B.
- LIVE VERIFICATION [2026-06-11 ~14:22]: both batch-1 dispatch-closes
  (implementer.done tx-0121, dispatch_aborted tx-0122) persisted to
  =.orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org= with zero manual appends — acceptance
  criterion 2 observed in production on the 981e3f3 binary with the project
  registered and board loaded. All four criteria factually green; residual
  silent-fallback danger lives in TASK-E9K1B. Manual DONE flip by manager
  (close-as-aborted left TODO; redispatch would re-run a false premise).

* DONE TASK-E9K1B tx_destination silent home-fallback: project-lookup failure must be an explicit error, not silent success :daemon:tx:reliability:bug:design:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-E9K1B
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Root cause harvested from TASK-RVEKT (run-20260611T104735): =tx_destination=
(=api.rs:4853=) routes a tx append to the project tx file only when the
project is found in =snap.board= AND =valid_project_root= succeeds. On any
lookup failure it silently falls back to the home tx path — and the manager's
disk sweep found NO tx file anywhere under =~/.orgasmic=, so fallback-routed
records vanish entirely while the client receives success + a tx-ID.
Production consequence observed repeatedly: dispatch-close records missing
from =.orgasmic/tx/*.org=, phantom open dispatches, manual tx surgery
(exactly what the active goal forbids).

Design decision (manager, pre-made for this task): a tx append that names a
project MUST fail loudly when that project cannot be resolved to a valid
root. Return an explicit error envelope (per the TASK-PYW86/TASK-DFEDX sanitization
pattern); never silently reroute. Requests that explicitly carry =tx_path= or
genuinely target home-scope tx (no project named) keep their current
behavior.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Project-named tx append with unresolvable project → error response
  (sanitized envelope), nothing written, no tx-ID returned.
- [ ] Regression test covering the lookup-failure path (board missing the
  project; invalid project root).
- [ ] Existing happy-path tx tests stay green; =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=
  passes.
- [ ] dispatch-close against a live registered project verified end-to-end:
  record lands in =.orgasmic/tx/YYYY-MM.org= with no manual append.

** Evidence
- Implementer: codex gpt-5.5 high (acp-stdio), run-20260611T114019. Left
  work uncommitted with an honest gate report — full daemon suite + fmt
  blocked; manager verified both blockers pre-exist on main (operator
  ab7bb93 dispatch_endpoint breakage; 10-site fmt drift, cleared in a chore
  commit) and committed as =5d518da=, merged =0b0b783=.
- Gates: tx_record integration tests 4/4; clippy -D warnings green.
- Live verify on rebuilt daemon (pid 75750, boot b2739ed9):
  =tx record --project no-such-project= → 400 "project could not be
  resolved", grep confirms ZERO writes in home + project tx; real-project
  probe persisted tx-0128; dispatch-close txs 0129/0130/0137-0139 all
  daemon-written — clean cycle holds on the new binary.
- Reviewer: codex (run-20260611T121229), verdict =approve=, zero findings;
  independently confirmed sanitized envelope (detail tracing-only), both
  call sites fail before tx-ID generation, no-write assertions. Optional
  hardening noted: blank-project regression + tx-tree snapshot (candidate
  fold into TASK-C2PQ3).
- Bonus evidence for TASK-ZSD0B: this acp run released cleanly on stream
  close — the 600s release lag is rmux-specific.

* DONE TASK-ZSD0B rmux run_complete does not release the run: stall detector reaps it 600s later with a mislabeled reason; last.txt carries marker text instead of worker summary :daemon:drivers:rmux:supervisor:lifecycle:instrumentation:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZSD0B
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/rmux.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs .orgasmic/gotchas.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-drivers
:END:

** Description
Live evidence from both batch-1 dispatches [2026-06-11]:

1. *Release lag*: TASK-CJWT3.1 session JSONL shows =run_complete= ("rmux
   end-of-turn marker observed") at 10:59:40Z but the lifecycle release only
   at 11:09:41Z — exactly the 600s stall window later. TASK-RVEKT shows the
   same pattern. For rmux runs, =run_complete= does not trigger supervisor
   release; the stall detector reaps the lease 10 minutes after the worker
   already finished. Consequence: leases (and redispatch) blocked ~10min per
   run, and the UI shows a live working badge for a worker that has exited.
2. *Mislabeled release reason*: the release event records
   =outcome: completed, reason: stall_timeout_exceeded= — a completed run
   must not carry a stall reason; recovery/telemetry consumers will
   misclassify it.
3. *last.txt content*: both =task-NNN-last.txt= files contain only the
   32-byte marker summary ("rmux end-of-turn marker observed") instead of
   the worker's final message; the real summary is only inside a 15-24MB
   stdout.log. tmux/codex paths write the worker summary — rmux should too
   (TASK-RF3J1.5 family).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =run_complete= on an rmux run triggers supervisor release promptly
  (test: release event within seconds of run_complete in the session JSONL,
  not after the stall window).
- [ ] Release events for completed runs carry a completion reason, never
  =stall_timeout_exceeded=.
- [ ] =last.txt= for rmux dispatches contains the worker's final summary
  (tail of the worker's last message), not the marker-observation string.
- [ ] Existing stall-detector behavior preserved for genuinely stalled runs
  (regression test).

** Evidence
- Implementer: codex gpt-5.5 high, run-20260611T124905, worker commit
  =91b4a15=, merged =c9bb352=. run_complete now triggers prompt release;
  completed runs carry a completion reason; rmux RunComplete.summary
  carries the worker-output tail (last.txt inherits it). New
  =tests/rmux_release.rs= (2 tests, mock driver via new async-trait
  dev-dep); stall-detector regression preserved.
- Gates at merge: rmux_release 2/2, drivers suite green, daemon lib 143
  passed, clippy -D warnings + fmt --check green (full dispatch_endpoint
  binary still blocked by the known ab7bb93 operator breakage).
- Daemon restarted on the merged binary (pid 41845, boot 8ce7fec9).
  Live verification of the prompt-release path lands with the next rmux
  dispatch (after unpause) — its release event must follow run_complete
  within seconds with a completion reason, and last.txt must carry prose.
- Reviewer pass DEFERRED (operator pause); task parked IN_REVIEW.

* DONE TASK-W43NY Task badges restate the same fact three ways: collapse kind/owner/sub-state into one performer pill :ui:tasks:kanban:badges:polish:dec_QWEQ8:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-W43NY
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/** ui/src/lib/**
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/components/tasks/ ui/src/lib/ .orgasmic/decisions.org
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
Operator-reported [2026-06-11, live screenshots]: a task row with an active
run renders three chips that restate one fact — =Implementer= (kind),
=agent.implementer= (owner), =Implementer.working= (run sub-state). The task
drawer adds the same trio next to the lifecycle pill. Three teal chips, one
meaning: "an implementer is working on this."

Collapse per the dec_QWEQ8 precedent (single combined pill with separator
dot) and dec_P7EY8 (performer-namespaced sub-state vocabulary):

- Live run: ONE performer pill — =Implementer · working= — replaces kind
  chip + owner chip + sub-state chip. Owner appears only when it carries
  information beyond the kind (e.g. a named worker like
  =implementer-claude-rmux= or a human), rendered inside the same pill or
  as a tooltip/secondary line, not a sibling chip.
- No live run: the plain kind chip stays as today.
- Drawer header: lifecycle pill + priority + the same single performer
  pill; no duplicate kind chip beside it.
- Parent-task chip (=TASK-CJWT3=-style) is a distinct fact — keep it.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task list row with an active run shows exactly one performer pill
  (kind + sub-state combined), plus lifecycle-independent chips only when
  they carry distinct facts.
- [ ] Task drawer header shows lifecycle, priority, and one performer pill —
  no chip restating the kind.
- [ ] No-live-run rendering unchanged (plain kind chip).
- [ ] =npm --prefix ui run build= green; existing UI unit tests pass; add a
  unit test for the pill-collapse rendering decision if testable at unit
  level.

* DONE TASK-HC7PW Structural-invariance guard on body-write ops + doctor lint for phantom headings :daemon:core:parser:security:hardening:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HC7PW
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
From the round-3 architecture critique (archive/org-harness-critique-round-3-reply.md,
point 1 — conceded): =OrgRewriter::set_section_body= / =set_node_body=
(org.rs:794/825) splice caller payloads verbatim with no structural
validation. A body containing a column-0 =* anything= line becomes a phantom
top-level heading at the next parse — silently, and the existing
parse-validation on writes cannot catch it because the poisoned file parses
fine into a different tree. Same class as the TASK-B2P1Z.1 Org-injection fix,
but for the body-edit surface (RPC/UI editor and any free-text field flowing
into a body write).

Two layers:
1. *Write-time guard*: after applying body-edit ops, re-parse and assert the
   heading structure (count + IDs + levels) is identical modulo the intended
   change; reject the edit otherwise (typed error per the sanitized-envelope
   pattern). Alternative escape-on-write (indent column-0 stars) is
   acceptable if invariance-check proves too strict for legitimate edits —
   implementer picks with rationale.
2. *Read-time lint*: doctor/index lint that flags task files whose level-1
   headings lack the expected task shape (=:ID:= property, known state
   keyword) — catches phantom headings from hand-written (CLI-absent
   fallback) writes after the fact. Precedent: =lint_goal_liveness= in
   index.rs.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Body-edit op whose payload would add/remove/relevel any heading is
  rejected with a typed error; legitimate body edits (incl. multi-paragraph
  prose, lists, src blocks) pass.
- [ ] Regression test: payload with column-0 =* X= line → rejected; same
  payload escaped/indented → accepted and round-trips byte-stable.
- [ ] Lint surfaces a task-file heading without =:ID:=/state keyword via the
  parse-errors channel (or doctor finding); test included.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon= green.

** Evidence
- Implementer: claude rmux, run-20260611T124916, worker commit =55de289=,
  merged =a4f2145=. Write-time =assert_structural_invariant= (re-parse +
  heading count/ID/level compare, single-edit undo on violation) + read-time
  phantom-heading lint through the parse-errors channel. New
  =tests/body_write_guard.rs= 28/28; core suite green; clippy+fmt green.
- Lint's first production catch, minutes after restart: the scaffold seed
  heading =* (empty)= in =.orgasmic/tasks/archive.org= — true positive by
  spec (non-task level-1 heading in a task file); manager removed the
  placeholder, parse_errors back to 0. Scaffold-template follow-up noted in
  handoff Divergence (operator owns shipped/ working area).
- Residual (worker-reported): invariant precondition = call immediately
  after a single replace_with_view; doc-commented. One extra re-parse per
  body write, negligible at human file scale.
- Reviewer pass [2026-06-11 18:19], codex gpt-5.5 high
  (run-20260611T144846-52062dba): =VERDICT=has-issues= 1H/2M/1L → task
  bounced IN_REVIEW→IN_PROGRESS (close tx-0160). The HIGH is an in-scope
  guard bypass (the guard misses the append/new-section body path); fix
  subtask TASK-HC7PW.1 filed. Full findings verbatim in tx-0160.
- CLOSED DONE [2026-06-11 18:34]: fix subtask TASK-HC7PW.1 landed (codex
  gpt-5.5 high, worker commit =46f7136=, merged =fa79c2d=, close
  implementer.done tx-0163). Guard now covers replace AND append/new-section
  body paths (fails closed with =BodyHeadingInjection=), parser is
  block-aware, leading-star escapes preserved. Manager diff-review + gates on
  main: body_write_guard 8/8 (incl. the =body_edit_rejects_column0_star_heading=
  bypass regression), core lib 92/92, clippy clean. Original AC fully met.
  Runtime note: the live daemon still runs the pre-fix binary — guard fix
  takes effect on the next daemon rebuild/restart (deferred, operator-timed).

* DONE TASK-HC7PW.1 Body-write guard bypass on append/new-section path + over-rejection of escaped & src-block bodies (review of TASK-HC7PW) :daemon:core:parser:security:hardening:bug:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HC7PW.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/body_write_guard.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
TASK-HC7PW reviewer (codex gpt-5.5 high, run-20260611T144846-52062dba) returned
=VERDICT=has-issues= 1H/2M/1L. The shipped guard (=assert_structural_invariant=)
only covers the *replace* body paths; the append/new-section path and several
legitimate-edit cases are wrong. Priority P2 (parent P3): a guard advertised as
closing the phantom-heading injection class but bypassable is worse than none.

- *HIGH (bypass — the security hole)*: =add_section= / missing-section
  =set_section_body= route through =upsert_section_text= → =append_section=
  (org.rs:1001-1003, 1062-1068; call site api.rs:7135-7137), which inserts the
  body WITHOUT the invariant guard. Payload =* Phantom= is accepted and
  reparses as a new top-level heading (reviewer repro: =top_headings=2=). Fix:
  guard the append/new-section body insertion too — route it through the same
  guarded path, or snapshot+assert the structural invariant around
  =append_section=.
- *MEDIUM (over-reject, breaks AC)*: =set_node_body= trims leading whitespace
  BEFORE the invariance check (org.rs:871-884), so a legitimately escaped body
  starting =" * Not a heading"= becomes =* Not a heading= and is rejected as
  =BodyHeadingInjection= (repro confirmed). Fix: do not trim leading body
  content before writing / compare invariance against the untrimmed body.
- *MEDIUM (over-reject, breaks AC)*: the invariant parser is not Org-block
  aware (org.rs:1083-1112): a valid body containing =#+begin_src org= with a
  literal =* Heading= line is rejected — violates the AC that src-block bodies
  pass unchanged. Fix: ignore heading-looking lines inside block delimiters.
- *LOW (test gap)*: byte-stability is asserted with =contains(...)= not full
  byte equality, section-body only (body_write_guard.rs:217; org.rs:1538-1545).
  Fix: full expected-file byte-equality for accepted escaped payloads, covering
  both =set_section_body= and =set_node_body=.

One-round FIX_SUBTASK; full reviewer detail (sites + repro + fix directions) is
in close tx-0160.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =* Phantom= payload via the append/new-section body path
  (=upsert_section_text= / =add_section=) is rejected with the same typed error;
  regression test reproduces the old =top_headings=2= bypass and proves it now
  fails closed.
- [ ] Escaped body beginning with leading whitespace (=" * x"=) is accepted and
  round-trips byte-stable (no pre-check trim).
- [ ] Body containing =#+begin_src ... * Heading ... #+end_src= is accepted and
  round-trips.
- [ ] Byte-stability assertions use full file equality for both
  =set_section_body= and =set_node_body=.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon= green (existing
  body_write_guard cases still pass).

** Evidence
(populated at task close)

* DONE TASK-C2PQ3 tx append durability: fsync before ack + cached project-tx sequence :daemon:tx:reliability:durability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-C2PQ3
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-E9K1B
:END:

** Description
From the round-3 critique (point 3a — conceded after source verification):

1. *Durability gap*: =append_tx_inner= (writer.rs:347) acks through the
   oneshot after the write syscall but never calls =sync_all=/=sync_data= —
   the rewrite path fsyncs (writer.rs:550), the append path does not. The tx
   log is the audit ground truth; a success response must mean the record
   survives a kernel crash. Low rate path — fsync-per-append is affordable.
2. *O(history) sequencer*: =next_project_tx_id= (writer.rs:380) re-reads and
   re-parses the ENTIRE tx directory on every project-sequenced append, and
   a single corrupt tx file fails ID assignment for all subsequent appends.
   Cache the max sequence per (project, month) in the writer task; fall back
   to a scan only on cache miss.
3. *Group commit (round-4 critique §3 — adopted)*: naive fsync-per-append
   serializes throughput to disk-flush latency (macOS =F_FULLFSYNC= can run
   10-20ms). At today's tx rates that would survive, but the serialized
   writer loop makes group commit nearly free: batch-drain whatever appends
   are queued in the mpsc, write them all, fsync ONCE, then ack all the
   oneshots. Make batching the design rather than retrofitting it later.

Scope overlaps TASK-E9K1B (same writer/api tx path) — hence DEPENDS_ON; do not
dispatch while TASK-E9K1B is in flight.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tx append response is sent only after the appended bytes are fsynced
  (=sync_data= acceptable); test asserts the ordering hook-level or via
  injected fs error.
- [ ] Concurrent appends are coalesced into one fsync (group commit): test
  drives N concurrent appends and asserts fewer syncs than appends, with
  every ack still ordered after the sync covering its bytes.
- [ ] Project-tx sequence assignment is O(1) amortized (cached); regression
  test proves no full-directory re-parse on the hot path.
- [ ] Corrupt sibling tx file no longer blocks new appends (degraded-scan
  behavior defined and tested).
- [X] =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= green (lib + new suite; 5 full-suite
  failures verified pre-existing on main, see Evidence).

** Evidence
- Worker: =implementer-cursor= / composer-2.5-fast,
  run-20260611T211608-604c81a; worker commit 8e91fbd; merged 862833a
  (--no-ff). Reviewer stage SKIPPED per operator override 2026-06-12 —
  manager reviewed the full diff directly.
- Implementation: =WriterCommand::Tx= batch-drains queued appends via
  =try_recv= (non-Tx command stashed and handled next iteration), writes
  all, =sync_data= once per touched file via =sync_tx_path=, then acks
  every oneshot; sync failure flips affected acks to Err.
  =ProjectTxSeqCache= caches per (project, month) with project_max
  fallback; directory scan only on cache miss; corrupt/unreadable siblings
  skipped with =warn!= (degraded scan accepts possible seq reuse if the
  corrupt file hid the true max — recorded tradeoff). =transaction_inner=
  also fsyncs the tx append before success and rolls back rewrites on sync
  failure.
- Tests (=tests/writer_durability.rs=, 4/4 green, serialized via static
  mutex over global hook counters): ack-after-fsync via injected failure;
  8 concurrent appends coalesce to fewer syncs than appends; hot path does
  not re-scan (12-file fixture, scan_count stays 1, exact IDs 0013/0014
  assert cross-month max semantics); corrupt sibling skipped (mints 0037
  past 2026-05's 0036).
- Manager verification in worktree: lib 143 green (one flaky
  =stall_detector_revalidates_after_driver_event_race= failure under
  parallel suite load, 5/5 green in isolation — pre-existing race test);
  =--no-fail-fast= full suite: only 3 dispatch_endpoint PID tests
  (cursor-agent resolves to a bash wrapper on this machine) + 2
  integration tests fail — all 5 replayed on main without these changes
  and fail identically (pre-existing, not regressions).

* DONE TASK-GBT5W Short-random ID minting on all task/decision creation paths + =id mint= CLI verb (re-scoped by dec_X72P5 reversal) :daemon:cli:tasks:identity:concurrency:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GBT5W
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs .orgasmic/decisions.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-cli
:END:

** Description
Live incident [2026-06-11 ~15:05]: two concurrent manager sessions each
allocated TASK-W43NY by reading sprint.org and taking max+1 — one filed "badge
collapse" (committed 4f8128a, dispatched), the other filed the structural
guard task minutes later uncommitted; the duplicate was caught at commit time
and renumbered to TASK-HC7PW by hand. This is the round-3 critique's §2.1
scenario reproduced exactly (parallel planners + incremental IDs + no
sequencer = collision). The answer is NOT decentralized UUIDs (dec_SSJVD): it
is routing allocation through the existing write authority.

Provide an atomic =next task ID= surface: daemon endpoint (e.g.
=POST /projects/:id/tasks/allocate-id=) + CLI verb (=orgasmic task
allocate-id= or equivalent) that reserves and returns the next TASK-NNN
under the writer lock. Manager doctrine update: when filing tasks, allocate
through the CLI when the daemon is reachable; max+1-by-eye remains the
documented daemon-free fallback (single-writer contexts only).

Round-4 critique notes [2026-06-11]: the "global bottleneck / SPOF" charge
against daemon allocation fails for this topology — every org write already
serializes through the daemon's single writer loop (dec_WH9PD), so ID
allocation adds one cheap call on an already-serial path, not a new
synchronization point. The critic's PK-vs-alias distinction (stable
surrogate key + display alias) is conceded as the correct multi-master
escalation and recorded in dec_SSJVD, replacing draft-ID reconciliation — his
objection that merging draft-IDs means rewriting prose cross-references is
right. Out of scope for this task.

RE-SCOPED [2026-06-11, dec_X72P5 REVERSED to shape 3b — short-random IDs,
permanent mixed scheme]: sequenced allocation is DEAD — there is nothing to
sequence. The incident history above stands as the motivation; the remedy
changed. Final scope: (a) every task/decision creation path (daemon
endpoints, CLI filing, dispatch-created subtask filing) mints a conforming
short-random ID per dec_X72P5a — 5 chars Crockford base32 (no I/L/O/U),
uppercase, ≥1 letter (all-digit mints re-rolled), class-prefixed
(=TASK-=/=dec_=); (b) =orgasmic id mint --class task|decision= prints a
conforming ID with NO daemon required (for hand-filing); (c) legacy numeric
IDs remain accepted as references everywhere; NO path mints numerics
post-cutover. Cross-clone collision/lint/repair is NOT this task — that is
TASK-QRD3Y.

EXTENDED [2026-06-11, full-migration escalation]: also covers =arch_XXXXX=
and =term_XXXXX= minting, and a CODE-READINESS AUDIT — find and fix every
code-level ID-shape assumption (validation regexes, numeric sorting,
parsing, subtask-suffix derivation, test fixtures, UI lookups) so all four
minted classes are accepted everywhere. This task is the precondition for
the migration execution (TASK-YRK1V); legacy IDs must keep resolving during
the interim window between this task landing and the migration running.

NO resolver verb ships (dec_X72P5b v4, greenfield-clean): legacy-ID lookup is
=git show id-migration | grep <old-id>= — a repo-local convention line, not
a product feature. The product carries zero knowledge of legacy ID
grammars; code comments / git history / archived docs (exempt from
migration per the carve-out) are bridged by the tagged migration commit.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All creation paths mint dec_X72P5a-conforming IDs: 5-char Crockford
  base32, uppercase, ≥1 letter, class prefix; property test over 10k mints
  asserts format + no all-digit output.
- [ ] =orgasmic id mint --class task|decision= prints a conforming ID with
  no daemon running.
- [ ] No code path mints a sequential numeric ID post-cutover (test greps
  creation paths / asserts mint output never matches =^TASK-[0-9]+$=).
- [ ] Legacy numeric IDs still resolve everywhere they are referenced
  (dispatch, dispatch-close, UI lookups) — regression test.
- [ ] manager-dispatch convention (or successor doc) names the minting rule
  for hand-filing (verb or local randomness; never max+1-by-eye).
- [ ] Code-readiness audit: report of every ID-shape assumption found, each
  fixed or justified; a minted-grammar ID round-trips through dispatch,
  dispatch-close, kanban render, and subtask derivation (integration test).

** Evidence
- Worker: =implementer-cursor= / composer-2.5-fast, TWO rounds. Round 1
  (run-20260611T214058-2e29047, commit a7efc93): =crates/orgasmic-core/
  src/id.rs= — =mint_node_id(NodeIdClass)= over CSPRNG =thread_rng=,
  Crockford alphabet const, 5-char stems re-rolled to ≥1 letter;
  validators (=is_valid_task_path_id=, =is_arch_id=, =is_dec_id=) moved
  out of schema.rs and widened to legacy+minted stems; graph create
  auto-mints when =id= omitted and REJECTS legacy sequential ids on
  create; glossary =--id= optional; =orgasmic id mint --class
  task|decision|architecture|term= daemon-free; 10k-per-class property
  test (40k mints). Audit table: 7 in-scope ID-shape assumptions, each
  fixed or justified (subtask suffix stays numeric, parent stem
  grammar-free; kanban = file order, children lexicographic). Round 2
  (run-20260611T215220-01cee3a, commit 800a94b): dispatch +
  dispatch-close resolution regression for BOTH grammars — minted and
  legacy TASK ids resolve the dispatch route and the =implementer.done=
  close path (synthetic open dispatch when hermetic spawn fails), tx log
  asserted keyed to the exercised id. Merged 13e8722 (--no-ff);
  dispatch-close tx-0183. Reviewer stage skipped per operator override —
  manager reviewed both rounds.
- Manager verification: id_mint 5/5, core 102/102, cli suites green in
  worktree; UI audited beyond worker report — no UI create flows exist
  (create-rejection cannot break UI), =inferNodeKind= prefix-only safe;
  one confirmed break =richText.tsx:109= numeric-only entity regex →
  filed TASK-84QVC.
- Convention AC closed manager-side: "Filing new nodes: mint IDs, never
  max+1" section added to shipped/prompt-studio/conventions/
  manager-dispatch.org (same cycle, commit e0f72f1).
- Residuals (accepted): graph create accepts caller-provided non-legacy
  custom ids by design (pre-minted via =id mint=, glossary slugs);
  TASK-QRD3Y's standing lint owns malformed-id detection. No daemon
  endpoint for top-level task filing exists yet — hand-filing keeps using
  =orgasmic id mint=.

* DONE TASK-GKTWY Worker transcript chat: missing initial prompt, raw stderr bubbles, unreadable tool-call groups :ui:manager:transcript:dx:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GKTWY
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/manager/** ui/src/lib/**
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/components/manager/ ui/src/lib/ .orgasmic/tmp/sessions/
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
Operator-reported [2026-06-11, live screenshots of the TASK-E9K1B codex peek
chat]. Three defects in ManagerChatTranscript, root causes verified against
=dispatch-TASK-145-implementer-20260611T114019.jsonl=:

1. *Initial prompt never renders* — the chat opens on the first assistant
   bubble. The compiled prompt IS persisted (lifecycle =run_meta= envelope,
   =driver_config.prompt_bundle_text=, seq 1) but =transcriptEntries()= drops
   every =kind: lifecycle= envelope (ManagerChatTranscript.tsx:380), and no
   harness emits a user-stream text_chunk for the dispatch prompt (0 in both
   the codex-acp and claude-rmux session files). Render an opening user-side
   bubble from =prompt_bundle_text= — collapsed by default (multi-KB
   bundle): label + first lines + expand.

2. *stderr renders as chat bubbles with raw ANSI* — =textChunkEntry()=
   filters only two codex log patterns; every other stderr chunk falls
   through to a system-role bubble labeled "STDERR" showing escape-code
   garbage (=[2m...[31mERROR...=, the codex websocket-500 retry noise).
   stderr must never be a chat bubble: route to a collapsed diagnostics row
   in the activity stream, strip ANSI.

3. *Tool-call groups are unreadable and hide current activity* —
   =ToolGroupBlock= summarizes as "Tool calls · 57 commands · 64 results ·
   205 events": counts carry no information about what the agent is DOING.
   Expanded, each command yields 2-3 near-duplicate rows (=command request=
   + =command started= + result). The summary line must name the latest
   activity (e.g. =run sed -n '1,220p' .orgasmic/project.org=) with counts
   secondary; pair request/started/result into one row per command with
   status; raw JSON stays behind disclosure.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Opening the chat for an acp or rmux worker run shows the dispatch
  prompt as the first (user-side, collapsed-expandable) bubble.
- [ ] No stderr-labeled chat bubble for any session; stderr reachable via a
  collapsed diagnostics disclosure with ANSI stripped.
- [ ] Tool-group summary line names the most recent activity; one row per
  command pairing request/result; live runs surface the in-flight command.
- [ ] Transcript-shaping logic extracted to a pure helper in =ui/src/lib/=
  with unit tests (precedent: runDockUtils.ts, performerPill.ts).
- [ ] =npm --prefix ui run build= green; vitest green.

** Evidence
- Implementer: claude rmux, run-20260611T121219, worker commit =187e3cd=,
  merged =a213e31= first try. New pure helper =ui/src/lib/transcriptUtils.ts=
  (prompt extraction from run_meta, stderr→diagnostics routing + ANSI strip,
  call_id triplet pairing, group summary selection) + 25 tests on fixtures
  derived from the real TASK-E9K1B session.
- Gates: ui build green; vitest 62/62 at merge.
- Reviewer: claude rmux (run-20260611T123629), verdict =APPROVE=, close
  tx-0148. Independently verified: prompt bubble from
  =driver_config.prompt_bundle_text= (10428 chars in the reference session),
  no lifecycle leak; no stderr path can reach TranscriptBubble; complete
  exec/cmd_exec/result triplets for all 5 sampled call_ids; live trailing
  group surfaces in-flight command; token/a11y conformance.
- Findings: M1 MEDIUM latent (multi-chunk stderr coalescing truncates
  visible+raw to first chunk) → filed TASK-GKTWY.1 FIX_SUBTASK with review
  L1 (orphaned-result test gap) folded in.

* DONE TASK-KBH4B cargo test/build of the daemon requires a working npm UI toolchain via build.rs: fresh worktrees and clean machines fail before any Rust code runs :daemon:build:dx:onboarding:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-KBH4B
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/build.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/Cargo.toml .orgasmic/gotchas.org
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/build.rs ui/package.json
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --test integration tx_record
:END:

** Description
Hit three times in one day [2026-06-11], all fresh-worktree contexts:
- TASK-E9K1B implementer (codex): had to run =npm --prefix ui ci= before
  =cargo test= would compile — "daemon build initially used an
  incompatible/global TypeScript".
- TASK-E9K1B reviewer (codex): targeted =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=
  gate died before reaching Rust tests — build.rs invoked
  =npm --prefix ui run build=, which failed TS5101 in the bare worktree.
- TASK-W43NY reviewer (claude): had to run vitest from the main checkout's
  node_modules because the worktree had none.

Every dispatched worker pays this tax, and it is a direct stranger-smoke
blocker (goal Reached-When item 4): =cargo test= on a clean clone fails
without a pre-warmed npm toolchain, with an error that says nothing about
the actual cause.

Decouple the Rust test path from the UI build. Implementer picks the
mechanism with rationale; acceptable shapes include: build.rs skips the UI
build unless building release/with a feature flag (embed-ui), an
=ORGASMIC_SKIP_UI_BUILD= escape hatch honored by CI docs and worker briefs,
or a committed placeholder dist for non-release builds. Whatever the pick:
the failure mode for a genuinely needed-but-broken UI toolchain must name
npm/node as the cause and the fix in one line. Record the rule in
=.orgasmic/gotchas.org=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon= passes in a fresh worktree with NO

** Description
Wire the dec_BP4NH advisory-code-marker rules into the two prompt states that operationally enforce them: griller =exploring= and implementer =reconciling=. TASK-D6Y0Y ships the base griller exploring prompt; this task extends it with the marker-grep substep, and authors the implementer reconciling prompt with the explicit-user-approval rule.

Surfaced as P2 items #3 and #4 in =archive/2026-05-21_convention-bundle-gap/findings.md=. Depends on TASK-ZA4EW to make the convention bundle and =\{\{conventions.code_markers\}\}= slot referenceable, and on TASK-D6Y0Y to provide the griller exploring prompt that this task extends.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =shipped/prompts/griller/exploring.org= includes a substep instructing griller agents to grep for marker IDs to detect whether existing code already commits to a choice, transcribed from spec.org:1075-1076.
- [X] =shipped/prompts/implementer/reconciling.org= exists and encodes the dec_BP4NH user-note rule from spec.org:1319-1326: implementers may touch files surfaced only by advisory-marker grep, but must raise an explicit question to the user before doing so.
- [X] Both prompts opt in to =\{\{conventions.code_markers\}\}= (or reference it explicitly), pending the slot-injection-scope decision flagged in findings.md as a placeholder griller question.
- [X] Dry-run compile of both prompts succeeds with the conventions slot resolved.

** Evidence

- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:57] Added =shipped/prompts/implementer/reconciling.org= with ={{conventions.code_markers}}= and the explicit =** Question= approval gate for advisory-marker-only files outside the claimed task's =:PRODUCES:= set.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:57] Implementer reconciling prompt uses =:STATE: fixing= because =reconciling= is a prompt id, not an implementer state in the current state machine.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:57] Verification passed: =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures=, prompt dry-runs for =griller analyzing= and =implementer reconciling=, and =git diff --check= for the prompt files.

** Description
TASK-V3DWJ implemented the shipped-vs-user override leg of dec_GRDFB's three-tier content model for skills, agent templates, prompt templates, conventions, project-scaffold, manager persona, and schema. The remaining two tiers — opt-in shipped subdirs ("optional") and install-by-URL ("hub") — are not yet wired and were deferred from TASK-V3DWJ acceptance criterion #5 on operator authorization. This task closes that gap.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] =orgasmic optional list / enable <name> / disable <name>= subcommands manage opt-in shipped subdirs (e.g. orgasmic/shipped/optional/<family>/<name>) with a per-home enablement registry under =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/optional.org=.
- [X] =orgasmic hub install <url> [--family X]= clones or fetches a hub content URL into =$ORGASMIC_HOME/user/<family>/<name>/= so the loader-rule resolves it before shipped defaults; supports git URLs at minimum.
- [X] =orgasmic hub list / hub remove <name>= round out the lifecycle.
- [X] All three families (skills, agent-templates, prompts) work identically through optional+hub flows; tests cover each family.
- [X] TASK-V3DWJ acceptance criterion #5 ("Optional and hub content installs work consistently…") becomes checkable once this task closes; flip the box in TASK-V3DWJ Evidence at completion.
- [X] orgasmic doctor reports broken optional/hub registrations.

** Evidence

* DONE TASK-DVQN3 Sweep additional glossary debt :glossary:debt:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-DVQN3
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           glossarist
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_MPAQT arch_QXS5W
:PRODUCES:         .orgasmic/glossary.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/glossary.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/glossary.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org shipped/** archive/21_05_26_init/spec.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-QTR08
:END:

** Description
TASK-QTR08 glossarist surfaced six additional terms used as plain-English nouns across arch_QXS5W, arch_MPAQT, the spec, and the convention-bundle audit but without =term:= entries in =.orgasmic/glossary.org=. None blocking; sweep when planner schedules. List:

1. =skill manifest= — distinct concept from raw skill bodies; arch_QXS5W interface bullet and findings.md reference it. Define alongside =prompt-bundle= (manifest is one of its inputs).
2. =persona= and =operating rules= — both used by arch_QXS5W's prompt-bundle composition list and =shipped/manager/persona.org=; undefined. Either one =persona= term or a paired definition.
3. =dry-run compile= — arch_QXS5W constraint and TASK-ZA4EW acceptance criterion use the phrase; needs a short defined term.
4. =spawn-time injection= vs =continuation injection= — arch_QXS5W distinguishes; could be one =prompt-injection= term with two flavours.
5. =finalize gate= — referenced in arch_MPAQT and findings.md with specific semantics (rejects pending decisions and cross-scope reference mismatches); undefined.
6. =semantic hash= — drives stale-propagation; =term_J5X55= mentions it under =:RELATES_TO:= but it has no entry.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Six (or paired-down equivalent) new =term:= headings exist with the existing glossary style (=:CANONICAL:=, =:AVOID:=, =:RELATES_TO:=, =:DEFINITION:=, =:DECIDED_AT:=).
- [X] Definitions remain abstract per arch_MPAQT constraint (no code paths, type signatures, function names).
- [X] arch_QXS5W / arch_MPAQT :GLOSSARY_REFS: extensions flagged for architector (out of glossarist scope).

** Evidence
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:46] Added glossary terms =skill-manifest=, =persona=, =operating-rules=, =dry-run-compile=, =prompt-injection=, =finalize-gate=, and =semantic-hash= with the existing canonical/avoid/relates/definition/decided-at style.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:46] Definitions stay abstract: no code paths, type signatures, or function names.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:46] Integrated the flagged architecture backrefs: arch_MPAQT now references =term_C3A8T= and =term_ERXMF=; arch_QXS5W now references =term_ZYE1R=, =term_C9YBH=, =term_H2GAN=, =term_VD1MY=, and =term_T2NSV=. No semantic hash bump: =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= is bookkeeping, not part of the canonical semantic hash input.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:46] Verification passed: =cargo test -p orgasmic-core --test fixtures=, =git diff --check=.

** Worklog
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:28] Dispatched a glossary-only worker with write scope limited to =.orgasmic/glossary.org=.
- [2026-05-21 Thu 22:46] Manager integrated the worker output and applied the arch_MPAQT / arch_QXS5W glossary-reference bookkeeping.

* DONE TASK-GFKFW Package macOS Tauri app with bundled daemon and remote Linux daemon mode :install:tauri:desktop:daemon:remote:release:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GFKFW
:PRIORITY:         P1
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_WZFAX
:PRODUCES:         src-tauri/** ui/** crates/orgasmic-cli/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/** README.md scripts/install.sh docs/**
:WRITE_SCOPE:      src-tauri/** ui/** crates/orgasmic-cli/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/** README.md scripts/install.sh docs/**
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/glossary.org docs/adr/0002-home-and-install-layout.org README.md CONTRIBUTING.md ui/** crates/** scripts/install.sh
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
The product's simple install path should be a prebuilt macOS Tauri app from day
one, not the contributor source-checkout installer. The macOS package must
bundle the React workbench, local daemon sidecar, CLI sidecar, shipped
workers/prompts/schema/scaffold, and first-run home setup so a normal Mac user
can install orgasmic without Rust, Node.js, npm, cloning the repository, or
running a Vite dev server.

The runtime split must be explicit: the app is the workbench, the daemon owns
project files and worker execution. The macOS app should default to its bundled
local daemon for projects on the Mac, and support a remote daemon profile for
projects that live on a Linux server. In remote mode, the daemon is installed
and supervised on the Linux host, project paths are Linux paths, workers run on
that host, and the macOS app only connects to that daemon.

The existing =scripts/install.sh= path remains contributor/dogfood mode and is
also the initial remote Linux daemon installer until a dedicated package exists.

For v0.0.1 free/dev distribution, the packaged Mac build is Apple Silicon-only
and ad-hoc signed. The README must document the expected Gatekeeper approval
path instead of implying a notarized Developer ID public installer.

For command-line-comfortable testers, provide a one-command terminal installer
that downloads the DMG with =curl= and installs =orgasmic.app= into an
Applications directory, defaulting to =~/Applications=. This normally avoids
browser-applied quarantine metadata without pretending the build is notarized.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] A macOS Tauri shell exists and loads the production workbench assets
      without a Vite dev server.
- [X] The macOS package includes the local daemon sidecar, CLI sidecar, and
      shipped content needed for workers, prompts, schema, and project
      scaffolds.
- [X] The packaged app starts, supervises, and reconnects to the bundled local
      daemon without requiring a manual =orgasmic serve= terminal.
- [X] First launch creates or migrates the Mac's =$ORGASMIC_HOME= and can add an
      existing Git repository located on the Mac as the first local project.
- [X] The app exposes backend profiles, including the built-in local daemon and
      user-added remote daemon URLs with bearer tokens.
- [ ] Remote Linux daemon setup is documented and works: install the daemon on
      the Linux host, run it as a =systemd --user= service, keep it bound to
      =127.0.0.1= by default, connect from macOS through an SSH tunnel, and add
      a backend profile pointing at the tunnel.
- [X] Remote project semantics are explicit: the daemon manages only
      repositories and workers on the machine where it runs; the macOS app does
      not project local paths into the remote daemon.
- [X] The bundled CLI can be exposed on PATH or invoked from a documented app
      location for =status=, =doctor=, =project init=, and automation.
- [X] README simple install remains app-first; CONTRIBUTING owns fork/source
      setup and =scripts/install.sh=.
- [X] Release-build verification covers at least macOS locally, with a clear
      follow-up matrix for Linux and Windows packages if they are not ready in
      the first implementation pass.
- [X] The app exposes a Tauri updater flow that shows when an update is
      available and refuses to install while any live run is in flight.
- [X] The updater flow lets users choose between =stable= and =nightly=
      channels in Settings.

** Notes
- Treat source install as contributor mode for app development and as the first
  documented remote Linux daemon bootstrap until a dedicated server package
  exists.
- Do not install macOS LaunchAgents on a remote Linux host. Local macOS
  autostart belongs to the app; remote Linux autostart belongs to the remote
  host's service manager, initially =systemd --user=.
- Keep the two-layer home rule: user overrides and runtime data stay in
  =$ORGASMIC_HOME= on the daemon host while shipped defaults come from the app
  bundle in normal local installs and from the checkout in contributor or
  remote-server installs.
- Until Developer ID signing and notarization exist, the downloadable Mac
  package is a tester artifact. Source builds remain the fallback for Intel Macs
  and users who do not want to approve an ad-hoc-signed app.
- The terminal installer may remove =com.apple.quarantine= from the installed
  app if a local DMG source already carried it. That is acceptable only because
  this is documented as an explicit free/dev tester install path.

** Evidence
- [2026-05-29 Fri 10:33] Implemented a Tauri v2 desktop shell under
  =src-tauri/=. The app bundles =ui/dist=, the =orgasmic= sidecar binary,
  shipped worker/prompt/schema/scaffold content, and README/CONTRIBUTING docs.

  =npm --prefix ui run tauri:build -- --bundles app= produced
  =src-tauri/target/release/bundle/macos/orgasmic.app=.
- [2026-05-29 Fri 10:33] Packaged-app runtime smoke passed with a fresh

  =/healthz= and authenticated =/daemon/status= responded, CORS allowed a

* DONE TASK-AJZCK Smoke-test rmux hybrid driver and Web Share integration :drivers:rmux:web_share:smoke:terminal:manager:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-AJZCK
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX arch_C87Z9 arch_MK2Q2
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/rmux.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs ui/src/components/manager/** shipped/workers/*rmux*.org docs/**
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ws.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs ui/src/components/manager/** ui/src/lib/types.ts shipped/workers/** docs/**
:READ_SCOPE:       CLAUDE.md .orgasmic/decisions.org .orgasmic/architecture.org .orgasmic/glossary.org crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** ui/src/components/manager/** shipped/workers/** Cargo.toml src-tauri/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon; npm --prefix ui run build
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-S81TJ TASK-G6EV7 TASK-B5GFJ TASK-GMR53
:END:

** Description
Smoke-test strategy 3 from the rmux exploration: add a hybrid rmux path that
uses =rmux-sdk = "0.5"= for typed session control while still bundling or
provisioning the =rmux= daemon binary. The smoke should prove whether rmux can
honestly become an alternative terminal driver to the current tmux+xterm path
without claiming that the SDK dependency alone is support.

Keep this as a bounded proof. Do not replace the tmux default or update the
manager architecture decisions as if rmux is production-ready until the live
smoke passes. If any rmux or Web Share capability is missing in the current
release, capture the exact command, daemon/API response, and follow-up shape
instead of papering over it with a fictional integration.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] A minimal =rmux= mode can create or reuse one detached session for a real
  harness command through the SDK, using either =RMUX_SDK_DAEMON_BINARY= or a
  PATH-discovered =rmux= binary.
- [X] Missing prerequisites are reported honestly: the manager driver catalog
  checks the rmux mode binary separately from the selected harness binary.
- [~] The browser manager can attach to the rmux session through the existing
  xterm bridge using =rmux attach-session=, or the task records a concrete
  incompatibility with command output. (Recorded as a deferred limitation: see
  Evidence. =rmux= ships =attach-session= but wiring it through the daemon PTY/
  xterm bridge + supervisor reattach is out of scope for this bounded smoke; the
  driver returns =NotReattachable= rather than fake a bridge.)
- [X] The driver emits the expected ready, text, completion, and failure events
  for a bounded smoke run while preserving supervisor lease/release semantics.
- [X] Web Share proof creates at least one spectator URL and one operator URL,
  or records the exact current rmux daemon/API limitation if either URL cannot
  be produced.
- [X] Operator URLs and pairing tokens are treated as privileged shell access:
  no token is committed, persisted unnecessarily, or exposed as a spectator
  link.
- [X] Verification covers registry/support-matrix tests, missing-binary tests,
  the relevant daemon/UI build checks when touched, and a documented live smoke
  command with output and date.

** Evidence
Implemented as a hybrid SDK-backed smoke driver (not a tmux replacement; see
dec_J4FYS). Files: =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/rmux.rs= (driver),
=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs= + =modes/mod.rs= (registry: =rmux= mode,
=("rmux","codex")= SUPPORTED pair), =crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs=
(mirrored pair), =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= (catalog =mode_binary= /
=mode_installed= separate from harness binary), =ui/src/lib/types.ts=,
=shipped/workers/implementer-codex-rmux.org=.

*** Live smoke (2026-06-05) — real rmux binary
Provisioned a real binary with =cargo install rmux --version 0.5.0= and pointed
=RMUX_SDK_DAEMON_BINARY= at it, then drove a detached session through the SDK
(=Rmux::ensure_session= + =Session::share=). Ready capabilities observed:
#+begin_example
PROBE: found=true source=env path=/tmp/rmux-smoke-root/bin/rmux
READY proto=rmux-smoke/1  inert=false
  session = orgasmic-rmux-smoke-run-128f3c13-d4ee-4086-b02e-77a6d5d7f85b
  rmux_binary = { found:true, path:/.../rmux, source:env }
  web_share = {
    attempted: true,
    spectator_url: "https://share.rmux.io/#t=<spectator-token-redacted>",
    operator_minted: true,
    operator_url_redacted: "https://share.rmux.io/#<operator-token-redacted>"
  }
EVENT=RunComplete { summary: "smoke done" }   (lease released)
#+end_example
A real detached session was created and BOTH a spectator URL and an operator URL
were minted; the operator token is surfaced only in redacted form and never
persisted.

*** Honest degradation (binary absent)
With =RMUX_SDK_DAEMON_BINARY= unset and no =rmux= on PATH the same code path
reports =inert=true=, =inert_reason="rmux_binary_missing"=, =session=null=, and
=web_share.attempted=false= — no fabricated session.

  (drivers 70+22, core 29+fixtures, daemon 122; rmux: 14 unit tests incl.

- =npm --prefix ui run build= → PASS (types.ts touched).

*** Land gate (2026-06-05)
- Gates re-verified at land: =cargo fmt --check= → PASS, =git diff --check=
  → PASS (run directly). Full =cargo test= / =cargo clippy --workspace -D

- Reviewer verdict: SHIP. Findings 0H/1M/2L. The one medium (SDK-error inert
