  driver + Web Share smoke proof").

*** Test-hygiene follow-up (2026-06-05)
A driver test on the rmux/tmux inert path leaks an orphan =sleep 3600= child
that inherits the test runner's stdio and holds those pipes open after the test
completes. As a result, piped =cargo test= invocations (e.g. =cargo test 2>&1 |
tail=) hang on pipe EOF until the orphan exits. Same family as TASK-HJBXF.3.
Filed as fix-subtask TASK-AJZCK.1 (P3).

*** Deferred (not claimed as working)
- In-app xterm reattach via =rmux attach-session= (driver returns
  =NotReattachable=).
- Supervisor wiring / default-pipeline routing (rmux is registered but nothing
  routes to it by default).
- Tunnel/frontend config so Web Share URLs are externally reachable, and Tauri
  sidecar bundling of the rmux binary.

** Notes
- Prefer the hybrid path: SDK-backed driver plus bundled/provisioned rmux
  binary. The SDK starts or connects to a daemon but does not remove the binary
  requirement.
- For the Tauri app, evaluate bundling rmux as a sidecar and setting
  =RMUX_SDK_DAEMON_BINARY=. For remote Linux daemon mode, evaluate packaged or
  provisioned rmux on the daemon host.
- Treat Web Share as an additive operator/spectator URL feature first. The
  current in-app xterm attach path can stay as the initial manager terminal
  surface if it works with =rmux attach-session=.

* DONE TASK-AJZCK.1 fix-subtask: rmux/tmux driver tests must reap spawned placeholder processes (orphan sleep 3600 holds inherited stdio open) :drivers:test-cleanup:fix:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-AJZCK.1
:PRIORITY:         P3
:END:

** Description
The rmux/tmux driver tests spawn a placeholder =sleep 3600= child (the bounded
smoke/inert-path harness command) and do not reap it when the test run
completes. The orphaned =sleep 3600= inherits the test runner's stdio and holds
those pipes open, so piped =cargo test= invocations (e.g. =cargo test 2>&1 |
tail=) hang on pipe EOF until the orphan eventually exits. The driver tests
must kill/wait the spawned placeholder process (or spawn it without inheriting
the parent's stdio) so the test run releases all inherited file descriptors on
completion. Same family as TASK-HJBXF.3.

** Evidence
[2026-06-05 Fri] Closed.

*** Scope correction
The title named the rmux/tmux *driver* tests, but the actual orphaned
=sleep 3600= was produced by the daemon supervisor test
=poll_direct_child_pid_prefers_worker_server_over_generic_sibling= in
=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs= (the only literal =sleep 3600= in the
tree). The drivers-crate =sleep 60/30= placeholders all run *inside* a tmux/rmux
session, so tmux owns their stdio and they are torn down with the session — a
clean `orgasmic_drivers` lib-test run leaves no orphans and its piped run
terminates promptly. The leaker is the fake =cursor-agent= wrapper script
=#!/bin/bash\nsleep 300 &\nexec -a worker-server sleep 300 &\nsleep 3600=: the
two backgrounded =sleep 300 &= children reparent to init (ppid 1), inherit the
test runner's stdout/stderr, and hold a piped =cargo test 2>&1 | tail= open on
EOF for ~5min. =wrapper.kill()/.wait()= only reaped the foreground =sleep 3600=.
The sibling test =direct_child_pid_finds_wrapper_child_process= (=sh -c "sleep
300 & cat"=) had the same backgrounded-orphan shape.

*** Fix (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs)
Both tests now: (a) =stdout(Stdio::null()).stderr(Stdio::null())= so spawned
children inherit no test fds (this alone breaks the EOF hang), AND (b)
=.process_group(0)= (CommandExt, stable 1.64 — MSRV-safe) so the wrapper is its
own group leader, then on cleanup =kill -TERM -<pgid>= reaps the whole group
(wrapper + backgrounded sleeps) before =wrapper.kill()/.wait()=. Belt-and-braces
per the task: null stdio so a kill miss can't re-create the hang, plus
group-kill so an orphan can't survive. =direct_child_pid_finds_wrapper_child_process=
gained =#[cfg(unix)]= (it now uses unix-only =process_group= /
=kill -TERM -pgid=), matching the already-unix-gated sibling test.

*** Gate results
- =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=: ok, 56 passed / 1 ignored / 0 failed (lib);
  integration + doc suites green.
- PROOF (regression): before fix, piping the two supervisor tests to =tail=
  hung 40s+ with orphaned =sleep 300=/=worker-server 300=/=sleep 3600= at ppid 1.
  After fix, the same direct-binary run pipes to =tail= and terminates in ~1s;
  =pgrep -fl 'sleep 3600'= → none; no =sleep 300=/=worker-server 300= orphans
  remain.
- =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers=: 73 lib tests green; one *pre-existing,
  environmental* failure =real_cursor_agent_stream_json_bridge_emits_events_and_releases=
  (the real =cursor-agent= CLI is on this host's PATH so the live smoke runs and
  times out without auth/network). Unrelated to this fix — no drivers-crate files
  were modified.
- =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings=: clean.
- =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check=: clean.

* DONE TASK-AJZCK.2 fix-subtask: real cursor-agent live smoke leaves a worker-server node process behind :drivers:cursor-agent:test-cleanup:fix:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-AJZCK.2
:PRIORITY:         P4
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:END:

** Description
Surfaced during TASK-AJZCK.1: the gated live smoke
=real_cursor_agent_stream_json_bridge_emits_events_and_releases= (runs only
when the real =cursor-agent= CLI is on PATH) leaves a =worker-server= node
process behind after the run — a latent leak in the same family as the
TASK-AJZCK.1 supervisor-test orphan, but in a live test in
=crates/orgasmic-drivers=. The test also times out environmentally
(auth/network) on hosts where cursor-agent is installed but not
authenticated; consider gating on an explicit env opt-in
(=ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE=1=) in addition to binary presence, and ensure
the spawned process tree is reaped on every exit path (kill+wait process
group, null stdio), mirroring the TASK-AJZCK.1 fix shape.

** Evidence
- *Date*: [2026-06-05 Fri].
- *Fix shape (crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/cursor_agent.rs)*: two parts.
  (1) *Double-gated* =real_cursor_agent_stream_json_bridge_emits_events_and_releases= on =ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE=1= IN ADDITION to =cursor-agent= binary presence. The env opt-in is checked first and emits a skip message; an unauthenticated cursor-agent host no longer flakes CI. The env var is documented in the test's doc-comment with the run command.
  (2) *Reap the process group* via a =ProcessGroupGuard(Option<u32>)= Drop-guard bound right after =acquire= as =let _reap = ProcessGroupGuard(session.pid);=. The driver spawns cursor-agent under =setsid= (its pid is the pgid, surfaced as =session.pid=) and cursor-agent forks a node =worker-server= child; =release= only kills the direct child, orphaning worker-server. On drop the guard runs =kill -- -<pgid>= with null stdio, reaping the whole subtree on success/assert-failure/panic. Mirrors the TASK-AJZCK.1 / TASK-HCFBT.5 (=kill -- -PID= + null stdio) reference shape.
- *Gates*: =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= EXIT=0; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= EXIT=0; =cargo fmt --check= EXIT=0; =git diff --check= EXIT=0.
- *Orphan proof*: default run skips the smoke (=ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE= unset → "set ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE=1 to opt in"). Live run =ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE=1 cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers --test cursor_agent real_cursor_agent= executed end-to-end (1 passed, 9.34s — a real cursor-agent turn, not a skip) and post-run =pgrep -fl "worker-server|cursor-agent"= → none.

* DONE TASK-AJZCK.3 fix-subtask: production release must reap the spawned process group, not just the direct child :drivers:subprocess:lifecycle:fix:medium:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-AJZCK.3
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/tests/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:END:

** Description
Surfaced during the TASK-AJZCK.2 close (commit =739a697=): the orphan that the
test-side =ProcessGroupGuard= now reaps in the cursor-agent live smoke ALSO
occurs in production dispatch. =subprocess_stream_json.rs='s release/exit path
(=kill_subprocess=) calls =child.kill()= only — the direct child dies but the
=setsid= process group it created (e.g. cursor-agent's forked =worker-server=
node process) survives every production release. Fix: reap the whole process
group on release (=kill -- -<pgid>= TERM, escalate to KILL after a grace
window), mirroring the now-established TASK-AJZCK.1/100.5/104.2 fix shape, with
a regression test where the spawned command forks a surviving child. Audit the
other subprocess-spawning drivers (acp_stdio child handling) for the same
direct-child-only kill while in there.

** Evidence
- *Date*: [2026-06-05 Fri].
- *Root cause*: =detach_subprocess= spawns the harness under =setsid= (child pid
  == pgid; descendants like cursor-agent's node =worker-server= inherit the
  group), but the release/exit path called =child.kill()= only, orphaning every
  forked descendant on each production release. TASK-AJZCK.2's test-side
  =ProcessGroupGuard= had only papered this over for the live smoke.
- *Fix shape*: added =#[cfg(unix)] reap_process_group(&mut Child)= in
  =subprocess_stream_json.rs= that signals the GROUP — =kill(-pgid, SIGTERM)=, a
  ~2s grace window polling =child.try_wait()=, then =kill(-pgid, SIGKILL)= for
  stragglers — and finally =child.wait()=s the direct child (reaping its zombie +
  surfacing exit status to =finalize_subprocess_exit=). =kill(2)= is declared via
  the same =extern "C"= idiom as the existing =setsid=. Release semantics
  (supervisor lease release, terminal events) are unchanged; only descendant
  reaping is added. Non-unix keeps prior =child.kill()=+=wait()=.
  =run_subprocess_stream_json='s release branch now calls =reap_process_group=.
- *acp_stdio audit conclusion*: =acp_stdio.rs= ALSO setsids (identical
  =detach_subprocess=) and ALSO killed the direct child only on all three
  termination paths — so it IS affected and was fixed the same way. It now
  imports =reap_process_group= and uses it on (a) the handshake-failure early
  return, (b) the terminal-emitted path (now sets =released=true= and falls
  through to the shared exit reap instead of a direct =child.kill()=), and (c)
  the final release/EOF/command-close exit block. Acquire-time
  stdin/stdout/stderr-unavailable error paths keep the direct =child.kill()=
  (child just spawned, no descendants forked yet).
- *Regression test*: =subprocess_release_reaps_whole_process_group= (=#[cfg(unix)]=,
  =driver_modes.rs=) drives the subprocess path with
  =sh -c 'sh -c ": MARKER; sleep 3600" & printf up; sleep 3600'=, asserts the
  marker'd descendant is alive BEFORE release and gone AFTER. A
  =ProcessGroupGuard= Drop-guard reaps defensively on any failure unwind with
  null stdio.
- *MSRV*: 1.78-safe (tokio =Child::try_wait/id/kill/wait=, no post-1.78 std).
- *Gates*: =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= EXIT=0; =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=
  EXIT=0; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= EXIT=0;
  =cargo fmt --check= + =git diff --check= clean.
- *Orphan proof*: post-suite =pgrep -fl "worker-server|sleep 3600|orgasmic-reap"=
  → none.
- *Live smoke*: =ORGASMIC_LIVE_CURSOR_SMOKE=1 cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
  --test cursor_agent real_cursor_agent= ran a real cursor-agent turn (1 passed,
  9.13s — not a skip) and post-run =pgrep -fl "worker-server"= → none, proving
  the production release path reaps the real =worker-server= (the test guard is
  now a no-op belt-and-suspenders).

* DONE TASK-G9KCN First-class CLI for goal lifecycle + reliable project-targeted tx append — stop hand-editing goal.org and hand-appending tx :cli:daemon:dx:manager:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-G9KCN
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
UPDATE [2026-06-12]: bumped P3→P2 — dec_ZQJPA (agents edit org state only
through the daemon) makes this task a dependency of the doctrine flip;
TASK-NP5H6 owns the create/property/body remainder of the write surface.

Two manager-workflow operations have no working CLI path, so the manager
hand-edits org files and hand-constructs tx records — slow and error-prone.
Surfaced live [2026-06-11] clearing goal-20260611: the flip took a manual
heading edit + a hand-built =manager.clear_goal= tx (synthesised TX_ID,
property drawer, appended to the project tx file by hand).

1. *Goal lifecycle has no verb.* =orgasmic --help= exposes no =goal= command;
   set/clear/supersede are done by editing =tasks/goal.org= directly
   (=GOAL=↔=CLEARED=/=SUPERSEDED=, =:STATUS:=, =:CLEARED_AT:=/=:CLEARED_BY:=
   / =:SUPERSEDED_AT:=) AND hand-appending a =manager.set_goal= /
   =manager.clear_goal= tx. Every goal change is a multi-step manual ritual.
2. *tx append is unreliable for project-targeted records.* =orgasmic tx record=
   exists but =--tx-path= defaults to =$ORGASMIC_HOME/state/tx/YYYY-MM.org=
   (home), and the known =tx_destination= home-fallback bug (TASK-RVEKT/TASK-E9K1B
   family) means a project record can silently vanish into =~/.orgasmic=. So
   goal/manual tx are still hand-appended to =.orgasmic/tx/= to be safe. Need an
   append that reliably targets the PROJECT tx file when =--project= is given
   and persists (fsync — see TASK-C2PQ3).
3. *Related — task state flips outside dispatch return 501.* =orgasmic task
   update --state done= → =501 "task heading mutations are graph rewrites; will
   land with the manager pivot" (tracked_by TASK-TQQQR)=. Yet =dispatch-close=
   flips task lifecycle reliably today, so the machinery exists — expose it for
   standalone task-state and goal-heading flips instead of the 501.

Design intent (operator, 2026-06-11): a working CLI to set/clear goals and to
add tx, so the manager never hand-edits =goal.org= or hand-builds tx drawers.
Coordinate with TASK-TQQQR (the manager-pivot graph-rewrite path) and TASK-GBT5W
(daemon ID allocation); this can land the goal + tx verbs ahead of the full
pivot by reusing the dispatch-close lifecycle-write path.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic goal set|clear|supersede= verbs flip =tasks/goal.org='s active
      heading (keyword + status + =*_AT=/=*_BY= props, =:REPLACES:= on set) AND
      append the matching =manager.*_goal= tx atomically, daemon-persisted to
      the PROJECT tx file. No manual org edit or tx construction needed.
- [ ] =orgasmic tx record --project <id>= reliably appends to that project's tx
      file (not the home fallback) and the record survives a daemon restart.
- [ ] Standalone task lifecycle flip works (=task update --state=, or a
      dedicated verb) — the 501 is replaced for at least the state-keyword flip.
- [X] Tests: goal set→clear→supersede round-trip persists in goal.org + tx;
      =tx record= lands in the project file; task-state flip persists.

** Evidence
- implementer-cursor / composer-2.5-fast,
  run-20260612T113715-d4a319bb9b06425683ae06d6bde2b60b, worker commit
  =13fecb4= merged --no-ff =d75c931=; manager review-and-fix =6c81101=:
  post-refresh handler =.expect()=s → ApiError (daemons don't panic on
  request races); goal statement/reached_when reject column-0 =*=/=#+=
  lines (the new write path must not bypass the TASK-HC7PW
  phantom-heading invariant); CLI =--project= no longer defaults to the
  literal "orgasmic" — resolves the enclosing project from cwd;
  manager.clear_goal / manager.supersede_goal / task.state_transitioned
  declared in shipped/schema/tx.org; goal-lifecycle keyword set
  declared in shipped/schema/state-machine.org (TODO_KEYWORDS mirror
  invariant).
- Delivered: =orgasmic goal set|clear|supersede= → daemon endpoints
  =POST /projects/:id/goal/{set,clear,supersede}= rewriting goal.org
  via OrgRewriter primitives + matching manager.*_goal tx in ONE
  =writer.transaction= (C2PQ3 durability); GOAL/CLEARED/SUPERSEDED
  added to the core TODO keyword allowlist (live-corpus reparse
  verified by fixtures suite; no live headings outside goal.org start
  with those words); =orgasmic task update --state= replaces the 501
  for state flips (title keyword + :STATE: + task.state_transitioned
  tx via the daemon writer; non-state mutations still 501 →
  TASK-TQQQR/TASK-NP5H6); restart-survival test for
  =tx record --project=.
- Gates on merged main: 3/3 new integration tests green; failure set =
  established baseline exactly (ZD72S 5 + the cli dispatch.rs
  load-flake family, previously verified pass-alone); clippy lib/bin +
  cli all-targets clean; fmt clean.
- DOGFOODED AT CLOSE: this very heading was flipped IN_REVIEW→DONE via
  =orgasmic task update TASK-G9KCN --state done= on the rebuilt daemon
  (pid 86815) — first production use; task.state_transitioned tx
  daemon-persisted. NEW-LAYOUT LIFECYCLE PROOF also complete (first
  full cycle on the TASK-7R3NS layout): worktree under
  .orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/task-g9kcn/ with git status clean /
  parse-errors 56 / projects 1 throughout; close left the stem dir
  holding ONLY the brief.
- Residual: standalone state flip does not validate transitions against
  the state machine (manager-authority verb — any→any, documented);
  dispatch-close's own lifecycle write still uses the local rewrite
  path; =orgasmic task update --project= still defaults to the literal
  "orgasmic" (pre-existing wart, same fix as goal.rs — fold into
  TASK-NP5H6).

* DONE TASK-RCP69 Pre-apply raw-body escape mode: persisting untrusted text (logs, stderr) must be the easy path, not a guard bounce :daemon:core:api:dx:hardening:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-RCP69
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/body_write_guard.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
From the round-4 architecture critique §2 (kernel adopted, framing rejected).
The TASK-HC7PW/TASK-HC7PW.1 guard fails closed on phantom-heading payloads: a body
write containing a column-0 =* line= bounces with =BodyHeadingInjection=.
Correct as the invariant — but it leaves no convenient path for the
legitimate case the critic names: persisting raw untrusted text (a worker's
stderr, a crash dump, a pasted log) whose lines happen to start with =*= or
=#+=. Today every caller must know to wrap/escape by hand or the write
bounces; the safe path should be the easy path.

Add an opt-in raw-content mode at the body-write API boundary (e.g.
=body_format: raw= on the body-edit ops): the daemon wraps the payload in
=#+begin_example ... #+end_example= and applies org comma-escaping to
embedded =*=/=#+= lines BEFORE splicing, so the payload round-trips
losslessly as verbatim content and can never alter structure.

The structural-invariance guard stays exactly as is. The critic proposed
pre-apply escaping INSTEAD of the post-apply check — rejected: silently
transforming default writes would mask genuine injection bugs and surprise
callers. Escaping is a convenience callers opt into for designated raw
content; the fail-closed guard remains the gate behind it.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Raw mode: payload with column-0 =* X= / =#+end_example= lines is
  accepted, wrapped, comma-escaped, round-trips byte-stable, and re-parses
  with zero structural change (guard still passes).
- [ ] Adversarial content cannot escape the wrapper (a payload containing its
  own =#+end_example= line is escaped, not interpreted as the block end).
- [ ] Default mode unchanged: unescaped phantom-heading payload still bounces
  with =BodyHeadingInjection= (existing body_write_guard suite green).
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon= green.

** Evidence
- Worker: =implementer-cursor= / composer-2.5-fast, TWO rounds. Round 1

  AddSection in api.rs via =prepare_body_edit=, 4 unit + 3 integration

  db8cd1d) fixed two manager-review findings vs the org spec: (1) payload
  without trailing newline glued =#+end_example= onto the last line —
  wrapper now closes on its own line; (2) lines already comma-prefixed
  before =*=/=#+= (=,* x=) were lossy (org strips one comma on
  extraction) — now gain exactly one more comma, rule mirrors Emacs

  placement fixed at integration (manager, trivial). Merged d9da9b9
  (--no-ff); dispatch-close tx-0175. Reviewer stage skipped per operator
  override — manager reviewed both rounds.
- AC walk: adversarial =#+end_example= payload comma-escaped, cannot
  close wrapper (unit + integration); raw round-trip byte-stable on disk,

* DONE TASK-T2FSY Identity scheme for multi-dev same-repo, across node classes (tasks, decisions, goals, tx/runs) — decide before TASK-GBT5W implements :architecture:identity:concurrency:design:
:PROPERTIES:

:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/tmp/design/task-157/

** Description
Operator direction [2026-06-11]: assume parallel work by several devs on the
same repo — different modules, WRITE_SCOPEs never overlap, but each dev runs
their own daemon on their own clone and =.orgasmic/= syncs through git. That
is multi-master: dec_SSJVD's revisit trigger ("the day two daemons must merge
graphs") fires the day a second dev joins, which for a coordination tool is
the adoption path, not an edge case. Per-clone sequencing (TASK-GBT5W) cannot
see the other clone's allocations — both daemons will hand out the same next
TASK-NNN. Same-file appends at least conflict textually at merge; a
sprint.org-vs-backlog.org pair merges SILENTLY → duplicate ID → ambiguous
DEPENDS_ON / dispatch / lifecycle targets. And every week of adoption embeds
more TASK-NNN references in prose and commit messages — re-keying later is
exactly the rewrite-every-reference cost conceded in the round-4 critique.
Decide now, while it is cheap.

Design the identity scheme and deliver the ADR draft. Leading option
(manager assessment — validate, don't rubber-stamp):
- *Stable key*: ULID per task in the property drawer (=:UID:= or a
  repurposed =:ID:=), generated at filing, collision-proof with zero
  coordination. Invisible in prose; costs one drawer line; anchors
  mechanical re-aliasing and disambiguates if the alias layer is ever
  violated.
- *Readable alias (the human surface)*: OPERATOR CONSTRAINT [2026-06-11]:
  NO checked-in actor registry — the =.orgasmic/actors.org= roster concept
  is rejected. No registration ceremony, no people-administration file in
  repo state; identity must ride what git already has, or not exist at
  all. Two candidate shapes remain; the ADR weighs them:
  1. /Plain TASK-NNN + merge-time repair/ (doctrine-consistent with
     dec_MK7AH detect-and-reconcile): each clone allocates max+1 locally;
     cross-clone duplicates are RARE (only filings concurrent between
     syncs), detection is certain (post-pull doctor lint over alias
     uniqueness), repair is mechanical (the ULID anchors each heading; the
     younger filing is renumbered and all in-repo references — DEPENDS_ON,
     org-file prose — rewritten in the same repair commit). Cost: an ID
     can change shortly after filing, inside a short window where few
     external references exist. Zero onboarding friction; IDs stay short.
  2. /Derived namespace, roster-less/: actor slug derived from existing
     git identity (=user.email= local-part, optionally +2-char hash) —
     nothing registered anywhere. Prevents instead of repairs; cost:
     longer IDs everywhere forever to handle a rare event, and
     same-human-two-clones still self-collides (same derived slug), so
     repair machinery is needed anyway.
  3. /Single collision-free ID, NO short sequential ID at all/ (operator
     probe [2026-06-11]). Architectural wins are real: no sequencer, no
     alias, no repair machinery, one immutable ID forever; any agent mints
     final IDs offline; hallucinated references become DETECTABLE (an LLM
     cannot guess a valid high-entropy ID — a fabricated ref dangles and
     the lint catches it, whereas a wrong-but-plausible TASK-C2PQ3 ref looks
     real and is uncatchable). Two sub-variants with very different
     usability:
     3a. /Full ULID (26 chars) as the only ID/. Usability cost lands
         exactly where this product is strongest: org files ARE a primary
         human surface (not a hidden DB), so prose refs, heading scans,
         and diffs carry 26-char noise; nothing is speakable or memorable
         (humans fall back to ambiguous titles); CLI/phone steering needs
         copy-paste (mitigable git-style with unambiguous-prefix
         addressing); and LLM context economy degrades — a ULID is ~10+
         tokens vs 3-4 for TASK-NNN, multiplied across every
         sprint/handoff/brief read, directly inflating the
         compiled-bundle-tokens metric dec_MX9RM optimizes. Precedent
         in-repo: run/tx IDs are already long and nobody suffers —
         because nobody SPEAKS them; tasks are the conversational
         currency, which is precisely where the pain would concentrate.
     3b. /Short random ID as the only ID/ (=TASK-7QK4= style, 5-6
         base32 chars): same decentralized wins as 3a in practice
         (birthday math: 32^6 ≈ 1e9 — collision odds negligible at
         realistic task counts, and the post-pull lint + ULID-grade
         regeneration repair the freak case), at a fraction of the
         usability cost: speakable, typeable on a phone, greppable,
         ~same token cost as TASK-NNN. Loses vs sequential: no
         chronology signal in the ID and weaker memorability than small
         integers. If short IDs are dropped, 3b dominates 3a on every
         usability axis; 3a's only edges are the theoretical guarantee
         and the embedded timestamp.
- *Merge-time lint*: doctor checks alias + stable-key uniqueness after
  pulls; a detected duplicate alias → mechanical rename anchored by the
  stable key.
- Single-actor repos (shape 2 only): decide whether the prefix is elided
  (plain TASK-NNN preserved for solo use — today's repos unchanged) or
  always-on for uniformity.

SCOPE EXTENSION [2026-06-11, operator]: the decision applies PER NODE CLASS,
not to tasks alone — and the system already practices both poles, which the
ADR should treat as evidence:
- /Already collision-free, decentralized/: tx records
  (=tx-20260611183800-e0a228e7=) and runs (=run-20260611T144846-52062dba=)
  use timestamp+random — high-rate, machine-generated, never spoken aloud;
  nobody suffers their length. Goals use date+slug
  (=goal-20260611-prod-ready-for-strangers=) — readable AND practically
  collision-free.
- /Sequential counters at risk/: TASK-NNN and dec_NNN (69 records) are the
  only max+1 classes. Decisions are LOWER risk today than tasks: one file,
  appends at the tail, so concurrent filings almost always conflict
  textually at merge instead of merging silently — but the conflict still
  forces manual renumbering, and the risk grows if decisions ever split
  across files (archive). Subtask IDs (TASK-HC7PW.1) derive from the parent
  and inherit its scheme.
- /Slug-named/: conventions, glossary terms, workers — collisions are
  semantic (two people naming the same concept), caught textually; out of
  scope beyond a lint mention.
The emergent principle the ADR should make explicit and unify: ID entropy
follows the node's REFERENCE PROFILE — the more a class is spoken/memorized
by humans and agents in conversation (tasks, decisions), the more readable
its ID must be; the more machine-minted and machine-consumed (tx, runs),
the more entropy it can carry. Whatever surface wins for tasks applies to
decisions too (dec_NNN has the same conversational profile), and the
repair/lint machinery (post-pull uniqueness lint + stable-key anchored
rename) must be built ONCE, generic over node classes — not task-specific.

Framings to engage honestly in the ADR rather than dismiss: raw ULID/UUID
everywhere (kills grep/diff/prose readability — a load-bearing product
property, dec_SSJVDa); the round-4 critic's central-daemon async alias
assignment (in multi-master there is no central daemon — locally-assigned
UNQUALIFIED aliases just move the collision from IDs to aliases). Also name,
but do not solve, the sibling gaps the same topology opens (cross-clone
lease/dispatch coordination, handoff.org as a shared-mutable singleton, tx
sequence merging) so the ADR bounds what identity does and does not fix.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR draft (decision + alternatives considered + migration path for
  existing TASK-NNN/dec_NNN IDs + single-dev story) delivered for manager
  integration into decisions.org (supersedes/extends dec_SSJVDa).
- [ ] Per-node-class identity matrix: tasks, subtasks, decisions, goals,
  tx, runs, slug-named classes (conventions/glossary/workers) — scheme,
  collision risk, detection + repair story for each, under one stated
  principle (reference profile decides entropy vs readability); repair
  machinery specified generic over classes.
- [ ] TASK-GBT5W re-scope proposal: what the allocator looks like under the
  chosen scheme (namespace parameter, stable-key generation point, registry
  check).
- [ ] Collision matrix covered: same-file concurrent append, cross-file
  silent merge, actor-name collision, offline filing, AND same human on two
  clones (laptop+desktop registered as the same actor — the namespace unit
  is the daemon, not the human; weigh serialize-by-pull-with-lint-repair vs
  per-clone slugs) — each with a detection + repair story.
- [ ] Explicit cost accounting: extra drawer line, ID length in prose, CLI
  typing burden, speakability/memorability (human conversation is a primary
  reference surface), phone-steering copy-paste, LLM token cost in compiled
  bundles (dec_MX9RM metric), and reference-failure modes
  (wrong-but-plausible sequential ref vs detectable dangling high-entropy
  ref) — judged against the readability baseline.

** Evidence
- RESOLVED IN-SESSION [2026-06-11 22:45], no dispatch: the 4-round design
  dialogue (this task's description IS the analysis — shapes 1/2/3a/3b,
  node-class census, reference-profile principle, registry rejection) ended
  with the operator choosing shape 1: keep TASK-NNN/dec_NNN. ADR landed as
  =dec_X72P5= (manager-written, per design-docs-live-in-decisions): merge-time
  repair as semantic merge conflict, first-to-main keeps the ID, incoming
  side renumbers to next-free-in-merged-tree, =:RENAMED_FROM:= + merge sha
  in the drawer, =:UID:= ULID anchor minted at filing; the operator's
  hash-qualified rename (=TASK-<short-hash>-NNN=) recorded as the documented
  fallback for late-discovered collisions (deterministic + provenance, but
  permanent namespace scarring as default). Follow-ups: TASK-GBT5W re-scoped
  (allocator + UID minting, gate lifted), TASK-QRD3Y filed (generic collision
  lint + mechanical repair). All four AC delivered via dec_X72P5 + the task
  description's matrix and cost accounting.
- REVERSED [2026-06-11 ~23:26]: operator reconsidered → shape 3b. New
  tasks/decisions mint short-random IDs (=TASK-XXXXX=, 5-char Crockford
  base32, ≥1 letter); legacy numerics FROZEN, max+1 abolished; permanent
  mixed scheme accepted for this repo; no separate =:UID:= (the random ID
  is the stable key); tx/runs/goals/slugs stand (already collision-free).
  dec_X72P5 rewritten in place (git holds the shape-1 version); TASK-GBT5W
  re-scoped to mint-on-create + =id mint= verb; TASK-QRD3Y re-scoped to
  mixed-scheme lint + re-mint repair (hash-qualified fallback moot).
- ESCALATED TO FULL MIGRATION [2026-06-11 ~23:45]: operator extended 3b to
  a full re-key — no mixed scheme; TASK/dec/arch/term classes all migrate
  to minted IDs with =:RENAMED_FROM:= provenance. dec_X72P5 rewritten again
  (third version; git holds all). TASK-YRK1V filed (first minted-ID node,
  migration execution, after TASK-GBT5W's code-readiness audit); TASK-GBT5W
  extended with the audit + arch/term minting; TASK-QRD3Y flipped to
  post-migration lint rules and gated on the migration.
- GREENFIELD-CLEAN MAPPING [2026-06-12 ~00:20]: operator flagged
  =:RENAMED_FROM:= as migration scar tissue (344 permanent drawer lines +
  product machinery for one repo's history). dec_X72P5 v4: the mapping is
  the tagged migration commit (=id-migration=); lookup =git show
  id-migration | grep <old-id>=; resolver verb dropped from TASK-GBT5W;
  TASK-QRD3Y lint reduced to duplicates + malformed (malformed check
  subsumes legacy-leftover detection generically); repair provenance =
  repair commit, no drawers anywhere. Migrated state is byte-for-byte

** Description
Implements dec_X72P5(c) lint + repair under the FULL-MIGRATION decision
(shape 3b escalated: short-random IDs everywhere, legacy re-keyed by
TASK-YRK1V). The random ID is the stable key; collisions are freak events,
but detection must be certain and repair mechanical. This lint is also the
standing guard that the migration stays complete.

1. *Lint (detection)*: post-pull/post-merge doctor lint flags, across all
   live org state (tasks, decisions.org, architecture.org, glossary.org):
   (i) duplicate IDs; (ii) malformed identity IDs (wrong alphabet/length,
   all-digit) — which GENERICALLY catches sequential-numeric leftovers and
   writers still on max+1 doctrine, with zero legacy-specific knowledge.
   The lint is GREENFIELD-CLEAN (dec_X72P5b v4): it scans identity positions
   in org state only — it knows no legacy grammar, polices no code prose,
   needs no exemption lists. One-time legacy-leftover verification belongs
   to TASK-YRK1V. Precedent: the phantom-heading lint through the
   parse-errors channel (TASK-HC7PW).
2. *Repair verb (mechanical resolution)*: =orgasmic doctor
   --fix-id-collisions= (or equivalent) — for a freak random duplicate,
   RE-MINT the incoming side (per merge-parent attribution; first-to-main
   keeps its ID) and rewrite ALL in-repo references in the same pass
   (heading, =DEPENDS_ON= / =PARENT_TASK= drawers, org-file prose);
   provenance is the repair COMMIT itself — no provenance drawers
   (dec_X72P5c, git-is-history). Ambiguous loose-prose refs: attribute by
   which merge parent's diff introduced the line; refuse with a clear
   message rather than guess. Idempotent: re-running after repair is a
   no-op.

3. *Riders from the post-migration audit [2026-06-12 Fri]* (the 6 legacy
   code markers the audit found were fixed manager-side same day):
   - Dangling-reference lint: reference positions — =:RELATES_TO:= /
     =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= tokens, and indexed advisory-marker ids — that
     resolve to no node =:ID:=. Generic, zero legacy knowledge. Known
     catches at filing: 17 bare slugs in glossary =:RELATES_TO:=
     (worker-run ×12 — likely deserves a real term) + ~17 more in
     cross-file =:GLOSSARY_REFS:=; all pre-existed the migration. Decide
     mint-term-or-prune per slug at implementation.
   - Marker-grammar alignment: scripts/id-migration.py
     =is_structured_marker_payload= requires an ids-only payload and
     =should_skip_path= drops =crates/*/tests/= wholesale, while the
     daemon's =parse_marker_payload= (index.rs) prefix-parses annotated
     markers anywhere — that mismatch is exactly how the migration missed
     6 markers. Pick ONE canonical marker grammar and make script and
     daemon share it (or spec it in one place both assert against).
     Concrete divergences verified live [2026-06-12 Fri]: (a) daemon
     =is_marker_id_byte= excludes =.= — subtask marker task_CJWT3.1
     indexes truncated as task_CJWT3; (b) the daemon line-scans string
     literals — id-migration.py's SELF_TEST_TREE fixtures index as
     dangling markers (task_156/task_999/task_1860D/dec_V056D →
     scripts/id-migration.py), same class as index.rs's dec_001..007
     test literals.

(The shape-1 renumber-to-next-free and hash-qualified late-collision
fallback are moot in random space — a re-mint costs the same as any other
new ID.)

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lint flags duplicate IDs (synthetic merged tree, both sides minted
  the same random ID) and malformed identity IDs (synthetic all-digit /
  wrong-alphabet cases — covers sequential leftovers generically); zero
  false positives on the real post-migration repo.
- [ ] Repair re-mints the incoming side, rewrites references across all
  org task files + decisions.org, leaves no provenance drawers (the repair
  commit is the provenance); re-run is a no-op (test).
- [ ] Decisions (dec_* in decisions.org) covered by the same lint + repair
  path as tasks (test).
- [ ] Ambiguous reference attribution refuses with an actionable message
  (test).
- [X] Gates: =cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-cli=,
  clippy -D warnings, fmt --check.

** Evidence
- implementer-cursor / composer-2.5-fast,
  run-20260612T101126-60a1ff9901d84503a67261d9dd52a347, worker commit
  =7ecd152= merged --no-ff =6cc4f15=; manager review-and-fix at
  integration (no reviewer stage per round-4 operator process), fix-ups
  in =4440acc=: select_incoming_occurrence refuses instead of guessing
  occs.last() when git attribution is empty (+ regression test);
  replace_id_token treats =.= as boundary unless it opens a =.<digit>=
  suffix (sentence-final refs now rewrite, + test); 3 annotated org.rs

- Delivered: orgasmic-core marker.rs (canonical grammar: ids-only

- Gates on merged main: TEST_CMD suites green except the documented

  (lib/bin all crates; --all-targets clean for core+cli; daemon

- FIRST REAL CATCH: duplicate TASK-VXB0T (both were legacy TASK-132 —

  CLI verb correctly refuses): BACKLOG OS-service-adapters task
  re-keyed to minted TASK-R3TWA, DONE lifecycle-v3 task keeps the id.
  Post-repair probe: 0 duplicates, 0 malformed.

  =:RELATES_TO:=/=:GLOSSARY_REFS:= (worker-run, task, state-machine,
  acp, tmux_tui, …) — pre-existing data debt, surfaces in parse-errors

** Description
Executes dec_X72P5b (operator: FULL migration, no mixed scheme). This is the
first minted-ID node in the repo — filed under the new grammar.

Inventory at filing [2026-06-11]: 217 task headings (incl. subtasks) across
sprint/backlog/archive, 70 dec records, 17 arch records, 40 =term:= glossary
entries; ~772 =dec_= refs, ~897 =arch_= refs, TASK refs throughout org state
+ tx records, 8 code files with =orgasmic:task_= markers, shipped/ prompt
specs, test fixtures.

Method (script-driven, ONE commit, daemon quiesced for the apply +
restarted after). TWO-PHASE EXECUTION (manager, 2026-06-12): live
=.orgasmic/= state keeps moving while a worker holds a worktree snapshot,
so the WORKER delivers the migration script + rehearsal evidence (script
run inside its own worktree copy: mapping built, rewrite applied, greps
clean, workspace tests green) but does NOT migrate live state; the
MANAGER executes the merged script on quiesced fresh main, runs the
verification battery (greps, tests, daemon restart + parse_errors 0, UI
smoke), and lands the rewrite as the ONE tagged commit. The rehearsal
worktree diff is discarded — only the script + doc line merge.
1. Build the mapping: mint one conforming ID per node (=TASK-XXXXX= /
   =dec_XXXXX= / =arch_XXXXX= / =term_XXXXX=); subtask suffixes derive from
   the parent's new ID (TASK-HC7PW.1 → TASK-<new148>.1); dec clause refs
   migrate with their clause letter (dec_SSJVDa → dec_<new071>a).
2. Rewrite longest-match-first (subtask/clause refs before parent refs)
   across ALL live surfaces: org headings + drawers (=:ID:=, =DEPENDS_ON=,
   =PARENT_TASK=, =GLOSSARY_REFS=, =tracked_by=, =:SOURCE:=), org prose
   (incl. ranges/shorthand like "TASK-E9K1B/TASK-ZSD0B"), tx records (re-keying is
   not falsification — git holds the originals; UI tx→task links must
   resolve), goal/handoff, conventions, the structured =orgasmic:task_*=
   code markers (9 lines), shipped/ specs (50 lines). EXEMPT per dec_X72P5b
   carve-out: free-text code comments (497 mention lines / 61 crates/ui
   files — point-in-time annotations, bridged by the tagged migration
   commit per dec_X72P5 v4) and test
   fixtures (synthetic self-consistent org data; TASK-GBT5W's audit keeps
   suites green).
3. Land as ONE commit and tag it =id-migration= — the commit diff IS the
   durable mapping; NO =:RENAMED_FROM:= drawers (dec_X72P5b v4: migrated
   state must be indistinguishable from greenfield). Document the lookup
   one-liner (=git show id-migration | grep <old-id>=) in
   conventions/contributing.org. Git history and =archive/= essays
   intentionally keep old IDs. Historical artifact names in evidence prose
   (dead branches/worktrees like =task-103.1-impl=) are left as-is — they
   name artifacts that no longer exist.
4. Verify: zero matches for legacy patterns (=TASK-[0-9]=, =dec_[0-9]+=,
   =arch_[0-9]=, =term:[a-z]= as identity) in =.orgasmic/= + =shipped/= +
   structured markers — exempting =archive/= and free-text code comments;
   =cargo test --workspace= green; daemon restart
   + doctor clean (parse_errors 0); UI smoke — kanban renders, task lookup
   by new ID, tx links resolve.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mapping built and applied for all four classes in ONE commit, tagged
  =id-migration=; the lookup one-liner documented in
  conventions/contributing.org; zero =:RENAMED_FROM:= or other migration
  traces in live state.
- [ ] Zero legacy-pattern matches in =.orgasmic/= + =shipped/= + structured
  markers (archive/ and free-text code comments exempt per dec_X72P5b) —
  grep proof recorded in evidence.
- [ ] Subtask (=.k=) and dec clause (=dec_NNNa=) references migrated
  consistently with their parents (no orphaned suffixes).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= green; daemon restarts clean with
  parse_errors 0; UI kanban + task lookup + tx-link smoke pass.
- [ ] Migration script checked in (idempotent, dry-run mode) so the
  procedure is reproducible for other repos adopting dec_X72P5.

** Evidence
- EXECUTED [2026-06-12]: migration commit =a0ffba0= tagged =id-migration=
  (68 files, ~4,492 line pairs) — the commit diff IS the durable old->new
  mapping; lookup forever: =git show id-migration | grep <old-id>=
  (documented in conventions/contributing.org). 258 nodes re-keyed (131
  tasks incl. subtasks, 70 decisions, 17 architecture, 40 glossary terms;
  TASK-YRK1V/TASK-ZD72S/TASK-84QVC already minted, skipped), 6,661

* DONE TASK-NP5H6 Complete the daemon write surface: task create + property/body verbs through the CLI — kill the remaining 501s :daemon:cli:api:write-authority:dx:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-NP5H6
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-core/src/
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-G9KCN
:END:

** Description
dec_ZQJPA makes the daemon the only org-state write path for agents — but the
write surface is incomplete, so the manager still hand-edits. The gaps:

1. =orgasmic task update --state/--property= returns 501 ("graph rewrites land
   with the manager pivot, tracked_by TASK-TQQQR") — TASK-TQQQR is DONE, so
   this debt is orphaned. The state-keyword flip half is in TASK-G9KCN's AC;
   this task owns the property half.
2. There is NO =task create= verb. Filing a task means hand-editing
   backlog.org (every filing burst to date, including the one that filed this
   task). Creation paths exist daemon-side for subtasks (api.rs ~1172/1758
   hardcode sprint.org targets — coordinate with TASK-2C3WZ's file-per-state
   defaults) and =glossary create= shows the create-verb shape.
3. No CLI verb for body/section writes, though the API machinery is complete:
   =POST /org/node/:id/edit= with OCC =base_version= (api.rs ~7214-7306),
   structural-invariance guard (TASK-HC7PW), and =BodyFormat raw= escape
   wrapping (TASK-RCP69) all exist — unexposed to the agent surface.

Everything lands through the daemon writer loop + structural guard; no new
write path bypasses them.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic task create= files a new heading through the daemon: mints the
  ID (TASK-GBT5W path), writes title/tags/properties/body, lands in the
  correct file (backlog.org; respect dec_QQYXM defaults once TASK-2C3WZ lands).
- [ ] =orgasmic task update --property KEY=VALUE= round-trips; the 501 is gone
  for both =--state= (G9KCN may land it first — then regression-test only)
  and =--property=.
- [ ] A body-write verb (e.g. =orgasmic node body set/append=) exposes
  /org/node/:id/edit incl. =--raw= passthrough to the TASK-RCP69 escape mode;
  OCC conflict (409) surfaces as a clear retryable CLI error.
- [ ] Decision/glossary/arch nodes have create-or-edit parity where a daemon
  path already exists; gaps documented in Evidence if intentionally deferred.
- [ ] All new verbs write through the writer loop + structural guard (no
  direct file writes); tests prove guard rejection still fails closed.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon, clippy -D warnings,
  fmt --check.

** Evidence
- One cursor round (composer-2.5-fast), run-20260612T120056; worker commit
  =27be53d=, merge =837309a=, manager fix-ups =148e8af=, close tx-0210.
- Delivered: =POST /projects/:id/tasks= (task create — auto-mint, legacy-id
  reject, parse-gated append to backlog.org behind a single re-point fn for
  TASK-2C3WZ, task.created tx); property path on the task-update route
  replacing the 501 (upsert_property, task.property_updated tx + tx.org
  mirror); CLI =task create/get/list/update= with cwd project resolution,
  =node body set|append= over /org/node/:id/edit (OCC base_version
  auto-fetch, 409 mapped to a retryable error), =decision/architecture
  create= verbs.
- Manager review-and-fix (=148e8af=): block-aware structural guard on create
  bodies (column-0 level-1 heading reject — HC7PW class, mirrors the G9KCN
  goal guard); STATE/ID rejected in the property path (lifecycle owns STATE,
  identity immutable); ID-as-property rejected on create; =append --raw=
  rejected (whole-body re-wrap hazard) and append now single-GETs.
- Gates: daemon lib 146/147 + stall-detector flake green isolated, 4 new
  guard tests; cli 28/31 + 3 BRXGG dispatch flakes green isolated;
  integration task_state_flip green; clippy daemon lib/bin + cli
  all-targets; fmt. Failure set identical to pre-merge baseline (ZD72S).
- Deferred: graph property revise ops (decision/architecture =action=
  payloads) and section-body edits stay daemon-API-only — no CLI verb yet.

* DONE TASK-BP5VB Runtime-first install/init: one canonical path, retire agent hand-edit fallbacks (dec_ZQJPA doctrine half) :install:onboarding:skill:conventions:scaffold:
:PROPERTIES:

:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      shipped/skills/orgasmic/ shipped/prompt-studio/ .orgasmic/entry.org .orgasmic/conventions/ crates/orgasmic-core/src/projects.rs crates/orgasmic-core/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       shipped/ .orgasmic/conventions/ crates/orgasmic-core/src/ crates/orgasmic-cli/src/

** Description
dec_ZQJPA part (b): install/init converges on one runtime-first path and the
fail-open agent-editing fallbacks are retired. Inventory of surfaces to change
(audited 2026-06-12):

- =entry.org= Write rules (+ scaffold mirror =shipped/skills/orgasmic/scaffold/
  entry.org= ~lines 48-56): "If it fails: match existing Org structure" →
  fail-fast: install/start the runtime, do not edit. Update the project map
  line when TASK-2C3WZ renames task files.
- =conventions/no-skill-installed.org= (~lines 38-43): direct goal/handoff
  write grant → becomes an install pointer (read-only orientation allowed).
- =conventions/orgasmic-tooling.org=: "Other =.orgasmic/= files: direct-edit
  unless a command says otherwise" → CLI-only for agents.
- =shipped/skills/orgasmic/references/init.md= (~5-8) + scaffold
  =tasks/sprint.org= bootstrap heading (~33-36): "no daemon needed" bootstrap →
  init installs/starts the runtime first; bootstrap edits run through it.
- =references/recall-resume.md= "When the bootstrap writes" (~106-117): gate on
  the runtime check.
- =conventions/manager-implementer.org= (~line 7): direct-edit clause scoped to
  source code only, org state stays daemon-mediated.
- =references/install.md= wizard: simplify to the one canonical order —
  install CLI+daemon → init → daemon-backed bootstrap; prune choice branches
  that exist only to support runtime-less editing.

DEPENDS_ON the write-surface tasks: prohibiting hand-edits before the verbs
exist would deadlock state maintenance (dec_ZQJPA phasing clause).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No instruction surface (entry.org, conventions/, skill references,
  scaffold mirrors, prompt-studio specs) permits an agent to hand-edit
  =.orgasmic/*.org=; the missing-runtime route is install instructions.
- [ ] =/orgasmic init= produces a project whose bootstrap task is executed
  against a running daemon; the scaffold bootstrap heading no longer claims
  daemon-free operation.
- [ ] =scaffold_manifest_matches_shipped_directory= and scaffold-parity tests
  green; compiled prompt bundles stay within dec_MX9RM token budgets.
- [ ] Stranger-smoke flow doc (install → init → dispatch → close) matches the
  new single path.

** Evidence
(populated at task close)

* DONE TASK-2C3WZ One task file per lifecycle state; transitions move headings between files (implements dec_QQYXM) :schema:tasks:layout:daemon:cli:ui:migration:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-2C3WZ
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-core/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs ui/src/components/OrgView.tsx shipped/skills/orgasmic/scaffold/ shipped/prompt-studio/ crates/orgasmic-core/tests/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/ crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/ ui/src/ shipped/ scripts/id-migration.py
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-NP5H6
:END:

** Description
Implements dec_QQYXM: =tasks/= holds one org file per kanban state
(backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review/done/cancelled.org); file membership is
the canonical state; a lifecycle transition = the daemon moving the heading
between files atomically with the keyword rewrite. =sprint.org= and
=archive.org= retire. Blast radius (audited 2026-06-12):

- core: =TASK_FILE_NAMES= (paths.rs:6) + =iter_task_file_paths=; scaffold list
  =SCAFFOLD_FILES= (projects.rs:85-101). =LifecycleStage= already has all six
  states (schema.rs:60-93) — no schema change.
- daemon: index iteration (index.rs ~514); creation defaults hardcode
  sprint.org (api.rs ~1172/1190/1758) → new tasks land in backlog.org;
  watcher rename-detection paths (watcher.rs ~512-553).
- cli: =apply_task_lifecycle_transitions= (manager.rs ~1310-1339) rewrites
  keyword + =:STATE:= in place → becomes a daemon-executed cross-file move
  (writer loop + structural guard; file and keyword may never disagree).
  =find_task_file_path= is already filename-agnostic.
- ui: =ORG_FILES= hardcoded list (OrgView.tsx ~25-28).
- shipped: scaffold tasks/ templates (6 state files + goal/handoff, no seed
  headings — the phantom-heading lint fires on =* (empty)= seeds);
  prompt-spec/doc references (manager.org ~32-34, manager-dispatch.org ~105,
  init.md, sprint_tasks.org context pack :FILE:, tx.org schema doc).
- tests: ~70 sprint.org references across daemon/cli/core suites.

TWO-PHASE method per TASK-YRK1V precedent: the worker lands code + scaffold +
a migration script (split live headings by their keyword; verify zero dupes,
every heading's file matches its keyword); the MANAGER executes the live
split on quiesced main as one commit. Live distribution at filing: 23
BACKLOG / 194 DONE / 3 CANCELLED / 0 others.

Coordinate: TASK-55DH1 decides whether the keyword/=:STATE:= property remain
as display copies — do not pre-empt; keep writing both, just relocated.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task discovery = the six state files; sprint.org/archive.org gone from
  code, scaffold, docs, and prompt specs.
- [ ] Lifecycle transition moves the heading to the target state file through
  the daemon writer atomically with the keyword rewrite; a crash cannot leave
  the heading in zero or two files (test).
- [ ] =task create= (TASK-NP5H6) and daemon subtask creation default to
  backlog.org.
- [ ] UI org-file list shows the new files; kanban renders correctly from
  file-scoped reads.
- [ ] Migration script + verify mode delivered; live split rehearsed in a
  worktree (manager executes on main separately).
- [ ] Gates: cargo test --workspace, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, UI build.

** Evidence
- One cursor round (run-20260612T125537); worker commit =0d84ac1=, merge
  =09737d9=, manager fix-ups =148c0b6=, close tx-0233. Delivered the six
  state files (paths.rs canonical list + lifecycle_stage_file_name),
  cross-file lifecycle moves as ONE writer.transaction with 1-2
  FileRewrites + crash-atomicity test at the writer boundary, CLI
  lifecycle transitions re-routed through the daemon (direct flock
  rewrites deleted — whole concurrent-writer hazard class gone), watcher
  rename tests, UI OrgView list, scaffold + docs, and
  scripts/task-split-migration.py (dry-run/apply/verify/self-test).
- Manager review-and-fix (=148c0b6=): task_state_flip_regression had
  merged STALE and failing (asserted the heading stayed in backlog.org —
  drowned in known flake noise); subtask creation appended a born-BACKLOG
  heading to the PARENT's file (file=state violation) — now lands in
  backlog.org; the scaffold bootstrap tree (TASK-C9V29+.1-.4, dec_056)
  had been deleted instead of relocated — restored into
  scaffold/tasks/backlog.org with its fixtures test + init.md anchoring;
  --apply was not idempotent (second run would wipe done.org) — sources
  now include the state files, self-test covers double-apply; identity
  lint duplicate test had both writes clobbering one file.
- Gates post-fix: core 116 lib green (+ fixtures 4 pending the live
  split by design), daemon lib 149/149 incl. the corrected cross-file
  flip test, cli bins 31, clippy daemon lib/bin + cli/core all-targets,
  fmt. Live split executed manager-side as ONE commit (see tx + the
  =task-split= commit); split verify + post-split fixtures + API smoke
  recorded in the handoff.
- Residual: split_headings in the migration script is line-based, not
  org-block-aware — verified zero non-heading column-0 star lines in the
  live corpus before executing; hazard documented for any future reuse.

* DONE TASK-7R3NS Dispatch worktrees live under the project's .orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/<task>/ next to the briefs :cli:dispatch:worktrees:layout:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-7R3NS
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/watcher.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-core/src/paths.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-WTJ5V
:END:

** Description
Operator directive 2026-06-12: worktrees move from the global
=/tmp/orgasmic-worktrees/task-<stem>= (hardcoded in =default_worktree()=,
manager.rs ~1504-1515) to the project's own dispatch dir —
=<project>/.orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/<task-stem>/worktree= — the same per-task
home the briefs already use (=dispatch_artifact_paths=, manager.rs ~1544-1556;
=project_dispatch_dir=, core paths.rs:54-58; TASK-SD0G1 precedent). One
=tmp/dispatch/<stem>/= directory then owns everything a dispatch produces:
brief, last.txt, stdout log, worktree.

Touch points: =default_worktree()= + the kind-default reuse checks
(manager.rs ~301-318); daemon =validate_dispatch_worktree_dir=
(api.rs ~3255-3262). =.orgasmic/.gitignore= already covers =tmp/=.

CRITICAL new hazard: a worktree under =.orgasmic/tmp/= contains its own
checked-out =.orgasmic/= tree. The daemon index, watcher, and any project
scan MUST NOT discover the nested copy as a project or double-index its org
files (the id-migration script already skips =.orgasmic/tmp/= for the same
class of reason — runtime artifacts). Prove the exclusion with a regression
test before flipping the default.

DEPENDS_ON TASK-WTJ5V: worktree delete authority moves daemon-side there
(PGID kill → waitpid → daemon deletes); land the relocation on top so the
daemon's delete path is born knowing the new location.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default dispatch worktree path = =<project>/.orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/
  <task-stem>/worktree= for all kinds (impl/review/arch stems preserved);
  daemon validation accepts it.
- [ ] Nested-project exclusion: daemon index/watcher never registers or
  parses org files inside =.orgasmic/tmp/= (regression test with a real
  =git worktree add= there).
- [ ] Dispatch → close cycle cleans the whole =<stem>/= dir via the
  TASK-WTJ5V daemon-side path; nothing left under tmp/dispatch after a clean
  close (brief retention per existing convention).
- [ ] =git status= in the parent repo stays clean with a live worktree
  (gitignore holds); =git worktree list/prune= behave.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon, clippy, fmt.

** Evidence
- implementer-cursor / composer-2.5-fast,
  run-20260612T111034-8a26b439e4b34beb9de8ec49bce52f65, worker commit
  =847778c= merged --no-ff =5e99b75=; manager review-and-fix =547fbc3=
  (no reviewer stage per round-4 process): prune helper
  (=prune_dispatch_stem_after_worktree= + =is_dispatch_stem_dir=) was
  duplicated verbatim cli/daemon — deduplicated into orgasmic-core
  paths.rs (the QRD3Y two-copies-drift class); nested-exclusion test
  gained the missing decoy-marker assertion (dec_999 must not leak into
  parse-errors); clippy needless-borrows in the new test.
- Delivered: =default_worktree(project_root, task, kind)= →
  =<project>/.orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/<stem>/worktree= (stems task-*/
  -review/-arch preserved); =normalize_path= (canonicalize with
  missing-leaf fallback) for macOS /var vs /private/var; cross-kind
  collision check normalized; close/cleanup prunes <stem>-last.txt +
  <stem>-stdout.log and retains the brief (guarded to the dispatch
  layout — old-location worktrees untouched); watcher flush() drops
  events under =.orgasmic/tmp/= (=dropped_tmp=); NEW
  =dispatch_worktree_layout.rs= regression — real git worktree add
  under tmp/dispatch: nested .orgasmic not registered as a project,
  decoy task not parsed, duplicate lint silent, decoy marker not
  scanned, watcher touch not ingested.
- Gates on merged main: 4/4 new tests green; failure delta vs baseline
  verified flake-not-regression — reviewer_close_verdict_ship /
  _recommended_subtasks fail under parallel load with the documented
  run-release signature and PASS run alone (--exact), same family as
  the handoff FLAKE note (dispatch.rs binary grew → more parallel
  pressure; TASK-BRXGG territory); continuation_early_exit passes
  isolated (known PID-test collateral); clippy lib/bin all crates +
  core/cli all-targets + the new test target clean; fmt clean.
- Layout note for managers: with briefs at
  =tmp/dispatch/<stem>/<stem>-brief.md= the worktree now lands in the
  SAME <stem>/ dir; after a clean close only the brief remains.

* DONE TASK-55DH1 Slim the org node property schema: drop derived/duplicated keys, promote relationships to edges :schema:architecture:design:adr:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-55DH1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      .orgasmic/decisions.org
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/ crates/orgasmic-core/src/schema.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/org.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/index.rs
:TEST_CMD:         true
:END:

** Description
Operator directive 2026-06-12: the property drawers carry redundant keys and
duplications that should be edges. Design task — deliverable is an ADR with a
per-key verdict (keep / derive / edge / drop) and a sized migration plan;
implementation is a follow-up filing.

Census across tasks/sprint+backlog, decisions, architecture, glossary
(2026-06-12, 396 drawers):

- STATE ×3: =:STATE:= (221) duplicates the heading keyword (written in
  lockstep, manager.rs ~1332-1333), and dec_QQYXM's file-per-state adds file
  membership as the canonical third copy. At most one redundant display copy
  should survive — which one, and lint-or-atomic for agreement.
- Derivable: =:LAST_UPDATED:= (221) duplicates git; =:KIND:= (251) is largely
  inferable from ID grammar + file (inferNodeKind precedent); =:DECIDED_AT:=
  (127) vs git when not semantically distinct.
- Empty placeholders shipped on every heading: =:BLOCKED_BY:=/=:PARENT_TASK:=/
  =:FIX_SUBTASK:= mostly blank; =:PARENT_TASK:= (167) duplicates the
  =<parent>.k= subtask ID grammar.
- Dispatch params on all ~220 task headings even when default:
  =:WORKER:=/=:WRITE_SCOPE:=/=:READ_SCOPE:=/=:TEST_CMD:= — candidates for
  project-config defaults with per-task override only.
- Edge-shaped properties: =:DEPENDS_ON:= (263), =:BLOCKED_BY:= (211 — inverse
  of DEPENDS_ON? decide one direction), =:RELATES_TO:= (40),
  =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= (70), =:IMPLEMENTS:=/=:PRODUCES:= (166, arch),
  =:MOTIVATED_BY:= (17), =:TRACKED_BY:= prose refs — one edge vocabulary,
  one canonical direction each, stored once.

Constraints: org files stay greppable/human-readable (dec_X72P5 readability
principle); the daemon index is the natural home for derived inverses; any
migration lands one-commit like id-migration (dec_X72P5b greenfield rule).

Amendment [2026-06-12]: include the task-identity redundancy question in the
schema verdict. The ADR/follow-up must explicitly decide whether task identity
is canonical in the heading token (=* BACKLOG TASK-XXXXX ...=), the =:ID:=
property, or both with an equality lint. Current implementation requires
=:ID: TASK-*= plus a lifecycle keyword for task indexing; if both survive, the
migration must define mismatch behavior and tests for =TaskHeading::from_heading=,
materialized-index task-shape detection, and the phantom-heading lint.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR in decisions.org: per-class property whitelist, edge vocabulary
  with canonical directions, per-key keep/derive/edge/drop verdict covering
  every key in the census.
- [ ] The three redundancy classes resolved explicitly: state×3, derivable
  timestamps/kind, empty placeholders + dispatch-param defaults.
- [ ] Migration plan sized (files touched, script-or-hand, verify mode) and
  follow-up implementation task(s) drafted.
- [ ] Parser/schema impact listed (schema.rs property allowlists, index
  edges, UI consumers).

** Evidence
- One claude-sonnet-4-6 round via rmux (run-20260612T123600); worker
  commit =c585a85=, merge =a3b5285=, close tx-0224. dec_HJENQ: per-key
  keep/derive/edge/drop verdicts across all four node classes, unified
  edge vocabulary (canonical directions, inverses always index-derived),
  7-step migration plan (ONE =property-migration= tagged commit), schema
  impact list. Live-corpus fixtures 28/28 in the worktree.
- Manager integration: follow-up drafts filed as real minted tasks —
  TASK-PD8W5 (execute migration, P2, DEPENDS_ON TASK-2C3WZ), TASK-VJXXC
  (edge index, P3), TASK-Q68WE (dispatch-param config defaults, P3);
  the ADR draft headings keep their placeholder names as prose (no lint
  impact, parse-errors stable at 56).
- Worker hiccup, recovered: the ADR was first written to the MAIN
  repo's decisions.org, then copied to the worktree and committed, but
  the stray main-repo edit was never reverted — manager verified
  byte-identical and reverted before merge. Brief discipline note: the
  parent-repo warning must ride EVERY brief, prose tasks included.
- ZSD0B live rmux probe PASSED: run released on completion (no 600s
  stall reap), rmux session gone, last.txt carries the worker summary.
- Amendment follow-through [2026-06-12, post-close]: the task-identity
  amendment landed in the closing commit (ffa5e6c) AFTER the worker round,
  so dec_HJENQ shipped without the required identity verdict and no
  follow-up carried it. Resolved post-close: identity verdict appended to
  dec_HJENQ Decision (=:ID:= canonical, heading token display copy,
  read-time equality lint); implementation filed as TASK-KY80Q.

:PRIORITY: P1
:PRODUCES: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/prompt_compiler.rs shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/base_worker.org shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/implementer.org shipped/prompt-studio/slots.org
:READ_SCOPE: shipped/prompt-studio/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/prompt_compiler.rs .orgasmic/decisions.org
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon prompt_compiler
:WORKER: implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/prompt_compiler.rs shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/*.org shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-parts/*.org shipped/prompt-studio/slots.org crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/**
:END:

** Description
The compiled dispatch bundle for workers is malformed. A real compiled
prompt (TASK-55DH1, run-20260612T123600) showed the manager dispatch
brief rendered THREE times — implementer Goal, Inputs Assignment, Inputs
Acceptance — read/write scope collapsed to the placeholder "See dispatch
brief.", and =Recent activity:= rendered as empty org-verbatim (===).

Verified root cause (2026-06-12):
=api.rs::compile_dispatch_prompt_bundle= (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/
api.rs:3486-3495) fills BOTH =task.description= and =task.acceptance=
from the brief, hardcodes both scopes to "See dispatch brief.", and
passes the brief a third time as the compile =reason= (api.rs:3519); the
indexed task record (description/acceptance/scopes on =TaskHeading=) is
available via the =ProjectIndex= argument but unused, and =task.activity=
is never inserted. Template side: =implementer.org:19= embeds
={{task.description}}= in Goal while the inherited =base_worker.org:34-35=
embeds it again as Assignment and Acceptance. This violates dec_MX9RM
(one canonical home per instruction; briefs carry delta only) and the
prompt_composer rules.

Fix composition so dispatch bundles are compact, role-specific, and
non-duplicated without losing required task context: hydrate task slots
from the canonical indexed record; carry the manager brief as a
dispatch-only input rendered exactly once.

Read first:
- shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/prompt_composer.org
- shipped/prompt-studio/conventions/manager-dispatch.org
- shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/base_worker.org + implementer.org
- crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs::compile_dispatch_prompt_bundle
- crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/prompt_compiler.rs
** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dispatch composition hydrates =task.description=, =task.acceptance=,
  =task.read_scope=, =task.write_scope=, and =task.title= from the canonical
  indexed task record, not from the manager dispatch brief.
- [ ] The manager brief rides a dedicated dispatch-only input (e.g.
  =dispatch.brief=) and appears EXACTLY ONCE in the compiled prompt, under
  one clearly labeled section presented as manager handoff content (so
  brief-embedded text such as commit-message suggestions reads as handoff
  metadata, not spec policy).
- [ ] =base_worker.org= and role specs no longer render the same task body
  in Goal, Assignment, and Acceptance — Goal stays short; task facts live
  in Inputs only.
- [ ] No placeholder or empty-verbatim values in compiled dispatch prompts:
  scopes come from the task record when present; an absent value omits the
  line or states it is missing — never "See dispatch brief." and never an
  empty === pair (current =Recent activity:= rendering).
- [ ] Contradictory guidance sharpened: task-brief claims must not conflict
  with the base-worker rule to inspect prior agent claims before relying
  on them.
- [ ] Regression test: compile an implementer dispatch for a fixture task
  whose description, acceptance, read scope, write scope, and brief are
  all distinct strings; assert each lands in its correct section and the
  brief appears exactly once in the rendered text.
- [ ] Existing Prompt Studio compile/lint behavior stays green for all
  shipped prompt specs.
- [ ] Gates: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon prompt_compiler= plus touched
  integration tests; =cargo fmt --check=.
** Evidence
(populated at task close)

* DONE TASK-KY80Q Heading-token vs :ID: equality lint on task/decision/arch nodes; mismatch tests (dec_HJENQ identity verdict) :schema:lints:identity:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-KY80Q
:PRIORITY: P3
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-core -p orgasmic-daemon
:WORKER: implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-core/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-core/tests/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:END:

** Description
Implements the dec_HJENQ node-identity verdict (TASK-55DH1 amendment, resolved
post-close). Today the heading ID token is an unenforced display copy:
=from_heading= strips it from the title when present and silently tolerates
absence or mismatch (=strip_prefix= fallback, schema.rs:288 task / 357
decision / 556 arch), and the phantom-heading lint (task_HC7PW,
index.rs::lint_phantom_task_headings) checks only =:ID: TASK-*= presence plus
a TODO keyword — a heading-token/=:ID:= mismatch produces no finding. Add a
read-time equality lint on the parse-errors channel: for task, decision, and
arch nodes, flag a heading title whose leading ID token disagrees with (or
omits) the drawer =:ID:=. Parse stays tolerant — =:ID:= wins for indexing, no
SchemaError on mismatch. Glossary terms are exempt (slug titles, dec_X72P5d).
** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Equality lint flags heading-token/=:ID:= mismatch and missing leading
  token for task, decision, and arch nodes via the parse-errors channel;
  glossary terms exempt.
- [ ] Parse stays tolerant on mismatch: =:ID:= canonical for indexing, no
  =SchemaError=; fallback semantics documented at the =strip_prefix= sites.
- [ ] Tests: =TaskHeading::from_heading= mismatch fallback, materialized-index
  task-shape detection under mismatch, phantom-heading lint equality cases
  (mismatch, missing token, exempt glossary slug).
** Evidence
(populated at task close)
