** Description
Drafted as TASK-PROP-MIGRATION inside dec_HJENQ (Consequences → follow-up
tasks). Run dec_HJENQ migration steps 1-7 as a script-driven batch on the
live corpus: drop :STATE:/:KIND:(tasks)/:LAST_UPDATED:/:PARENT_TASK:/
:FIX_SUBTASK:, fold non-empty :BLOCKED_BY: into :DEPENDS_ON:, strip
blank/default dispatch params. ONE commit tagged =property-migration=
(dec_X72P5 method). Schema impact per the ADR: TaskHeading/TaskSummary
field removals, manager lifecycle write path stops emitting
:STATE:/:KIND:/:LAST_UPDATED:. TWO-PHASE like the id migration: worker
authors + rehearses the script in its worktree; the manager executes on
quiesced main.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Steps 1-7 land as one tagged =property-migration= commit; fixtures
  and schema.rs/index.rs/write paths updated in the same commit.
- [ ] cargo test --workspace no worse than the pre-existing set;
  fixtures live-corpus reparse green; daemon reload + task list + UI
  board state counts verified.
- [ ] New headings post-migration carry only the slimmed property set
  (per-key verdicts in dec_HJENQ).

** Evidence
- Authoring round: implementer-cursor (run-20260612T175048; commits
  =bcec386= + =1beb388=, merged code+script only as =b62bad9= — rehearsal
  corpus changes excluded at merge). Script: steps 1-7, --verify/--apply/
  --dry-run/--self-test, idempotent double-apply.
- Manager review-and-fix (4 commits): drawer lines now preserved verbatim
  so the migration diff is exactly the dropped/merged lines (the worker
  version re-serialized every drawer — 22k-line churn incl. tx files;
  self-test now asserts formatting preservation and tx untouchability);
  .orgasmic/tmp/ excluded from scans; rustfmt pass (worker's fmt PASS
  claim was stale); worker-seed :KIND: corruption from the fixture
  bulk-strip ({kind} template var dropped, implementer/babysitter kinds
  swapped) — fixed 2 REAL dispatch_endpoint regressions the worker had
  misclassified as parallel flakes (verified failing isolated on merged,
  passing isolated at baseline 8f448fa).
- LIVE EXECUTION: quiesced (0 runs), apply 4 files -1308/+8 (backlog/
  cancelled/done/in_progress), verify OK, second apply 0 files, ONE
  commit =9e353e8= tagged =property-migration=. tx/decisions/architecture
  byte-untouched.
- Smoke on rebuilt daemon (pid 93825): parse-errors EXACTLY 56, 235
  tasks indexed, TASK-VJXXC backlog→todo→backlog cross-file round-trip —
  rewritten drawer carries no :STATE:/:KIND:.
- Pre-existing failures stand (3 dispatch_endpoint pid/subprocess +
  flaky continuation_early_exit + integration auto_spawn/post_projects/
  nested_worktree — all verified failing at baseline).

* DONE TASK-9TFBD Dispatch flips race the auto-spawn watcher; auto-spawn launches implementers unbriefed in the project root :daemon:supervisor:dispatch:regression:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-9TFBD
:PRIORITY: P1
:READ_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs .orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:WORKER: implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:END:

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No code path spawns a worker from a =task.state_transitioned= event:
  the watcher chain (=maybe_emit_auto_spawn= → =AutoSpawnRequested= →
  =auto_spawn_consumer=/=handle_auto_spawn=) is deleted, including the
  =auto_spawn= origin arm of the spawn pipeline and its tx/session naming.
- [ ] Regression test: flipping a task to in_progress via the state verb
  produces NO run (supervisor run table empty, no auto-spawn tx/session).
- [ ] Regression test: manager dispatch of a task with =:WORKER:= set —
  WITHOUT the parking workaround — yields exactly ONE run with dispatch
  origin and no rollback flip.
- [ ] Babysitter attach unaffected: =build_babysitter_auto_spawn= intact,
  =dispatch_endpoint_auto_spawns_babysitter_jsonl= passes.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon (known pre-existing failures
  excepted); cargo fmt --check.
** Evidence
- One implementer-cursor round (run-20260612T214531, worker commit
  =08bf328=): watcher chain deleted end-to-end (consumer boot wiring,
  =AutoSpawnRequested= payload, =maybe_emit_auto_spawn= + handler +
  origin arm; net -1317 lines); babysitter attach untouched; two
  regression tests added (state-flip spawns nothing; explicit dispatch
  with =:WORKER:= needs no parking).
- Manager gates on merged main: exactly the 5 known pre-existing
  failures, fmt clean, both new tests green.
- Rebuilt daemon (pid 45585) live probes: in_progress flip on
  TASK-VJXXC produced ZERO runs (the incident scenario), round-trip
  clean. The :WORKER:-parking dispatch workaround is RETIRED.
- Bonus: the KY80Q equality lint caught its first production drift
  during smoke — =decision create= had written dec_WC1PW's heading
  without the ID token (parse-errors 57→56 after one-line interim
  repair); filed TASK-38GGG (create-verb audit + title repair verb).
** Description
RESHAPED per dec_WC1PW [2026-06-13]: the original race-fix scope is
superseded — remove the task-state auto-spawn watcher outright instead of
patching it. Original incident evidence (tx-0253/0254/0255, rogue
unbriefed codex editing main, salvage branch =task-smfhy-rogue-salvage=)
is recorded in dec_WC1PW Context.

Remove the watcher chain end to end: =maybe_emit_auto_spawn= (api.rs) and
its call site in the tx-append path, the =AutoSpawnRequested= event
payload, =auto_spawn_consumer= + =handle_auto_spawn= + =AutoSpawnRequest=
and the consumer's startup wiring, the =auto_spawn= origin arm of the
shared worker spawn pipeline (session naming, tx record origin), and
=auto_spawn_stage_eligible=. Delete tests asserting watcher behavior
(e.g. =auto_spawn_event_acquires_run_and_records_run_created_once= —
pre-existing-failing anyway). DO NOT touch the babysitter attach:
=build_babysitter_auto_spawn= and =dispatch_endpoint_auto_spawns_babysitter_jsonl=
are part of explicit dispatch and must keep passing. Explicit manager
dispatch becomes the only path from a task transition to a running worker;
future reactive logic arrives as the declarative event layer per
dec_WC1PW, not as daemon consumers.

* DONE TASK-KM5K1.1 dispatch instrumentation gap follow-up: live cursor-agent reviewer path still drops last.txt + stdout.log :daemon:dispatch:instrumentation:bug:cursor-agent:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-KM5K1.1
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-cursor
:IMPLEMENTS:
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-KM5K1
:END:

** Description
Filed retroactively after a live dogfood gap. TASK-KM5K1 was reviewer-clean and merged at =2364104=, but BOTH cursor-agent reviewer dispatches in this session (=run-20260525T054413= for TASK-KM5K1 reviewer + =run-20260525T054415= for TASK-N2CMS reviewer) continued to leave =/private/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-XXX-review-last.txt= + =/private/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-XXX-review-stdout.log= unpopulated.

The TASK-KM5K1 reviewer (correctly) called this out as out-of-scope-note-1: "Regression tests use codex acp-ws simulated driver, not cursor-agent. Both =dispatch_run_complete_writes_last_path_and_commit_pending_signal= and =dispatch_run_complete_writes_artifacts_after_delayed_release= populate =last_path= via the Lifecycle release reason ('dispatch endpoint test release'), so the =stream: assistant= branch added at =api.rs:3254-3256= is not directly exercised in CI." — But the gap wasn't filed as a finding because it was "low-risk." Then immediately the next live dispatches dogfood-failed.

Suspected: TASK-KM5K1's fix correctly handles the simulated test driver path BUT misses the live cursor-agent =stream: assistant= text_chunk extraction path OR the reviewer-kind dispatch flow has a different artifact-write surface than implementer-kind.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reproduce: dispatch a real cursor-agent reviewer kind via =orgasmic manager dispatch --kind reviewer --worker reviewer-cursor=. Verify =/private/tmp/orgasmic-codex/task-XXX-review-last.txt= remains empty/missing.
- [ ] Add a focused dispatch_endpoint or integration test that uses cursor-shaped session JSON (=stream: assistant=, =text_chunk= events) instead of the simulated lifecycle release. Without this, future regressions stay invisible.
- [ ] Fix the live cursor-agent path so artifacts populate for BOTH implementer and reviewer kinds.
- [ ] Regression test must FAIL on a current-HEAD checkout before your fix and PASS after.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace + clippy + fmt + diff= all green. MSRV 1.78.

** Notes
:FIX_SUBTASK: t. Closes the dogfood gap left open by TASK-KM5K1's reviewer-clean close. P2 because the audit-gap continues to bite every cursor-agent dispatch — but workaround (read session JSONL directly) is viable. Not blocking.

** Evidence
- Implementer: cursor-agent (implementer-cursor, composer-2.5-fast), ~15min wall, self-commit =651121f= on =task-066.1-impl=. Merged no-ff onto main.
- Files: =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= +85/-12 (precedence reorder + 2 unit tests), =crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs= +168/-0 (cursor-shaped session JSON integration test).
- Root cause identified: =dispatch_last_summary_from_session= (=crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs:3257-3300=) returned the Lifecycle::Release reason BEFORE checking for =run_complete= summary or assistant =text_chunk= content. The TASK-KM5K1 fix added the =stream == "assistant"= condition for text_chunk extraction, but the existing precedence put Lifecycle::Release first — so on the live cursor-agent path, the release reason ("dispatch-close" or empty) won every time. Fix: reorder to =run_complete= summary → assistant =text_chunk= → release reason.
- Tests: 345 → 348 (+3 cursor-shaped session envelope tests).
- Gates: =cargo test --workspace= clean, =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean, =cargo fmt --check= clean, =git diff --check= clean (after stripping trailing whitespace from daemon-written :REASON: line in tx file — pre-existing daemon-side issue at =record_dispatch_started=, separate from this task).
- Dispatch close: =implementer.done= tx-20260525-org-0305. =:FIX_SUBTASK: t= auto-flipped IMPLEMENT → DONE.
- Side finding (NEW; not blocking, follow-up candidate): this very dispatch's session emitted 530+ =text_chunk= events ALL with =stream: system= (cursor-agent "thinking deltas"), zero with =stream: assistant=. So the fix correctly handles the assistant-text path AS DESIGNED, but cursor-agent in composer-2.5-fast mode may emit its real assistant output ONLY at terminal-event points OR with a different stream classification. Recommend follow-up: investigate cursor-agent stream taxonomy (is "assistant" actually emitted? when?) and possibly extend the extractor to fall back to system thinking deltas IF no assistant chunks AND no run_complete summary present. Filed as candidate follow-up after cluster close.
- Also: =spawn_dispatch_completion_watcher= still polled until process exit on this dispatch (no terminal session marker), which means the daemon-side artifact-write only triggered after the run-ended classifier rather than after a clean terminal event. This is the second-half of the live-cursor-agent dispatch loop — separate from the precedence fix.


* DONE TASK-YHA6V codex ACP run_complete summaries are bare status markers; last.txt drops the worker report :drivers:codex:dispatch:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-YHA6V
:PRIORITY: P2
:READ_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/codex.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers -p orgasmic-daemon
:WORKER: implementer-cursor
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:END:

** Description

Every codex acp-stdio dispatch leaves last.txt as the bare marker "codex
turn completed" while the worker's full markdown report sits unread in the
session JSONL (e.g. dispatch-TASK-SMFHY-implementer-20260612T171049.jsonl:
304 assistant text_chunks containing the complete Changed/Gates report;
last.txt = 21 bytes).

Verified root cause, two layers:
1. ADAPTER: =emit_turn_completion= (orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/
   codex.rs:646-665) maps =turn/completed= to
   =RunComplete { summary: Some("codex turn <status>") }= — a status
   marker fabricated from the status field, never the turn's output.
   ="thread closed"= (codex.rs:596-602) has the same defect.
2. DAEMON: =dispatch_last_summary_from_session= (orgasmic-daemon/src/
   api.rs:4032-4101) prefers ANY non-empty run_complete summary over the
   assembled assistant text — the placeholder always shadows the real
   report.

Contrast: subprocess-stream-json distills accumulated assistant text at
exit (=SubprocessExitSummary::distill=), which is why cursor dispatches
produce real last.txt summaries; rmux distills the terminal tail.

Preferred fix shape (minimal): the codex adapter emits =summary: None=
for generic status markers (completed / thread closed) so the daemon's
existing =or(assistant_summary)= fallback assembles the report; carry a
real summary only when the protocol provides actual content. Keep
RunFail's error path as is. Add an adapter unit test (turn/completed with
no content → None) and a daemon-side test that a session with assistant
chunks + marker-only run_complete yields the assistant report in
last.txt.
** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A codex acp dispatch whose session carries assistant text yields a
  last.txt containing the worker's final report, not a status marker.
- [ ] turn/completed and thread/closed with no real content emit
  =RunComplete { summary: None }=; statuses with genuine protocol content
  may carry it. RunFail error mapping unchanged.
- [ ] Adapter unit test: turn/completed (no content) maps to summary None.
- [ ] Daemon test: session with assistant chunks + marker-only
  run_complete writes the assembled assistant report to last.txt.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers -p orgasmic-daemon;
  cargo fmt --check.
** Evidence
Fixed manager-direct [2026-06-13] (operator-directed: dev-process fix while the ZD72S+BRXGG dispatch ran). Both root-cause layers addressed exactly as scoped:
1. ADAPTER: emit_turn_completion non-failed arm and thread/closed now emit RunComplete { summary: None } — no fabricated markers; RunFail mapping untouched (codex.rs).
2. DAEMON: dispatch_last_summary_from_session demotes legacy pre-fix markers (is_generic_completion_marker: "codex turn <status>" single-token, "thread closed") to last-resort fallback so old sessions also resolve to the assembled assistant report (api.rs).
Tests: adapters::codex turn_completed_without_content_emits_summary_none, thread_closed_emits_summary_none, turn_failed_still_maps_to_run_fail; api::tests dispatch_last_summary_prefers_assistant_report_over_generic_completion_marker, dispatch_last_summary_uses_marker_when_nothing_else_exists, generic_completion_marker_detection_is_narrow — all green.
Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers -p orgasmic-daemon — failure set identical to the pre-change baseline (hermes roundtrip + post_projects + PID trio + nested_worktree, all owned by in-flight TASK-ZD72S; live_rmux render test is a load-flake, passes isolated). fmt clean; drivers clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; daemon clippy dead-code debt verified IDENTICAL on clean main (ZD72S territory).
LIVE NOTE: production daemon still runs the old binary; the fix reaches real last.txt files at the next rebuild+restart (scheduled with the ZD72S integration — that run's own report still needs the session-jsonl workaround, the last time).

* DONE TASK-ZD72S Fix pre-existing daemon test failures: 2 integration assertions + 3 environment-dependent dispatch PID tests :daemon:tests:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZD72S
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/hermes.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/integration.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Found during TASK-C2PQ3 integration review [2026-06-12]: =cargo test -p
orgasmic-daemon= is NOT green on main, independent of any in-flight work.
Five failures, verified by replaying on main at fa49b27:

1. =tests/integration.rs= — real assertion failures (not environment):
   - =post_projects_scaffolds_and_registers_uninitialized_project=
     (assertion at integration.rs:512)
   - =auto_spawn_event_acquires_run_and_records_run_created_once=
     (panic at integration.rs:1580)
   Diagnose whether the code or the tests drifted; fix whichever is wrong.
2. =tests/dispatch_endpoint.rs= — 3 PID-detection tests fail on machines
   where =cursor-agent= resolves to a bash wrapper rather than an inner
   worker-server child (=dispatch_response_pid_is_inner_subprocess_child=,
   =dispatch_response_pid_prefers_worker_server_child=,
   =dispatch_subprocess_exit_synthesizes_run_complete_from_system_tail=;
   PoisonError cascades at dispatch_endpoint.rs:1991/2152). Make the tests
   hermetic (fake harness binary on PATH) instead of depending on the
   machine's real cursor-agent install. The PoisonError additionally
   cascades through a shared static and makes unrelated neighbors in the
   same binary fail intermittently depending on scheduling (observed:
   =continuation_early_exit_auto_releases_stuck_lease= failed in a full
   run 2026-06-12, passes 3/3 isolated) — fix the poison cascade too
   (poison-recovering lock or per-test state).

3. =orgasmic-drivers= lib: =adapters::hermes::tests::
   real_hermes_websocket_fixture_roundtrip= asserts kind "implementer"
   but the fixture yields "worker" — verified failing identically on
   clean main 2026-06-12 (pre-dates the ID migration; likely the
   worker-kind drift, 18b30a4). Diagnose fixture vs adapter and fix
   whichever drifted.

A red baseline suite hides real regressions — every drain-queue
integration this week had to replay failures on main to classify them.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both integration.rs failures diagnosed with root cause stated in
  Evidence; fixed code-side or test-side as appropriate.
- [ ] dispatch_endpoint PID tests pass without depending on the host's
  real =cursor-agent= (hermetic fake harness or explicit skip-with-reason
  when the harness binary is absent).
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --no-fail-fast= fully green on a
  machine with cursor-agent installed.

** Evidence
Combined round with TASK-BRXGG: implementer-codex-acp (gpt-5.5, effort medium), one round, worker commit =085f09e=, merge =605835f=, close tx-0327. Root causes (all test-side): post_projects_scaffolds = scaffold fixture lagged the 2C3WZ file-per-state split (now seeds all six state files); PID trio = host cursor-agent dependency → hermetic compiled-C fake harness (argv-renamed worker-server children, requires only cc) + PoisonError cascade fixed with into_inner() recovery; nested_dispatch_worktree = fixture double-write left zero tasks (pre-existing, not in the original five); hermes fixture expected legacy kind 'implementer' vs RunKind::Worker 'worker' (test-side drift per 18b30a4). auto_spawn test was already deleted with TASK-9TFBD. Bonus: api.rs SpawnFailureKind dead-code clippy debt deleted — daemon lib clippy -D warnings PASSES for the first time. Manager integration fix-up =4c86136=: wait_for_event_count converted from yield-budget to 10s deadline (last stall_detector flake under parallel load). GATE: cargo test --workspace green 3x consecutive from the dogfood checkout with the production daemon live on 4848 (after the fix-up; first attempt was 2/3 with exactly that flake). cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --no-fail-fast fully green. Daemon rebuilt+restarted on the merged binary (pid 56994), parse-errors steady at 56.
REVIEWER PASS (reviewer-claude-rmux, claude-fable-5 effort high, run-20260613T010822, close tx-0334): VERDICT ship. Independently re-ran daemon lib (150 green), targeted endpoint quintet, integration post_projects, drivers hermes+codex quartet, full cli — all green. Findings applied at =eddd472=: (MAJOR) dispatch-close now fails on a /runs liveness-probe error instead of silently pruning (regression test dispatch_close_fails_when_liveness_probe_unreachable); (MINOR) PID tests skip-with-warning without a C compiler; (MINOR) loosened endpoint assertions comment-point at the supervisor unit test owning the strict guarantee. * DONE TASK-BRXGG cli dispatch tests are not parallel-safe from the dogfood checkout (dispatch-close release race) :cli:daemon:tests:flake:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-BRXGG
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/dispatch.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/cli_parity.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs .orgasmic/gotchas.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-CJXKM
:END:

** Description
Two flake observations against the same surface, root cause not yet pinned:

1. [2026-06-11 handoff Divergence] =orgasmic-cli --test dispatch=
   dispatch-close tests fail under full-suite parallel load with "daemon
   request failed ... /runs/<id>/release — is the daemon reachable?" (4/13
   failed in =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli=, 1/13 in the isolated suite, 0 when
   run alone twice). Load-dependent race in the dispatch-close → run-release
   path; same family as the ab7bb93 dispatch DX races.
2. [2026-06-11 manager session] full =cargo test --workspace= from this
   checkout with the production daemon live on 4848 failed
   =manager_dispatch_status_close_done_with_stub_codex= (1/13); passed clean
   in isolation (7.7s). This is the test TASK-CJXKM hardened (bind_port 65533
   fail-fast), so either TASK-CJXKM's exit condition is still unmet or this is
   observation 1 wearing the other test's name.

Goal: root-cause and fix so the FULL workspace suite is parallel-safe from
the dogfood checkout while a production daemon runs. The fix may live in test
isolation (per-test daemon lifecycle, port allocation, env-var inheritance)
or in the daemon's release path — follow the evidence, do not paper over with
retries or =--test-threads 1=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause written up in Evidence (which process, which race, why
  load-dependent).
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= passes 3 consecutive runs from this checkout
  with a production daemon live on 4848.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-cli= passes 3 consecutive runs standalone.
- [ ] No retry loops, sleeps-as-sync, or forced serial test execution
  introduced as the primary fix.

** Evidence
Fixed in the combined TASK-ZD72S round (worker commit =085f09e=, merge =605835f=, close tx-0328). ROOT CAUSE: cmd_dispatch_close pruned worktree artifacts BEFORE releasing the still-live daemon run; under parallel-suite load the run's completion watcher wrote last.txt/stdout into the pruned stem (and the release endpoint raced the cleanup), producing 'daemon request failed ... /runs/<id>/release'. Load-dependent because the watcher only loses the race when the scheduler is saturated. FIX: release the live run first, then cleanup + lifecycle (crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs); supervisor live_direct_child_pids gained a ps-based fallback merged with pgrep results. No retries, sleeps, or serial execution introduced. GATES: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli green 3x standalone (worker) + green inside 3x full-workspace runs from this checkout with the production daemon live (manager, after the =4c86136= fix-up). The new release-before-prune path ran live during this round's own dispatch-close.
REVIEWER PASS (claude-fable-5 high, run-20260613T010822, close tx-0335): VERDICT ship. Reviewer confirmed the release-before-prune ordering and the failure matrix (ambiguous double-fault leaves resources in place per the WTJ5V invariant), and found the residual MAJOR: unwrap_or_default on the liveness probe — fixed at =eddd472=, probe failure now fails the close before any cleanup.

:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-core --lib real_post_migration_repo_lint_probe -- --ignored
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-QRD3Y
:END:

** Description
TASK-QRD3Y's dangling-reference lint flags 56 pre-existing bare slugs in
=:RELATES_TO:= / =:GLOSSARY_REFS:= drawers (e.g. worker-run ×~14, task,
state-machine, acp, tmux_tui) — none ever had a glossary term entry; all
pre-date the id migration. They now surface in the parse-errors/doctor
channel by design. Per-slug data decision: mint a real =term_XXXXX=
glossary entry (worker-run is the strongest candidate) or prune the
token from the drawer. Pure org-state data work, no code.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every slug flagged by the lint on this repo either has a minted
  glossary term (with the slug as heading title) or is pruned from the
  referencing drawer; rationale per slug recorded in Evidence.
- [ ] Real-repo lint probe reports 0 dangling-reference findings;
  parse-errors channel clean on a daemon restart.

** Evidence
Executed manager-direct through daemon verbs [2026-06-13] (org-state data work — dec_ZQJPA rules out worktree file edits; daemon-lane worker would write live state unsupervised). Census: 56 dangles, 29 distinct slugs, across glossary RELATES_TO (33), decisions GLOSSARY_REFS (21), architecture GLOSSARY_REFS (2).

MINTED 6 terms (recurring core concepts): worker-run x14+run -> term_3YTBK (Worker run); supervisor x3 -> term_GMTWY (Supervisor); question-system x3 -> term_465BG (Question system); shipped-content x2 -> term_5SV2N (Shipped content); user-overrides x2 -> term_W0T9S (User overrides); task-state x2 + state-machine -> term_9B3V8 (Task state machine). Glossary create headings rendered clean (no GBT5W-era prefix residual).

REWRITTEN to existing terms (concept already defined): task x5 -> term_RG690 (Task heading); tx -> term_YC32J (Tx file); decision + adr x2 -> term_P94BF (Decision graph; adr on term_P94BF itself dropped as self-reference); architecture -> term_H5YHC (Architecture graph); convention -> term_Z5XDF (Convention bundle); driver-event -> term_KH8TX (Worker driver).

PRUNED 14 slugs (rationale): glossary x2 (meta-reference to the surface itself); backlog (covered by Task heading + Task state machine); retro/planner/griller/architector (worker kinds — defined by shipped/workers specs, not glossary concepts); snapshot (retired machinery per project.org constraints); acp/tmux_tui (transport names, dec_HY3E0 GLOSSARY_REFS unset as empty per slimmed-property doctrine); audit-trail/append-only (self-descriptive properties of Tx file); git-collaboration/project-opt-in (vague relations, no definable concept); reconciliation x2 (ambiguous between Boot reconciliation term_Z672G and the future BJHKN drift comparator — neither fits both contexts).

37 drawer rewrites applied 37/37 via node prop set/unset. GATES: parse_errors 0 live AND after cold daemon restart (pid 18766) — first zero ever; real_post_migration_repo_lint_probe green.

* DONE TASK-J2RZT Expose handoff and goal nodes through the org-node read/edit surface :daemon:cli:write-authority:

** Description
Found at TASK-BP5VB integration: the runtime-first doctrine
(manager-handoff.org) prescribes handoff updates via =orgasmic node body
set --section ... handoff-current=, but GET /org/node returns 404 for
handoff-current — tasks/handoff.org (and goal.org) are not in the
node-indexed file set (TASK_FILE_NAMES covers sprint/backlog/archive
only; goal verbs are bespoke endpoints). The manager's
highest-frequency write therefore remains a direct-edit exception,
re-creating the dec_ZQJPA phasing gap the doctrine was meant to close.
Make the HANDOFF heading (and the goal headings, read-only at minimum)
resolvable through /org/node + /org/node/:id/edit so section/property
verbs work on them; keep the HC7PW structural guard and OCC semantics.
Coordinate with TASK-2C3WZ task-file renames (file set changes).

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic node body set|append --section "Done so far"
  handoff-current= and =orgasmic node prop set handoff-current LIVENESS
  <sha>= work end-to-end against a live daemon.
- [ ] manager-handoff.org + recall-resume.md drop their interim
  direct-edit exception wording.
- [ ] Regression test: handoff node GET + section edit + property edit
  round-trip.

** Evidence
implementer-codex-acp (gpt-5.5 medium) one round, worker commit =9c49798=, merged =9c49798= into main, close tx-0395. NodeLayer gained Goal + Handoff: handoff-current resolves without --kind, goal-* ids resolve read-only (writes rejected with a pointer to goal set/clear/supersede — thin-goal doctrine preserved); neither file joined TASK_FILE_NAMES so task-shape lints stay silent; HC7PW guard + OCC ride the existing edit path. Regression test org_node_handoff_get_section_and_property_round_trip covers GET (kind/todo/tags/source), section edit, property edit, base_version progression, parse-errors 0. Doctrine: manager-handoff.org + recall-resume.md direct-edit exception wording REMOVED — node verbs are now the canonical handoff flow (the installed skill updated via symlink). Manager gates on main post-merge: workspace 0 failures, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, fmt clean. Daemon rebuilt+restarted (pid 85027), parse-errors 0. Manager review direct (small surgical diff — no reviewer stage per session process). FIRST DISPATCH WITH A REAL last.txt (TASK-YHA6V fix live-proven: 1906-byte report, not the 20-byte marker). Residual: non-prefixed historical goal ids need --kind goal (noted by worker; acceptable — all live goal ids carry the goal- prefix).

* DONE TASK-PYZ9W tx appender must survive inode swap under the file path (git is a sanctioned concurrent writer) :daemon:tx:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-PYZ9W
:PRIORITY: P2
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:WORKER:      implementer-codex-acp
:END:

Incident 2026-06-13 (recovered, see tx-0323..0328 :RECOVERED: notes): a manager git stash/pop replaced .orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org with a new inode while the daemon held its C2PQ3 group-commit append handle. The daemon kept acking and fsyncing tx appends — into the orphaned inode. Six records (tx-0323..0328) were invisible on disk until manually reconstructed; dispatch-status showed a phantom open dispatch. Evidence: lsof fd 14w inode 433635903 vs path inode 434035963; file mtime frozen at the stash-pop instant; in-memory tx_count kept advancing.

Root cause: the tx appender caches the open handle for group-commit batching and never detects that the directory entry now points elsewhere. Git operations and human edits are sanctioned concurrent writers (concurrent-writer convention), so silent divergence is a data-loss bug, not operator error.

Fix direction: before each append batch, stat-compare the handle inode (fstat) against the path (stat); on mismatch reopen the path and invalidate the ProjectTxSeqCache entry. Alternative: reopen per batch (measure — C2PQ3 added the cache for group-commit throughput). Audit other long-lived append handles (session jsonl writers are per-run, lower risk).
** Evidence
implementer-codex-acp (gpt-5.5 medium), combined round with TASK-38GGG, worker commit =bde3202=, merged into main, close tx-0409. writer.rs: TxWriter handles now wrapped in CachedTxWriter capturing dev/ino (FileIdentity) at open; process_tx_batch + append_tx_inner + transaction_inner re-stat the path before each append and, on identity mismatch, drop the handle (forces reopen) and clear ProjectTxSeqCache. Regression test tx_append_reopens_after_path_inode_swap_and_rescans_sequence reproduces the exact incident: append 0013, rename a replacement file (0040) over the path, append again -> 0041 lands in the file AT THE PATH, 0013 absent. Session JSONL writers left unchanged (per-run, lower risk, scoped out). Manager gates: workspace 0 failures, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, fmt clean; daemon rebuilt+restarted pid 20193 parse-errors 0. Closes the data-loss bug from the 2026-06-13 stash/pop incident.

* DONE TASK-38GGG decision create emits tokenless headings (KY80Q lint catch); audit create verbs + add title repair verb :daemon:cli:schema:lints:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-38GGG
:PRIORITY: P3
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:WORKER: implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-cli/src/ crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/
:END:

Caught in production by the TASK-KY80Q equality lint (its first real
finding, 2026-06-13): =orgasmic decision create= wrote dec_WC1PW's heading
as =* <title>= without the leading ID token, violating the dec_HJENQ
identity amendment (heading token is the display copy; every other
decision heading carries it). The manager repaired the one heading by
interim direct edit — there is no title-edit verb to do it through the
daemon. Fix the create path(s) and close the verb gap:

- =decision create= must emit =* <id> <title>= (and accept tags).
- Audit the other node-create verbs for the same omission (task create
  already emits the token; check glossary/architecture create paths if
  they exist).
- Add a daemon-surface way to repair a heading title (title-edit verb or
  equivalent), so the next drift needs no direct edit.
- The KY80Q lint is the regression net: creating a node through every
  daemon create verb must yield zero heading-token findings.
** Evidence
implementer-codex-acp (gpt-5.5 medium), combined round with TASK-PYZ9W, worker commit =bde3202=, close tx-0410. api.rs render_graph_heading: decision + architecture create now emit the leading ID token (* dec_X title / * arch_X title), with a title==id guard to avoid a doubled token when no title is given; glossary + task already tokened. node_display_title rewritten to strip the prefix via the drawer :ID: (not blind split_once) and to SHOW the full title when a heading is tokenless, so drift surfaces rather than hides. Title repair: the existing /org/node/:id/edit set_title op now recomposes a tokenless heading from the drawer :ID: while preserving TODO keyword + tags. Regression: daemon_create_verbs_emit_heading_tokens_without_identity_lint (every create verb -> zero KY80Q heading-token findings) + org_node_title_edit_repairs_tokenless_heading_from_drawer_id. Closes the dec_WC1PW tokenless-create gap (KY80Q's first production catch); the manager no longer needs an interim direct edit to repair a heading title. Gates green; parse-errors 0.

Index exposes default_test_cmd/default_write_scope; TaskSummary gained test_cmd. ** Description
Drafted as TASK-EDGE-INDEX inside dec_HJENQ. GraphIndex tracks arch-node
edges (reads/writes/...) but not task dependency or motivation edges. Add
edge indexing for DEPENDS_ON (task-task, arch-arch), IMPLEMENTS
(task-arch), PRODUCES (task-artifact), MOTIVATED_BY (arch-decision) per
the dec_HJENQ edge vocabulary (one canonical direction; inverses are
ALWAYS index-derived, never stored). Expose inverse queries (blocked_by,
implemented_by, motivates) through the daemon API and CLI.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The four edge kinds indexed with canonical directions; inverse
  queries available via API + CLI; no stored inverse properties anywhere.
- [ ] Tests cover dangling-target behaviour (consistent with the QRD3Y
  dangling-reference lint channel).

** Evidence
implementer-codex-acp (gpt-5.5 medium) one round, worker commit =1966c21=, merged =96f089d=, close tx pending. Unified GraphEdgeSummary edge set on GraphIndex (architecture_edges kept as a type alias for the existing arch graph response). Four canonical edge kinds indexed at build/refresh: depends_on (task-task, arch-arch), implements (task-arch), produces (task-artifact/arch), motivated_by (arch-decision); TaskSummary.implements/.produces threaded from parsed headings. Inverses are DERIVED at query time (no stored inverse anywhere). API GET /graph/edges?node&dir=out|in|both&kind&relation with relation aliases (blocked_by/blocks/implemented_by/produced_by/motivates); CLI orgasmic graph edges. Dangling edge targets (TASK-/arch_) surface on the parse-errors channel (lint_dangling_graph_edges), not dropped/fatal. Manager gates: workspace green except the known live_rmux load-flake (passes isolated); clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean. CRITICAL CHECK: live-corpus parse-errors stays 0 with the new edge lint active (daemon pid 41457) — no real edge dangles. CLI smoke: implemented_by on arch_QXS5W derived the real incoming implements edges. AWAITING Opus-4.8-xhigh reviewer pass before DONE flip.
REVIEWER PASS (reviewer-claude-rmux, claude-opus-4-8 xhigh, run-20260613T112032, ~12m): VERDICT ship-with-fixes, no blocker. Reviewer confirmed: no inverse persisted, no arch-edge double-counting, all 8 relation directions correct, architecture_edges type-alias byte-compatible, structured produces targets skip artifact creation. Findings APPLIED manager-direct: (HIGH) produces artifact-node push deduped — built once after the task loop, skip ids already present (a shared artifact no longer appears N times in graph.nodes); (MEDIUM) dangling-edge lint guard extended to dec_/term_ targets + motivated_by->dec_ regression test; (NIT) /graph/edges rejects an explicit dir/kind conflicting with the relation alias. The MEDIUM fix EXPOSED 11 pre-existing dangling references (3 phantom decision ids dec_M50VK/dec_PWRG1/dec_TQZTE — cited in MOTIVATED_BY/DEPENDS_ON drawers of 5 arch nodes + TASK-C9V29 but never actual decision records, no heading history, no rename mapping). Pruned all 6 drawers via node prop set (same as VVF8S); parse-errors back to 0 live AND after cold restart (pid 84248). Gates after fixes: daemon suite green, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, fmt clean. Tier 2 COMPLETE.

* DONE TASK-T3ENY Audit and fix tx TIME field composition: local-date/UTC-clock skew in daemon /tx and dispatch-close :daemon:tx:cli:dispatch:telemetry:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-T3ENY
:PRIORITY:         P4
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/ crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs .orgasmic/tx/2026-06.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Observed on tx-20260610-orgasmic-0106 (reviewer.done TASK-K5RG2, manually
appended [2026-06-11]): the =TX_ID= uses the UTC date (=20260610=) while
=TIME= was recorded as =[2026-06-11 Thu 21:10:00]= (local midnight-crossing
date). Mismatch: UTC clock value with local date, or vice versa. The same
skew may apply to daemon-generated /tx records and to dispatch-close tx
bodies built in =crates/orgasmic-cli/src/manager.rs=.

Audit scope:
1. Where does the daemon build the =:TIME:= value when handling =POST /tx=?
   (Likely =Utc::now()= or =Local::now()= — verify it's consistent with TX_ID.)
2. Where does the CLI dispatch-close build timestamp strings for the tx body
   it sends? (=close_aborted_request= / =close_done_request= helpers in
   =manager.rs=.)
3. =TX_ID= convention is =tx-YYYYMMDD-<project>-NNNN= — confirm the date
   component matches the =:TIME:= date. If not, fix whichever is wrong to be
   consistently UTC.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All daemon-generated and CLI-generated tx records have =:TIME:= date
  matching =TX_ID= date component (both UTC, or both local with a note).
- [ ] No tx records written during a midnight-crossing window get mismatched
  dates. Preferably both use UTC.
- [ ] Unit or integration test covering tx timestamp generation around midnight.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test --workspace, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium, parallel batch-1, worker commit =4ff78c3=, merged, close tx-0471. AUDIT FINDING: the daemon /tx path was ALREADY UTC-consistent — =now= is =Utc::now()= in both post_tx and prepare_api_tx, so TX_ID and :TIME: always shared the same UTC instant. The reported skew (tx-20260610-orgasmic-0106) was a MANUALLY-appended record (hand-written with a local date), not daemon output. Fix HARDENS the already-correct path: extracted named UTC helpers (tx_time_string_utc / tx_preserve_id_timestamp_utc / tx_project_date_utc / tx_file_month_utc) routed through both daemon /tx paths so the UTC intent is explicit and greppable; confirmed CLI dispatch-close (manager.rs) composes neither value (posts /tx, daemon builds them). Regression test tx_timestamp_helpers_use_utc_date_at_local_midnight_boundary pins fixed instants incl. 2026-06-10T21:10:00Z. Gates: workspace green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. NOTE: a literal MSRV-1.78 build is blocked by a pre-existing transitive dep (idna_adapter v1.2.2 needs edition2024) — workspace-wide, not from this patch; filed as a follow-up.

** Description
The org-shell→orgasmic rename commit =fcd2cac= stripped the =push: branches:
[main]= trigger from both nightly workflows, leaving only
=workflow_dispatch:=. This contradicts TASK-GFKFW's Evidence ("builds the signed
Apple Silicon Tauri app on pushes to main") — nightlies no longer auto-publish
and no updater channel auto-populates. Either re-add the =push: branches:
[main]= block to both workflows, or codify dispatch-only as intentional with an
Evidence note on TASK-GFKFW explaining why.

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium, parallel batch-1, worker commit =c8f6139= (branch task-gfkfw.1-impl), merged, close tx-0475. Restored =push: { branches: [main] }= to the =on:= block of both .github/workflows/nightly-macos.yml and nightly-android.yml (alongside the existing workflow_dispatch:), matching TASK-GFKFW's stated auto-publish-on-push intent; no job steps/secrets/permissions touched. Gates: git diff --check clean, both files valid YAML. No .orgasmic/ edits (manager owns task state).

* DONE TASK-84QVC Entity-link regex in UI prose rendering must accept minted IDs :ui:identity:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-84QVC
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/lib/richText.tsx ui/src/lib/__tests__/
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/lib/richText.tsx crates/orgasmic-core/src/id.rs
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
TASK-GBT5W's UI audit found the one minted-grammar break in the UI:
=KEY_OR_ENTITY_RE= (ui/src/lib/richText.tsx:109) linkifies only numeric
stems — =TASK-\d+(\.\d+)*=, =dec_\d+=, =arch_\d+(\.\d+)*=. Minted IDs
(dec_X72P5a: 5-char Crockford base32 stems with at least one letter, e.g.
=TASK-ZD72S=, =TASK-YRK1V=) already exist in live state and render as
plain text instead of clickable entity links.

Extend the entity alternation to accept both grammars per stem:
numeric (legacy) OR 5-char Crockford-with-letter (minted), keeping the
subtask suffix =(\.\d+)*= for TASK/arch. Keep the pattern well-delimited
(=\b= guards) so prose with bare uppercase words is not over-matched —
the minted stem class is =[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{5}= with at least one
letter; require the class prefix as today.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =TASK-ZD72S=, =TASK-YRK1V.1=, =dec_8KX2M=, =arch_8KX2M= linkify in
  prose; =TASK-CJWT3=, =dec_X72P5=, =arch_C87Z9.3= still linkify (legacy
  regression).
- [ ] Plain uppercase 5-letter words without a class prefix do NOT match.
- [ ] =npm --prefix ui run build= green; unit test for the regex added if
  a test surface exists for richText.
- [ ] Verified in Chrome (claude-in-chrome) on a task body containing
  both grammars.

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium, parallel batch-1, worker commit =71fa797=, merged, close tx-0477. Extended KEY_OR_ENTITY_RE (ui/src/lib/richText.tsx) so each stem alternation accepts numeric (legacy) OR a 5-char Crockford-with-≥1-letter minted stem via lookahead =(?=[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{0,4}[A-HJKMNP-TV-Z])[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{5}=; class prefix still required, (.\d+)* subtask suffix kept, \b guards keep bare uppercase words from matching. Unit test ui/src/lib/__tests__/richText.test.ts asserts minted (TASK-ZD72S, TASK-YRK1V.1, dec_8KX2M, arch_8KX2M) + legacy (TASK-CJWT3, dec_X72P5, arch_C87Z9.3) linkify and bare HELLO does not. Gates: npm run test (6 files, 66 tests) green, npm run build green. CHROME VERIFIED (claude-in-chrome, manager, dev server :5180 serving the merged tree): in the TASK-84QVC detail prose, minted id TASK-GBT5W renders as a teal entity link (and TASK-84QVC in the activity feed); code-span ids stay correctly unlinked. Minted-ID prose linkification confirmed live.

* DONE TASK-9NTQY Daemon start readiness window: 10s CLI timeout reports failure while the daemon is still booting :daemon:cli:dx:reliability:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-9NTQY
:PRIORITY: P3
:READ_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_lifecycle.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/lib.rs
:TEST_CMD: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_lifecycle.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/
:WORKER:      implementer-codex-acp
:END:

** Description
Under CPU contention (parallel release builds + cargo-running workers,
2026-06-12 ~15:24-15:27) the daemon took 20-35s between the boot
auto-reattach pass and binding 4848 (boots cf0110d9, 284ceef8).
=orgasmic daemon start/restart= gives up at 10s, reports 'daemon did not
become ready' + 'check the logs', and =daemon status= says stopped until
the late bind completes — the operator (or a manager script) reasonably
kills the healthy-but-late daemon or starts a second one (both happened
live: pid 32017 killed mid-boot, split-brain status for ~3 minutes). The
pid file is written at spawn and status self-heals once the daemon binds;
the failure is the misleading CLI verdict inside the window.

Sketch: distinguish 'still starting (pid alive, port unbound)' from
'failed (process exited)'; either wait-with-progress past 10s while the
pid is alive, or report 'still booting — check =daemon status= shortly'
instead of an error. Consider logging bind-start before the index parse
so the gap is attributable in daemon.out.log.

** Acceptance Criteria
- =daemon start= on a slow boot (pid alive, port not yet bound) does not
  report failure while the process is alive and making progress; the
  still-booting case is distinguishable from a crashed daemon in both
  exit code and message.
- =daemon status= during the window reports the spawned pid as starting,
  not stopped.
- Regression test covering the slow-bind window (delayed-bind test daemon
  or injected readiness delay).

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium, worker commit =fdf16a9=, merged, close tx-0493. Adds LocalDaemonState::Starting(pid) for the pid-alive/port-unbound window (read from the spawn-written pid file). daemon start/restart now: running -> exit 0; still-booting (pid alive past 10s) -> exit 0 with 'still booting — check daemon status shortly' + pid; crashed (pid exited) -> non-zero 'process exited before becoming ready'. daemon status reports 'starting' with pid during the window instead of 'stopped'. Pre-bind log marker ('daemon starting pre-bind boot work') added in lib.rs so the gap is attributable in daemon.out.log. Injectable ORGASMIC_TEST_BIND_DELAY_MS hook drives the regression tests (status=Starting for alive+unbound, start-timeout=StillBooting for alive pid, exited pid still fails). Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli + workspace green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean; worker probe with a 30s bind delay confirmed exit-0 still-booting + status=starting + the pre-bind marker. Manager restart on the merged binary (pid 52100) returned normally (fast bind), parse-errors 0.

* DONE TASK-EXFTV claude-acp adapter simulated-mode gate fires on empty endpoint even when stdio spawn is available :drivers:acp:stdio:claude:bug:dispatch:dogfood:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-EXFTV
:PRIORITY:         P5
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/**
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** shipped/workers/implementer-claude-acp.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:END:

** Description
Surfaced live during TASK-KSFG0 dispatch [2026-05-27 Wed 18:03]: =implementer-claude-acp= worker dispatches always land in simulated mode even when the =claude= binary (Claude Code CLI) is on PATH. Root cause at =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs:155-162=:

#+begin_src rust
let endpoint_empty = cfg.endpoint.as_deref().map(str::is_empty).unwrap_or(true);
let simulated = endpoint_empty || !claude_available();
if simulated { return Ok(HarnessRequest::Simulated { ... }); }
#+end_src

The gate requires =endpoint= to be non-empty for real-wire mode. For ACP-WS that makes sense (endpoint = WS URL). For ACP-stdio it does not — the adapter already declares =stdio_spawn= at lines 130-145 (=claude --bare -p --input-format stream-json ...=) but the gate short-circuits to simulated when =endpoint= is empty, which is the default for the stdio worker spec.

Net effect: =implementer-claude-acp= has NEVER had its real-wire stdio path validated end-to-end. The codex acp-stdio path (TASK-HY0QT → TASK-KFGDS.1) got the live-dogfood treatment; the claude acp-stdio path silently degrades.

Symptom under TASK-KSFG0 dispatch (=run-20260527T150326-b794fd856b8f4b489f05e79acacd12aa=): seq=0 acquire → seq=1 =Ready{simulated:true}= → seq=2 babysitter_spawned → silence. No work, no completion, no failure. Manager aborted via =dispatch-close --status aborted= (=tx-20260527-org-0400=) and switched TASK-KSFG0 to =implementer-codex-stdio=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =simulated= flag flips to false when =cfg.endpoint= is empty AND =claude_available()= returns true AND the worker's =:DRIVER:= is =acp-stdio= (stdio spawn path). The endpoint-empty short-circuit must NOT apply to stdio mode.
- [ ] Existing ACP-WS behavior preserved: empty endpoint + WS driver still simulated (endpoint is required for WS).
- [ ] Real-wire dogfood: dispatch a tiny task (e.g., a doc-comment-only edit) via =--worker implementer-claude-acp= and confirm the session JSONL shows: seq=0 acquire → seq=1 =Ready{simulated:false}= → seq=2+ TextChunk / ToolCall envelopes from a real =claude --bare= subprocess → terminal RunComplete. Pattern matches TASK-YFM6X dogfood for codex acp-stdio.
- [ ] Mock-peer fixture covers the gate logic (endpoint-empty + stdio + claude_available → real; endpoint-empty + WS + claude_available → simulated; endpoint-empty + stdio + !claude_available → simulated).
- [ ] No new clippy warnings; MSRV 1.78; cargo test --workspace green.

** Notes
- *Out of scope*: redesigning the adapter; this is a one-condition gate fix.
- *Related dogfood missing*: =implementer-claude-tmux= is also unproven for live dispatches; that may surface as a separate task later. =implementer-codex-stdio=, =implementer-cursor=, =reviewer-codex-stdio=, =reviewer-cursor= are all fully dogfooded.
- *Filed in TASK-KSFG0 follow-up commit* alongside switching TASK-KSFG0 :WORKER: → =implementer-codex-stdio=.

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium, worker commit =94d3b3a=, merged, close tx-0473. Gate fix: claude.rs implements upgrades_simulated_to_subprocess() + stdio_initial_payload so empty endpoint + claude_available + ACP-stdio upgrades the simulated request into a real =claude --bare= subprocess (mirrors the proven codex-acp mechanism); ACP-WS stays simulated; ORGASMIC_DRIVER_SIMULATE=1 still forces simulated. Fixture tests (stub claude binary) cover all three gate cases. Gates: workspace green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. LIVE DdOGFOOD PASSED [2026-06-13 ~14:17, manager]: dispatched throwaway probe TASK-E29XC via --worker implementer-claude-acp against the REAL claude binary; session JSONL showed acquire -> Ready{simulated:false, kind:worker} -> text_chunk(system) -> text_chunk(assistant) — real-wire stdio with live assistant output, exactly the AC pattern (pre-fix this was simulated:true then silence). Probe aborted post-capabilities-check (tx-0508), worktree+branch removed, TASK-E29XC cancelled. implementer-claude-acp real-wire stdio is now validated end-to-end for the first time.

* DONE TASK-GD0W1 Run telemetry gaps for PTY dispatch runs: sub_state, role in RunSummary, babysitter policy :daemon:supervisor:runs:telemetry:rmux:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GD0W1
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs shipped/workers/
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs shipped/workers/ shipped/schema/dec_P7EY8.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Three telemetry gaps observed on rmux+claude PTY dispatch runs (TASK-K5RG2
reviewer run-20260610T210144, TASK-CJXKM implementer run-20260610T210146):

*Gap A — no initial sub_state on spawn:* Live runs surface =sub_state: null=
(see =orgasmic run show= output). Per dec_P7EY8, a run in the working phase
should carry =<role>.working= sub-state from the moment it acquires. Fix:
set sub_state to =reviewer.working= / =implementer.working= on acquire in
the supervisor, not just when the first heartbeat arrives.

*Gap B — dispatched role missing from RunSummary:* Reviewer runs surface
=kind: "implementer"= in the run record (=orgasmic run show= → =run.kind=).
The UI currently parses the role out of the session filename as a workaround.
Fix: RunSummary / the task-summary run record should carry the worker kind
(reviewer/implementer/architector) from the dispatch metadata, not infer it
from the session filename.

*Gap C — rmux worker templates have no babysitter configured:* The first
(wrong) dispatches auto-spawned babysitters; the correct rmux+claude workers
did not (run list showed no companion babysitters). Decide and document
whether rmux workers should have a babysitter_worker field. If yes, name the
appropriate babysitter template; if no, document the rationale in
=.orgasmic/gotchas.org= so future operators don't expect one.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] *Gap A:* =orgasmic run show <rmux-run-id>= shows =sub_state:
  reviewer.working= (or =implementer.working=) from the first live event.
- [ ] *Gap B:* =run.kind= in the run record (daemon API + =run show=) matches
  the dispatched role (reviewer/implementer), not the harness kind.
- [ ] *Gap C:* Decision documented: either rmux worker templates include a
  =babysitter_worker= field and a babysitter is spawned, or =gotchas.org=
  records the explicit "no babysitter for rmux workers" policy.
- [ ] Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, MSRV 1.78.

** Evidence
codex gpt-5.5 medium (re-dispatch after a silent stall — see below), worker commit =1c23f99=, merged, close tx-0522. Gap A: sub_state set to <role>.working on acquire/continuation/reattach (initial_working_sub_state) instead of None-until-heartbeat. Gap B: RunSummary.kind now serializes the dispatched ROLE string (implementer/reviewer/babysitter/…); added RunSummary.run_kind carrying the old supervisor lease axis (worker/babysitter) for consumers that need it. MANAGER-VERIFIED the run.kind repurposing is non-breaking: rec.role already encoded 'babysitter' (supervisor.rs:612/856 RunKind::Babysitter -> "babysitter"), so the UI TaskAgentBadges kind==='babysitter' distinction still holds; RunsView/TaskView already used role ?? kind. Gap C: gotchas.org documents that rmux workers intentionally do NOT auto-spawn babysitters (rationale: interactive PTY proof surfaces; companion would need a second rmux pane and the babysitter prompt is implementer-tuned). Regression tests: acquire_sets_working_sub_state_from_role_before_heartbeat, get_run_exposes_dispatched_role_as_kind_and_initial_sub_state. Gates: workspace green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, parse-errors 0; daemon rebuilt+restarted pid 62505. DISPATCH NOTE: the FIRST run (run-...T141628) SILENT-STALLED — codex thread+Ready but turn never started (0 turn/started, 0% CPU, no worktree change 32min; heartbeats masked it from stall detection). Aborted tx-0515, re-dispatched clean. The heartbeat-masks-hung-turn gap is real and in this task's telemetry domain — candidate follow-up.

* DONE TASK-GYMSP cursor-ACP adapter + ("acp-stdio","cursor-agent") driver pair :drivers:cursor:acp:observability:hardening:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GYMSP
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor_acp.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/mod.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/sandbox.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/jsonrpc.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer-acp.org shipped/workers/implementer-composer-acp.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor_acp.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/mod.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/sandbox.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/jsonrpc.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer-acp.org shipped/workers/implementer-composer-acp.org
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/claude.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/jsonrpc.rs crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:END:

** Description
Cursor 2.0 ships =cursor-agent acp= — JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio implementing the Agent Client Protocol (https://cursor.com/docs/cli/acp, https://agentclientprotocol.com). Our current cursor wiring (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor.rs=, 1486 lines) uses =--print --output-format stream-json= with manual prefix-dedup parsing and *post-hoc* tool-policy filtering — TASK-QSBDB's allowlist downgrades unsafe shell calls to telemetry AFTER the native shell tool already ran.

ACP gives us: typed turn lifecycle (=initialize=/=session/new=/=session/prompt= → =StopReason=), typed tool-call events (=kind ∈ read|edit|delete|move|search|execute|think|fetch|other=, =status ∈ pending|in_progress|completed|failed=), and crucially a *pre-execution* permission gate via =session/request_permission= that lets us map orgasmic's SandboxAllowlist semantics to ={allow_once, reject_once, ...}= and BLOCK unsafe calls before they run.

Add a =CursorAcpAdapter= mirroring the existing =adapters/claude.rs= ACP adapter (~962 LOC), register the =("acp-stdio","cursor-agent")= pair in =SUPPORTED= alongside the existing =("acp-stdio","claude")= and =("acp-stdio","codex")= entries, and ship a new =implementer-composer-acp= agent template cloning =implementer-composer.org=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/adapters/cursor_acp.rs= implements =HarnessEventAdapter= with =stdio_spawn= returning the =cursor-agent acp= subprocess (auth via =CURSOR_API_KEY= env), =stdio_session_init= building =initialize= + =session/new= envelopes per the protocol spec, =parse_event= translating =session/update= variants (=plan=, =agent_message_chunk=, =tool_call=, =tool_call_update=) to =DriverEvent=, and =try_handle_approval= responding to =session/request_permission= using existing SandboxAllowlist semantics.
- [ ] =("acp-stdio", "cursor-agent")= pair added to =SUPPORTED= in =crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/lib.rs=.
- [ ] Legacy alias =cursor-acp= → resolves to the new pair (sibling of the existing =cursor-agent= → =subprocess-stream-json= alias).
- [ ] =shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer-acp.org= clones =implementer-composer.org= with =:DEFAULT_DRIVER: acp-stdio=, =:DEFAULT_HARNESS: cursor-agent=, =:KIND: implementer=.
- [ ] =StopReason::refusal= and =StopReason::max_turn_requests= map to a sensible =DriverEvent= (likely =RunFail= with a typed reason). Document the mapping inline.
- [ ] =session/cancel= wired to driver-side cancellation (replaces SIGKILL).
- [ ] Tool-policy enforcement *pre-execution* via permission-gate response (not post-hoc filter): use the existing SandboxAllowlist + path-canonicalization helpers from =cursor.rs:730-1022= (the TASK-QSBDB hardening) but apply them in =try_handle_approval= instead of after the fact. Reject unsafe calls with ={outcome:"selected", optionId:"reject_once"}=.
- [ ] Sandbox-vs-permission tradeoff documented: legacy =--print --sandbox enabled= sets a hard filesystem boundary; ACP has no =--sandbox= flag (relies entirely on permission prompts). Decide whether to keep both code paths or deprecate =--print=. For this task: ship ACP alongside =--print=; deprecation decision deferred.
- [ ] Tests: at least 5 unit tests covering session_init envelope shape, tool_call event translation, tool_call_update status transitions, permission-request handling (allow + reject paths), and stop-reason mapping. Mirror the test patterns in =modes/acp_stdio.rs= and =adapters/claude.rs=.
- [ ] =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= green; =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; MSRV 1.78.
- [ ] No changes to existing =adapters/cursor.rs= (=--print= path stays untouched for now).

** Notes
- *Migration cost: small-to-medium.* Closest analogue is =adapters/claude.rs= (962 LOC). The =acp-stdio= mode runtime (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs=, 793 LOC) and JSON-RPC handshake + approval dispatch (=crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/jsonrpc.rs=, 343 LOC) are already generic over the adapter and battle-tested by both claude and codex. New code: ~600-900 LOC adapter + tests.
- *Stability wins over =--print= path:* typed completion (StopReason discriminator vs scraping ={"type":"result"}=), graceful cancellation (session/cancel vs SIGKILL), session resume (session/load with stored sessionId), typed tool-call kinds (no manual suffix-stripping/camelCase→snake_case translation), typed permission gate (pre-execution allow/reject) vs post-hoc telemetry.
- *Observability wins:* first-class plan + tool_call_update status transitions (pending → in_progress → completed/failed), partial tool-call field updates, structured agent_message_chunk deltas (no prefix-dedup needed), cursor/* extensions (ask_question, create_plan, update_todos, task, generate_image).
- *Risks / unknowns (from research):* (a) auth-state sharing between =--print= and =acp= modes is undocumented — needs empirical check. (b) Permission-prompt latency may degrade vs =--sandbox enabled=. (c) MCP scope: only =.cursor/mcp.json= MCP servers work in ACP. (d) StopReason::refusal/max_turn_requests have no clean RunComplete/RunFail analogue.
- *Out of scope*: deprecating =adapters/cursor.rs= =--print= path (separate task once ACP proves itself); cursor-ACP for the reviewer agent role (separate template if desired); UI changes; daemon-side WorkerDriver changes (the existing trait is sufficient).
- *Source*: research conducted [2026-05-28 Thu 12:20] (cursor-ACP comparison agent report inline in manager session). Cursor docs at https://cursor.com/docs/cli/acp; ACP protocol spec at https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/{prompt-turn,tool-calls,session-setup}.

** Evidence
- codex gpt-5.5 medium, worker commit =856f5b7=, merged as =663c36a= after manager diff review and fixes.
- Shipped =CursorAcpAdapter= for =cursor-agent acp=, registered =("acp-stdio","cursor-agent")= plus legacy =cursor-acp= while preserving =cursor-agent= -> =subprocess-stream-json=.
- Manager fixes: added discoverable =shipped/workers/implementer-composer-acp.org= because current loader uses =shipped/workers/=, kept the requested legacy =shipped/agent-templates/implementer-composer-acp.org=, widened task scope properties, fixed ACP edit/delete/move permission behavior for worktree-only writes, and updated shipped-worker fixture count.
- Verification after manager fixes: =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers= green; =cargo test --workspace= green; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings= clean; =cargo fmt --check= clean; =git diff --check= clean.
- Earlier flakes during worker self-check: one workspace run hit =dispatch_close_prunes_stem_dir_leaving_brief=; targeted rerun passed and final full workspace rerun passed. No live =cursor-agent acp= dogfood was performed; residual risk is real Cursor ACP wire/auth behavior.

* DONE TASK-BJHKN Phase 2: drift comparator CLI + // arch: marker bootstrap sweep :daemon:cli:drift:architecture:phase-2:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-BJHKN
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-stdio
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_C87Z9 arch_MPAQT
:PRODUCES:         crates/orgasmic-cli/src/architecture_drift.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** crates/orgasmic-core/src/** crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/** ui/src/**
:READ_SCOPE:       .orgasmic/architecture.org crates/** ui/src/** shipped/**
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test --workspace
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-ENXWA TASK-YWRG5
:END:

** Description
Implement the two-way drift comparator from dec_BP4NH refinement [2026-05-27]:
1. Walk the repo, find all =// arch: arch_NNN.M= (and =// @arch arch_NNN.M= for TypeScript) markers at file top. Build a map: file → leaf IDs (comma-list allowed for multi-component files).
2. Parse =.orgasmic/architecture.org= via the TASK-YWRG5 parser. For each implementation-backed leaf with =:SOURCE_PATHS:=, read its source paths.
3. Drift report categories: (a) paths in =:SOURCE_PATHS:= missing on disk; (b) paths existing but missing the matching marker; (c) markers in code with no matching leaf path enumerated; (d) markers referencing a leaf ID that doesn't exist.
4. CLI surface: =orgasmic architecture drift= → human-readable report + non-zero exit on drift. =--json= flag for CI consumption.
5. *Bootstrap sweep* (part of this task): add =// arch:= markers to all files referenced from the new =:SOURCE_PATHS:= in TASK-ENXWA's architecture.org. One-time large-touch sweep across =crates/**= and =ui/src/**=.

Grilling context: dec_BP4NH refinement [2026-05-27].

** Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] =orgasmic architecture drift= runs against the repo and reports the four drift categories above. Exit code: 0 if clean, non-zero if any drift exists.
- [ ] =--json= flag produces machine-readable output suitable for CI.
- [ ] All files enumerated in =.orgasmic/architecture.org= shipped-leaf =:SOURCE_PATHS:= carry a top-of-file =// arch: arch_NNN.M= marker (or comma-list for multi-component files).
- [ ] Files NOT in any leaf's =:SOURCE_PATHS:= and without a marker do not flag drift (only shipped leaves are enforced).

- [ ] Marker bootstrap sweep is committed in a SEPARATE commit from the comparator implementation so review can isolate the two changes.
- [ ] =cargo test --workspace= green; clippy clean; MSRV 1.78.

** Notes

- *Out of scope*: in-app drift surface (TASK-MW5BX); automatic drift detection on file-watch (defer).

** Evidence

Existing shipped source files have matching top-of-file markers; no marker-without-leaf-path or unknown-leaf drift remained.
- Manager gates green: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli architecture_drift`; `cargo test --workspace`; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `git diff --check`.
- Residual drift is intentionally reported, not masked, because the two architecture paths do not exist on disk and architecture rewrite / placeholder file creation was outside this marker bootstrap task.
