* DONE TASK-R3TWA Add persistent OS service adapters for CLI-owned daemon lifecycle :cli:daemon:lifecycle:service:cross-platform:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-R3TWA
:PRIORITY:         P2
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-cli/tests/** README.md .orgasmic/decisions.org
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-cli/src/** crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/** README.md .orgasmic/decisions.org
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-cli --test cli_parity; cargo test -p orgasmic-cli daemon_lifecycle
:END:

** Description
dec_SCVKG gives CLI-only users automatic local daemon lifecycle through a
cross-platform detached-process adapter. That removes the immediate tmux/manual
terminal requirement, but it does not yet provide login/reboot persistence
through each operating system's native service manager.

Implement persistent service adapters behind the same logical CLI contract:
=orgasmic daemon start|stop|restart|status= and auto-start for daemon-backed
commands must keep their semantics. The adapter should pick the native owner
where available: macOS LaunchAgent (=launchctl=), Linux =systemd --user= when
available, and Windows Service or a documented non-admin scheduled-task/process
owner if service installation requires elevated privileges. Preserve
=orgasmic serve= as the foreground debug primitive and keep explicit
=ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL= targets externally owned.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] macOS adapter writes/loads/unloads a user LaunchAgent under
      =~/Library/LaunchAgents= and logs to =$ORGASMIC_HOME/logs=.
- [X] Linux adapter uses =systemd --user= when available and falls back with a
      clear diagnostic when no user service manager exists.
- [X] Windows adapter has a real process owner strategy that works without the
      desktop app, with elevation requirements documented.
- [X] =orgasmic daemon status= reports the selected adapter and whether it is
      persistent or detached-only.
- [X] Tests cover adapter selection/rendered service definitions without
      requiring root/admin privileges.

** Evidence
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Implementer branch `task-r3twa-impl` committed `f91981b` and merged no-ff to main as `26f8eed` (`Merge TASK-R3TWA: persistent daemon service adapters`).
- Added CLI daemon service adapters: macOS user LaunchAgent, Linux `systemd --user` with explicit detached-process fallback, Windows least-privilege per-user Scheduled Task. `orgasmic daemon status` now reports `adapter`, `persistence: installed=... enabled=...`, and optional `persistence_detail`. `ORGASMIC_DAEMON_URL` remains externally owned; local start/stop/restart refuse it.
- Manager review patch added the `dec_SCVKG` decision update and corrected the Windows scheduled task XML declaration to UTF-8 to match the generated file bytes. Host service managers were not invoked; render/selection/status behavior is covered by pure tests.
- Gates on dispatch worktree: `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli --test cli_parity`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli daemon_lifecycle`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli daemon_service`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli --test daemon_lifecycle`, `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace`, `git diff --check` all passed.
- Gates on merged main: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli --test cli_parity`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli daemon_lifecycle`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli daemon_service`, `cargo test -p orgasmic-cli --test daemon_lifecycle`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `git diff --check` all passed.
- Dispatch closed as done with tx `tx-20260613-orgasmic-0564`; cleanup requested with worktree remove and branch delete.

* DONE TASK-ZRZYB Validate worker templates at save-time, not spawn-time (dry-run endpoint + doctor lint) :daemon:api:workers:dx:validation:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-ZRZYB
:PRIORITY:         P5
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/ ui/src/**
:READ_SCOPE:       crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/content.rs crates/orgasmic-core/src/workers.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon
:END:

** Description
Worker templates are already hot-reloaded: =spawn_worker_run= reads the worker
=.org= fresh from disk on every dispatch (=load_stage_worker= →
=content::load_worker= → =load_worker_path=, no cache), and =collect_worker_paths=
globs both =shipped/workers/= and =~/.orgasmic/user/workers/= each call. So a user
editing a template's model / provider / driver / effort / persona sees it applied
on the next spawn with no daemon restart — good.

The weakness is *feedback timing*. An invalid edit (unknown model id, unsupported
driver/harness pair, malformed property drawer, missing linked skill) does not
surface until the user actually dispatches, at which point the spawn fails late
with a =WorkerLoad= / parse error. For users who experiment with their own worker
templates frequently, this is a slow, surprising loop.

Add save-time validation so a broken template is flagged before dispatch:
- A dry-run / validate endpoint that loads a worker by id (or accepts inline org
  source), runs the same parse + schema + =driver_for_mode_harness= +
  linked-skill resolution that =spawn_worker_run= uses, and returns structured
  ok/errors WITHOUT acquiring a run.
- Surface it in the worker/template editor UI (inline error on save/edit).
- Optionally extend the existing doctor lint to sweep =workers/*.org= and report
  unresolvable driver/harness, model, or skill references.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] A validate endpoint resolves a worker (by id and/or inline source) and returns the same failure classes =spawn_worker_run= would hit (parse error, schema error, unsupported driver/harness, missing skill) as structured results, without spawning a run or holding a lease.
- [X] The worker/template editor surfaces validation errors at save/edit time through the Settings worker pipeline/status surface backed by =GET /workers/validate=.
- [X] Doctor lint (or the validate path) reports unresolvable driver/harness, model, or linked-skill references across =workers/*.org=; this landed as the validate-path sweep endpoint.
- [X] Tests cover a valid template plus each failure class.

** Evidence
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Implemented worker template validation before spawn: =GET /workers/validate= sweeps shipped/user worker templates; =POST /workers/validate= validates either ={id}= or inline =source=; responses return structured =ok/errors= diagnostics and stop before any supervisor lease/acquire.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Validation reuses worker parsing/schema, linked-skill resolution, =driver_for_mode_harness=, and driver config validation. Diagnostics cover =parse_error=, =schema_error=, =unsupported_driver_harness=, =missing_skill=, =driver_config=, read/not-found errors.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Settings worker pipeline now consumes =/workers/validate= and marks invalid workers inline, with expandable diagnostic messages for broken templates.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Gates passed: =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon validate_worker_source=; =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon --test integration worker_validate_routes_return_structured_diagnostics=; =cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon=; =cargo fmt --check=; =npm --prefix ui run typecheck=; =git diff --check=.

* DONE TASK-BTESP Workspace MSRV 1.78 broken by transitive idna_adapter edition2024 requirement :build:msrv:deps:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-BTESP
:PRIORITY: P3
:TEST_CMD: rustup run 1.87.0 cargo check --workspace
:WRITE_SCOPE: Cargo.toml Cargo.lock .orgasmic/gotchas.org
:END:

TASK-T3ENY's MSRV-1.78 gate probe found =rustup run 1.78.0 cargo check --workspace= now fails: transitive dep =idna_adapter v1.2.2= requires Cargo =edition2024= (Rust 1.85+), unsupported by 1.78. The stated workspace MSRV (Cargo.toml workspace.package + gotchas.org) is no longer achievable. Decide: (a) pin idna_adapter / its parent (url/idna) to a pre-edition2024 version in Cargo.lock, (b) raise the workspace MSRV to the real floor and update the gotchas.org MSRV entry, or (c) document 1.78 as aspirational. Whichever — make =cargo check= on the declared MSRV pass again, or correct the declared MSRV so it is truthful.
** Evidence
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Reproduced the stale declared-MSRV failure: =rustup run 1.78.0 cargo check --workspace= fails before build because Cargo 1.78 cannot parse edition-2024 crates. First blocker was =idna_adapter v1.2.2=; after a temporary pin proved this was broader, the next blocker was =clap_builder v4.6.0=.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Chose the task's allowed option (b): raise the workspace MSRV instead of pinning a growing set of 2026 transitive dependencies. =cargo metadata --locked= showed current dependency metadata reaching Rust 1.87.0 (WASI/wit-bindgen family), with active deps already at 1.85-1.86.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Updated =Cargo.toml= workspace =rust-version= to =1.87= and refreshed the MSRV gotcha to explain why 1.78 is no longer truthful. Restored temporary =idna_adapter= / =clap_lex= pins, so =Cargo.lock= has no intentional change.
- [2026-06-13 Sat] Gates passed: =rustup run 1.87.0 cargo check --workspace=; =cargo check --workspace= on the default toolchain.

* DONE TASK-HCFBT.4 Remove codex-acp :STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200 overrides once heartbeats are proven live :drivers:codex:acp:reliability:cleanup:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-HCFBT.4
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-codex-acp
:IMPLEMENTS:       arch_A53QX
:PRODUCES:         shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org
:WRITE_SCOPE:      shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org
:READ_SCOPE:       shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org crates/orgasmic-drivers/src/modes/acp_stdio.rs
:TEST_CMD:         cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers
:DEPENDS_ON:       TASK-HCFBT.3
:END:

** Description
TASK-HCFBT.3 made the codex-acp adapter (=acp-stdio= mode) emit liveness
=DriverEvent::Heartbeat= events every ~30s during quiet turns, so the
supervisor's stall detector resets on genuine activity even while codex buffers
a long subprocess (e.g. =cargo test --workspace=). That removes the need for the
band-aid =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= (2h) overrides shipped on the codex-acp
worker templates.

This task removes those overrides and returns codex-acp workers to the built-in
600s default — but ONLY after a real live codex-acp dispatch has demonstrated
that heartbeats actually hold a substantive (>10min quiet) run under the 600s
default. Do not close on unit tests alone; the whole point is to validate the
end-to-end daemon→adapter→supervisor heartbeat path in production.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] A live codex-acp dispatch runs a workload with a >10-minute quiet
  subprocess window (e.g. =cargo test --workspace= in a worktree) WITHOUT a
  =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS:= override and is NOT released with
  =stall_timeout_exceeded= — confirmed by the run's session JSONL showing
  interleaved =type:"heartbeat"= events during the quiet window.
- [X] Remove the =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= property from
  =shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org= and
  =shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org=.
- [X] clippy + fmt + diff-check clean (likely no =.rs= touched).

** Evidence
- Removed =:STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS: 7200= from both shipped codex-acp worker templates:
  =shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org= and
  =shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org=. Verification:
  =rg -n "STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS" shipped/workers/implementer-codex-acp.org shipped/workers/reviewer-codex-acp.org=
  exited 1 with no matches.
- Live proof dispatch:
  =run-20260613T200459-25f244b7229d42c8a4d165941e8e0990= using
  =implementer-codex-acp= / =acp-stdio= / harness =codex=. Session JSONL:
  =.orgasmic/tmp/sessions/dispatch-TASK-HCFBT.4-implementer-20260613T200459.jsonl=.
  The run metadata had =simulated:false= and no =stall_timeout_secs= driver
  override, so it used the built-in 600s stall timeout.
- The live worker ran the required quiet subprocess
  =python3 -c 'import time; time.sleep(660)'=. The session recorded the tool
  call at 2026-06-13T20:05:44Z and completed/released at
  2026-06-13T20:16:56Z with lifecycle outcome =completed= and reason
  ="driver stream closed"=. The JSONL contains 18 =heartbeat= driver events
  during the quiet window and no =stall_timeout_exceeded= record. Worker summary
  confirms no files were edited by the proof worker.
- Gates:
  =cargo test -p orgasmic-drivers > /tmp/orgasmic-task-hcfbt4-drivers-tests.log 2>&1=
  passed; =cargo fmt --check > /tmp/orgasmic-task-hcfbt4-fmt-check.log 2>&1=
  passed; =cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings > /tmp/orgasmic-task-hcfbt4-clippy.log 2>&1=
  passed after applying three mechanical =clippy::unnecessary_map_or= rewrites in
  =crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs= that were required for the workspace-wide
  clippy acceptance gate; =git diff --check= passed; =orgasmic status= reported
  =parse_errors: 0=.

* DONE TASK-GKTWY.1 Coalesced stderr diagnostics truncate to the first chunk (review M1 of TASK-GKTWY) :ui:manager:transcript:bug:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GKTWY.1
:PRIORITY:         P3
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/manager/** ui/src/lib/**
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/components/manager/ManagerChatTranscript.tsx ui/src/lib/transcriptUtils.ts
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run test -- --run
:END:

** Description
TASK-GKTWY reviewer finding M1 (MEDIUM, latent): in =coalesceTextChunks=,
consecutive stderr chunks sharing =mergeKey: 'tool:stderr-diagnostics'=
append clean text to =previous.text= but never update =previous.activity=.
ActivityRow renders only =activity.preview=/=activity.raw= (first chunk),
and =entry.text= is ignored when =activity= exists — so chunks 2..N are
invisible and the raw-behind-disclosure guarantee breaks. Doesn't trigger
in the evidence session (single stderr chunk) but will under repeated
websocket-retry noise.

Fix direction (from review): when merging tool-diagnostics entries, also
append the incoming clean text to =previous.activity.preview= and the
incoming raw to =previous.activity.raw=. Also cover review L1: unit test
for orphaned command-result silent-drop in =groupToolEntries=.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] Multi-chunk consecutive stderr renders all chunks in the visible row
  and full raw behind disclosure (unit test with 3+ chunk fixture).
- [X] Orphaned command-result behavior covered by a test (L1).
- [X] vitest green.

** Evidence
- Moved =coalesceTextChunks= into =ui/src/lib/transcriptUtils.ts= and kept
  =ManagerChatTranscript.tsx= wired to the shared helper.
- Fixed stderr diagnostic coalescing so consecutive
  =tool:stderr-diagnostics= chunks append not only =entry.text= but also
  =activity.preview= and =activity.raw=. This keeps both the visible row and
  disclosure body complete for repeated stderr websocket diagnostic chunks.
- Added a 3-chunk regression in =ui/src/lib/__tests__/transcriptUtils.test.ts=
  covering merged visible preview, raw disclosure content, and merge-key
  stripping. Added the L1 test proving an orphaned =command result= with a
  =callId= and no request/start pair is silently dropped by =groupToolEntries=.
- Gates: =npm --prefix ui run test -- --run src/lib/__tests__/transcriptUtils.test.ts=
  passed (27 tests); =npm --prefix ui run test -- --run= passed (7 files, 70
  tests); =npm --prefix ui run typecheck= passed.

* DONE TASK-SV032 UI run-dock polish: ended-run tab label degrades to run-id; Enter in worker xterm misses PTY :ui:run-dock:rmux:polish:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-SV032
:PRIORITY:         P4
:WORKER:           implementer-claude-rmux
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/
:READ_SCOPE:       ui/src/components/ ui/src/lib/types.ts ui/src/lib/api.ts
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run test && npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
Two small run-dock papercuts observed during TASK-K5RG2/TASK-CJXKM dispatch session:

*Issue A — ended-run tab label degrades to raw run-id:* After a PTY dispatch
run finishes, its dock tab shows the bare =run-20260610T...= run-id string
instead of a human-readable label (task id + role, e.g. "TASK-K5RG2 reviewer").
The label is presumably built from a run record field that is absent or null
on ended runs. Identify which field(s) the tab label uses and ensure the
label is stable across the run lifecycle (active → ended).

*Issue B — Enter key in worker xterm does not reliably reach the PTY:* When
clicking into an embedded worker xterm pane (rmux-backed session) and pressing
Enter, the keystroke occasionally does not reach the terminal. Likely a focus
or event-propagation issue in the xterm component: the xterm element may not
hold keyboard focus after a mouse click, or the event bubbles up and gets
consumed by the dock container. Fix focus acquisition on xterm click so Enter
reliably writes to the PTY.

** Acceptance Criteria
- [X] *Issue A:* Tab label for ended PTY dispatch runs shows task id + role
  (e.g. "TASK-K5RG2 reviewer"), not a raw run-id. Consistent with in-progress
  tab labels.
- [X] *Issue B:* Clicking into an embedded xterm pane and pressing Enter
  reliably sends the keystroke to the PTY. Manual verification in Chrome.
- [X] =npm --prefix ui run build= stays green. No off-limits files touched.

** Evidence
- Issue A implementation: =RunDock.tsx= caches readable =runTabTitle(run)= labels by live run id, and =runDockLabels.ts= resolves an open worker tab from the live run first, the cached readable label second, and the raw run id only as a last fallback. This keeps active -> ended tab labels readable inside the browser session.
- Issue A regression coverage: =ui/src/components/manager/__tests__/runDockLabels.test.ts= covers active live-run labels, cached ended-run labels, and the raw run-id fallback when no live/cached label exists.
- Issue B implementation: =ManagerTmuxPane.tsx= focuses the xterm terminal on pointer/mouse capture for the terminal host, so a click inside the embedded pane moves keyboard focus to xterm before Enter is pressed while preserving xterm child handlers.
- Browser smoke completed on the canonical local UI =http://127.0.0.1:5180/=. Launched a safe rmux custom manager run =run-20260613T205006-04e3724bb6eb4178b36ed8dfa676c6bf=, clicked the embedded xterm textbox (=Terminal input=), typed =echo sv032-enter-smoke=, pressed Enter, and observed the terminal echo plus output =sv032-enter-smoke=. The custom run was then stopped from the UI.
- Cleanup proof: =orgasmic run list | jq .live= returned =[]= after stopping the temporary custom run.
- Gates passed after the label-helper regression test was added: =npm --prefix ui run test= (8 files, 73 tests) and =npm --prefix ui run build= (includes =tsc --noEmit= and Vite production build). Only files under =ui/src/components/= were edited for implementation/tests.

* DONE TASK-GFKFW.3 fix-subtask: make updater live-run guard authoritative in Rust install_app_update :install:updater:tauri:fix:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GFKFW.3
:PRIORITY:         P4
:WRITE_SCOPE:      src-tauri/src/lib.rs ui/src/components/SettingsView.tsx
:TEST_CMD:         cargo check -p orgasmic-desktop; npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
The live-run guard for app updates is UI-side only: =SettingsView.installUpdate=
pre-checks =/recovery/status= =live_runs=, then calls the Rust
=install_app_update= command, which downloads/installs unconditionally —
leaving a check→install TOCTOU window. Have =install_app_update= re-check
=/recovery/status= itself so the block is authoritative.

** Evidence
- Implementation: =install_app_update= now verifies a pending update exists, then runs a Rust-side active-run guard before taking and installing the pending update. This preserves the pending update if the guard blocks.
- Authoritative guard: desktop builds call =assert_no_live_runs_before_update=, which reads the local daemon auth token, fetches =GET /recovery/status= from =127.0.0.1:4848= with Bearer auth, parses =live_runs=, and fails closed when the daemon cannot be verified or when live runs exist. The UI pre-check remains only early feedback.
- Scope note: the required =cargo check -p orgasmic-desktop= gate runs inside the nested =src-tauri= workspace. From the repo root the same package selector is not visible. Running the nested gate revealed the pre-existing =src-tauri/Cargo.lock= was stale for =orgasmic-core='s existing =rand= dependency, so the lockfile refresh is included as gate-required state.
- Gates passed: =cargo check -p orgasmic-desktop= from =src-tauri/= and =npm --prefix ui run build=.

* DONE TASK-GFKFW.4 fix-subtask: shadcn Select for update channel + downgrade-aware comparator note :install:updater:ui:polish:fix:low:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:               TASK-GFKFW.4
:PRIORITY:         P5
:WRITE_SCOPE:      ui/src/components/SettingsView.tsx src-tauri/src/lib.rs
:TEST_CMD:         npm --prefix ui run build
:END:

** Description
Polish: the update-channel selector is a raw =<select>= amid shadcn =Select=
components (SettingsView.tsx:447-451) — swap it for shadcn =Select=. Also the
updater =version_comparator= (=release.version != current=, lib.rs:125) treats
channel-switch downgrades (nightly 0.99.x → stable 0.0.1) as updates; add a
clarifying comment or guard if downgrade-on-channel-switch is undesired.

** Evidence
- Replaced the raw update-channel =<select>= in =SettingsView.tsx= with the existing shadcn =Select= primitives already used in the file, sourcing options from =UPDATE_CHANNELS= so the UI stays aligned with =UpdateChannel=.
- Added a downgrade-aware comparator comment in =src-tauri/src/lib.rs=: channel selection is explicit, so switching from nightly back to stable should still surface the selected channel release even when its semver is lower than the installed nightly build. The comparator behavior remains =release.version != current=.
- Gate passed: =npm --prefix ui run build=.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Constraints rewritten: positions resolve local localStorage > shared graph_layout.org baseline > deterministic default (was localStorage-only, never files); active tasks (backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review) are INCLUDED with backbone edges only (was tasks-excluded); geometry and curation are editable while topology stays read-only / no node create-delete (was fully read-only); glossary and artifacts off by default with glossary as a left column.

* DONE TASK-D3FQJ Provision harness/driver PATH in generated daemon service definitions :daemon:install:dispatch:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-D3FQJ
:IMPLEMENTS: dec_2D5BC
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_service.rs crates/orgasmic-cli/src/doctor.rs
:END:

Description: Implement dec_2D5BC. The generated daemon service definitions set only ORGASMIC_HOME, so under launchd (and systemd/windows) the daemon runs with a minimal PATH and cannot find tmux/rmux/claude - every dispatch goes inert (inert_reason tmux_missing / binary_missing:claude). This is the default macOS install path (select_adapter_for_host keys on platform only), so it ships broken.

Acceptance Criteria:
- render_macos_launch_agent writes a PATH into EnvironmentVariables that prepends the user's tool dirs (/opt/homebrew/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, ~/.local/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin or the login-shell PATH captured at install/start), in addition to ORGASMIC_HOME.
- The Linux systemd user unit and the Windows scheduled-task siblings get the equivalent PATH provisioning.
- And/or: harness + driver binaries are resolved via a login shell or stored absolute paths at spawn time so the daemon PATH is not a single point of failure.
- orgasmic doctor checks that the configured harness (claude) and driver (tmux/rmux) binaries resolve under the daemon's effective PATH and warns actionably when they do not.
- Test: the generated plist/unit contains a PATH covering the tool dirs (extend the existing render_macos_launch_agent test at daemon_service.rs ~line 567); a doctor test asserts the missing-binary warning fires.
- Verify: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli; cargo clippy -p orgasmic-cli.

Note: validated live on 2026-06-15 - dispatch only worked after relaunching the daemon manually with the zsh -l login PATH; the launchd-managed daemon stayed PATH-blind.

Description: Implement dec_KTF04 so the Graph page can render superseded decisions gray. Stop deleting a superseded decision; mark it derived-superseded when a present decision's :SUPERSEDES: points at it.

Acceptance Criteria:
- DecisionSummary / decision graph nodes carry a derived superseded:bool computed from :SUPERSEDES: backrefs across present decisions (no status field, no tag added).
- /decisions and /graph/nodes expose the flag; accepted = present AND not a supersession target.
- DecisionsView hides superseded decisions by default (toggle to show).
- Convention recorded: the superseding flow keeps the old heading (no delete); covered by dec_KTF04.
- Tests: a fixture with X superseded by Y yields X.superseded=true, Y.superseded=false. cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon.
** Evidence
Merged as b9b30e6 (worker commit a3ed277, implementer-claude-rmux). index.rs derives superseded via a two-pass load_decisions (collect :SUPERSEDES: targets, then set the flag) plus a project-wide apply_superseded_flags for cross-file safety; superseded:bool added to DecisionSummary and GraphNodeSummary (false for non-decision layers); api.rs test helper updated; types.ts gains superseded?:boolean on both types; DecisionsView hides superseded by default with a count-gated Show/Hide toggle. Gates (manager-verified post-merge): cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon = 167 unit + integration green except the pre-existing skill_routes_include_shipped_and_user_markdown_skills failure (independently confirmed failing on clean main b35ec10 at integration.rs:799 - not a regression); the new test superseded_flag_derived_from_supersedes_backrefs asserts dec_X.superseded=true / dec_Y.superseded=false on BOTH /decisions summaries and /graph/nodes; clippy -p orgasmic-daemon -D warnings clean; ui typecheck clean. Manager diff review: clean, in-scope (4 files), no out-of-scope churn. Reviewer-claude-rmux pass pending.
Reviewer pass: reviewer-claude-rmux (claude-opus-4-8), run ...645ac6c, verdict APPROVE - all findings LOW/non-blocking. L1 (apply_superseded_flags redundant + misleading comment), L2 (:SUPERSEDES: whitespace-only parse; comma-separated would silently fail, self-supersession unhandled), L3 (toggle missing aria-pressed) all fixed in bf204bd: apply_superseded_flags is now the sole project-wide setter with an honest comment; SUPERSEDES splits on whitespace and commas and skips self-refs; aria-pressed added. Re-verified: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon 167 unit + new test green (only the pre-existing skill_routes failure remains), clippy clean, tsc clean. Final: TASK-8BRYP DONE.

* DONE TASK-AQMTA Daemon: /graph/layout endpoint over graph_layout.org overlay :daemon:graph:
:PROPERTIES:
:DEPENDS_ON: TASK-Z30V0
:ID: TASK-AQMTA
:IMPLEMENTS: arch_M8JQT

Description: Persist and serve the layout overlay (term_5N4M6) decided in dec_WWAHT. Overlay-only: never persists topology.

Acceptance Criteria:
- On-disk format .orgasmic/graph_layout.org: one heading per intentionally-placed node id, geometry/curation in the property drawer (:X: :Y: :W: :H: :HIDDEN: :PINNED:). Parsed by the core org engine; missing file = empty overlay.
- GET /graph/layout?project= returns the overlay as JSON (map node id -> {x,y,w,h,hidden,pinned}).
- PATCH /graph/layout upserts one node's entry through the flocked org writer (reuse upsert_property / the direct-edit writer); concurrent writes safe.
- Pruning of entries for nonexistent node ids is optional cleanup, not required for correctness.
- Tests: PATCH then GET round-trips one node; two PATCHes to different nodes do not conflict. cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon.
** Evidence
Merged as 0e5b21e (worker commit 65036ce, implementer-claude-rmux/sonnet-4-6). core: GraphLayoutEntry (x/y/w/h Option<i64>, hidden/pinned Option<bool>) + parse_graph_layout_overlay(&OrgFile)->HashMap keyed by :ID:, re-exported from lib.rs. api.rs: /graph/layout GET (missing file -> {}) + PATCH (partial upsert via OrgRewriter::upsert_property for existing nodes; render_layout_heading + layout_file_append for new; re-parse validation; flocked writer.rewrite_file). On-disk: "* LAYOUT <id>" with :X:/:Y:/:W:/:H:/:HIDDEN:/:PINNED:. New tests tests/graph_layout.rs: patch_then_get_roundtrips_one_node + two_patches_to_different_nodes_do_not_conflict, both pass. Gates (manager-verified post-merge): cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green except the pre-existing skill_routes failure; clippy -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-core -D warnings clean. Scope note: touched core/lib.rs (re-export, a necessary consequence) beyond the declared schema.rs; index.rs not needed. Worker-disclosed limitation: concurrent (not sequential) PATCH read-modify-write could lose a concurrent update to a different node - matches the existing daemon org-write pattern; sequential PATCH (the AC) is safe. Reviewer-claude-rmux pass pending.
Reviewer pass: reviewer-claude-rmux (opus-4-8), run ...f1b60a, REQUESTED_CHANGES. Confirmed good: overlay-only invariant, project scoping (matches get_graph_nodes), missing-file behavior, parser edge cases, no path-traversal. Findings: (MED) patch_graph_layout read-modify-write is OUTSIDE the writer flock so concurrent PATCHes to different nodes can lose an update - AC says "concurrent writes safe", existing tests only cover sequential; (LOW) node_id interpolated into org unvalidated (contained: bearer-auth, fixed path, overlay-only); (LOW) create writes HIDDEN/PINNED f vs partial-update leaving them None. Manager decision: fix (AC requires it; clean atomic-RMW fix exists; daemon-only so parallelizable with JH9DD). Tracked as TASK-3VEM8 (:PARENT_TASK: TASK-AQMTA, :FIX_SUBTASK: t), in flight. AQMTA -> DONE once TASK-3VEM8 lands.
Concurrency hardening TASK-3VEM8 merged 4334bbf (atomic RMW in writer + node_id validation + curation symmetry, with an 8-round concurrent-PATCH test). AQMTA AC "concurrent writes safe" now satisfied. TASK-AQMTA DONE.

* DONE TASK-3VEM8 Harden /graph/layout PATCH: atomic read-modify-write, node_id validation, curation symmetry :daemon:graph:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-3VEM8
:IMPLEMENTS: dec_WWAHT
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/api.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/writer.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/tests/graph_layout.rs
:END:

Description: Address reviewer-claude-rmux findings on TASK-AQMTA (merge 0e5b21e) so /graph/layout PATCH meets the AC requirement "concurrent writes safe".

Acceptance Criteria:
- patch_graph_layout performs read + upsert + write ATOMICALLY under the writer flock (no read outside the lock). Extend the writer task (writer.rs rewrite path) with a read-modify-write command or closure so two concurrent PATCHes to different nodes both persist.
- New test: two PATCHes to different nodes issued CONCURRENTLY (tokio::join!) from one starting file both survive (assert both entries present). Existing sequential graph_layout tests still pass.
- node_id validated against the id grammar before interpolation into the heading; invalid ids -> 400, never written.
- Create/update curation symmetry: a newly-created node omits :HIDDEN:/:PINNED: when the request omits them (GET reads None), matching the partial-update path; create and update must be consistent.
- Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon green (except the pre-existing skill_routes failure); clippy -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-core -D warnings clean.
** Evidence
Merged 4334bbf (worker commit 5a5f0e3, implementer-claude-rmux/opus-4-7). writer.rs: new WriterCommand::Mutate + FileMutate{path, transform: Box<dyn FnOnce(&str)->Result<Vec<u8>> + Send>} + mutate_file_inner - opens+flocks the path, reads, applies the transform, writes via .tmp + atomic rename, all under one exclusive flock; both PATCHes also serialize through the single writer task. patch_graph_layout runs its read+parse+upsert+write inside that closure (never reads outside the lock). is_safe_node_id (known class prefix + [A-Za-z0-9.] body) rejects injection with 400. render_layout_heading omits absent fields (curation symmetry). New tests: concurrent_patches_to_different_nodes_both_persist (8-round tokio::join!, fails pre-fix passes post-fix), invalid_node_id_is_rejected_and_not_written. Gates (manager-verified post-merge): graph_layout 4/4, daemon suite green except pre-existing skill_routes, clippy -p orgasmic-daemon -p orgasmic-core -D warnings clean. Manager review: atomic RMW correct; honors fs2::FileExt MSRV gotcha. Satisfies AQMTA concurrent-writes-safe AC.

Description: New Graph page (route /projects/$projectId/graph + sidebar nav) reusing NodeGraph, fed by the live cross-layer graph (/graph/nodes + /graph/edges) joined with /graph/layout. Implements the read-only-topology surface from dec_WWAHT.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Deterministic default band layout: superseded decisions (gray, top) -> current decisions -> architecture (leaves nested in subsystem cards) -> active tasks (bottom); glossary as a left column when enabled. Same topology yields same default positions (no Math.random/Date).
- Default curation = decisions union architecture union active-tasks; glossary + artifacts off by default with toggles.
- Backbone edges on by default (motivated_by, implements, supersedes) drawn top-down; dense edges (depends_on, dataflow, task deps) as off-by-default toggle layers; containment as nesting.
- Topology read-only: clicks inspect (existing NodeModal back-stack); no node create/delete.
- cd ui && npm run typecheck passes.

Merged 31c950d (worker commit 60f260f, implementer-claude-rmux/opus-4-7). New GraphView.tsx (579 lines): fetches /graph/nodes + /graph/edges + /graph/layout + tasks/decisions/architecture/glossary; deterministic band layout (sort-by-id localeCompare, no Math.random/Date) - superseded(gray) -> current decisions -> architecture(subsystem cards, leaves nested via React Flow parentNode+extent) -> tasks -> artifacts; glossary left column when enabled; applies /graph/layout overlay x/y per id when present. Curation toggles: Glossary off, Artifacts off, Backbone(motivated_by/implements) on, depends_on/dataflow off. Click -> NodeModal drawer-stack (dec/arch/term); tasks -> /tasks; read-only topology (drag-persist is CKRYB). NodeGraph.tsx generalized (NodeRole decision|task|glossary, superseded/taskStage data, NodeGraphCanvas extracted, ArchitectureView preserved). router graphRoute (path graph); AppShell graph nav (Network icon). Gates (manager-verified post-merge): ui typecheck exit 0; npm run build exit 0. Handled both flagged gaps: supersedes edge absent -> gray band from superseded flag; active-task filter via fetchProjectTasks. Residual: NO live visual verification (running :4848 daemon predates the graph endpoints - needs operator restart/rebuild); band width may need tuning at scale; arch-view saved viewport resets once. Reviewer-claude-rmux pass pending.
Reviewer pass: reviewer-claude-rmux (opus-4-8), run ...99a0d3. ArchitectureView regression traced (buildGraph byte-identical; only edge-anchor delta). Findings fixed in 63a0bde: F2 (MED) /graph/layout error blanked the page -> now fail-soft to {} + default band layout (also lets the page render against a daemon that predates the endpoint); F1 arch leaf edges anchor Right->Left explicitly; F3 artifacts toggle relabeled + comment corrected. F4 (buildFlowGraph determinism/done-exclusion/default-edge unit test) folded into TASK-CKRYB which modifies the same function. typecheck + npm build green. TASK-JH9DD DONE.

* DONE TASK-CKRYB UI: three-tier layout precedence, persist-on-drag, two-level reset and update nudge :ui:graph:

:END:

Description: Implement the local-first precedence and reset model from dec_WWAHT.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Per-node resolution: machine-local localStorage > shared graph_layout.org (baseline, term_FJ883) > deterministic default. Per-node granularity (a node present in the shared overlay is owned wholesale by the tier that defines it; local presence wins over shared).
- Dragging persists geometry/curation to localStorage only (never PATCHes shared, never touches git). Viewport (pan/zoom) stays machine-local (existing mechanism).
- Publish action PATCHes the shared overlay via /graph/layout (the deliberate write path; also the graph_curator agent's path).
- Two-level reset: global Reset to shared map (clear all local) and per-node snap-to-shared (right-click). 
- Quiet shared-map-updated nudge when the baseline changes for locally-overridden nodes, with a Reset affordance.
- cd ui && npm run typecheck passes.
** Evidence
Merged d46a18f (worker commit 905f833, implementer-claude-rmux/opus-4-7). api.ts: patchGraphLayout(project, entry) PATCH /graph/layout + GraphLayoutPatchEntry. GraphView.tsx: applyOverlay resolves per-node local>shared>default (owned-wholesale); buildFlowGraph exported with localOverrides; localOverrides + baselines hydrated from localStorage (graph-layout-local:<project>, graph-layout-baseline:<project>) with isLayoutEntry validation + try/catch; handleNodeDragStop writes geometry to localStorage ONLY (manager-verified: never calls patchGraphLayout, never network/git - the dec_WWAHT invariant); handleNodeContextMenu right-click snap-to-shared; handleResetAll global reset; handlePublish is the ONLY write-to-shared path; driftedNodeIds drives the quiet shared-map-updated nudge with Reset affordance. NodeGraph.tsx: onNodeDragStop/onNodeContextMenu props on NodeGraphCanvas; ArchitectureView call site unchanged. New __tests__/buildFlowGraph.test.ts (4 cases: determinism, active-task filtering [done excluded], default-curation edges, three-tier precedence) = the folded JH9DD F4. Gates: ui typecheck exit 0; npm run test 9 files / 77 tests pass incl. the 4 new. Residual: live browser UX deferred to operator restart; publish PATCHes serially. Reviewer-claude-rmux pass pending.
Reviewer (opus-4-8, run ...98103c): all invariants confirmed (drag never writes shared/git, three-tier precedence owned-wholesale, two-level reset clears baselines, no ArchitectureView regression). Findings fixed in 126c5ac: HIGH (drag coords are React Flow floats but GraphLayoutEntry x/y are i64 -> publish of a dragged node failed; now Math.round at handleNodeDragStop); MEDIUM (publish now advances baselines[id] to the published entry so a self-publish is not read as external drift; local override stays - local-first); LOW (added owned-wholesale partial-local + reorder-determinism tests -> 6 buildFlowGraph cases). typecheck + vitest green. TASK-CKRYB DONE.

* DONE TASK-E18NC Harden daemon state restoration across restart (persist + recover the supervisor run table) :daemon:reliability:recovery:restart:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-E18NC
:WRITE_SCOPE: crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/runtime.rs
:END:

Description: A daemon restart currently clears the supervisor in-memory run table (crates/orgasmic-daemon/src/supervisor.rs), so live runs / dispatch records are lost and a manager session that triggers the restart loses its own run record. This is why the "never restart the daemon from a manager session" convention existed; the operator lifted that restriction on 2026-06-16, and this task makes restarts actually safe so the lifted restriction is backed by real restoration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The supervisor persists enough run/dispatch state durably (run records + sub-states, dispatch leases, worktree/brief/last/stdout paths, owner pids) that after `orgasmic restart` the prior run table is RESTORED or CLEANLY RECONCILED, never silently dropped.
- A live manager run survives a restart (its run record is recovered) or is cleanly handed to the existing recovery flow (run list ambiguous / start_recovery_run) rather than orphaned.
- Orphan dispatches remain detectable via `orgasmic manager dispatch-status` after a restart; pre-flight manager.dispatch_started tx without a matching close are surfaced.
- Once restoration is reliable, `orgasmic restart` without `--force` no longer needs to refuse over a manager run — or it documents precisely what is restored vs reconciled.
- Test: boot a daemon, register a run/dispatch, restart the daemon, assert the run/dispatch state is restored or correctly classified (not lost).
- Cross-check: the lifted restart restriction in conventions/orgasmic-tooling.org is satisfied by this restoration.
** Evidence
Implemented recovery-aware controlled restart: daemon API now proceeds over live manager runs, pauses acquisition, emits live_manager_recovery warnings, and tests recovery classification through session-log replay. CLI restart now bypasses the plain-stop live-manager guard while plain stop still protects live manager sessions. Added temporary daemon runtime override support for local source rebuild/restart, with bundle update clearing the override so vendor updates regain control. Added shipped daemon-rebuild-restart-policy convention and linked it from the manager prompt; updated README and shipped install/update references with the correct local-source restart path.


Verification: cargo fmt; cargo clippy -p orgasmic-cli -p orgasmic-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon restart; cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon manager_session_after_restart_is_recovered_not_lost; cargo test -p orgasmic-cli. Full cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon still has the pre-existing skill_routes_include_shipped_and_user_markdown_skills failure recorded in handoff.

* DONE TASK-32R7Z Implement dec_B4147 release pipeline: local-first manual runtime builds :release:ci:build:install-distribution:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-32R7Z
:END:

Implements dec_B4147 (relates dec_YKFZX, arch_WZFAX; sibling dec_2D5BC). Split releases into two fully-manual lines: runtime/CLI built locally -> `stable`; apps -> manual CI to a separate `apps` release.

Work required:
- scripts/package-runtime.sh: add a cargo-zigbuild path for linux-x86_64 + linux-aarch64 with a pinned old glibc floor; reuse a prebuilt ui/dist; stamp a per-target `version` into runtime-manifest.json.
- crates/orgasmic-daemon/build.rs: skip `npm run build` when ui/dist/index.html already exists, so the UI builds once and is reused across all four target compiles.
- scripts/publish-runtime.sh (NEW): clean-tree guard (refuse dirty/HEAD-mismatch); build UI once; build darwin-aarch64 (native), darwin-x86_64 (rustup cross), linux-x86_64 + linux-aarch64 (zigbuild); temp-keychain-sign the darwin binaries from ~/.tauri (stable DR); smoke test (native run; x86_64 via Rosetta; linux via `docker run --version`); MERGE only the built per-target entries into stable's runtime-latest.json (with per-target version) and replace only those tarballs.
- scripts/install.sh: prefer the per-target `version` field when naming the runtime dir (so a lagging Windows entry is truthful).
- .github/workflows/runtime-bundles.yml: add single-target (Windows) mode; merge-not-clobber publish; set upload-artifact retention-days: 1.
- .github/workflows/release-macos.yml + release-android.yml: drop the `push: tags: v*` trigger; dispatch-only; publish to the `apps` release/tag; take app version from tauri.conf.json (decoupled from workspace Cargo.toml).
- delete .github/workflows/release-runtime.yml.
- revert the v0.0.5 app-file bumps (tauri.conf.json, ui/package.json, src-tauri/Cargo.toml) per the CLI=workspace / app=tauri.conf split.
- add `// orgasmic:dec_B4147` markers to touched source; add an arch_WZFAX.3 sub-node (SOURCE_PATHS + TESTS) for the publish pipeline; keep `orgasmic architecture drift` clean.
- preflight (maintainer machine): brew install zig + cargo install cargo-zigbuild + rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.

Acceptance: a local `scripts/publish-runtime.sh` builds + signs + publishes darwin x2 and linux x2 to the `stable` release with a merged manifest; a Windows CI dispatch merges its entry without clobbering; app workflows build only via dispatch to `apps`; no workflow triggers on `v*`; `orgasmic architecture drift` is clean.
** Evidence
Implemented dec_B4147 across commits 29723d5 (pipeline), 08d2e95 (rustup-toolchain fix in publish-runtime.sh), 06d0956 (gitignore /dist). Real publish of runtime 0.0.5 to the stable release: darwin-aarch64 + darwin-x86_64 (codesigned, DR identifier com.theaspirational.orgasmic), linux-x86_64 + linux-aarch64 (cargo-zigbuild, glibc 2.17 floor). publish-runtime.sh dry-run + real run exit 0; smoke tests passed (native / Rosetta / docker amd64 + arm64). Merged manifest preserved the pre-existing windows-x86_64 entry (merge-not-clobber) and stamped commit 06d09568 + per-target version. Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-cli green; -p orgasmic-daemon green except the pre-existing red integration.rs:799 skill_routes test; clippy clean; architecture drift adds zero new findings (the 2 residual arch_MK2Q2.4/.5 are pre-existing UI debt). Added arch_WZFAX.3 and taught the drift scanner to read shell sources + '# arch:' markers. Dropped the v* push trigger from all workflows, deleted release-runtime.yml, repointed app workflows + updater endpoint to the decoupled apps release, reverted the v0.0.5 app-file bumps to 0.0.4. NOTE: a real Windows single-target runtime-bundles.yml dispatch was not executed (CI cost); its merge-not-clobber path is validated locally. NOTE: stale app assets still sit on the stable release from the old coupled flow (out of scope; apps release not yet populated).

Work (ordered for safety):
1. Windows runtime refresh. Dispatch runtime-bundles.yml with tag=stable only=windows-x86_64 (gh workflow run runtime-bundles.yml -f tag=stable -f only=windows-x86_64). The stable manifest's windows-x86_64 entry is a stale build from the old flow and lacks the per-target version field (shows v? in runtime-latest.json). Windows MSVC cannot build on the Mac, so this stays CI.
2. Populate the apps release. Dispatch release-macos.yml and release-android.yml (both dispatch-only now). Confirm the apps release/tag gets the DMG + orgasmic.app.tar.gz(.sig) + latest.json and the signed APK + android-latest.json, with --latest=false so stable keeps the latest badge. Confirm the desktop updater endpoint https://github.com/theaspirational/orgasmic/releases/download/apps/latest.json resolves. DO THIS BEFORE step 3 so there is never a window with zero app downloads available.
3. Retire stale app assets from the stable release. After apps live on the apps release, delete the leftover app assets still on stable from the old coupled flow: latest.json, orgasmic.app.tar.gz, orgasmic.app.tar.gz.sig, orgasmic_stable_aarch64.dmg, orgasmic_0.0.5_5_android_aarch64.apk, android-latest.json. Leave all runtime-* assets + runtime-latest.json. Outward-facing/destructive: requires explicit maintainer approval at execution.
4. Investigate building the apps locally on this Mac (extend the local-first model). macOS app: native tauri:bundle:mac with TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY material in ~/.tauri (updater key id DB25DA5DF5271BBC). Android APK: needs NDK + Java + the upload keystore (~/.tauri/org-shell-android-upload.jks + .password). All signing material is already on-machine. Assess whether a scripts/publish-apps.sh (mirroring publish-runtime.sh: build -> sign -> publish to the apps release) is worthwhile, or whether apps should stay on CI. Output: a feasibility note, and if adopted, a new decision or a dec_B4147 amendment.

Acceptance: Windows runtime entry on stable rebuilt with a per-target version via the single-target dispatch (merge-not-clobber verified); apps release populated with current macOS + Android artifacts and the updater endpoint resolving; stable release carries runtime assets only; a written decision/feasibility note on local app builds.
** Evidence
[step 4 feasibility — local app builds on this Mac, 2026-06-23]

BOTH apps are buildable locally with ZERO provisioning — only env-var alignment. Full local-first parity with CI is achievable.

macOS app: READY NOW.
- Toolchain: tauri-cli 2.11.1, node v22.22.3, rustup target aarch64-apple-darwin.
- Build identical to CI: `npm --prefix ui run tauri:bundle:mac` => `tauri build --target aarch64-apple-darwin --bundles app,dmg`.
- Updater key on-machine: ~/.tauri/orgasmic-updater.key (id DB25DA5DF5271BBC) matches src-tauri/tauri.conf.json pubkey; password in ~/.tauri/orgasmic-updater.key.password. Export TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY / _PASSWORD like the CI secret.
- NOTE: release-macos.yml does NOT Apple-codesign/notarize the .app/.dmg — ships only the Tauri minisign updater signature. The ~/.tauri/orgasmic-codesign.p12 cert is for the RUNTIME CLI binary, not the Tauri app. A local build matches CI (no Apple notarization); notarization would be a separate additive improvement.
- Stage->apps: orgasmic_<ver>_aarch64.dmg, orgasmic.app.tar.gz(.sig), latest.json.

Android APK: READY NOW (correction — toolchain IS installed via Homebrew; earlier "missing SDK/NDK" was a stale ANDROID_HOME).
- Installed at /opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools: platforms;android-36, build-tools;35.0.0 (+36.0.0), ndk;29.0.14206865 (exact CI version), platform-tools. JDK 17 present (openjdk@17 17.0.17) — matches CI's temurin 17.
- All 4 rust android targets installed; upload keystore ~/.tauri/org-shell-android-upload.jks + .password on-machine; tauri:android:build already prepends ~/.cargo/bin to PATH.
- The ONLY blocker was env: ANDROID_HOME pointed at nonexistent ~/Library/Android/sdk. Script must set its own ANDROID_HOME/ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools, ANDROID_NDK_HOME=$SDK/ndk/29.0.14206865, JAVA_HOME=openjdk@17 (do NOT rely on the user's shell env), exactly as publish-runtime.sh self-sets ~/.cargo/bin.
- Signing/version: write src-tauri/gen/android/keystore.properties (keyAlias/password/storeFile->jks) + stamp tauri.android.conf.json versionCode = major*10000+minor*100+patch, as CI.

Recommendation: ADOPT scripts/publish-apps.sh mirroring publish-runtime.sh (clean-tree+HEAD==origin/main guard; build->sign->stage->publish to the `apps` release with --latest=false; replace only built targets). Build BOTH darwin-arm macOS app AND android-aarch64 APK locally — the Mac can fully sign and produce both. CI release-macos.yml/release-android.yml stay as dispatch fallback. Frame as a dec_B4147 AMENDMENT (same "build locally what the Mac can sign, keep CI for what it can't" principle; only the Windows RUNTIME genuinely must stay CI). Optional shell hygiene for the maintainer: fix the stale ANDROID_HOME in ~/.zshenv to /opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools, but the script must not depend on it.

* DONE TASK-Y2ZQJ Fix ZEFEY review findings: validator, canonical Org parsing, writer atomicity :artifacts:daemon:fix:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-Y2ZQJ
:IMPLEMENTS: arch_ARSPJ
:END:

One-round fix of the 8 reviewer.finding tx entries from the TASK-ZEFEY pass (run-20260703T194200). Mandatory items 1-4; LOWs 5-8 are in scope and small.

1. HIGH validate_mdx (artifacts.rs:110-144): replace the flat byte-scan with top-level structural validation - the daemon validates only what it can see without a real MDX parser: top-level block tags must be registered (BLOCK_TYPES), balanced, and well-formed; everything INSIDE a registered block's body is opaque and never scanned for unknown tags (that is where generics like Vec<Item>, f::<String>(), and DSL content legitimately live). Unclosed or malformed top-level tags are rejected. Deep/nested validation defers to the TASK-T25XQ JS/MDX parser. MANAGER RULING: empty or zero-registered-block content is REJECTED (an artifact must contain at least one registered top-level block). Use the reviewer's probe strings as the unit-test corpus with expected outcomes per this policy: <Code>let v: Vec<Item> = f::<String>();</Code> accepted; <EntityRelationship>A<Order>B</EntityRelationship> accepted (body opaque); bare prose, empty string, and unclosed <RichText rejected.
2. MEDIUM canonical parsing (artifacts.rs:148-172, 347-365): route artifact.org and reviews.org reads through orgasmic_core OrgFile::parse; delete the duplicate inline parsers. On write (comment_org_block :253-275), escape or reject comment bodies whose lines begin with * so a body can never truncate the heading structure or inject org syntax.
3. MEDIUM writer discipline: move archive_current_mdx and directory creation onto the writer path (or fold into the submit writer.transaction).
4. MEDIUM atomicity: comment add and consume each become a single writer.transaction bundling file rewrite + tx append, matching submit.
5. LOW duplicate artifact.created on retry (api.rs:8828): fold creation intent into the atomic transaction or make it idempotent.
6. LOW headerless reviews.org branch (artifacts.rs:504-511) seeds the #+title header; GET /artifacts/:id?version=N distinguishes no-such-version (404 with explicit message) from no-such-artifact.
7. LOW escape :PROMPT:/:TITLE: to single-line on write (tx.org:206 already promises this).
8. LOW WS events carry the artifact's actual state, not hardcoded "submitted".

MANAGER RULING (reviewer open question): GET /artifacts/:id current-version comment list EXCLUDES consumed comments by default; add include_consumed=true query param (archived-version views in TASK-EDQPG will use it). Adjust the round-trip test accordingly.

Gates: cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon and -p orgasmic-cli (redirect to files); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; fmt on touched files. New unit tests for the validator corpus, leading-* escaping round-trip, and atomicity (no partial state after simulated failure where testable).

All 8 findings addressed (validator rewrite scan_tag_header opaque bodies + zero-block reject; canonical OrgFile::parse for artifact.org/reviews.org + leading-* comma-escape; writer-mediated archive/dir; atomic add/consume single writer.transaction; idempotent artifact.created; typed VersionNotFound 404; single-line TITLE/PROMPT; truthful WS state) plus both manager rulings (zero-block reject, include_consumed default-exclude). Gates: manager independently re-ran cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon on the worktree = 201 lib + all integration binaries green; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings on merged tree = clean; worker: cli 71/71 (--test-threads=1), fmt clean, live HTTP production-path probe on isolated ORGASMIC_HOME. rmux report path clobbered again (TASK-B05AM recurrence); report+gates rescued from harness transcript. Reviewer pass pending. Residuals: item-5 idempotency in-process-only (cross-restart needs durable state); #+begin_/#+end_ comment-body injection not hardened -> filed as follow-up.

M1 — per-artifact async mutex (ApiState::artifact_write_lock) serializes the reviews.org read->transaction window in post_artifact_feedback + post_artifact_feedback_consume, restoring the serialization the atomic-transaction refactor dropped while keeping finding-#4 atomicity. * DONE TASK-B05AM rmux stall detector kills idle pane after orgasmic-eot and clobbers last.txt :dispatch:rmux:tooling:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-B05AM
:PRIORITY: high
:END:

Evidence: TASK-ZEFEY implementer run-20260703T185733 completed fully (gates green, commit 9a5f043, printed its report and the [orgasmic-eot:run-...] marker at 22:21), yet .orgasmic/tmp/dispatch/task-ZEFEY/task-ZEFEY-last.txt contains only "stall_timeout_exceeded" and the manager had to rescue-close the dispatch. Cause: after eot the interactive pane sits idle; the rmux driver's stall detector eventually fires, kills the session, and writes the stall reason to last.txt instead of preserving the already-emitted worker report.

Expected: the eot marker should finalize the run (record implementer.done, snapshot the report into last.txt) BEFORE any stall accounting; stall_timeout_exceeded must never overwrite a completed run's report. Same family as the old codex-acp stall overrides (TASK-HCFBT.4) but rmux-specific and post-completion.

Acceptance: a scripted rmux dispatch that emits eot then idles past the stall window ends with implementer.done recorded, the worker report preserved in last.txt, and no stall_timeout_exceeded surfaced for the completed run.
** Evidence
[2026-07-03] FIXED (clobber acceptance met): commit ed6a6fc. Root cause pinned empirically from the real ZEFEY session (dispatch-TASK-ZEFEY-implementer-20260703T185733.jsonl): line 9267 run_complete{summary:'stall_timeout_exceeded'} immediately followed by line 9268 release{reason:'stall_timeout_exceeded',outcome:failed}. The rmux/tmux driver's release(reason) (rmux.rs:1384) synthesizes RunComplete{summary:reason} when the run never emitted its own terminal event (eot marker missed by the render stream, then stall detector fired); dispatch_last_summary_from_session ranked that synthesized sentinel above the real transcript (9263 assistant chunks present but shadowed), so last.txt became 'stall_timeout_exceeded'. Fix: in dispatch_last_summary_from_session, when the run_complete summary echoes the release reason, drop it and fall back to the assistant transcript tail (32 KiB cap). Driver/supervisor unchanged (their tests still assert the Release lifecycle reason + the reason passed to release()). Teeth-verified regression test stall_release_synthetic_run_complete_does_not_shadow_worker_report (fails without the fix) + genuine_run_complete_summary_is_still_preferred guards the normal path. Gates: daemon 204 lib + integration green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. SPLIT: the daemon-restart-mid-run 'no last.txt at all' mode (observed on Y2ZQJ, reattach kills the watcher) is a distinct mechanism needing RunMeta path persistence -> filed as TASK-567JG.

* DONE TASK-2ZQSB Artifactor worker kind, prompt spec, and generation launch path :artifacts:supervisor:prompt-studio:
:PROPERTIES:
:DEPENDS_ON: TASK-ZEFEY
:ID: TASK-2ZQSB
:IMPLEMENTS: arch_045Q0
:END:

Add WorkerKind::Artifactor and the prompt spec shipped/prompt-studio/prompt-specs/artifact-generator.org (default generating instructions + block-vocabulary reference + slots for subject nodes, user prompt, and regen context). Implement POST /artifacts/generate {nodes, prompt} and POST /artifacts/:id/regenerate {extraPrompt?}: create/advance the artifact record, assemble context (subject node content, graph neighborhood, full repo read; on regen also prior artifact.mdx + current-version comments with authors/resolution + extra prompt), and launch through the existing dispatch machinery under the synthetic lease id artifact.generate:{artifact_id}. The lease is the per-artifact mutex; the artifact enters regenerating state for the run's duration. Worker write path is orgasmic artifact submit only.

Acceptance: pressing generate on a real node (via curl) produces a validated artifact.mdx authored by an actual worker run visible in orgasmic run list, with artifact.created/.submitted tx entries; a second generate on the same artifact while one runs is refused by the lease; a deliberately invalid submit is retried by the worker, never written.
** Notes
[2026-07-03] UNBLOCKED. The manager hold (do not build on the artifact store until it is hardened + race-safe) is lifted: TASK-ZEFEY (store) + TASK-Y2ZQJ (8 reviewer findings) + M1 concurrency fix are all DONE and reviewer-verified (store now serializes reviews.org RMW per artifact). Cleared to start: artifactor worker kind + generation launch path (arch_045Q0).
** Evidence
[2026-07-03] Landed: merge 6f8b88b (worker c37a5c0, implementer-claude-rmux sonnet-5, +1368/11 files). WorkerKind::Artifactor + shipped/workers/artifactor.org + artifact-generator prompt spec + slots; POST /artifacts/generate + /:id/regenerate launch a real worker run via spawn_worker_run under the artifact.generate:{id} lease (per-artifact regen mutex, LeaseHeld->409); submit-only write path; release-watcher reverts a stuck-regenerating artifact (only-if-still-regenerating, reuses the Y2ZQJ M1 write lock) with artifact.generation.failed tx. Worker self-caught+fixed a lease-ordering bug via live probe (mutate-before-lease closed comments + spurious tx on a 409'd regenerate) -> gated on lease acquire first. Manager diff review: lease-first ordering verified in both handlers; regenerate re-reads reviews.org fresh under the M1 lock before consume+flip. Gates (merged tree): core green (126+29), daemon 216 lib + integration green, cli 100% single-threaded (parallel 1-fail = known SJQ9V flake, no cli code touched), clippy -D warnings clean, architecture drift clean, parse_errors 0. Live probe (isolated serve --port 0): artifactor run visible in run list (task_id=artifact.generate:ART-HHPSK), artifact.created tx, concurrent regenerate->409 zero mutation, invalid submit->422 no write, valid retry->validated mdx+version bump+artifact.submitted, comment closed out (CONSUMED_CIDS), force-release->watcher revert both cases. B05AM fix validated: last.txt landed clean after a 56m run (no clobber). Qualified gap: end-to-end LLM turn not driven (sandbox claude had no creds); downstream contract proven by direct submit. arch_ARSPJ prose updated with artifact.generation.failed. Reviewer pass pending.
[2026-07-03] Reviewer pass (reviewer-claude-rmux opus-4-8 xhigh, run-20260703T233904, ~10.5m): VERDICT ship-with-fixes. Verified clean: lease-first ordering (both handlers), release-watcher revert safety, submit-only write path (consistent w/ architector/reviewer/griller precedent), M1 lock reuse (no deadlock, bounded), context assembly (slots filled). Findings: M1 MEDIUM (pre-existing: post_artifact_submit lacked the per-artifact lock) + L1/L2 LOW. All fixed inline, commit 510d8d9: submit now wrapped in artifact_write_lock (teeth-verified test artifact_concurrent_resubmits_do_not_lose_version_bumps — 8 concurrent resubmits collapse to 1 version without the lock); consumed_cids predicate matched to consume_all_open_comments (!(resolved&&consumed)); doc comment lists all 4 writers. Gates: daemon 217 lib + integration green, clippy clean, fmt clean. last.txt landed clean (B05AM fix holding). Release-watcher flip-vs-instant-crash = same class as TASK-567JG restart gap (out of scope).

* DONE TASK-RH4M5 Hot-session artifact generation: rmux artifactor default + live followup routing :artifacts:daemon:runtime:
:PROPERTIES:
:DEPENDS_ON: TASK-RCWFF
:ID: TASK-RH4M5

Make the hot session the default generation loop (dec_S02E2, arch_045Q0.2).

- Add an rmux-driven artifactor worker record (DRIVER rmux, HARNESS claude) and make it the default for artifact generation; keep the acp-stdio record as an alternate.
- Generate launches the run as today, but the run persists after the first submit (session stays in the pane; artifact flips to submitted, run stays leased).
- Regenerate branches on the lease: a live run holding artifact.generate:{id} gets the followup routed via send_input — version-N close-out (archive + mark consumed) happens exactly as the cold path, then the followup payload (consumed comments with authors/anchors + extra prompt) is pasted as the next turn. No live lease-holder -> cold spawn, unchanged.
- State discipline per round: regenerating on followup send, submitted on submit; the per-artifact write lock covers the new branch; release-watcher revert semantics unchanged (guards session death mid-round).
- Session lifetime: explicit end (release) plus an idle timeout (config default); daemon restart orphans the session -> next round cold-spawns (accepted, same class as TASK-567JG).

Acceptance: two consecutive grilling rounds on one artifact reuse one run_id and round 2 pays no spawn/context-assembly cost; killing the session mid-round reverts to submitted@vN via the watcher; after a daemon restart the next regenerate cold-spawns cleanly; tx log shows regenerated/submitted per round with correct versions.

Filed the tooling bug separately.

** Notes
[2026-07-03] Carried from the TASK-Y2ZQJ reviewer pass (finding L2): the daemon-side validate_mdx is a TOP-LEVEL STRUCTURAL validator only — it locates a registered block's body by its literal matching </Name> close string and treats the body as opaque. Consequence: a block body containing its own literal </Name> terminates the scan early, and nested same-name blocks are not tracked. This is by design (dec_GPV4G: MDX is opaque to the daemon). The real MDX/JSX parser lives HERE in T25XQ (the block renderer): it must handle nesting, literal-close-string-in-body, and deep validation. Do not assume the daemon validated block internals.

Build the member layer per arch_Z8CW2 as revised by dec_KF2MR (grants, not a role column; actions, not role checks): $ORGASMIC_HOME/user/auth/members.org records (name, token hash, :GRANTS: <project-or-*>=<role>, exact project beats *, no grant = no access); orgasmic member add/revoke/list (add mints *=<role>, repeatable --grant <project>=<role> for scoped mints); POST /login (paste token -> long-lived named session cookie); session middleware resolving member identity + grants on every request; ONE authorization seam require(session, project, action) with a static role->capability table (viewer / editor / artifacts + admin = daemon token) and a route->action map across the whole API surface, not just /artifacts/*; GET /me capability snapshot; WS gated in both directions (sessions.watch + coarse event-kind filter on subscribe; sessions.interact -- admin-only v1 -- on input); capability-driven UI (nav + affordances from /me; live rmux streams render read-only xterm for members). Then the comment surface: selection-to-pin capture reusing the PlanCommentAnchor shape (textQuote, targetNodeId/path, canvas coords), POST /artifacts/:id/comments, threads rendered with member names in ArtifactView, live over WS. Two-axis thread state: open/resolved settable by members; consumed only via regeneration.

Acceptance: two browsers, two members (one editor, one viewer) minted via CLI, both sign in at /login; the viewer pins a comment to a diagram node and it appears in the editor's browser over WS under the viewer's name without refresh; the viewer sees no Generate/Regenerate affordances and the daemon refuses those routes for the viewer session; the viewer opens a live rmux session stream read-only and the daemon rejects their input; a third member minted with --grant <project>=artifacts sees only the Artifacts surface (nav and routes), gets 403 on the tasks/graph APIs and on any project not in their grants, and their WS receives only artifact.* events; revoking the viewer kills their session; secrets are absent from the repo tree (hash-only in members.org, host-local).

* DONE TASK-EDQPG Regeneration loop: version archive, comment consumption, lock, and version browser :artifacts:daemon:ui:

Close the loop end to end: Regenerate button (admin/editor) with optional extra-prompt field -> POST /artifacts/:id/regenerate archives the closing version (versions/vN.mdx + its comments marked consumed) and launches the artifactor with prior artifact + all current-version comments (authors, resolution state, anchors) + extra prompt in context; artifact regenerating state locks the comment box with a banner in every open browser (WS); on submit, the new version renders with a clean comment surface. Version switcher in ArtifactView browses archived versions together with their comment threads. artifact.regenerated tx entries complete the audit trail.

Acceptance (full two-member smoke, per the feature verification): editor generates a visual-plan artifact on a task node; viewer pins comments; editor presses Regenerate with an extra prompt; commenting locks live in the viewer's browser; when the run completes, versions/v1.mdx exists with the viewer's comment archived+consumed, v2 renders clean, the version switcher shows v1 with its thread, and the tx log tells the whole story in order.
Note (dec_S02E2): regenerate may now route to a live hot-session run instead of cold-spawning (TASK-RH4M5). The UI contract here is unchanged — regenerating state, lock banner, version switcher — but the loop should feel conversational when a session is live.
** Evidence
[2026-07-05 Sun] DONE. Merged --no-ff 45c4546 (worker commit e02307a, implementer-claude-rmux claude-sonnet-5 high, run-20260705T073712, ~26m).

The regeneration loop was ~90% pre-built by 2ZQSB/T25XQ/RH4M5; a manager Explore gap analysis scoped EDQPG to two real gaps + one tightening, all landed:
1. Extra-prompt UI: RegenerateArtifactDialog (optional Textarea) gated behind canGenerate; regenerate() threads {extraPrompt} to POST /artifacts/:id/regenerate. Backend/client/type already accepted extraPrompt.
2. Archived-version comment threads: load_artifact_detail scopes comments to c.version==N for an archived view (version Some(v), v != current), returning that version own consumed thread regardless of include_consumed, while the current/live view is unchanged (include_consumed governs; criterion-5 invariant). Client sends include_consumed=true only for archived views.
3. assemble_regen_context now folds each comment resolved flag into the worker context.

GATES (manager independently re-ran on merged main): cargo test -p orgasmic-daemon = 255 lib + all integration binaries pass, 0 failed, including 3 new EDQPG tests (get_artifact_archived_version_returns_that_versions_own_comment_thread; get_artifact_archived_version_over_real_http_returns_scoped_thread; load_artifact_detail_scopes_comments_to_archived_version). cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean. rustfmt clean on touched files. ui: npm run typecheck clean, vitest 133 passed (15 files, +7), npm run build green. Worker also ran a live real-HTTP round-trip (isolated Daemon::run, OS port) proving archived-version GET returns v1 body + only the consumed v1 comment while the current view returns v2 body + only the open v2 comment.

Review: manager inline (operator-accepted review gate [2026-07-04]); diff correct, criterion-5 invariant tested, include_consumed UI logic verified safe (never sent for the current view, so no consumed leak).

QUALIFIED: the full live two-BROWSER two-MEMBER smoke with a real LLM-driven regenerate (goal Reached-When) exercises pre-existing WS-lock/hot-session machinery (already verified in 2ZQSB/RH4M5/DJGM5) and needs LLM creds; deferred to operator acceptance. The EDQPG code delta itself is fully covered deterministically by the new real-HTTP daemon test + vitest.

* DONE TASK-VXXQC Make `orgasmic update` launchd-aware (or set SO_REUSEADDR on the daemon listener) :bug:cli:daemon:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-VXXQC
:END:

`orgasmic update` (any channel) fails and rolls back on hosts where the daemon runs under a launchd LaunchAgent with KeepAlive=true (the maintainer Mac: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/orgasmic.daemon.plist, label orgasmic.daemon). update stops the running daemon and starts a new one directly, but launchd immediately respawns the OLD daemon and reoccupies 0.0.0.0:4848, so the new binary hits `bind: Address already in use` -> "daemon exited before becoming ready after 20s" -> runtime links rolled back. The tarball downloads fine into ~/.orgasmic/runtimes/<version>-<target>/; only the `current` symlink is reverted.

Observed 2026-07-06 updating to 0.0.8-nightly.20260706.1783315823. The new binary boots cleanly on the production home (2 projects, ~1386 tx, parse_errors 0) in ~1.2s when started once on a drained port — the failure is purely the launchd/KeepAlive + EADDRINUSE port race, not a state/migration issue. Compounding factors: (a) a killed serving daemon leaves accepted-connection sockets in TIME_WAIT and the listener does not set SO_REUSEADDR, so a respawn during TIME_WAIT re-fails; (b) `orgasmic status` auto-spawns a competing daemon when it cannot reach one, which re-triggers the race during recovery.

Manual workaround that works: launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/orgasmic.daemon -> pkill -9 -f "orgasmic serve" -> poll lsof :4848 until drained -> ln -sfn the new runtime dir to ~/.orgasmic/current -> launchctl bootstrap the plist -> verify via curl (not `orgasmic status`).

Fix options (one or both):
1. Make orgasmic update restart launchd-aware: detect the managing LaunchAgent, launchctl bootout it (KeepAlive stops), swap the runtime link, then launchctl bootstrap it — instead of stopping/starting the daemon process directly.
2. Set SO_REUSEADDR (consider SO_REUSEPORT) on the daemon TCP listener so a fresh bind succeeds while a prior socket is in TIME_WAIT, closing the race window regardless of how the restart is driven.
Also consider: orgasmic status should not auto-spawn a daemon during an update/restart window (or should be race-safe).

Acceptance: `orgasmic update --channel nightly` succeeds end-to-end on a launchd-managed host (daemon returns on the new binary, KeepAlive intact, parse_errors 0) with no manual intervention; add a test or documented manual verification.
** Evidence
Root cause: stop_macos_launch_agent discarded launchctl bootout's result (let _ =); a failed/slow unload left the LaunchAgent loaded+KeepAlive-supervised, so killing the pid directly let launchd respawn it before the restart's own bootstrap, losing the EADDRINUSE race. Ruled out the SO_REUSEADDR theory: empirically confirmed tokio's TcpListener::bind already sets SO_REUSEADDR by default on this host (reuseaddr_probe test), so that was not the gap.

Fix (crates/orgasmic-cli/src/daemon_service.rs, commit 75ddb4e): stop_macos_launch_agent now checks the job is actually loaded before bootout, requires bootout to succeed, and polls (bounded 5s) to confirm the unload completed before returning -- bailing with a clear error instead of silently racing a respawn. Not-currently-loaded (e.g. a bare orgasmic serve) is left untouched (no regression for non-launchd-managed daemons). Two new unit tests cover the bounded-wait helper via an injectable probe.

Gates: cargo build -p orgasmic-cli clean; targeted + workspace cargo test green (one pre-existing unrelated flake under full-workspace concurrency, doctor::tests::daemon_staleness_warns_for_git_commits_since_boot, already tracked as TASK-SJQ9V -- passes standalone); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean on the touched file.

LIVE VERIFICATION (real production host, operator-approved): published nightly 0.0.8-nightly.20260706.1783321648 (commit 75ddb4e) via scripts/publish-runtime.sh --channel nightly (dry-run then live, all 4 targets + smoke green), then ran orgasmic update --channel nightly for real against this Mac's production ~/.orgasmic (launchd-managed, KeepAlive=true). Result: exit 0, no manual intervention. Daemon came back with a new pid (22998 -> 17253) and boot_id, on the new version, tx_count preserved (1387), parse_errors 0, exactly one launchd job in state=running, port 4848 bound exactly once by the new pid, cloudflared tunnel reconnected on its own. Old runtimes auto-pruned per dec_B4147 retention.

Gotcha discovered mid-verification (recorded in gotchas.org + memory): overwriting an in-use codesigned macOS binary IN PLACE (same inode) causes ALL subsequent execs of that path to be silently SIGKILLed (exit 137, zero output) even though codesign --verify reports it valid on disk; delete-then-recreate (fresh inode) fixes it immediately. Not related to the shipped fix itself -- hit only because verification manually swapped the production binary file to test before the real nightly existed.

Residual, out of scope for this task: the 'orgasmic status auto-spawns during an update window' compounding factor from the original filing was not addressed (no reproduction needed it once the launchd race was closed); left as a known follow-up if it resurfaces.

* DONE TASK-ZNQ8F Fix parses_every_shipped_worker count assertion (19 -> actual) :tests:housekeeping:
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: TASK-ZNQ8F
:END:

crates/orgasmic-core/tests/fixtures.rs:543 asserts count==19 shipped workers, but shipped/workers/ has 20 (.org files); the test is RED on main (verified 2026-07-04, independent of TASK-DJGM5). A 20th worker (e.g. reviewer-claude-rmux.org) was added without bumping the count. Fix: confirm all 20 are intended-valid workers, then bump the assertion to 20 (or exclude the non-worker file if 19 is intentional). Trivial but currently breaks 'cargo test -p orgasmic-core'.
** Evidence
